Core structs:

```cpp
struct FAscentiaTargetCandidate
{
    TWeakObjectPtr<AActor> Actor;
    FName SocketOrWeakPointName;
    FVector WorldLocation;
    FVector ScreenLocation;
    float Distance;
    float ViewAngleDegrees;
    float Score;
    FGameplayTagContainer TargetTags;
    bool bLineOfSight;
    bool bIsHostile;
    bool bIsWeakPoint;
};

struct FAscentiaTargetingRequest
{
    FGuid RequestId;
    TWeakObjectPtr<AActor> SourceActor;
    FGameplayTag TargetingProfileTag;
    FGameplayTag AbilityOrActionTag;
    FVector SourceLocation;
    FVector AimDirection;
    FVector2D InputVector;
    bool bRequireLineOfSight;
    bool bAllowWeakPoints;
    bool bServerValidated;
};

struct FAscentiaTargetingResult
{
    FGuid RequestId;
    bool bSucceeded;
    FAscentiaTargetCandidate PrimaryTarget;
    TArray<FAscentiaTargetCandidate> Candidates;
    FGameplayTag FailureReasonTag;
    TArray<FText> Warnings;
};

struct FAscentiaTargetLockState
{
    bool bLocked;
    TWeakObjectPtr<AActor> LockedActor;
    FName LockedSocketOrWeakPoint;
    FGameplayTag LockModeTag;
    float LockAgeSeconds;
    float LostLineOfSightSeconds;
    float LastValidDistance;
    FVector LastKnownTargetLocation;
};

struct FAscentiaCombatCameraProfile
{
    FGameplayTag ProfileTag;
    float ArmLength;
    float MinArmLength;
    float MaxArmLength;
    float CameraLagSpeed;
    float RotationLagSpeed;
    float TargetScreenOffsetX;
    float TargetScreenOffsetY;
    float CollisionProbeSize;
    float WallSlideRecoverySpeed;
    float LockBreakAngleDegrees;
    bool bIgnoreOwningPawn;
    bool bUseTargetFraming;
};

struct FAscentiaAimAssistProfile
{
    FGameplayTag ProfileTag;
    float MaxAssistAngleDegrees;
    float MaxAssistDistance;
    float StickinessSeconds;
    float MagnetismStrength;
    float FrictionStrength;
    bool bAppliesToMouse;
    bool bAppliesToGamepad;
    FGameplayTag AccessibilityGroupTag;
};
```

Subsystem and component boundary:

```cpp
UCLASS()
class UAscentiaTargetingSubsystem : public UGameInstanceSubsystem
{
    GENERATED_BODY()

public:
    FAscentiaTargetingResult ResolveTarget(
        const FAscentiaTargetingRequest& Request) const;

    void ResolveTargetAsync(
        const FAscentiaTargetingRequest& Request,
        FAscentiaTargetingResultDelegate Completion);

    FAscentiaTargetingAuditReport AuditTargetingProfiles(
        const FAscentiaTargetingAuditRequest& Request) const;
};

UCLASS(ClassGroup=(Ascentia), meta=(BlueprintSpawnableComponent))
class UAscentiaTargetLockComponent : public UActorComponent
{
    GENERATED_BODY()

public:
    bool TryAcquireLock(FGameplayTag TargetingProfileTag);
    bool TryCycleLock(int32 Direction);
    void ClearLock(FGameplayTag ReasonTag);
    FAscentiaTargetLockState GetLockState() const;
};
```

Rules:

- Targeting truth is not camera truth. The camera may frame a target, but damage,
  projectile spawn, weak-point hit, and loot/interaction authority must consume a
  server-valid targeting result or a locally predicted result with validation.
- Lock-on is a mode, not a permanent target. Line of sight, distance, target
  death, target phase, cinematic control, fog-wall state, and player input can
  clear or downgrade lock.
- Free camera, soft melee assist, hard lock, ranged aim, throwable arc, spell
  ground target, and boss weak-point targeting are separate profiles.
- Mouse and gamepad aim assist are different policies. Gamepad may use friction
  and magnetism; mouse should default to minimal or no magnetism.
- Target cycling must be deterministic and explainable: screen side, angle,
  distance, hostility, visibility, and weak-point priority must be visible in
  the audit report.
- Camera collision ignores the owning pawn and weapon attachments but collides
  with level geometry and hard objects. It should slide/recover rather than pop.
- Accessibility settings can reduce camera lag, shake, motion blur, lock-on
  camera strength, reticle opacity, aim assist strength, and target switching
  speed.
- Bosses expose target sockets/weak points through data, not one-off Blueprint
  branches.

Designer-facing tools:

```text
EUW_AscentiaTargetingProfileLab
  Runs targeting profiles from selected pawn/camera fixtures and shows candidate
  scores, filter reasons, line-of-sight failures, and chosen target.

EUW_AscentiaLockOnFixtureRunner
  Replays lock acquire/cycle/break scenarios against arena enemies, boss weak
  points, stairs, ledges, walls, dead targets, and late-spawned enemies.

EUW_AscentiaCombatCameraRigLab
  Previews free, lock-on, boss, and ranged camera profiles with spring-arm
  collision, wall slide, target framing, and accessibility scaling.

EUW_AscentiaAimAssistTuningLab
  Compares mouse/gamepad aim assist, friction, magnetism, stickiness, dead zones,
  sensitivity, and accessibility groups.

EUW_AscentiaTargetingPresetAudit
  Scans Gameplay Targeting presets, custom tasks, Blueprint tasks, required data
  stores, async usage, and missing debug draw support.
```

Evidence:

```text
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P11/Targeting/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P11/LockOn/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P11/CombatCamera/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P11/AimAssist/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P11/TargetingPresets/{RunId}/report.json
```

Proof matrix:

```text
Targeting_LockOn_AcquireNearestVisibleHostile
Targeting_LockOn_CycleLeftRight
Targeting_LockOn_BreakOnDeathDistanceLOS
Targeting_MeleeSoftAssist_NoHardCameraLock
Targeting_RangedAim_GamepadAssist
Targeting_RangedAim_MouseNoMagnetism
Targeting_ThrowableArc_GroundOrActor
Targeting_SpellProjectile_LockAssist
Targeting_BossWeakPoint_SocketSelection
Camera_Free_CollidesAndSlidesWalls
Camera_LockOn_FramesTargetWithoutSelfCollision
Camera_BossArena_NoWallPop
Accessibility_AimAssistAndCameraStrengthScale
Packaged_TargetingProfiles_NoMissingPresetTasks
```

Implementation tickets:

```text
P11T-001 Targeting profile/request/result structs and resolver facade
  Add Ascentia targeting structs, profile assets, failure tags, and a resolver
  that can wrap native traces now and Gameplay Targeting presets later.

P11T-002 Target lock component extraction
  Move lock acquire/cycle/break state into `UAscentiaTargetLockComponent` with
  Blueprint-visible state and HUD-friendly view data.

P11T-003 Gameplay Targeting preset bridge
  Add preset references, source context construction, immediate request wrapper,
  async request wrapper, and debug/audit output.

P11T-004 Combat camera profile assets and spring-arm policy
  Add camera profile assets, camera collision/wall-slide policy, target framing,
  boss/ranged profiles, and accessibility scaling.

P11T-005 Aim assist input modifier/profile bridge
  Add mouse/gamepad aim assist profiles, Enhanced Input modifier hooks, dead-zone
  tuning, and settings rows.

P11T-006 Boss weak-point and target socket contract
  Add target socket/weak-point data, boss target profile rows, and selection
  proof for phase-specific weak points.

P11T-007 Designer targeting labs
  Add targeting profile, lock-on fixture, combat camera, aim assist, and preset
  audit EUWs.

P11T-008 Dashboard and package gates
  Block milestone target/camera claims when target profiles, camera collision,
  preset tasks, weak points, or accessibility scaling evidence is missing.
```

First next package:

1. Implement P11T-001 and P11T-002 before expanding ranged, throwable, spell, or
   boss weak-point targeting.
2. Implement P11T-004 before more camera tuning is buried in pawn Blueprints.
3. Implement P11T-003 once the native resolver facade is stable enough to map to
   Gameplay Targeting presets without changing gameplay callers.

## 7. Items, Affixes, Loot, and Equipment

### 7.1 Player-facing goal

Items should be the long-term build engine. The player should compare base item,
quality, affixes, requirements, weight, moveset, skill, damage schools, status,
defense, and utility effects. Good gear should create choices, not only larger
numbers.

### 7.2 Current Ascentia state

Working pieces:

- `UAscentiaItemDefinition`, `UAscentiaAffixDefinition`,
  `UAscentiaLootTableAsset` as PrimaryDataAssets.
- `UAscentiaItemRegistrySubsystem` registers native fallbacks and scans primary
  assets.
- `UAscentiaLootSubsystem` rolls deterministic and weighted entries, applies
  affixes, and generates item names.
- `UAscentiaInventoryComponent` owns item stacks and equipment slots.
- `UAscentiaInventoryScreen` provides native UI.
- `FAscentiaItemDefinitionRow`, `FAscentiaAffixRow`,
  `FAscentiaWeaponTypeRow`, `FAscentiaLootTableRow`, and related row types exist
  in `AscentiaTypes.h`.

Core gap:

- row structs and PrimaryDataAssets are not fully reconciled;
- native fallback registry still carries too much content;
- affix power is still too narrow;
- equip effects exist in concept but are not yet the full source of item power.

### 7.3 AAA target behavior

The item system should be built as two linked truths:

1. Bulk numeric truth in DataTables.
2. Object/reference truth in PrimaryDataAssets.

DataTables are right for hundreds of weapons, armor pieces, affixes, status
payloads, skill scalars, enemy stats, loot weights, and progression curves.
PrimaryDataAssets are right for assets with object references, soft references,
asset bundles, per-content editor notes, and identity.

The registry should ingest both into one runtime view:

```text
FAscentiaResolvedItemDefinition
- ItemTag
- SourceRowName
- SourceAssetId
- DisplayName
- Category
- Slot
- QualityFloor
- Weight
- Requirements
- WeaponTypeTag
- BaseDamageProfile
- DefenseProfile
- GrantedEffects
- GrantedAbilitySet
- Mesh/Icon/Grip
- LootRules
- VendorRules
```

### 7.4 Affix engine

Affixes should become modifier-tagged GE producers:

```text
FAscentiaResolvedAffix
- AffixTag
- PrefixOrSuffix
- DisplayPattern
- MinMagnitude
- MaxMagnitude
- ModifierTags
- RequiredItemCategories
- RequiredWeaponFamilies
- RequiredQuality
- Weight
- GameplayEffectClass
- SetByCallerMagnitudes
- ExclusionGroups
- RequiredWorldTier
```

Example modifier tags:

```text
Ascentia.Mod.Damage.Slash.Flat
Ascentia.Mod.Damage.Fire.Percent
Ascentia.Mod.Status.Poison.Buildup
Ascentia.Mod.Resistance.Frost.Flat
Ascentia.Mod.Skill.AshenCleave.Cooldown.Percent
Ascentia.Mod.Resource.Breath.Regen.Flat
Ascentia.Mod.Weight.Reduction.Percent
Ascentia.Mod.OnHit.Bleed.Chance
```

This directly supports Diablo-style meaningful choices: one item can be locally
strong but globally awkward because its modifier tags do not support the current
build threshold, resource loop, resistance plan, or skill identity.

### 7.5 Equipment to combat profile

Combat should not ask inventory for one damage bonus. Combat should ask for an
effective profile.

```text
FAscentiaWeaponProfile
- SourceItemTag
- WeaponFamily
- WeaponTypeTag
- MovesetTags
- PairedMovesetTags
- SkillTag
- BaseDamagePackets
- DamageSchoolMultipliers
- StatusPayloads
- PoiseDamage
- GuardDamage
- CritProfile
- ScalingProfile
- ResourceCostProfile
- AnimationSetTag
- ContactShapeProfile
- CueProfile
```

The profile is built from:

- item definition row;
- weapon type row;
- rolled affixes;
- equip GameplayEffects;
- stance/two-hand/off-hand state;
- stat thresholds/passives;
- world-tier modifiers;
- difficulty/accessibility modifiers.

The combat component consumes the profile to build attack packets. Inventory
does not calculate combat math. It provides equipped item facts.

### 7.6 Designer surface

Designers should see:

- `DT_Items_*`
- `DT_WeaponTypes`
- `DT_Affixes`
- `DT_LootTables`
- `PDA_Item_*` only where object references are needed;
- `PDA_LootTable_*` for curated encounter/chest tables;
- `PDA_AbilitySet_*` for granted abilities;
- item editor validation report:
  - missing icon;
  - missing mesh;
  - invalid slot/category pairing;
  - missing weapon type;
  - invalid affix pool;
  - invalid requirement;
  - invalid quality;
  - missing cue profile;
  - uncookable soft reference.

### 7.7 Loot pipeline

Loot table execution should be:

```text
LootSource context
  -> LootTableTag
  -> table rows by world tier, source type, enemy family, biome, objective state
  -> deterministic entries
  -> weighted entries
  -> quality roll
  -> affix count roll
  -> affix pool filters
  -> stack build
  -> name generation
  -> drop presentation
  -> inventory/loot window handoff
  -> telemetry
```

The loot subsystem should remain deterministic for tests by seed. Designer
preview tooling should expose "roll this table 1000 times" and show distribution.

### 7.8 Validation

Production validation rules:

- all item tags unique;
- all row names stable;
- all loot entries reference valid item or nested table tags;
- all affixes have at least one modifier tag;
- affix min <= max;
- affix pool is reachable by at least one item category;
- no production item uses placeholder mesh/icon unless explicitly allowed;
- stack limits are sane;
- vendor value and sell price obey economy rules;
- equipment slot matches category;
- weapon family has moveset and animation fallback;
- primary assets scan path includes the asset;
- soft references resolve in editor and cook.

### 7.9 Data pipeline implementation contract

This is the first fully expanded pipeline contract because it unblocks nearly
every later phase: P13 stat/build depth, P14 weapon breadth, P15 spell content,
P16 itemization/economy, P17 region loot, and P18 enemy rewards.

#### 7.9.1 Decision

Ascentia should keep `UAscentiaItemRegistrySubsystem` as the gameplay-facing
facade for now, but it should be reshaped around Data Registry principles:
ordered data sources, stable row IDs, tag-based lookup, explicit fallback and
override rules, and a resolved read model.

Do not replace it immediately with UE's Data Registry plugin. The local registry
already bridges PrimaryDataAssets, native fallbacks, loot, inventory, and UI.
Switching facades before the row schema stabilizes would add churn without
solving the authoring problem. The correct move is:

1. make the local registry behave like a production data registry;
2. keep the public lookup API stable;
3. leave a future adapter path to `UDataRegistry` if row volume, async lookup, or
   editor dropdown tooling demands it.

#### 7.9.2 Source of truth

The tracked source path should be:

```text
Ascentia Data.xlsx or sheet exports
  -> tracked CSV files
  -> generated UDataTable assets
  -> resolved registry records
  -> gameplay/UI read models
```

The `.xlsx` is useful for editing, but CSV is the version-control truth because
it diffs cleanly. Generated `.uasset` DataTables are cook/runtime artifacts.
They can be regenerated from CSV and should not be the only surviving copy of a
row.

#### 7.9.3 Ownership

Programmers own:

- row structs that inherit from `FTableRowBase`;
- native validation rules;
- registry ingest and precedence;
- save-compatible IDs;
- runtime read models;
- commandlet/report generation.

Technical Designers own:

- GameplayTag taxonomy;
- row naming rules;
- import/export scripts;
- editor utility widgets;
- validation triage.

Designers own:

- spreadsheet values;
- DataTable rows after import;
- PrimaryDataAsset object references;
- GameplayAbility Blueprint subclasses;
- GameplayCue Blueprint subclasses;
- montage/cue/audio/VFX assignments;
- tuning curves.

No production item, skill, boss move, loot table, affix, or enemy reward should
require C++ after its native contract exists.

#### 7.9.4 Precedence

Use one explicit precedence stack:

```text
native fallback
  < DataTable row stats/rules
  < PrimaryDataAsset object references and marked overrides
  < runtime item instance rolls
```

Native fallback is only the playable floor. It keeps smoke tests alive when no
content is synced. It is not the design source.

DataTables own bulk numeric and rule content:

- item category, slot, weight, value, stack limit;
- stat requirements;
- weapon family/type/moveset tags;
- base damage profile;
- defense profile;
- affix ranges and pools;
- loot weights;
- skill costs/cooldowns/ranges;
- enemy stat curves.

PrimaryDataAssets own object references and per-asset presentation:

- icon;
- static or skeletal mesh;
- grip/socket transform;
- Niagara trail;
- GameplayAbility class if object-bound;
- GameplayCue or cue profile references where tag-only is insufficient;
- Designer notes;
- explicit handcrafted override fields.

Runtime item instances own rolled state:

- item seed;
- rolled quality;
- selected prefix/suffix affix tags;
- rolled affix magnitudes;
- durability or corruption later;
- socket/gem contents later;
- unique instance ID if needed for save/transactions.

The registry must be able to explain every resolved field. A Designer should be
able to ask "why is Rusted Sword damage 10?" and see "native fallback" or
"DT_Items_Melee row RustedSword" or "PDA_Item_RustedSword explicit override."

#### 7.9.5 Row and asset contract

Current row structs are already close:

- `FAscentiaItemDefinitionRow`
- `FAscentiaWeaponTypeRow`
- `FAscentiaAffixRow`
- `FAscentiaSkillDefinitionRow`
- `FAscentiaLootTableRow`
- `FAscentiaCraftingRecipeRow`
- `FAscentiaItemNamePatternRow`
- `FAscentiaAttributeThresholdRow`

The missing runtime bridge is a resolved record:

```text
FAscentiaResolvedItemDefinition
- ItemTag
- RowName
- PrimaryAssetId
- SourceLayerByField
- DisplayName
- Category
- QualityFloor
- EquipmentSlot
- StackLimit
- Weight
- VendorValue
- StatRequirements
- WeaponFamily
- WeaponTypeTag
- MovesetTags
- BaseDamagePackets
- EquipEffects
- GrantedAbilitySetTag
- Icon
- WeaponMesh
- WornMesh
- GripTransform
- SwingTrailSystem
- TrailOffset
- ValidationSeverity
```

This should be a read model, not necessarily a saved object. Inventory saves
item tags and rolled instance state. Runtime resolves the latest content version
through the registry unless a future migration requires frozen historical items.

#### 7.9.6 Ingest algorithm

Registry startup should follow this shape:

```text
RegisterNativeFallbacks()
LoadConfiguredDataTables()
ValidateDataRows()
MergeRowsIntoResolvedRecords()
IngestScannedPrimaryAssets()
OverlayAssetRefsAndMarkedOverrides()
BuildAffixPools()
BuildLootTables()
EmitContentRegistryReport()
```

Implementation notes:

- `LoadConfiguredDataTables` should read soft paths from project settings or a
  `UAscentiaContentDataSettings` object, not hardcoded package paths.
- `ValidateDataRows` should run before overlay so bad rows are caught even when
  fallback content hides the issue.
- `OverlayAssetRefsAndMarkedOverrides` must never silently replace stats unless
  the field is explicitly marked as an override in the DataAsset.
- `BuildAffixPools` should precompute category/family/quality/tag filters for
  speed and report unreachable affixes.
- `EmitContentRegistryReport` writes a JSON report under
  `Saved/Automation/Evidence/content_registry/<timestamp>/report.json`.

#### 7.9.7 Designer-facing tools

This pipeline must be visible in the Editor:

- Editor Utility Widget: `EUW_AscentiaContentSync`
  - export sheet or accept already-exported CSV;
  - import CSV into DataTables;
  - rebuild registry preview;
  - run validators;
  - open the latest report.
- DataTables under `/Game/Ascentia/Design/DataTables/`
  - `DT_Items_Melee`
  - `DT_Items_Armor`
  - `DT_WeaponTypes`
  - `DT_Affixes`
  - `DT_LootTables`
  - `DT_Skills`
  - `DT_CraftingRecipes`
  - `DT_ItemNamePatterns`
- PrimaryDataAssets under `/Game/Ascentia/Design/`
  - `PDA_Item_*`
  - `PDA_AbilitySet_*`
  - `PDA_Boss_*`
  - `PDA_WorldZone_*`
- Data preview widget: `EUW_AscentiaResolvedContentBrowser`
  - search by GameplayTag;
  - show resolved source per field;
  - show missing presentation assets;
  - simulate loot rolls;
  - simulate stat requirements against a sample character.

The key rule: if a Designer needs to tune, inspect, or repair it, the surface
must exist in the Editor. Logs are evidence, not the primary authoring UI.

#### 7.9.8 Validation gates

The content pipeline needs four validator layers.

Structural validation:

- CSV headers match row structs;
- required columns exist;
- row names are stable;
- enum strings parse;
- numeric ranges parse and are finite.

Reference validation:

- item tags exist in the GameplayTag dictionary;
- loot entries reference valid item or nested loot tags;
- skill rows reference valid ability classes;
- cue tags are under `GameplayCue.Ascentia.*`;
- PrimaryDataAsset scan paths are registered;
- soft object paths resolve.

Semantic validation:

- item category matches slot;
- weapon family has a moveset;
- weapon moveset has attack and dodge fallback animations;
- affix min <= max;
- affix pool is reachable;
- loot weights sum to a useful nonzero distribution;
- stat requirements are compatible with expected phase progression;
- vendor values obey economy rules;
- placeholder assets are allowed only in prototype folders or with an explicit
  prototype tag.

Runtime proof:

- remote scenario loads the registry and checks counts;
- sample items resolve from DataTable, DataAsset, and fallback;
- loot roll is deterministic by seed;
- inventory display shows the resolved item;
- equip applies the expected effects;
- logs contain no missing package, missing tag, or invalid soft path warnings.

#### 7.9.9 Implementation order

1. Add `UAscentiaContentDataSettings` with soft references to the generated
   DataTables and a Blueprint-readable getter.
2. Add tracked `Tools/data/` scripts for sheet-to-CSV and CSV-to-DataTable
   import, plus a single sync command.
3. Add `FAscentiaResolvedItemDefinition` and source-layer metadata.
4. Teach `UAscentiaItemRegistrySubsystem` to ingest DataTables between native
   fallbacks and scanned assets.
5. Add `content_registry` report output and a playtest scenario that verifies
   fallback, row, asset overlay, loot, and inventory display.
6. Add `EUW_AscentiaContentSync` and `EUW_AscentiaResolvedContentBrowser`.
7. Promote validation to `IsDataValid` and `UEditorValidatorBase`, then wire it
   into commandlet validation.

This order lets the game keep running while content authority moves out of C++.
It also makes every migration step inspectable inside the Editor.

## 8. World, Streaming, Exploration, and Encounter Space

### 8.1 Player-facing goal

The open world should produce adventure, threat, recovery, discovery, build
pressure, and authored surprise. It should not be a map of chores. It should
support Landscry-generated scale while preserving handmade encounter intent.

### 8.2 Current Ascentia state

Working pieces:

- authored `MVP_Arena`;
- Below pocket space;
- encounter zones;
- fast-travel point;
- discovery actor;
- world tier/lift gate;
- world-state subsystem;
- objective tracker;
- fog wall actor;
- boss definition scaffold.

Current gap:

- no World Partition level yet;
- no Data Layer taxonomy;
- no streaming-aware save identity;
- no zone content pipeline;
- no open-world nav/AI scale plan;
- no map screen production model.

### 8.3 AAA target behavior

P17 should not simply import a big terrain. It should establish the world
production rules:

```text
World Partition persistent level
- Data Layer: Terrain_Base
- Data Layer: Biome_Dressing_*
- Data Layer: POI_*
- Data Layer: Encounter_*
- Data Layer: Quest_State_*
- Data Layer: DungeonEntrances
- Data Layer: FastTravel
- Data Layer: Debug
- Runtime layer toggles for world-state variants
- Editor layers for organization and performance
```

Each zone should have:

- zone definition asset;
- world partition cell strategy;
- biome tag;
- music state;
- encounter budget;
- traversal budget;
- reward budget;
- map reveal rules;
- fast-travel rules;
- landmark language;
- validation report.

### 8.4 Boss-area coupling

Elden Ring's production lesson is directly relevant: major boss gameplay should
shape area design. For Ascentia:

- area premise chooses boss family and movement scale;
- boss movement scale chooses arena topology;
- arena topology chooses approach route and checkpoint/campsite pressure;
- route pressure chooses resource economy and enemy density;
- reward identity chooses loot table and narrative state;
- narrative state chooses Data Layer variants and world-state tags.

Document this before building each major POI:

```text
POI Design Card
- Name
- Biome
- Core emotion
- Landmark silhouette
- Entry routes
- Exit routes
- Major boss or elite premise
- Combat space dimensions
- Traversal verbs required
- Enemy families
- Resource pressure
- Reward promise
- World-state changes
- Data Layers
- Save/respawn behavior
- Validation checklist
```

### 8.5 Streaming-aware actor identity

Open-world actors need stable identity that survives streaming and save/load.

Every persistent world actor should expose:

```text
PersistentWorldId
WorldStateTagsOnActivate
WorldStateTagsRequired
WorldStateTagsBlocked
ZoneTag
DataLayerTag
RespawnPolicy
SavePolicy
```

Do not save raw actor pointers. Save stable IDs and tags.

### 8.6 Designer surface

Designers should see:

- World Partition map;
- Data Layers Outliner;
- `PDA_ZoneDefinition`;
- `BP_AscentiaEncounterZone`;
- `BP_AscentiaFastTravelPoint`;
- `BP_AscentiaDiscoveryActor`;
- `BP_AscentiaFogWall`;
- `BP_AscentiaCampsite`;
- validation panel for zone completeness;
- map preview utility;
- encounter reset preview;
- loot preview;
- nav/debug overlay.

### 8.7 Validation

World validation should catch:

- POI has no zone definition;
- encounter zone has no spawn budget;
- fast travel point lacks discovery state;
- campsite too close/far from boss gate by design rule;
- fog wall not linked to encounter or boss;
- world-state tags are unregistered or misspelled;
- actor marked persistent but has no persistent ID;
- runtime Data Layer has no intended state;
- nav missing for combat spaces;
- no player recovery route after Below/POI completion;
- missing map icon or label;
- missing music state.

### 8.8 P17 world architecture contract

P17's deliverable is not "a bigger map." It is the first repeatable open-world
production pipeline. The first zone can be small, but it must prove:

- World Partition level creation and cook;
- Data Layer taxonomy;
- Landscry/PCG import path;
- HLOD and nav builder gates;
- zone definition assets;
- encounter placement rules;
- persistent actor IDs;
- save/load after streaming;
- map/discovery surfaces;
- performance budgets.

The MVP arena remains the combat test map. P17 should create a fresh
World Partition map, for example:

```text
/Game/Ascentia/World/WP_Ascentia_Zone01
```

Keep `MVP_Arena` as the fast smoke map. Do not convert it into the production
world unless the conversion is explicitly needed for regression coverage.

#### 8.8.1 World asset layout

```text
/Game/Ascentia/World/
  WP_Ascentia_Zone01
  DataLayers/
    DL_Editor_TerrainBlockout
    DL_Editor_ArtPass
    DL_Runtime_BaseTerrain
    DL_Runtime_Biome_Forest
    DL_Runtime_Biome_Ruin
    DL_Runtime_POI_*
    DL_Runtime_Encounter_*
    DL_Runtime_Quest_*
    DL_Runtime_Debug
  HLOD/
    HLOD_TerrainFar
    HLOD_Cliffs
    HLOD_FoliageLarge
    HLOD_Settlement
    HLOD_POI
  PCG/
    PCG_Biome_Forest
    PCG_Biome_Ruin
    PCG_RoadSpline
    PCG_Clutter_Detail
  Zones/
    PDA_WorldZone_*
    PDA_POI_*
    PDA_RegionLoot_*
```

Editor-only layers organize work. Runtime layers represent gameplay variants and
streamable groups that world state can control. Do not put frequently reused
base assets into many runtime layers; widely shared runtime-layer assets can
hurt streaming performance.

#### 8.8.2 Data Layer taxonomy

Use naming that tells both role and lifetime:

```text
DL_Editor_<TeamOrPass>_<Name>
DL_Runtime_Base_<Name>
DL_Runtime_Biome_<BiomeTag>
DL_Runtime_POI_<POITag>
DL_Runtime_Encounter_<EncounterTag>
DL_Runtime_Quest_<QuestStateTag>
DL_Runtime_WorldState_<StateTag>
DL_Debug_<Purpose>
```

Rules:

- Base terrain and always-visible landmarks should be runtime-loaded by default.
- Quest/variant layers should map to validated world-state tags.
- Encounter layers should not contain unrelated biome dressing.
- Debug layers never cook unless explicitly allowed.
- A POI can contain both editor and runtime layers, but runtime state must be
  documented in its `PDA_POI`.
- Level Instances inherit Data Layer assignment from their instance actor by
  default, so POI kit pieces must be checked for unexpected missing layer refs.

#### 8.8.3 Zone definition asset

```text
PDA_WorldZone_*
- ZoneTag
- DisplayName
- BiomeTags
- WorldTier
- PersistentLevel
- Bounds
- RuntimeDataLayers
- EditorDataLayers
- HLODLayers
- PCGGraphs
- NavPolicy
- SmartObjectPolicy
- EncounterBudget
- EnemyFamilies
- LootTableTag
- MusicState
- AmbientCueProfile
- MapRevealPolicy
- FastTravelPolicy
- CampsitePolicy
- RequiredTraversalVerbs
- RequiredWorldStateTags
- CompletionWorldStateTags
- PerformanceBudget
- ValidationNotes
```

The zone asset is the production handshake between world art, design, combat,
AI, audio, UI, save, and tools. A zone that lacks this asset is not production
content.

#### 8.8.4 POI card asset

```text
PDA_POI_*
- POITag
- ZoneTag
- CoreEmotion
- LandmarkSilhouette
- EntryRoutes
- ExitRoutes
- EncounterDefinition
- BossDefinition
- TraversalRequirements
- ResourcePressure
- RewardPromise
- MapIcon
- DiscoveryText
- DataLayers
- SmartObjects
- CampsiteDistanceRule
- FogWallPolicy
- WorldStateChanges
- ValidationChecklist
```

This formalizes the Elden Ring lesson already noted above: boss and area design
shape each other. A major POI card should be approved before detailed dressing
or encounter population begins.

#### 8.8.5 Landscry and PCG import contract

Treat Landscry output as source material, not final game structure.

```text
Landscry package
  -> import staging map/folder
  -> classify terrain, cliffs, roads, foliage, POIs, props
  -> assign Data Layers and HLOD Layers
  -> convert repeated structures to Level Instances or Packed Level Blueprints
  -> replace throwaway enemies/loot with Ascentia encounter assets
  -> run PCG/HLOD/nav/smart-object builders
  -> validate zone and POI cards
  -> promote into WP_Ascentia_Zone01
```

Required reports:

- imported actor count by class;
- missing materials/meshes;
- non-Nanite/high-poly risk list;
- collision complexity report;
- nav-affecting actor count;
- Data Layer assignment coverage;
- HLOD layer assignment coverage;
- unsupported asset/plugin references;
- scale and bounds report.

PCG rules:

- partition large-area PCG generation so it streams with World Partition;
- use hierarchical generation for large/medium/small detail separation;
- assign generated actors to the same Data Layer and HLOD Layer as the source
  PCG component when appropriate;
- do not generate gameplay-critical pickups or bosses as anonymous decoration;
- generated gameplay actors still need stable IDs or deterministic generation
  IDs if they can be saved, looted, killed, unlocked, or discovered.

#### 8.8.6 Persistent identity and save policy

Open-world save data should speak stable IDs:

```text
FAscentiaWorldActorId
- WorldTag
- ZoneTag
- POITag
- LocalActorId
```

Native component:

```text
UAscentiaWorldIdentityComponent
- PersistentWorldId
- ZoneTag
- POITag
- RuntimeDataLayerTag
- SavePolicy
- RespawnPolicy
- DiscoveryPolicy
- WorldStateTagsRequired
- WorldStateTagsBlocked
- WorldStateTagsSetOnActivate
```

Save only:

- persistent IDs;
- state tags;
- defeated unique enemies/bosses;
- looted one-shot containers;
- opened gates/doors/lifts;
- discovered map elements;
- campsite/fast-travel activation;
- generated deterministic seeds when needed.

Do not save:

- raw actor pointers;
- transient streaming cell paths;
- generated actor UObject names;
- runtime-only references that can change after a rebuild.

#### 8.8.7 Encounter and recovery placement

Every zone has a pressure graph:

```text
Campsite -> Route -> Minor Encounter -> Resource Choice -> POI -> Elite/Boss -> Reward -> Recovery
```

Designer rules:

- campsite distance to boss gate is data-driven;
- fast travel requires discovery/attunement;
- boss-adjacent respawn points are separate from full campsites;
- enemy density is budgeted by encounter definition, not hand-waved;
- escape routes and sightlines are deliberate;
- traversal blockers declare required verbs;
- resource pickups are placed against combat pressure and return distance.

#### 8.8.8 Map and discovery model

Map UI should be backed by world data, not a screenshot:

```text
FAscentiaMapRegion
- RegionTag
- ZoneTag
- Bounds
- RevealState
- MapTextureOrRenderTarget
- LandmarkMarkers
- POIMarkers
- FastTravelMarkers
- PlayerNotes
- FogOfWarPolicy
```

Discovery actors should set stable tags and expose display data. The map screen
should read `PDA_WorldZone`, `PDA_POI`, and discovery state, not scrape placed
actors every frame.

#### 8.8.9 Builder and validation gates

P17 build proof should include:

```text
WorldPartitionBuilderCommandlet - builder=WorldPartitionNavigationDataBuilder
WorldPartitionBuilderCommandlet - builder=WorldPartitionSmartObjectCollectionBuilder
WorldPartitionBuilderCommandlet - Builder=PCGWorldPartitionBuilder
World Partition HLOD build
DataValidation commandlet
Cook/package smoke for WP_Ascentia_Zone01
Remote playtest: zone load, discovery, encounter, rest reset, fast travel, save/load
```

If a commandlet is too slow for every edit, run it as a phase gate and keep a
lighter "zone completeness" validator for daily iteration.

#### 8.8.10 Designer-facing world tools

Build or reserve:

- `EUW_AscentiaZoneValidator`
- `EUW_AscentiaLandscryImportAudit`
- `EUW_AscentiaPOICardBrowser`
- `EUW_AscentiaMapPreview`
- `EUW_AscentiaEncounterBudgetPreview`
- `EUW_AscentiaWorldStateLayerPreview`

Each tool should use Designer language:

- "POI has no map marker"
- "Encounter budget exceeds zone budget by 12"
- "Runtime quest layer has no world-state tag"
- "Actor is marked persistent but has no PersistentWorldId"
- "Smart Object collection needs rebuild"
- "HLOD layer missing for 342 static mesh actors"

The editor surface is part of the architecture, not a nice-to-have.

#### 8.8.11 Zone generation profile

The first World Partition zone should be driven by a profile, even if the first
profile is tiny. This prevents Landscry, PCG, hand placement, UDS, encounters,
and map data from becoming separate checklists.

```text
PDA_WorldGenerationProfile
  ProfileTag
  TargetWorld
  SourcePackagePolicy
  LandscryImportPolicy
  PCGGraphSet
  DataLayerPolicy
  HLODPolicy
  NavBuildPolicy
  SmartObjectBuildPolicy
  AtmosphereProfile
  BiomeSurfaceProfile
  ZoneValidationProfile
  EvidenceOutputPolicy

PDA_WorldPartitionBuildProfile
  WorldPath
  RuntimeGridPolicy
  CellSizePolicy
  LoadingRangePolicy
  BuilderSteps
  BuilderCommandletArgs
  CookProfile
  SmokeScenario
```

Generation should be staged:

```text
Imported
  source content is present but not trusted

Classified
  terrain, roads, cliffs, foliage, buildings, POI candidates, and props are
  tagged

Normalized
  naming, folders, materials, collision, scale, Data Layers, HLOD Layers, and
  provenance are assigned

Authored
  POI cards, encounter zones, traversal routes, map markers, discovery actors,
  campsites, and fast travel are connected

Built
  PCG, HLOD, nav, Smart Object collections, map tiles, and validation reports
  are generated

Accepted
  Designer human verdict plus automated evidence says the zone is production
  usable
```

Generated content should enter staging first:

```text
/Game/Ascentia/World/Staging/Landscry/<PackageId>/
/Game/Ascentia/World/Staging/PCG/<RunId>/
/Game/Ascentia/World/Staging/ImportedPacks/<SourceId>/
```

Promotion into `/Game/Ascentia/World/WP_Ascentia_Zone01` requires an import
report and a human acceptance note. Generated terrain is fast; unreviewed
generated terrain is debt.

#### 8.8.12 World import and provenance report

Every Landscry/PCG/world import should produce a report:

```text
FAscentiaWorldImportReport
  RunId
  SourcePackageId
  SourceTool
  SourcePromptOrRecipeHash
  ImportedActorCount
  ImportedAssetCount
  ClassifiedActorCounts
  CreatedDataLayers
  CreatedHLODLayers
  PCGGraphsApplied
  MaterialsNormalized
  CollisionWarnings
  ScaleWarnings
  MissingReferences
  UnsupportedPlugins
  GeneratedPersistentIds
  PromotionCandidates
  HumanVerdict
```

Asset provenance:

```text
FAscentiaWorldContentProvenance
  AssetPath
  SourceTool
  SourcePackageId
  SourceLicenseNote
  ImportedBy
  ImportedUtc
  NormalizationSteps
  AcceptedForProduction
  EvidencePath
```

`EUW_AscentiaContentProvenanceBrowser` should expose this for Landscry output,
PCG-generated assets, Marketplace/Fab assets, UDS presets, and Asset Factory
meshes. If a cliff mesh breaks collision or a material fails weather response,
the team should know where it came from and which tool accepted it.

#### 8.8.13 Traversal route graph and recovery

Open-world traversal should be authored as a graph, not inferred from terrain.

```text
PDA_TraversalRoute
  RouteTag
  ZoneTag
  StartAnchor
  EndAnchor
  RequiredTraversalVerbs
  OptionalTraversalVerbs
  EnemyPressureBand
  ResourcePressureBand
  ReturnRoutePolicy
  FallRecoveryPolicy
  MapHintPolicy
  ProofScenario

FAscentiaTraversalAnchor
  AnchorTag
  WorldTransform
  ZoneTag
  POITag
  AnchorType
  RequiredState
  BlockedState
  RecoveryPriority
```

Route proof should answer:

- Can the player reach the POI with current verbs?
- Can the player leave after completion?
- Is there a return path after a drop, cave exit, lift, ladder, or Below pocket?
- Does fall recovery place the player at a fair anchor?
- Are enemies/nav/loot on the route consistent with the resource pressure band?
- Does the map hint point toward the route without becoming a quest checklist?

`EUW_AscentiaRoadAndTraversalPlanner` should own the Designer workflow:
route splines, anchors, slope heatmaps, fall-risk markers, return-route checks,
nav-link candidates, and proof screenshots.

#### 8.8.14 Streaming sources, travel, and fast-travel preloading

World Partition streaming should be part of the gameplay contract. Teleports,
fast travel, dungeon exits, Below returns, respawns, and boss-arena recoveries
need a preload policy.

```text
PDA_StreamingSourceProfile
  ProfileTag
  TargetGrid
  LoadingRange
  TargetState
  Priority
  ShapePolicy
  HLODLayerPolicy
  TimeoutSeconds
  LoadingScreenPolicy

FAscentiaWorldTravelRequest
  RequestId
  SourceLocation
  TargetAnchor
  TargetZone
  TravelType
  StreamingSourceProfile
  RequiredWorldState
  FailurePolicy
```

Rules:

- A fast-travel point must activate a streaming source at the destination before
  moving the player.
- Respawn and death-echo recovery should use the same anchor/preload path as
  fast travel, not a separate teleport shortcut.
- Travel proof should verify "streaming completed" or timeout behavior.
- Player-facing loading screens should be driven by shell/platform policy from
  P21, while the world system owns streaming readiness.

#### 8.8.15 PCG ownership and gameplay boundaries

PCG can create density and variation, but gameplay-critical results still need
stable identity. Separate PCG output into:

```text
CosmeticGenerated
  grass, rocks, tiny clutter, distant dressing

NavigationalGenerated
  road dressing, path blockers, climb markers, cover props

GameplayCandidateGenerated
  chest candidates, shrine candidates, enemy camp candidates, POI markers

AuthoredGameplay
  accepted interactables, enemies, bosses, rewards, gates, map markers
```

Rules:

- Cosmetic generated content can be regenerated freely if it has no save state.
- Navigational generated content needs nav/collision/traversal validation.
- Gameplay candidates must be promoted into authored assets before shipping.
- Authored gameplay actors need stable IDs and Data Layer/world-state policy.
- PCG graphs should be versioned by profile and report graph version/hash in
  evidence.

This lets Landscry/PCG accelerate density without letting anonymous generated
actors own progression, loot, bosses, or save state.

#### 8.8.16 World performance and readability budgets

World scale needs budgets before the zone is beautiful.

```text
PDA_WorldPerformanceBudget
  ZoneTag
  TargetPlatformProfile
  MaxLoadedCells
  MaxVisibleHLODActors
  MaxNaniteInstances
  MaxNonNaniteTriangles
  MaxFoliageInstances
  MaxDynamicLights
  MaxNiagaraWeatherSystems
  MaxSmartObjects
  MaxNavDirtyAreas
  MaxAudioEmitters
  ScreenshotProofSet

FAscentiaWorldPerformanceReport
  ZoneTag
  BuildProfile
  LoadedCellCount
  ActorCountsByDataLayer
  HLODCounts
  PCGGeneratedCounts
  NavBuildWarnings
  SmartObjectWarnings
  FrameBudgetSamples
  ReadabilityScreenshots
```

Readability is part of performance. A zone can hit frame budget and still fail
if fog, foliage, weather, or clutter hides attack silhouettes, loot beams,
interaction prompts, routes, or map landmarks. `EUW_AscentiaLightingMoodLab`,
`EUW_AscentiaMaterialSurfaceLab`, and `EUW_AscentiaMapBakeLab` should all feed
the world performance/readability report.

#### 8.8.17 P17 proof report

P17 should write one report per zone build:

```text
FAscentiaWorldZoneBuildReport
  RunId
  ZoneTag
  WorldPath
  BuildProfile
  ImportReportPath
  DataLayerReport
  HLODReport
  PCGReport
  NavReport
  SmartObjectReport
  TraversalReport
  MapBakeReport
  PerformanceReport
  SaveLoadStreamingReport
  CookPackageReport
  HumanVerdict
  BlockingIssues
  SuggestedRepairs
```

Dashboard cards:

```text
World Zone
  last successful zone build
  blocking Data Layer/HLOD/PCG/nav issues
  traversal route status
  map/discovery status
  performance/readability status
  packaged smoke status
```

Remote proof names:

```text
world_partition_boot
zone_streaming_smoke
world_zone_build
land_scry_import_audit
pcg_generation_gate
data_layer_state_gate
hlod_nav_smartobject_build
traversal_route_proof
map_discovery_proof
world_save_load_streaming
```

#### 8.8.18 P17 starter implementation package

The first P17 package should create one small World Partition production lane,
not a giant open world. The point is to prove that generated terrain, PCG,
Data Layers, POIs, traversal, map/discovery, streaming, nav, HLOD, and
performance evidence can converge into one Designer-visible zone build.

Runtime module layout:

```text
Source/Ascentia/Public/World/AscentiaWorldZoneTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/World/AscentiaWorldZoneSubsystem.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/World/AscentiaWorldIdentityComponent.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/World/AscentiaWorldTravelTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/World/AscentiaStreamingSourceTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/World/AscentiaTraversalTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/World/AscentiaMapRegionTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/World/AscentiaWorldPerformanceTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/World/AscentiaWorldGenerationProfile.h
Source/Ascentia/Private/World/...
```

Editor module layout:

```text
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/World/AscentiaWorldZoneValidation.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/World/AscentiaLandscryImportValidation.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/World/AscentiaPCGGenerationValidation.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/World/AscentiaTraversalValidation.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/World/AscentiaWorldPerformanceValidation.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Commandlets/AscentiaWorldZoneBuildCommandlet.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Commandlets/AscentiaLandscryImportAuditCommandlet.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Commandlets/AscentiaWorldTravelAuditCommandlet.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Tests/AscentiaWorldIdentityTest.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Tests/AscentiaTraversalRouteTest.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Tests/AscentiaWorldZoneReportTest.cpp
```

Subsystem boundaries:

```text
UAscentiaWorldZoneSubsystem : UWorldSubsystem
  ResolveZoneForLocation(Location)
  ResolveZoneDefinition(ZoneTag)
  ResolvePOI(POITag)
  BuildWorldTravelRequest(TargetAnchor)
  QueryZoneRuntimeState(ZoneTag)

UAscentiaWorldIdentityComponent : UActorComponent
  owns persistent world identity, zone/POI tags, save policy, respawn policy,
  discovery policy, and world-state gates

UAscentiaWorldTravelSubsystem : UWorldSubsystem
  validates fast travel, respawn, Below return, dungeon exit, and preload policy
  waits for World Partition streaming readiness before final placement

UAscentiaTraversalSubsystem : UWorldSubsystem
  resolves traversal anchors, return routes, fall recovery, and route proof data

AscentiaEditor world tooling
  owns Landscry import audit, PCG generation reports, Data Layer validation,
  HLOD/nav/smart-object build status, map bake proof, and performance reports
```

Starter content folders:

```text
/Game/Ascentia/World/WP_Ascentia_Zone01/
/Game/Ascentia/World/DataLayers/
/Game/Ascentia/World/HLOD/
/Game/Ascentia/World/PCG/
/Game/Ascentia/World/Zones/
/Game/Ascentia/World/POIs/
/Game/Ascentia/World/Traversal/
/Game/Ascentia/World/Map/
/Game/Ascentia/World/Performance/
/Game/Ascentia/World/Staging/Landscry/
/Game/Ascentia/World/Staging/PCG/
/Game/Ascentia/World/Staging/ImportedPacks/
```

Starter assets:

```text
WP_Ascentia_Zone01
PDA_WorldZone_Zone01_MVP
PDA_POI_Zone01_FirstRuin
PDA_WorldGenerationProfile_Zone01_MVP
PDA_WorldPartitionBuildProfile_Zone01_MVP
PDA_TraversalRoute_Zone01_CampsiteToFirstRuin
PDA_StreamingSourceProfile_FastTravel_MVP
PDA_WorldPerformanceBudget_Zone01_MVP
PDA_MapRegion_Zone01_MVP
PDA_WorldContentProvenancePolicy_MVP
```

Required Data Layer assets:

```text
DL_Editor_TerrainBlockout
DL_Editor_ArtPass
DL_Runtime_Base_Terrain
DL_Runtime_Biome_Forest
DL_Runtime_Biome_Ruin
DL_Runtime_POI_FirstRuin
DL_Runtime_Encounter_FirstRuin
DL_Runtime_WorldState_FirstRuinCleared
DL_Runtime_FastTravel
DL_Debug_WorldValidation
```

World identity contract:

```text
FAscentiaWorldActorId
  WorldTag
  ZoneTag
  POITag
  LocalActorId
  GenerationSourceId

FAscentiaWorldActorSaveRecord
  Version
  ActorId
  SavePolicy
  StateTags
  bActivated
  bLooted
  bDefeated
  bDiscovered
  RespawnState
  DeterministicSeed
```

Landscry import stages:

```text
Import
  copy source package into staging and record provenance

Classify
  terrain, roads, cliffs, water, foliage, building shells, POI candidates, props

Normalize
  naming, folders, materials, collision, scale, Data Layers, HLOD Layers

Promote
  accepted terrain/POI candidates move into `WP_Ascentia_Zone01`

Build
  PCG, HLOD, nav, smart-object collection, map region, and validation reports

Accept
  human verdict plus automated report marks zone production-usable
```

Starter proof matrix:

```text
WorldPartition_Boot_Zone01
DataLayer_Taxonomy_AssignmentCoverage
LandscryImport_StagingOnly
LandscryImport_ClassifyNormalizePromote
PCG_BiomeForest_AssignedLayersAndHLOD
HLOD_Build_Zone01
Nav_Build_Zone01
SmartObjectCollection_Build_Zone01
Traversal_CampsiteToFirstRuin_ReturnPath
WorldIdentity_SaveLoad_LootedChest
FastTravel_PreloadedDestination
MapDiscovery_FirstRuinReveal
Performance_LoadedCellsBudget
PackagedSmoke_ZoneLoadDiscoverReturn
```

Editor surfaces:

```text
EUW_AscentiaWorldDirector
  create/open zone profile, run build steps, view blocking issues, and publish
  zone build evidence

EUW_AscentiaLandscryImportAudit
  inspect staging package, classify actors, assign provenance, normalize folders,
  flag unsupported plugins, and promote accepted assets

EUW_AscentiaPOIComposer
  edit POI card, entry/exit routes, boss/elite premise, reward promise, map icon,
  world-state changes, Data Layers, and validation checklist

EUW_AscentiaRoadAndTraversalPlanner
  place route splines, anchors, recovery points, slope/fall risk markers,
  return-route checks, and proof screenshots

EUW_AscentiaMapBakeLab
  preview map region, reveal policy, discovery tags, POI markers, fast travel,
  and rendered/baked map outputs

EUW_AscentiaWorldStateLayerPreview
  toggle fixture world-state tags and show expected runtime Data Layer state

EUW_AscentiaWorldPerformanceBudget
  inspect loaded cell counts, HLOD counts, PCG counts, nav warnings, actor counts
  by Data Layer, readability screenshots, and budget failures
```

Commandlet contracts:

```text
UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe Ascentia.uproject -run=AscentiaLandscryImportAudit
  -SourcePackage=/Game/Ascentia/World/Staging/Landscry/Zone01
  -GenerationProfile=/Game/Ascentia/World/Zones/PDA_WorldGenerationProfile_Zone01_MVP
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P17

UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe Ascentia.uproject -run=AscentiaWorldZoneBuild
  -World=/Game/Ascentia/World/WP_Ascentia_Zone01
  -BuildProfile=/Game/Ascentia/World/Zones/PDA_WorldPartitionBuildProfile_Zone01_MVP
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P17

UnrealEditor.exe Ascentia.uproject /Game/Ascentia/World/WP_Ascentia_Zone01
  -run=WorldPartitionBuilderCommandlet
  -builder=WorldPartitionNavigationDataBuilder

UnrealEditor.exe Ascentia.uproject /Game/Ascentia/World/WP_Ascentia_Zone01
  -run=WorldPartitionBuilderCommandlet
  -builder=PCGWorldPartitionBuilder

Tools/run_p17_zone_smoke.ps1
  -Scenario ZoneStreamingSmoke
  -ReportRoot Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P17
```

Evidence outputs:

```text
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P17/WorldZoneBuild/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P17/LandscryImport/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P17/PCGGeneration/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P17/DataLayers/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P17/HLOD/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P17/Nav/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P17/Traversal/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P17/MapDiscovery/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P17/StreamingTravel/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P17/Performance/{RunId}/report.json
```

Implementation tickets:

```text
P17-001 World Partition sandbox map
  Create `WP_Ascentia_Zone01`, required Data Layer assets, HLOD layer assets,
  PCG folder layout, and `PDA_WorldZone_Zone01_MVP`.

P17-002 World identity component and save fixture
  Implement `UAscentiaWorldIdentityComponent`, `FAscentiaWorldActorId`,
  `FAscentiaWorldActorSaveRecord`, and save/load proof for one persistent chest
  or gate.

P17-003 Landscry import audit first slice
  Implement staging folder policy, provenance records, classify/normalize report,
  unsupported plugin detection, Data Layer/HLOD coverage, and human verdict.

P17-004 Zone generation and build profiles
  Add `PDA_WorldGenerationProfile_Zone01_MVP`,
  `PDA_WorldPartitionBuildProfile_Zone01_MVP`, commandlet args, and evidence
  output policy.

P17-005 PCG/Data Layer/HLOD gate
  Add one partitioned PCG biome graph proof, verify generated actors inherit
  expected Data Layer/HLOD assignment, and report graph version/hash.

P17-006 Traversal route and recovery proof
  Add `PDA_TraversalRoute_Zone01_CampsiteToFirstRuin`, anchors, return-route
  proof, fall recovery proof, and route screenshots.

P17-007 Streaming travel and fast-travel preload
  Add `PDA_StreamingSourceProfile_FastTravel_MVP`,
  `FAscentiaWorldTravelRequest`, destination preload proof, timeout behavior,
  and respawn/Beyond-return reuse policy.

P17-008 Map region and discovery proof
  Add `PDA_MapRegion_Zone01_MVP`, FirstRuin discovery data, reveal tags,
  POI marker, fast-travel marker, and map preview evidence.

P17-009 Performance/readability budget report
  Add `PDA_WorldPerformanceBudget_Zone01_MVP`,
  `FAscentiaWorldPerformanceReport`, loaded cell counts, actor counts by layer,
  HLOD/PCG/nav warnings, and readability screenshots.

P17-010 P17 packaged proof suite
  Run World Partition boot, zone streaming, Data Layer state, PCG/HLOD/nav,
  traversal, map discovery, save/load streaming, fast travel, and packaged smoke
  reports.
```

Validation gates:

- any production zone without `PDA_WorldZone_*` fails validation;
- any persistent actor without `UAscentiaWorldIdentityComponent` fails validation;
- any runtime Data Layer without owner world-state policy fails validation;
- any Landscry/PCG asset promoted without provenance report and human verdict
  fails validation;
- any gameplay-critical generated actor not promoted to authored identity fails
  validation;
- any POI without entry/exit routes, map marker, reward promise, and recovery
  route remains staging-only;
- any fast-travel/respawn/dungeon-exit path without streaming preload proof fails
  validation;
- any zone over loaded-cell, HLOD, PCG count, nav, or readability budgets blocks
  phase completion.

Recommended sequencing:

1. Create `WP_Ascentia_Zone01` and the Data Layer/HLOD folder taxonomy.
2. Add `PDA_WorldZone_Zone01_MVP` and `UAscentiaWorldIdentityComponent`.
3. Run one Landscry staging import through audit without promoting gameplay.
4. Add one accepted POI card, route, discovery marker, and persistent chest.
5. Run PCG/HLOD/nav builder gates and publish one zone build report.
6. Only then add more POIs, weather/UDS complexity, encounter density, or large
   generated regions.

### 8.9 Open-world traversal verbs, mounts, water, and route gameplay package

Section 8.8 proves that a world zone can be loaded, generated, validated, and
traversed as a route graph. This companion package owns the moment-to-moment
verbs that make those routes playable: mantle, vault, ladder, lift, squeeze,
drop, swim, boat, mount, dismount, traversal unlocks, one-way routes, recovery,
AI nav-link parity, and encounter restrictions.

Ascentia should not treat traversal as a pile of one-off Blueprint triggers. A
ledge climb, ladder, boat, mount, and lift are all gameplay transitions between
intent, movement mode, animation, collision, camera, world state, nav, save, and
encounter rules. If the route graph says a POI requires `Traversal.Mantle.Low`,
Designers need an asset, preview, proof report, map hint, nav-link policy, and
failure reason.

Engine-facing posture:

- The current GASP/Mover path should remain the prototype locomotion spine, but
  Mover is still an experimental engine feature. Every Ascentia traversal mode
  needs a data contract, test map, and fallback/upgrade note instead of assuming
  sample-project behavior is production-stable.
- Use Mover-style movement modes for sustained traversal states such as ladder,
  climb, swim, boat, mount, zipline, and custom pathed motion. Use layered moves
  for temporary forces, root-motion overlays, launch arcs, and animation-driven
  traversal pieces. Use transitions for entering/exiting modes from authored
  anchors, Smart Objects, nav links, water volumes, and interaction requests.
- Keep combat motion and world traversal related but separate. A dodge roll off
  a ledge, a mantle, and a ladder mount all consume player intent and collision
  facts, but they have different authority, cancel, camera, invulnerability,
  stamina/Breath, and interruption rules.
- Water is a world system, not just a material. Rivers, lakes, oceans, swimming,
  boats, wet surfaces, VFX, audio, combat restrictions, AI avoidance, and map
  route hints need one Ascentia policy layer.
- Navigation Links and Smart Objects should expose traversal affordances to AI
  and tooling. Player traversal does not have to use AI nav directly, but the
  same authored gap/drop/ladder/lift facts should be visible to nav builds and
  encounter validation.

The subsystem boundary:

```text
UAscentiaTraversalSubsystem : UWorldSubsystem
  ResolveTraversalVerb(Request)
  FindAnchorForIntent(Character, InputIntent, VerbFilter)
  BeginTraversal(Request)
  EndTraversal(Result)
  QueryRouteCapability(CharacterProfile, RouteTag)
  ResolveFallRecoveryAnchor(Location, ZoneTag)
  EmitTraversalEvidence(Event)

UAscentiaTraversalAnchorComponent : UActorComponent
  exposes verb, route, zone, entry/exit transforms, Smart Object handle,
  NavLink identity, Designer note, and last-safe-anchor eligibility

UAscentiaTraversalAbilityBridge
  translates Gameplay Ability activation, tags, costs, interruption,
  and cooldown rules into traversal requests without letting abilities own
  world-route authority
```

Designer-facing rules:

- Every traversal verb has a profile asset. No route, anchor, or Blueprint actor
  should hardcode verb rules.
- Every traversal anchor has a verb, zone, route, transform, required tags,
  blocked tags, preview mesh or gizmo, and Designer note.
- Every one-way drop has a return-route policy or an explicit "intended trap"
  narrative/encounter note.
- Every ladder, lift, boat, mount, and zipline has an exit recovery policy and
  save/load restore policy.
- Water boundaries declare whether the player can swim, drown, boat, mount,
  fight, cast, recover death echoes, and place loot.
- Traversal unlocks use GameplayTags and save facts. A map route should say
  "requires `Traversal.Mantle.High`," not "requires the Blue Key Blueprint."
- AI and enemies either understand the authored traversal affordance through
  nav links/Smart Objects, or the encounter marks the affordance player-only.
- Encounter spaces must declare which traversal verbs are suppressed during boss
  locks, fog walls, invasion/coop later, and cinematic moments.
- Camera collision and presentation are part of traversal acceptance. A ladder
  that works mechanically but hides the player inside geometry fails.

Designer tools:

```text
EUW_AscentiaTraversalVerbBoard
EUW_AscentiaTraversalAnchorPainter
EUW_AscentiaTraversalRoutePlayabilityLab
EUW_AscentiaWaterTraversalLab
EUW_AscentiaMountPrototypeLab
EUW_AscentiaTraversalNavLinkDoctor
EUW_AscentiaTraversalSaveRestoreLab
```

`EUW_AscentiaTraversalVerbBoard` lists every verb, profile, required animations,
movement mode, costs, combat policy, cancel policy, camera policy, AI policy,
and last proof result.

`EUW_AscentiaTraversalAnchorPainter` lets Designers place anchors in the level,
assign route/zone/verb tags, preview entry and exit transforms, and show bad
facing, missing return routes, or missing last-safe-anchor coverage.

`EUW_AscentiaTraversalRoutePlayabilityLab` runs route fixtures: start at anchor,
move through verb chain, fight/loot/rest/save where relevant, return, fall off
edges, reload, and compare expected route capability to actual result.

`EUW_AscentiaWaterTraversalLab` previews water bodies, swim/boat/death policies,
exit anchors, wetness/material response, audio/VFX, map route hints, and combat
restrictions.

`EUW_AscentiaMountPrototypeLab` previews mount/dismount anchors, camera, capsule
fit, slopes, steps, gates, water, combat suppression, save/load, and map route
speed assumptions.

`EUW_AscentiaTraversalNavLinkDoctor` compares authored traversal anchors to
NavLink Proxy actors, generated nav links, Smart Objects, and enemy route
capability. It should flag "player can cross but AI cannot" unless that is
explicitly intended.

P17T starter tickets:

- P17T-001: Add traversal verb/profile structs, first MVP verb profile assets,
  and `EUW_AscentiaTraversalVerbBoard`.
- P17T-002: Add `UAscentiaTraversalAnchorComponent`,
  `EUW_AscentiaTraversalAnchorPainter`, route/zone/verb coverage audit, and
  first mantle/ladder/lift anchors.
- P17T-003: Add traversal request/result resolver and route playability proof
  for `PDA_TraversalRoute_Zone01_CampsiteToFirstRuin`.
- P17T-004: Add water traversal profile, swim/exit/death policy validation,
  and `EUW_AscentiaWaterTraversalLab`.
- P17T-005: Add mount prototype profile, mount/dismount validation, slope/gate
  proof, and `EUW_AscentiaMountPrototypeLab`.
- P17T-006: Add nav-link/Smart Object parity audit so player and AI traversal
  differences are explicit.
- P17T-007: Add traversal save/restore proof for ladders, lifts, water, mounts,
  one-way drops, and fall recovery anchors.
- P17T-008: Add traversal package gates, dashboard cards, commandlets, and
  evidence trend rows.

Recommended implementation order:

1. P17T-001 through P17T-003 first. Define verbs, anchors, requests, and one
   route proof before adding new movement spectacle.
2. P17T-006 should happen alongside route proof so AI/player affordance
   differences are intentional.
3. P17T-004 and P17T-005 are larger open-world features; keep them as prototype
   profiles until the first zone route, camera, save, and nav proofs are stable.
4. P17T-007 prevents save/load and death-loop surprises before traversal verbs
   become progression gates.
5. P17T-008 turns traversal from a fun prototype into a production lane.

### 8.10 Ambient population, ecology, patrols, and world-life package

The first open zone should not be empty terrain with combat arenas dropped into
it. It also should not become an uncontrolled crowd simulation. This companion
package owns the middle layer: ambient NPCs, wildlife, non-combat patrols,
road traffic, campsite life, Smart Object activities, world-state population
changes, density budgets, and persistence.

Engine posture:

- Mass is appropriate for high-count, data-oriented, non-combat populations:
  wildlife herds, birds, distant travelers, settlement background actors, or
  simple road traffic. Its entities are data fragments processed by stateless
  processors, so Ascentia should not hide bespoke combat authority inside Mass.
- StateTree is appropriate for ambient individual behavior where readable states
  matter: idle, work, patrol, rest, react to weather, flee, investigate, return.
- Smart Objects are reservation/query data for world activities, not the logic
  itself. The interactor still owns execution, animation, ability, and gameplay
  effects.
- World Partition, Data Layers, HLOD, and PCG define where population sources
  live, stream, and build. PCG-generated candidate points can propose ambient
  life, but shipped gameplay-critical actors need stable identity or a documented
  reset policy.

Runtime module layout:

```text
  FleeProfileTag

  ReadabilityTag
  PersistencePolicyTag
  ScalabilityGroupTag

FAscentiaAmbientActivity
  ActivityTag
  SmartObjectDefinition
  RequiredUserTags
  ActivityTags
  AnimationPolicyTag
  DurationRange
  ReservationPolicyTag
  InterruptionPolicyTag
  WorldStateEffects

FAscentiaPopulationBudget
  BudgetTag
  MaxSpawnedActorsPerCell
  MaxMassEntitiesPerCell
  MaxTickingStateTrees
  MaxSmartObjectClaims
  MaxAudioEmitters
  MaxNiagaraEmitters
  SignificanceBands

FAscentiaPopulationSaveRecord

  SpeciesTag
  LastKnownTransform
  WorldStateTags

```

Subsystem boundary:

```text
UAscentiaPopulationSubsystem : UWorldSubsystem
  ResolvePopulationProfile(ZoneTag, Context)
  RegisterPopulationSource(SourceComponent)
  EvaluatePopulationCell(CellContext)
  RequestAmbientSpawn(SourceTag, SpeciesTag)
  RequestAmbientDespawn(StablePopulationId, ReasonTag)
  NotifyWorldStateChanged(Tag)
  NotifyTimeWeatherChanged(Context)
  BuildPopulationDebugView(ZoneTag)
  WritePopulationEvidence(OutputPath)
```

Rules:

- Combat enemies, elites, and bosses still resolve through P18 encounter and
  enemy definitions. Ambient population may escalate into combat only through a
  declared bridge that hands authority to P18.
- Every population source has an owner zone, biome, Data Layer policy, budget,
  world-state requirements, and evidence path.
- PCG may generate candidate points, density masks, and biome hints. It may not
  silently create persistent named NPCs, unique enemies, quest-critical animals,
  or one-off loot state.
- Smart Objects used by ambient life must declare tags, slot count, reservation
  rules, activity duration, interruption policy, and player/AI access policy.
- Mass entities stay non-critical until a dedicated Mass combat architecture is
  approved. Important NPCs and authored combatants stay Actors with stable save
  identity.
- Population must be readable. It should support mood, biome identity, and
  discovery, but it may not hide combat threats in unreadable visual noise.
- Density budgets include audio, VFX, animation ticking, StateTree count, Smart
  Object claims, and loaded-cell population, not only actor count.
- Save/load stores only the state that matters: unique ambient actors, killed or
  harvested persistent entities, important Smart Object claims, and source
  cooldowns. Generic background life can respawn from deterministic seeds.

Designer-facing tools:

```text
EUW_AscentiaPopulationEcologyLab
  inspect zone population profiles, species, source bounds, density budgets,
  time/weather/world-state gates, and latest evidence

EUW_AscentiaAmbientRouteAndPatrolLab
  preview road travelers, guard patrols, wildlife graze/flee loops, return
  routes, nav coverage, and readable player encounter bands

EUW_AscentiaSmartObjectActivityBoard
  inspect Smart Object definitions, slots, tags, reservations, behavior data,
  player/AI access, Data Layer ownership, and collection build status

EUW_AscentiaMassStateTreeBudgetLab
  compare Actor, StateTree, and Mass representations by count, significance,
  animation ticking, LOD, and scalability group

EUW_AscentiaWorldLifeScenarioRunner
  run morning/noon/night, clear/rain/storm, pre/post POI completion, and
  save/load population fixtures
```

Evidence folders:

```text
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P17/Population/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P17/AmbientRoutes/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P17/SmartObjectActivities/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P17/PopulationBudgets/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P17/WorldLifeScenarios/{RunId}/report.json
```

Commandlet contracts:

```text
UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe Ascentia.uproject -run=AscentiaPopulationAudit
  -Zone=/Game/Ascentia/World/Zone01/PDA_WorldZone_Zone01_MVP
  -PopulationProfile=/Game/Ascentia/World/Population/PDA_PopulationProfile_Zone01_MVP
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P17/Population

UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe Ascentia.uproject -run=AscentiaPopulationScenario
  -Map=/Game/Ascentia/World/Zone01/WP_Ascentia_Zone01
  -ScenarioProfile=/Game/Ascentia/World/Population/PDA_WorldLifeStateRules_MVP
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P17/WorldLifeScenarios
```

Starter proof matrix:

```text
Population_Zone01_SourceCoverage
  every population source has zone, biome, Data Layer, budget, and owner asset

Population_PCGCandidates_NotPersistentGameplay
  generated candidate points can spawn generic ambient life but cannot create
  persistent named NPCs, unique loot, or quest-critical state

Population_SmartObjectReservations
  campsite, guard post, and forage spot expose slots, tags, reservation policy,
  player/AI access, and collection build status

Population_StateTreeAmbientTraveler
  traveler enters idle, walk, rest, flee, return, and weather-react states with
  no Behavior Tree combat dependency

Population_MassBackgroundBudget
  Mass background entities scale by significance and low-spec settings while
  authored Actors remain stable

Population_TimeWeatherWorldStateVariants
  morning/noon/night and clear/rain/storm profiles produce expected population
  differences without breaking POI readability

Population_SaveLoadPersistence
  killed, harvested, dismissed, or unique ambient entities persist correctly,
  while generic background life regenerates from source policy

Population_CellStreamingAndDespawn
  crossing World Partition cell boundaries respects spawn/despawn radii,
  cooldowns, Smart Object claims, and budget caps

Population_NoCombatAuthorityLeak
  ambient actors that escalate to combat hand off through P18 encounter/enemy
  authority and do not run private damage/loot logic
```

Implementation tickets:

```text
P17P-001 Population profile, source, species, budget, and save structs
  Add `FAscentiaPopulationProfile`, `FAscentiaPopulationSource`,
  `FAscentiaAmbientSpecies`, `FAscentiaPopulationBudget`, and
  `FAscentiaPopulationSaveRecord` with DataAsset shells.

P17P-002 Population subsystem and source component
  Add `UAscentiaPopulationSubsystem`, `UAscentiaPopulationSourceComponent`,
  zone/cell evaluation, deterministic source seeds, and evidence output.

P17P-003 Ambient StateTree and Smart Object first slice
  Add one traveler StateTree, one wildlife StateTree, campsite/guard/forage
  Smart Object definitions, reservation audit, and player/AI access policy.

P17P-004 PCG candidate and Data Layer/HLOD bridge
  Read PCG candidate points and generated actor metadata, validate Data Layer
  and HLOD inheritance, and block persistent gameplay creation from anonymous
  generated points.

P17P-005 Mass background prototype
  Add a non-combat Mass background profile for distant ambient life with
  significance, LOD, scalability, and no-combat-authority proof.

P17P-006 Population scenario and save/load proof
  Add time/weather/world-state fixtures, streaming spawn/despawn proof, and
  persistence tests for unique vs generic ambient life.

P17P-007 Designer population labs and dashboard cards
  Add population ecology, route/patrol, Smart Object, Mass/StateTree budget, and
  scenario runner EUWs with P17 dashboard integration.
```

Recommended implementation order:

1. Build P17P-001 and P17P-002 after the first World Partition zone profile
   exists, but before filling the zone with ambient content.
2. Add P17P-003 before authoring many campsite, patrol, or wildlife activities.
3. Add P17P-004 before PCG is allowed to propose population candidate points.
4. Keep P17P-005 explicitly non-combat until P18 has a reason to adopt Mass for
   anything more serious than distant background simulation.
5. Add P17P-006 before save/death-loop work depends on world-life state.

### 8.11 Cartography, map discovery, compass, markers, and fast-travel package

The P17 world contract already says that zones need map/discovery data. This
package makes that a production system instead of a few HUD counters and placed
markers. In a questless open-world ARPG, the map is not just navigation UI. It
is a design language for curiosity, memory, return routes, danger, traversal
requirements, fast travel, and spoiler control.

Primary references:

- World Partition supports generating a minimap for the World Partition editor
  and exposes a World Partition Minimap Builder commandlet:
  <https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/world-partition-in-unreal-engine>
- The World Partition Builder Commandlet reference documents the
  `WorldPartitionMiniMapBuilder` route:
  <https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/world-partition-builder-commandlet-reference>
- UMG best practices apply to runtime map, minimap, compass, marker, and tooltip
  widgets:
  <https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/umg-best-practices-in-unreal-engine>
- Gameplay Tags are the right shared identity layer for regions, POIs,
  discoveries, spoiler bands, map marker types, and unlock states:
  <https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/using-gameplay-tags-in-unreal-engine>
- Data Layers organize actors in World Partition at editor and runtime, so map
  layers should read Data Layer intent instead of scraping arbitrary actors:
  <https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/world-partition---data-layers-in-unreal-engine>
- PCG with World Partition can inherit Data Layer and HLOD assignments, which
  matters for generated landmark/icon candidates:
  <https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/using-pcg-with-world-partition-in-unreal-engine>


Runtime policy:

- The map reads `PDA_WorldZone`, `PDA_POI`, `PDA_MapRegion`, discovery actors,
  save state, and world-state tags. It does not scrape placed actor labels from
  the level.
- World map, minimap if used, compass, HUD objective hints, NPC clues, and
  memory clues resolve from the same region/marker/discovery facts.
- Map markers are Gameplay Tag-driven. Display text, icon, tooltip, spoiler
  policy, compass visibility, and accessibility priority are authored data.
- Fog/reveal is not a quest checklist. Reveal policy should support broad
  region discovery, local landmark reveal, player pins, and hidden-spoiler rows
  without turning every objective into a glowing task marker.
- Fast travel is a world-state and anchor contract. Attunement, destination
  preload, streaming readiness, death-loop safety, and return-route policy must
  be visible in the map tool.
- PCG can propose landmark or point-of-interest candidates, but accepted map
  markers are authored records with stable IDs before they affect saves.
- Runtime map textures may begin as editor-baked images, but production builds
  need package/cook evidence for every referenced texture, icon, marker style,
  and localization key.
- Accessibility settings can enlarge icons, reduce clutter, increase contrast,
  alter compass density, expose text alternatives, and suppress low-priority
  animated pings without changing discovery facts.

Designer surfaces:

```text
EUW_AscentiaCartographyBoard
  Shows regions, POIs, discovery state, marker definitions, spoiler bands,
  localization keys, save fragments, and latest map evidence.

EUW_AscentiaMapBakeLab
  Runs map/minimap bake profiles, World Partition minimap builder integration,
  Data Layer filters, tile output, label policy, and screenshot comparisons.

EUW_AscentiaDiscoveryMarkerCompassLab
  Previews marker clustering, compass density, HUD overlap, icon contrast,
  tooltip text, distance bands, combat suppression, and accessibility profiles.

EUW_AscentiaFastTravelMapRouteLab
  Previews attunement, destination preload, route safety, traversal
  requirements, death-loop anchors, streaming readiness, and save/load state.
```

Evidence:

```text
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P17/Cartography/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P17/MapBakes/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P17/MapMarkers/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P17/FastTravelMap/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P17/Cartography/{RunId}/screenshots/
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P17/Cartography/{RunId}/tiles/
```

Proof gates:

```text
Cartography_RegionDefinition_Zone01
MapBake_WorldPartitionMinimapBuilder_Zone01
MapReveal_DiscoveryActor_SaveLoad
MapMarker_POI_Landmark_FastTravelCoverage
Compass_CombatClutterSuppression
FastTravel_Attunement_Preload_ReturnSafety
MapAccessibility_IconScaleContrastTextAlternatives
PCG_MapCandidate_NoAnonymousPersistentMarker
Packaged_MapAssets_CookCoverage
```

Implementation tickets:

```text
P17C-001 Cartography profile and map region structs
  Add `FAscentiaMapRegion`, cartography profiles, region Gameplay Tags, and
  validation against `PDA_WorldZone` and Data Layer policy.

P17C-002 Map reveal and save fragment
  Add `FAscentiaMapRevealState`, save/load migration, discovery actor bridge,
  and proof that reveal state persists without leaking hidden spoiler rows.

P17C-003 Map marker definition and style set
  Add marker definitions, icon/style DataAssets, localization keys, tooltip
  policy, compass/world-map policy, and marker coverage audit.

P17C-004 Map bake profile and commandlet
  Add bake profiles, World Partition minimap builder wrapper, output paths,
  tile metadata, package/cook coverage, and screenshot comparison evidence.

P17C-005 Compass and marker clutter lab
  Add clustering, priority, distance bands, combat suppression, text-scale,
  contrast, icon scale, and accessibility fixtures.

P17C-006 Fast travel map route proof
  Add attunement state, destination preload checks, traversal requirements,
  death-loop anchor safety, save/load proof, and route safety reports.

P17C-007 Dashboard and package gates
  Add P17 dashboard cards and package blockers for missing region data, missing
  marker styles, uncooked map textures, invalid fast-travel anchors, and
  accessibility failures.
```

Recommended implementation order:

1. Build P17C-001 and P17C-002 immediately after the first zone profile exists.
2. Add P17C-003 before POI, discovery, and fast-travel content multiplies.
3. Add P17C-004 before any map screenshot or minimap texture becomes a milestone
   artifact.
4. Add P17C-005 before the HUD/compass gets redesigned around placeholder icon
   density.
5. Add P17C-006 before fast travel becomes a real progression or death-loop
   recovery feature.

### 8.12 Atmosphere, weather, time-of-day, lighting mood, and readability package

Ultra Dynamic Sky is the obvious accelerator candidate here, but it should not
become the architecture. Ascentia needs a provider-agnostic atmosphere layer that
lets Designers choose mood, weather, time of day, readability, material response,
audio state, accessibility scale, and performance budget from visible data. UDS
can supply the sky/weather implementation while Ascentia owns the contract.

Primary references:

- Sky Atmosphere is Unreal's physically based sky/atmosphere system with
  ground-to-space and time-of-day use cases:
  <https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/sky-atmosphere-component-in-unreal-engine>
- Volumetric Clouds are material-driven, scalable, and designed to work with Sky
  Atmosphere and Sky Light real-time capture:
  <https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/volumetric-cloud-component-in-unreal-engine>
- Exponential Height Fog and Volumetric Fog are the engine path for distance fog,
  ground haze, light shafts, and participating media in the camera frustum:
  <https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/exponential-height-fog-in-unreal-engine>
  and
  <https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/volumetric-fog-in-unreal-engine>
- Post Process Effects and color grading are the readable final-image layer for
  exposure, bloom, tone mapping, camera mood, and screen effects:
  <https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/post-process-effects-in-unreal-engine>
  and
  <https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/color-grading-and-the-filmic-tonemapper-in-unreal-engine>
- Material Parameter Collections are the intended global material-control bridge
  for effects such as wetness, snow, dust, destruction, or zone-wide tint:
  <https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/using-material-parameter-collections-in-unreal-engine>
- Scalability groups and Device Profiles are the supported path for keeping
  expensive atmosphere, post, shadow, and effects choices within platform budget:
  <https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/scalability-reference-for-unreal-engine>
- Ultra Dynamic Sky is a third-party sky and weather system with drag-and-drop
  setup, time-of-day, weather, lighting, and material-weather features:
  <https://www.fab.com/listings/84fda27a-c79f-49c9-8458-82401fb37cfb>
  and
  <https://www.ultradynamicsky.com/>
