
Runtime policy:

- Every runtime atmosphere change goes through `UAscentiaAtmosphereSubsystem`.
  Raw UDS actor edits are allowed only in sandbox maps and must appear as bridge
  debt in the tool report.
- UDS, native Unreal sky actors, or any future provider implement
  `IAscentiaAtmosphereProvider`. Profiles and Gameplay Tags are the stable
  Ascentia API.
- Weather is presentation-only unless `FAscentiaWeatherGameplayPolicy` explicitly
  opts a system in. Rain should not change movement, AI perception, spawns, or
  stealth because a visual preset changed.
- Weather and time-of-day state serialize as compact tags plus normalized time.
  Provider-private values are reconstructed from profiles, not saved as level
  actor state.
- Material response routes through P23S surface profiles and
  `MPC_AscentiaAtmosphereState`. Wetness, snow, ash, dust, and dry-down are data,
  not per-material special cases.
- Audio response routes through P20A music, Soundscape, ambience, and mix
  profiles. Weather can request an audio state; it does not own audio playback.
- Population and encounter response routes through P17P and P18 opt-in gates.
  "Night spawns more enemies" is a world-state rule, not an atmosphere side
  effect.
- Accessibility profiles can reduce fog density, lightning flash, screen
  effects, precipitation opacity, wind particles, camera shake, and audio
  intensity without changing save/world facts.
- Performance budgets are profile fields. Cloud, fog, shadow, post, VFX, and
  weather-material costs need per-scalability expectations before a profile is
  promoted to milestone content.

Designer surfaces:

```text
EUW_AscentiaAtmosphereDirector
  Shows atmosphere profiles, provider adapter, UDS preset source, zone/biome
  bindings, active state, raw-provider debt, latest evidence, and promotion
  status.

EUW_AscentiaWeatherTimeScenarioLab
  Runs clear/noon, dusk, rain, night, storm, interior, cave mouth, combat, loot,
  boss fog-wall, fast-travel arrival, and respawn scenarios against profiles.

EUW_AscentiaLightingMoodLab
  Compares screenshot grids for combat silhouettes, interact prompt contrast,
  loot readability, landmark visibility, map/compass clarity, and accessibility
  variants.

EUW_AscentiaAtmospherePerformanceBudgetLab
  Captures cost snapshots per scalability bucket, profile, weather state,
  viewport scenario, and hardware/device profile target, then writes blockers.
```

```text
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P17/WeatherTime/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P17/LightingReadability/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P17/AtmospherePerformance/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P17/Atmosphere/{RunId}/screenshots/
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P17/Atmosphere/{RunId}/profile_diffs/
```

Implementation tickets:

```text
P17W-001 Atmosphere provider interface and profile data
  Add `IAscentiaAtmosphereProvider`, `UAscentiaAtmosphereSubsystem`,
  `FAscentiaAtmosphereProfile`, provider policy, UDS adapter shell, and raw
  provider debt reporting.

P17W-002 Weather and time-of-day profile structs
  Add weather/time profile assets, transition requests, normalized save state,
  and proof that clear/rain/storm/night states survive save/load.

P17W-003 Biome mood and lighting readability lab
  Add biome mood profiles, screenshot-grid fixtures, combat silhouette scoring,
  prompt/loot/landmark contrast checks, and accessibility variants.

P17W-004 Material, audio, and population bridges
  Bridge atmosphere state into P23S Material Parameter Collections, P20A audio
  profiles, P17P population gates, and P18 AI perception only through explicit
  opt-in policy.

P17W-005 Weather gameplay policy strictness
  Add `FAscentiaWeatherGameplayPolicy`, blockers for implicit movement/AI/spawn
  side effects, and dashboard rows for every weather-affecting system.

P17W-006 Atmosphere performance budget lab
  Add cloud, fog, post, VFX, shadow, and weather-material cost reporting by
  scalability bucket and device/profile target.

P17W-007 Accessibility and photosensitivity pass
  Add low-fog, low-flash, low-motion, precipitation-opacity, wind-particle, and
  audio-intensity scaling proof for atmosphere profiles.

P17W-008 Dashboard and package gates
  Add P17 dashboard cards and package blockers for missing profiles, raw UDS
  debt, unreadable lighting, broken material/audio bridges, failed weather
  save/load, and over-budget weather states.
```

Recommended implementation order:

1. Build P17W-001 before adopting UDS outside prototype maps.
2. Add P17W-002 before time-of-day or weather affects saves, respawns, or
   fast-travel arrival.
3. Add P17W-003 before the first real zone art pass, because readability gets
   expensive to fix after mood lighting spreads.
4. Add P17W-004 and P17W-005 before weather is allowed to touch materials,
   audio, population, or AI.
5. Add P17W-006 and P17W-007 before a weather-heavy arena or biome becomes a
   milestone target.

### 8.13 Zone delivery pipeline, blockout, playtest, art pass, streaming, and signoff package

P17 should not be written as "make a big level." It needs a repeatable zone
delivery pipeline that lets Ascentia move from Landscry/world concept to
blockout, playable proof, content lock, art pass, optimization, package proof,
and final signoff. This is the production bridge between the world systems in
sections 8.8 through 8.12 and the team-operations package in section 15.20.

The key discipline is that every zone state must be named. A graybox zone,
playable traversal route, art-pass cell, combat encounter pocket, and release
candidate cell are different artifacts with different gates. If those gates are
not explicit, a beautiful area can still fail because nav is missing, World
Partition cells stream late, HLODs are stale, a Data Layer is wrong, PCG output
has no provenance, or performance has only been tested from the editor camera.

Primary references:

- World Partition stores a large world in one persistent level divided into
  streamable grid cells, works with OFPA, Data Layers, Level Instancing, and
  HLOD, and supports runtime streaming sources, editor region loading, minimap
  building, HLOD building, cooking, and runtime debugging.
  https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/en-us/unreal-engine/world-partition-in-unreal-engine
- Data Layers are part of the World Partition workflow and are the correct
  level-organizing primitive for alternate world states, quest/world-state
  content, gameplay sets, and editor authoring contexts.
  https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/en-us/unreal-engine/world-partition---data-layers-in-unreal-engine
- World Partition HLODs are generated through the Build HLODs workflow or
  commandlet and should be treated as production artifacts with freshness,
  budget, and package proof.
  https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/en-us/unreal-engine/world-partition---hierarchical-level-of-detail-in-unreal-engine
- Level Instancing supports reusable level chunks. Ascentia should use it for
  authored room kits, camps, ruins, caves, arena modules, and POI kits instead
  of duplicating bespoke set-dressing across the open world.
  https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/en-us/unreal-engine/level-instancing-in-unreal-engine
- PCG is intended to give artists and designers fast, iterative procedural
  tools ranging from asset utilities to entire worlds. Ascentia should bridge
  Landscry and PCG through provenance, deterministic seeds, staging folders, and
  Designer review, not opaque one-click generation.
  https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/en-us/unreal-engine/procedural-content-generation-framework-in-unreal-engine
- Unreal Navigation generates a tiled navmesh from collision geometry and uses
  polygon costs, generation modes, nav links, and avoidance options. Zone
  delivery gates must include player traversal, AI traversal, Smart Object
  access, combat pockets, and ledge/stair/water exceptions.
  https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/en-us/unreal-engine/navigation-system-in-unreal-engine
- MassEntity is a data-oriented framework using fragments, archetypes, chunks,
  processors, and MassEntityConfig assets. It is useful for ambient population
  scale, but zone delivery should keep combat authority outside Mass until a
  specific system proves it needs that representation.
  https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/en-us/unreal-engine/overview-of-mass-entity-in-unreal-engine
- Unreal Insights captures high-rate telemetry and includes Timing, Memory,
  Asset Loading, Cooking, Networking, and Slate analysis. Zone signoff should
  attach trace files and summarized budgets rather than relying on editor feel.
  https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/en-us/unreal-engine/unreal-insights-in-unreal-engine

```text
FAscentiaWorldPartitionProof
  Map
  RuntimeGrid
  CellSize
  LoadingRange
  LoadedRegions
  LocationVolumes
  StreamingSources
  DataLayerStates
  MinimapBuildState
  HLODBuildState
  CookState
  RuntimeDebugRows

FAscentiaZoneNavTraversalProof
  ZoneTag
  NavmeshTiles
  AgentProfiles
  SmartObjectLinks
  TraversalAnchors
  NavLinks
  BlockedCombatPockets
  WaterAndLedgeExceptions
  PlayerAIRouteParity
  BlockingFindings

FAscentiaZoneSignoffReport
  ZoneTag
  Milestone
  SourceRevision
  BuildArtifact
  PlaytestRoutes
  StreamingProof
  NavProof
  HLODProof
  PCGProvenance
  PerformanceTrace
  AccessibilityProof
  DesignerVerdicts
  FinalVerdict
```

Milestone model:

```text
Concept
  Paper map, mood board, combat intent, traversal verbs, POI list, risks.

Blockout
  Walkable graybox, scale proof, main route, first combat pocket, rough gates.

Playable
  Player start, route objective, core traversal, camera proof, encounter proof,
  rest/respawn/fail state, map/compass stubs.

ContentAlpha
  Encounter definitions, loot, NPC/world-state hooks, guidance, save/load,
  fast-travel or return route, placeholder art still allowed by policy.

ArtAlpha
  Kit pieces, Level Instances, PCG pass, lighting mood, surface response,
  collision, nav, HLOD, minimap, and accessibility screenshots.

Optimization
  World Partition streaming proof, HLOD freshness, Insights trace, asset weight,
  memory/load/cook budget, scalability variants.

ReleaseCandidate
  Packaged route proof, no forbidden placeholders, no missing source records,
  no stale HLOD/minimap/nav evidence, signed Designer/QA verdicts.
```

Rules:

- A zone cannot skip milestone gates because it "looks done." Visual quality is
  one signal; playability, streaming, nav, save, performance, accessibility, and
  package proof are separate signals.
- Landscry and PCG output starts in staging with provenance and deterministic
  generation metadata. Promotion into production cells requires Designer review,
  source/license records, collision/nav checks, and redirector-safe move plans.
- Level Instances are the default for reusable authored modules such as camps,
  ruins, cave rooms, arena shells, shrine kits, and Below-compatible rooms.
- Data Layers are authored as production state, not scratch folders. Quest,
  boss-defeated, shortcut-open, population, weather/gameplay, and debug layers
  need naming policy and runtime-state proof.
- Every milestone has a playable route. A screenshot-only zone cannot advance
  beyond ArtAlpha.
- Combat pockets must prove camera collision, traversal exits, enemy nav,
  leash/reset policy, loot/corpse path, presentation readability, and recovery
  anchors.
- World Partition proof includes editor region loading, Location Volumes,
  runtime streaming sources, fast-travel preload, minimap state, HLOD state,
  package/cook state, and debug command output.
- HLOD, minimap, navmesh, and PCG generated artifacts have freshness checks.
  If source actors, Data Layers, landscapes, or generation seeds changed after
  the last build, signoff blocks.
- Mass is allowed for distant ambient population and crowd-like simulation after
  P17P proof. Combat encounters remain actor/AI-authority until a specific
  Mass combat design earns that complexity.
- Zone signoff attaches Unreal Insights traces and summarized Timing, Memory,
  Asset Loading, and Cooking rows for the target route, not just average FPS.

Designer tools:

```text
EUW_AscentiaZoneDeliveryBoard
EUW_AscentiaBlockoutPlaytestLab
EUW_AscentiaWorldPartitionProofBoard
EUW_AscentiaZoneNavTraversalGate
EUW_AscentiaZoneHLODBudgetBoard
EUW_AscentiaPCGProvenanceGate
EUW_AscentiaZonePerformanceTraceBoard
EUW_AscentiaZoneSignoffBoard
```

`EUW_AscentiaZoneDeliveryBoard` shows every zone, current milestone, blocking
findings, latest evidence, owner verdicts, source revision, and next required
gate.

`EUW_AscentiaBlockoutPlaytestLab` runs named routes from anchors, captures
travel time, camera issues, fall/death events, combat pocket outcomes, guidance
state, and human notes.

`EUW_AscentiaWorldPartitionProofBoard` previews grid, cells, loaded regions,
Location Volumes, streaming sources, Data Layer states, minimap build state,
HLOD build state, and cook proof.

`EUW_AscentiaZoneNavTraversalGate` compares player traversal anchors, AI nav,
Smart Object links, nav links, ledge/water exceptions, and combat-pocket access.

`EUW_AscentiaZoneHLODBudgetBoard` shows HLOD layers, stale builds, estimated
draw/triangle/material cost, source actor drift, and scalability variants.

`EUW_AscentiaPCGProvenanceGate` shows PCG graphs, Landscry source package,
generation seed, generated asset roots, accepted/rejected outputs, source
records, and collision/nav/material findings.

`EUW_AscentiaZonePerformanceTraceBoard` links Insights trace files, summary CSVs,
route markers, asset-loading rows, cooking rows, memory rows, and blocking
budget findings.

`EUW_AscentiaZoneSignoffBoard` produces the final milestone verdict with
Designer, engineering, QA, audio, lighting, encounter, accessibility, and
performance rows.

Commandlets:

```text
AscentiaZoneDeliveryAudit
  -Zone=/Game/Ascentia/World/Zones/PDA_WorldZone_Zone01
  -Policy=/Game/Ascentia/World/Zones/PDA_ZoneDeliveryPolicy_MVP
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P17/ZoneDelivery

AscentiaWorldPartitionProof
  -Map=/Game/Ascentia/Maps/WP_Ascentia_Zone01
  -Profile=/Game/Ascentia/World/Zones/PDA_ZoneStreamingProofProfile_Zone01
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P17/WorldPartitionProof

AscentiaZoneNavTraversalGate
  -Profile=/Game/Ascentia/World/Zones/PDA_ZoneNavTraversalProofProfile_Zone01
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P17/ZoneNavTraversal

AscentiaZoneHLODBudgetAudit
  -Profile=/Game/Ascentia/World/Zones/PDA_ZoneHLODBudgetProfile_Zone01
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P17/ZoneHLOD

AscentiaZoneSignoff
  -Profile=/Game/Ascentia/World/Zones/PDA_ZoneSignoffProfile_Zone01
  -Milestone=Playable
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P17/ZoneSignoff
```

Evidence:

```text
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P17/ZoneDelivery/{RunId}/zone_delivery_report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P17/BlockoutPlaytests/{RunId}/route_report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P17/WorldPartitionProof/{RunId}/wp_report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P17/ZoneNavTraversal/{RunId}/nav_traversal_report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P17/ZoneHLOD/{RunId}/hlod_budget_report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P17/PCGProvenance/{RunId}/pcg_provenance_report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P17/ZonePerformance/{RunId}/zone_trace_summary.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P17/ZoneSignoff/{RunId}/signoff_report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P17/ZoneSignoff/{RunId}/screenshots/
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P17/ZoneSignoff/{RunId}/traces/
```

Proof matrix:

```text
ZoneDelivery_MilestoneGate_Blockout
ZoneDelivery_MilestoneGate_Playable
ZoneDelivery_MilestoneGate_ArtAlpha
ZoneDelivery_MilestoneGate_Optimization
ZoneDelivery_MilestoneGate_ReleaseCandidate
BlockoutRoute_MainLoop_StartToReturn
WorldPartition_StreamingSource_FastTravelPreload
WorldPartition_DataLayers_RuntimeStateValid
WorldPartition_Minimap_HLOD_CookFresh
ZoneNav_PlayerAIRouteParity
ZoneCombatPocket_CameraLeashRecoveryLoot
PCGProvenance_LandscrySeedAcceptedOutputs
ZonePerformance_InsightsTraceAttached
ZoneSignoff_NoForbiddenPlaceholderAssets
ZoneSignoff_AllDisciplineVerdictsCaptured
```

P17Z starter tickets:

- P17Z-001: Add zone delivery profile, milestone gate structs, policy assets,
  and `EUW_AscentiaZoneDeliveryBoard`.
- P17Z-002: Add blockout playtest route assets, route runner, human verdict
  capture, and `EUW_AscentiaBlockoutPlaytestLab`.
- P17Z-003: Add World Partition proof audit for grid, streaming sources, loaded
  regions, Location Volumes, Data Layers, minimap, HLOD, and cook state.
- P17Z-004: Add zone nav/traversal gate that compares player anchors, AI nav,
  Smart Objects, nav links, combat pockets, ledges, stairs, water, and recovery
  anchors.
- P17Z-005: Add HLOD budget/freshness board and stale generated-artifact
  blockers for HLOD, minimap, nav, and PCG outputs.
- P17Z-006: Add PCG/Landscry provenance gate with deterministic seed, source
  package, generated roots, accepted outputs, and promotion state.
- P17Z-007: Add zone performance trace board that summarizes Insights Timing,
  Memory, Asset Loading, and Cooking evidence for named routes.
- P17Z-008: Add zone signoff board and package blockers for milestone exit,
  placeholder policy, discipline verdicts, and dashboard cards.

Recommended implementation order:

1. Build P17Z-001 before the first real Zone01 expansion beyond the MVP arena.
2. Add P17Z-002 while the zone is still graybox. Route pain should shape the
   space before art makes it expensive.
3. Add P17Z-003 and P17Z-004 before World Partition, traversal, and AI content
   multiply across cells.
4. Add P17Z-005 and P17Z-006 before PCG/Landscry output is promoted into
   production roots.
5. Add P17Z-007 before optimization begins, so every trace has the same route
   and scenario identity.
6. Add P17Z-008 before any zone milestone is called "done" in dashboard or
   release notes.

## 9. AI, Enemies, Encounters, and Bosses

### 9.1 Player-facing goal

Enemies should feel authored, legible, and systemic. Trash enemies teach. Elites
test. Bosses define areas. Groups create pressure without becoming noise.

### 9.2 Current Ascentia state

Working pieces:

- `AAscentiaEnemyCharacter`;
- `AAscentiaEnemyAIController`;
- Behavior Tree hooks;
- `UAscentiaBossDefinition`;
- boss phase telemetry;
- fog wall actor;
- corpse loot;
- basic aggro/chase/attack;
- native boss presentation telemetry.

Gap:

- enemy behavior is still shallow;
- no enemy data row pipeline;
- no scalable group coordination;
- no EQS positioning model;
- no enemy ability-set authoring standard;
- no full boss move grammar.

### 9.3 AAA target behavior

Enemy production should split into:

```text
EnemyDefinition
- identity, stats, resistances, poise, resources
- movement model
- perception model
- ability set
- behavior tree
- EQS queries
- loot table
- presentation bundle
- encounter budget cost
- world tags
```

The AI stack should be:

- Behavior Tree for readable high-level flow;
- Blackboard for current facts;
- EQS for environment choices such as spacing, cover, flank, retreat, ranged
  position, pack spacing, and arena anchors;
- GameplayAbilities for attacks and special moves;
- Utility scoring for move selection when the enemy has many valid options;
- group coordination component for packs and boss adds;
- Mass only for non-combat crowds or distant simulation until combat AI needs
  justify a Mass architecture.

### 9.4 Boss grammar

Each boss needs:

```text
BossDefinition
- DisplayName
- LoreTag
- ArenaTag
- Phase rows
- Health thresholds
- Ability sets per phase
- Behavior tags per phase
- Music states
- Fog wall policy
- Intro presentation
- Defeat presentation
- Loot table
- Rematch/reset policy
```

Each boss move needs:

```text
BossMoveDefinition
- MoveTag
- AbilityClass
- Montage
- Range band
- Angle band
- Min/Max cooldown
- Resource cost
- Telegraph cue
- Active damage windows
- Recovery windows
- Cancel/interruption rules
- Root motion policy
- Arena constraints
- Counterplay note
```

### 9.5 Designer surface

Designers should author:

- `PDA_EnemyDefinition_*`
- `PDA_BossDefinition_*`
- `BT_Enemy_*`
- `BB_Enemy_*`
- `EQS_*`
- `GA_EnemyAttack_*`
- `GC_Ascentia_*`
- `PDA_EncounterDefinition_*`
- spawn budgets and encounter zones.

### 9.6 Validation

AI validation should catch:

- enemy has no ability set;
- boss phase has no behavior tag;
- BT references missing blackboard keys;
- EQS query missing context;
- ability range does not match BT range;
- attack montage lacks damage notifies;
- boss fog wall not linked;
- encounter budget exceeds zone budget;
- enemy loot table missing;
- world-tier scaling row missing;
- AI cannot navigate to required combat anchors.

### 9.7 P18 AI and encounter implementation contract

P18's purpose is to turn "one enemy that can chase and attack" into a production
enemy pipeline. The first deliverable is not enemy count. It is the contract that
lets many enemies be authored without rewriting the pawn every time.

#### 9.7.1 Production split

Use this ownership model:

```text
Enemy pawn/controller
  -> physical embodiment, ASC, perception, movement, animation, telemetry

Enemy definition asset/rows
  -> identity, stats, resistances, abilities, loot, budget cost, presentation

Behavior Tree / StateTree
  -> readable high-level behavior flow

EQS
  -> spatial decisions

GameplayAbilities
  -> attacks, special moves, resource spends, cooldowns, damage windows

Encounter definition
  -> which enemies, where, when, budget, group rules, reset/save policy
```

The enemy pawn should become boring. Interesting enemy behavior should live in
data, behavior assets, ability assets, animations, and encounter context.

#### 9.7.2 Enemy definition

```text
PDA_EnemyDefinition_*
- EnemyTag
- DisplayName
- EnemyFamilyTags
- WorldTier
- AttributeProfileTag
- ResistanceProfileTag
- PoiseProfileTag
- ResourceProfileTag
- PerceptionProfileTag
- MovementProfileTag
- AbilitySet
- BehaviorTree
- StateTree
- BlackboardAsset
- EQSProfiles
- LootTableTag
- CorpseLootPolicy
- EncounterBudgetCost
- SpawnTags
- FactionTag
- VoiceCueProfile
- HitReactionProfile
- DeathPresentationProfile
- SmartObjectUsageTags
- NavAgentType
- ValidationNotes
```

Rows own bulk stats. PrimaryDataAssets own references. This mirrors the item
pipeline: broad numeric content belongs in DataTables, object references and
presentation bundles belong in assets.

#### 9.7.3 Attribute and scaling rows

```text
FAscentiaEnemyStatRow
- EnemyTag
- WorldTier
- Level
- MaxHealth
- MaxBreath
- BreathRegen
- Armor
- Resistances
- MaxPoise
- AttackPowerScalar
- RewardStones
- XPOrProgressionValue
- EncounterBudgetCost
```

```text
FAscentiaEnemyFamilyRow
- EnemyFamilyTag
- DefaultBehaviorTree
- DefaultAbilitySet
- DefaultPerceptionProfile
- DefaultLootTableTag
- DefaultMovementProfile
- AllowedBiomeTags
- AllowedEncounterTags
```

Scaling should be region/tier based first. Avoid hidden global multipliers that
make Designers unable to explain why an enemy is strong.

#### 9.7.4 AI stack decision

Default combat enemy stack:

```text
AIController
  -> Perception
  -> Behavior Tree
  -> Blackboard
  -> EQS
  -> GameplayAbilities
  -> Combat motion request
```

Use Behavior Trees for combat because they are readable, already scaffolded in
Ascentia, and easy for Designers to branch by phase, range, target state,
resource state, and cooldown state.

Use EQS for spatial choice:

- melee approach point;
- circle/strafe point;
- ranged line-of-sight point;
- retreat point;
- flank point;
- pack spacing point;
- boss arena anchor;
- summon/add spawn point;
- return-to-post point.

Use StateTree for:

- ambient behaviors;
- noncombat schedules;
- guard post states;
- patrol/rest/investigate loops;
- Smart Object usage;
- lightweight world NPCs;
- future distant simulation.

Use Mass for:

- distant noncombat crowds;
- wildlife/flocks;
- background settlement life;
- traffic-like movement;
- large numbers of non-combat agents.

Do not move core boss combat into Mass. Bosses need bespoke animation, combat
motion, ability, camera, and presentation control.

#### 9.7.5 Blackboard contract

Core blackboard keys:

```text
TargetActor
HomeLocation
LeashAnchor
CurrentEnemyTag
CurrentBehaviorTag
CurrentPhaseTag
CurrentPhaseIndex
HealthFraction
BreathFraction
PoiseFraction
TargetDistance
TargetAngle
HasLineOfSight
DesiredCombatRange
SelectedAbilityTag
SelectedMoveTag
SelectedEQSLocation
EncounterTag
GroupRoleTag
IsLeashing
IsReturningHome
```

Boss-specific keys:

```text
BossPhaseId
BossPhaseBehaviorTag
ArenaTag
FogWallSealed
LastMoveTag
MoveCooldownGroup
EnrageState
AddsAliveCount
```

Every native BT service/task should document which keys it reads and writes.
Validators should fail when a BT asset lacks required keys.

#### 9.7.6 Ability and move selection

Enemy attacks should be GameplayAbilities. Behavior assets choose when to ask
for them; abilities own cost, cooldown, montage, motion, damage windows, cues,
and telemetry.

Move definition:

```text
PDA_EnemyMove_*
- MoveTag
- AbilityClass
- RequiredEnemyFamilyTags
- RangeMin
- RangeMax
- AngleMin
- AngleMax
- CooldownTag
- CooldownSeconds
- ResourceCost
- Weight
- PhaseTags
- TargetStateRequirements
- SelfStateRequirements
- MotionProfile
- TelegraphCue
- RecoveryCue
- CounterplayNotes
```

When many moves are valid, use utility scoring:

```text
Score =
  range score
  + angle score
  + phase weight
  + cooldown availability
  + target state bonus
  + recent move penalty
  + group role adjustment
  + Designer-authored weight
```

Keep the scoring inspectable. Designers should be able to see "why did the boss
choose this move?"

#### 9.7.7 Encounter definition

```text
PDA_EncounterDefinition_*
- EncounterTag
- ZoneTag
- POITag
- WorldTier
- EncounterType
- Budget
- SpawnGroups
- PatrolRoutes
- SmartObjectSets
- LeashPolicy
- ResetPolicy
- SavePolicy
- AggroPolicy
- RewardPolicy
- MusicState
- CompletionWorldStateTags
- FailureWorldStateTags
- RuntimeDataLayers
- DebugNotes
```

Spawn group:

```text
FAscentiaEncounterSpawnGroup
- GroupTag
- EnemyDefinitions
- SpawnTransformsOrAnchors
- PatrolRouteTag
- GroupRoleTags
- SpawnConditionTags
- RespawnPolicy
- MaxAlive
- AlertPropagationRadius
```

The current `AAscentiaEncounterZoneActor.ManagedSpawns` is the MVP editor
surface. Production should move the authored plan into `PDA_EncounterDefinition`
and let the placed zone actor reference it.

#### 9.7.8 Group coordination

Groups need explicit roles:

```text
Ascentia.AI.Role.Frontliner
Ascentia.AI.Role.Flanker
Ascentia.AI.Role.Ranged
Ascentia.AI.Role.Caster
Ascentia.AI.Role.Support
Ascentia.AI.Role.SummonedAdd
```

Group coordinator responsibilities:

- assign roles on spawn;
- throttle simultaneous attacks;
- prevent all melee enemies from occupying the same slot;
- broadcast alert state;
- reserve flank/attack positions;
- release reservations on death/leash/reset;
- expose group telemetry.

This can begin as `UAscentiaEncounterGroupComponent` on the zone actor and
graduate later if AI population grows.

#### 9.7.9 Leash, return, and persistence

Every enemy needs:

```text
HomeLocation
LeashRadius
HardLeashRadius
ReturnBehavior
RespawnPolicy
PersistencePolicy
```

Policies:

- trash enemies respawn on rest;
- unique elites may stay dead until world reset;
- story bosses stay dead after defeat;
- summoned adds despawn with encounter;
- patrol enemies return to route;
- ambushers reset hidden state when out of combat unless discovered state says
  otherwise.

P18 should eliminate "AI only sees player 0." Targets should come from
perception, aggro policy, team/faction rules, and later multiplayer relevance,
not a direct player-zero lookup.

#### 9.7.10 Smart Objects

Smart Objects should define level-authored AI activities:

- guard post;
- patrol wait point;
- ambush hide point;
- ritual site;
- chair/campfire/workstation;
- ladder/door/lift interaction;
- ranged perch;
- boss arena anchor;
- summon point;
- environmental hazard use.

Each Smart Object needs tags, reservation rules, and validation. World Partition
Smart Object collection builder should be part of P17/P18 gates when Smart
Objects are present.

#### 9.7.11 Boss move grammar

Bosses are encounter systems, not large enemies with more health.

Boss production assets:

```text
PDA_BossDefinition_*
PDA_BossPhase_*
PDA_BossMove_*
BT_Boss_*
BB_Boss_*
EQS_Boss_*
GA_BossMove_*
PDA_BossArena_*
```

Boss move requirements:

- telegraph readable before active frames;
- range/angle bands;
- cooldown and repeat penalty;
- resource or stamina logic when useful;
- root-motion/motion-warp policy;
- arena collision policy;
- active damage notifies;
- recovery/cancel windows;
- punish/counterplay note;
- camera/rumble/cue profile;
- phase availability;
- group/add interactions;
- validation.

Each boss phase should change at least one of:

- move weights;
- new move unlocks;
- arena state;
- add pressure;
- music state;
- resource economy;
- counterplay rhythm.

If a phase only changes health-bar text, it is presentation, not a real phase.

#### 9.7.12 Debug and proof

AI proof must be visible, not only logged. Use:

- UE AI debugger for BT/EQS/perception state;
- in-world debug labels for encounter/group role;
- blackboard key snapshots;
- selected move telemetry;
- EQS chosen location telemetry;
- ability activation counts;
- leash/return telemetry;
- group attack-slot telemetry.

Remote scenarios:

- `bt_asset_ai`: designer BT starts, fallback tick AI disabled, target syncs,
  move task succeeds, ability fires.
- `enemy_leash`: enemy aggroes, pursues, exceeds leash, returns home, resets.
- `enemy_economy`: Breath costs/rejects/regens under Designer policy.
- `encounter_budget`: zone spawns budgeted groups and reports live/dead/reset.
- `enemy_eqs_spacing`: two melee enemies choose different attack anchors.
- `boss_move_grammar`: boss selects legal moves by range/phase/cooldown.
- `boss_persistence`: story boss defeat persists after rest/save/load.

#### 9.7.13 Designer-facing AI tools

Build or reserve:

- `EUW_AscentiaEnemyDefinitionBrowser`
- `EUW_AscentiaEncounterBudgetPreview`
- `EUW_AscentiaBossMoveMatrix`
- `EUW_AscentiaAIValidationPanel`
- `EUW_AscentiaEQSPreview`

Useful messages:

- "Enemy DraugrKnight has BehaviorTree but no Blackboard"
- "BT_Boss_Draugr reads BossPhaseId but BB_Draugr lacks that key"
- "Boss move CairnLeap has root motion required but montage has no root motion"
- "Encounter RuinGate exceeds budget: 42 / 30"
- "Enemy Archer has ranged ability but no line-of-sight EQS query"
- "Story boss has RespawnPolicy RestRespawn; expected PersistentDefeated"

## 10. UI, UX, Settings, and Accessibility

### 10.1 Player-facing goal

The UI should make deep systems understandable without flattening them. The game
should support readable combat, inventory comparison, build planning, settings,
remapping, accessibility, and low-friction iteration.

### 10.2 Current Ascentia state

Working pieces:

- native Canvas HUD with core combat/debug panels;
- native inventory screen;
- loot window;
- menu screen;
- save-slot panel;
- objective tracker;
- tutorial prompt subsystem;
- many settings candidates recorded in docs.

Gap:

- no unified settings registry;
- no final UMG style system;
- no input remap UI;
- inventory comparison is not PoE/D4 grade;
- no accessibility profiles;
- no content-driven tooltip model.

### 10.3 Settings registry

Create a settings spine before P21 UI:

```text
UAscentiaSettingsSaveGame
- Audio sliders
- Visual feedback intensity
- Camera shake scale
- Rumble scale
- Damage number visibility/size
- Lock-on behavior
- Input mode preferences
- Text scale
- Subtitle/background settings
- Colorblind palette
- Combat assist toggles where design permits
- Tutorial verbosity
- Objective hint frequency
```

Native code reads settings through a single accessor. Feature code does not own
its own one-off setting unless it registers it.

### 10.4 Inventory UI target

The inventory should show:

- grid placement;
- equipment slots;
- item comparison;
- requirements;
- base damage and damage schools;
- status buildup;
- affixes and ranges;
- weight/equip load impact;
- granted skill;
- moveset family;
- sell/vendor value;
- source/rarity;
- lock/favorite;
- quick actions;
- controller navigation.

Tooltip data should be a view model, not text assembled ad hoc in Slate/UMG.

### 10.5 UI/UX/settings starter architecture package

Section 10 owns the runtime player-facing UI contract. P20 owns presentation
cue routing and UI performance evidence. P21 owns shell flow, platform/package
proof, and final CommonUI shipping polish. This section is the bridge: every
HUD, inventory, prompt, tooltip, settings row, accessibility profile, and
Designer UI tool should have a visible data contract before the project grows
more menus.

The posture is:

- use CommonUI for layered menus, focus routing, gamepad navigation, and
  platform glyph support;
- use Enhanced Input as the source of gameplay action truth, including
  remappable actions;
- use event-driven UMG updates and invalidation-aware widgets instead of
  per-frame attribute binding for common HUD and inventory fields;
- use `FText`, String Tables, and localization keys for player-facing copy;
- make accessibility metadata part of the widget/data contract, not a late
  QA checklist;
- keep Designers in Editor-facing Blueprint/EUW tools for themes, settings,
  prompts, glyphs, accessibility profiles, and tooltip previews.

Core runtime layout:

```text
Source/Ascentia/Public/UI/
  AscentiaUIViewModelTypes.h
  AscentiaHUDViewModel.h
  AscentiaInventoryViewModel.h
  AscentiaTooltipViewTypes.h
  AscentiaSettingsRegistry.h
  AscentiaInputGlyphTypes.h
  AscentiaAccessibilityTypes.h
  AscentiaUIModelSubsystem.h
Source/Ascentia/Private/UI/
  AscentiaUIModelSubsystem.cpp
  AscentiaSettingsRegistry.cpp
  AscentiaInputGlyphTypes.cpp
  AscentiaTooltipViewTypes.cpp
  AscentiaAccessibilityTypes.cpp
```

Core editor layout:

```text
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/UI/
  AscentiaUIContractAudit.h
  AscentiaSettingsRegistryAudit.h
  AscentiaInputGlyphAudit.h
  AscentiaTooltipAudit.h
  AscentiaAccessibilityAudit.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/UI/
  AscentiaUIContractAudit.cpp
  AscentiaSettingsRegistryAudit.cpp
  AscentiaInputGlyphAudit.cpp
  AscentiaTooltipAudit.cpp
  AscentiaAccessibilityAudit.cpp
```

Blueprint/DataAsset roots:

```text
/Game/Ascentia/UI/HUD
/Game/Ascentia/UI/Inventory
/Game/Ascentia/UI/Common
/Game/Ascentia/UI/Settings
/Game/Ascentia/UI/Tooltips
/Game/Ascentia/UI/Themes
```

Subsystems:

```text
UAscentiaUIModelSubsystem
  BlueprintCallable GetHUDViewModel(LocalUserIndex)
  BlueprintCallable GetInventoryViewModel(LocalUserIndex)
  BlueprintCallable ResolveItemTooltip(ItemInstanceId, CompareAgainst)
  BlueprintAssignable OnHUDViewModelChanged
  BlueprintAssignable OnInventoryViewModelChanged
  BlueprintAssignable OnPromptRowsChanged

UAscentiaSettingsRegistrySubsystem
  BlueprintCallable GetSettingDefinition(SettingTag)
  BlueprintCallable GetSettingValue(SettingTag, LocalUserIndex)
  BlueprintCallable PreviewSettingChange(Request)
  BlueprintCallable ApplySettingChange(Request)
  BlueprintCallable RevertPreviewChanges(LocalUserIndex)
  BlueprintAssignable OnSettingChanged

UAscentiaInputGlyphSubsystem
  BlueprintCallable ResolveGlyph(Request)
  BlueprintCallable ResolvePromptRow(InputActionTag, LocalUserIndex)
  BlueprintCallable GetCurrentInputDevice(LocalUserIndex)
  BlueprintAssignable OnInputDeviceChanged

UAscentiaAccessibilitySubsystem
  BlueprintCallable GetActiveProfile(LocalUserIndex)
  BlueprintCallable ApplyAccessibilityProfile(ProfileTag, LocalUserIndex)
  BlueprintCallable GetScaledCueValue(CueTag, RawValue)
  BlueprintAssignable OnAccessibilityProfileChanged
```

Rules:

- Widgets read view models and subsystem events. They do not crawl player,
  inventory, ability, or item actors at paint time.
- HUD and inventory fields are updated from explicit events, not raw per-frame
  UMG bindings.
- Tooltip text is resolved from item/spell/status view models and localization
  keys. Slate/UMG widgets may format rows, but they do not own item math.
- Prompt rows are keyed by input action tag or mapping name. No widget writes
  literal "Press E", "LMB", or gamepad button names except as fallback debug
  text in validation-only builds.
- Settings changes move through preview/apply/revert requests. A setting row
  must declare whether it applies instantly, on confirmation, on map reload, or
  on restart.
- Accessibility profiles scale presentation cues from P20 and input friction
  from P21. They do not change competitive/gameplay rules unless a setting is
  explicitly tagged as an assist.
- Every user-facing string is `FText` backed by a literal namespace, String
  Table key, or asset text field. Converting to `FString` is treated as an
  integration boundary, not a UI storage format.
- CommonUI layer policy is visible to Designers, but section 10 only defines
  runtime view-model and prompt contracts. P21 owns the final shell/layer stack.

Designer tools:

```text
EUW_AscentiaUIContractAudit
  Scans HUD/menu/inventory widgets for tick bindings, missing view-model
  sources, hardcoded prompt text, missing accessible text, and missing
  localization keys.

EUW_AscentiaSettingsRegistry
  Edits `PDA_SettingsRegistry_MVP`, previews setting values, shows apply policy,
  writes fixture change requests, and links rows to settings widgets.

EUW_AscentiaInputGlyphBinder
  Shows every Enhanced Input action/mapping used by player-facing prompts,
  previews keyboard/mouse/gamepad glyphs, and flags unmapped or stale icons.

EUW_AscentiaTooltipPreview
  Lets Designers pick item/spell/status fixtures and inspect the generated
  tooltip view model, comparison deltas, warning rows, and localization keys.

EUW_AscentiaAccessibilityMatrix
  Shows cue categories from P20, setting rows from P21, UI widgets, prompt hold
  policies, and the active accessibility profile impact.

EUW_AscentiaWidgetPerformanceAudit
  Runs the UMG audit profile, lists bound attributes, Canvas/Overlay depth,
  Retainer/Invalidation usage, widget counts, and expensive animation rows.
```

Proof matrix:

```text
Proof                                Evidence
HUD does not crawl pawn on tick       UIContract report has zero actor-crawl rows
Inventory comparison survives locale  Tooltip audit with long text + number format
Prompts follow remap/device switch    InputGlyph audit and PIE device-switch clip
Settings can preview/revert safely    Settings report and fixture transaction log
Low-motion profile affects cues       Accessibility matrix and P20 cue report
Screen-reader text exists for menus   Accessibility audit missing text count is 0
UMG avoids raw attribute bindings     WidgetPerformance report bound count is 0
CommonUI shell handoff is ready       Layer policy report links to P21 package
```

Implementation tickets:

```text
UX-001 View-model contracts
  Add `FAscentiaUIViewModelHeader`, HUD/inventory/tooltip view structs, and
  `UAscentiaUIModelSubsystem` events. Route current HUD/inventory data through
  the subsystem without changing gameplay behavior.

UX-002 Settings registry
  Add `PDA_SettingsRegistry_MVP`, `FAscentiaSettingDefinition`,
  preview/apply/revert request/result structs, and the first safe rows for
  audio, text scale, motion scale, camera shake, rumble, damage numbers, and
  tutorial verbosity.

UX-003 Input glyph facade
  Add `UAscentiaInputGlyphSubsystem`, glyph DataAssets/DataTables, and an audit
  that fails on hardcoded prompt labels for gameplay actions.

UX-004 Tooltip view-model proof
  Connect P16 item resolver output to `FAscentiaItemTooltipView`, add fixture
  items, comparison deltas, long-text tests, and localization-key validation.

UX-005 Accessibility profiles
  Add default, low-motion, and color-assist profiles. Wire presentation cue
  scaling through P20 and prompt/input friction through P21 without changing
  core combat difficulty by accident.

UX-006 Widget performance audit
  Add Editor audit for tick, paint, raw attribute binding, Canvas/Overlay depth,
  expensive animations, missing invalidation policy, and oversized widget trees.

UX-007 CommonUI shell bridge
  Define the layer policy and action routing handoff to P21 so settings,
  inventory, loot, pause, and character screens do not fight for focus.

UX-008 Dashboard/evidence card
  Add dashboard rows for UI contract, settings registry, glyph coverage,
  tooltip coverage, accessibility coverage, and widget performance.
```

First next package:

1. Implement UX-001 before redesigning the visual UI. The data/view contract
   should exist while Canvas HUD/native screens still work.
2. Implement UX-002 next, because many P20/P21 presentation and shell choices
   depend on settings.
3. Implement UX-003 before adding more prompt widgets.
4. Implement UX-004 before expanding inventory/economy content.

### 10.6 Accessibility acceptance, inclusive settings, and assistive UX package

Section 10 already says accessibility metadata belongs in the UI contract. This
package makes that statement enforceable. Accessibility is not a single menu
page. It is a production acceptance layer across input, text, contrast, captions,
audio, motion, flashes, difficulty assists, tutorial timing, shell focus,
photo mode, combat feedback, and store metadata.

Reference posture:

- Use the Xbox Accessibility Guidelines as the game-specific checklist shape:
  input alternatives, subtitles/captions, text display, contrast, audio
  customization, screen narration, haptics, UI focus, time limits, destructive
  actions, visual distractions, and photosensitivity all become validation rows.
- Use WCAG 2.2 thresholds as the UI baseline where they map cleanly to game UI:
  standard text contrast at least 4.5:1, large text at least 3:1, and text
  scaling up to 200 percent without losing content or function.
- Use CommonUI for input routing, focus handling, action bars, gamepad
  navigation, style data, and glyph-aware shell surfaces.
- Use Enhanced Input as the source of remappable action identity. Accessibility
  settings must affect mappings, holds, repeats, dead zones, sensitivity, and
  prompt labels through action tags rather than widget-local strings.
- Treat photosensitivity and motion safety as content acceptance gates. A warning
  splash is not a substitute for avoiding unsafe flashes, intense screen shakes,
  and full-screen strobing effects.

Core structs:

```cpp
struct FAscentiaAccessibilityProfileState
{
    FGameplayTag ProfileTag;
    float TextScale;
    float UIScale;
    float SubtitleScale;
    float SubtitleBackgroundOpacity;
    float MotionScale;
    float CameraShakeScale;
    float FlashIntensityScale;
    float RumbleScale;
    float AimAssistScale;
    FGameplayTag ColorPaletteTag;
    FGameplayTag AudioMixProfileTag;
    FGameplayTag PromptHoldPolicyTag;
    bool bMonoAudio;
    bool bScreenNarrationRequested;
    bool bReduceRepeatedPresses;
    bool bExtendedTutorialTimers;
};

struct FAscentiaInclusiveInputRule
{
    FGameplayTag ActionTag;
    bool bRemappable;
    bool bRequiresHold;
    bool bRequiresRepeatedPresses;
    float DefaultHoldSeconds;
    FGameplayTag AlternativeActionTag;
    FGameplayTag ConflictGroupTag;
    FText AccessibilityNote;
};

struct FAscentiaReadableTextRule
{
    FGameplayTag WidgetTag;
    float MinimumPixelHeight1080p;
    float LargeTextPixelHeight1080p;
    float MaximumScale;
    float MinimumContrastRatio;
    bool bAllowsBackgroundPanel;
    bool bSupportsScrollOrExpansion;
    FGameplayTag TextCasePolicyTag;
};

struct FAscentiaSubtitleAccessibilityPolicy
{
    FGameplayTag PolicyTag;
    float MinimumPixelHeight1080p;
    float MaximumCharactersPerSecond;
    int32 MaximumLines;
    bool bSpeakerLabelRequired;
    bool bDirectionIndicatorRequired;
    bool bBackgroundRequired;
    float DefaultBackgroundOpacity;
    bool bPreviewRequired;
};

struct FAscentiaMotionSafetyPolicy
{
    FGameplayTag PolicyTag;
    float MaxCameraShakeScale;
    float MaxMotionBlurScale;
    float MaxScreenDistortionScale;
    bool bDisableCameraBob;
    bool bReduceFullScreenImpacts;
    bool bDisableAutoCameraSpin;
};

struct FAscentiaPhotosensitivityCheck
{
    FGameplayTag SourceAssetTag;
    FString AssetPath;
    float LuminanceDelta;
    float FlashFrequencyHz;
    float ScreenCoverageFraction;
    float DurationSeconds;
    bool bFailsPolicy;
    FText DesignerAction;
};
```

Subsystem boundary:

```cpp
UCLASS()
class UAscentiaAccessibilitySubsystem : public ULocalPlayerSubsystem
{
    GENERATED_BODY()

public:
    FAscentiaAccessibilityProfileState GetProfileState() const;
    bool ApplyProfile(FGameplayTag ProfileTag);
    bool ApplySetting(FGameplayTag SettingTag, const FAscentiaSettingValue& Value);
    float ScalePresentationValue(FGameplayTag PresentationTag, float RawValue) const;
    FAscentiaInclusiveInputRule GetInclusiveInputRule(FGameplayTag ActionTag) const;
    FAscentiaSubtitleAccessibilityPolicy GetSubtitlePolicy() const;
};
```

Rules:

- Accessibility settings appear before first gameplay whenever possible. If a
  first-run cinematic or voiced intro exists, subtitles are enabled by default or
  configurable before playback.
- Every remappable action must update prompts, tutorials, controller maps, and
  glyph rows. Remap persistence is verified by action identity, not localized
  text.
- Holds and repeated presses need alternatives for gameplay-critical actions,
  destructive confirmations, photo mode, loot, interaction, revive/summon, and
  menu confirmations.
- Important gameplay information cannot rely on color alone. Status, rarity,
  hostility, poison, bleed, objective state, loot quality, and weak points need
  icon, shape, label, animation, or audio alternatives.
- Text and UI scale must not overlap or clip at 200 percent. Long German-style
  pseudo-localized text and controller glyph labels are part of the fixture.
- Subtitle and caption settings include size, background opacity, speaker label,
  line breaks, readable duration, and preview in a realistic game context.
- Audio settings split music, voice, gameplay-critical SFX, ambient SFX, UI, and
  narration. Mono audio and dynamic range profiles are acceptance rows.
- Camera shake, hit-stop, motion blur, full-screen distortion, chromatic
  aberration, controller rumble, and high-intensity flashes are all profile
  scaled. Boss presentation cannot bypass the profile.
- Photosensitivity checks run on authored Niagara flashes, material flicker,
  UI full-screen flashes, lightning/weather profiles, hit impacts, death
  effects, and cinematics before milestone acceptance.
- Accessibility assists that alter gameplay difficulty are tagged separately
  from presentation accessibility so Designers can reason about balance and
  achievements.

Designer-facing tools:

```text
EUW_AscentiaAccessibilityAcceptanceBoard
  Shows every XAG/WCAG-inspired acceptance row, owner system, latest evidence,
  blocking severity, platform/store metadata claim, and human verdict.

EUW_AscentiaTextScaleAndContrastLab
  Previews all player-facing widgets at 100/150/200 percent text scale with
  pseudo-localization, contrast ratios, disabled-state contrast, and clipping.

EUW_AscentiaSubtitleCaptionLab
  Previews subtitle size, background opacity, speaker labels, direction
  indicators, reading speed, line breaks, and realistic combat/cinematic context.

EUW_AscentiaInclusiveInputLab
  Audits remaps, holds, repeated presses, analog/digital alternatives, input
  conflict groups, tutorial prompt updates, and device-switch glyphs.

EUW_AscentiaMotionAndFlashSafetyLab
  Reviews camera shake, rumble, flashes, full-screen post effects, VFX bursts,
  lightning, hit impacts, boss phase effects, and low-motion/low-flash profiles.

EUW_AscentiaAccessibilityStoreTagAudit
  Compares claimed accessibility/storefront tags against evidence reports so the
  team does not overclaim support.
```

```text
A11Y-001 Accessibility profile state and subsystem

A11Y-002 Text scale and contrast audit

A11Y-005 Motion and flash safety scaling

  post process, weather, cinematics, and boss phase events.

A11Y-006 Photosensitivity audit
  Add flash/flicker scan data, manual review rows for risky VFX/cinematics, and
  package blockers for policy failures.

A11Y-007 Audio clarity and mono/dynamic range profiles
  Add mix profile settings for music, voice, gameplay SFX, ambient SFX, UI,
  narration, mono audio, and dynamic range.

A11Y-008 Store metadata and dashboard gate
```
