```text
  overclaimed or missing evidence.
```

First next package:

1. Implement A11Y-001 through A11Y-003 after UX-001 through UX-003 so profile,
   setting, and glyph contracts exist first.
2. Implement A11Y-004 before any intro cinematic, voiced tutorial, or narrative
   milestone is accepted.
3. Implement A11Y-005 and A11Y-006 before P20 boss/VFX presentation ramps up.
4. Implement A11Y-008 before P21 storefront or platform accessibility claims.

Interconnections:

- P11 combat motion uses prompt rows and settings for dodge/attack feedback.
- P13 damage math writes HUD/status rows but does not own widget formatting.
- P14/P15 weapons and magic feed tooltip/action rows through the same view
  model path.
- P16 itemization owns item identity and transaction truth; section 10 owns
  how that truth becomes inspectable, comparable UI data.
- P20 presentation reads accessibility/profile scaling from section 10 before
  playing cue intensity.
- P21 shell owns CommonUI layer activation and settings persistence after the
  section 10 registry defines the rows.
- P21 platform/storefront accessibility claims read evidence from section 10.6
  before release metadata is accepted.
- P12 narrative, P20 presentation, and P14/P15 targeting cannot bypass subtitle,
  low-motion, low-flash, input, and contrast acceptance rows.
- Section 11 tooling owns the EUW shells and report browser patterns for all
  UX evidence.

## 11. Production Tooling and Validation

### 11.1 Why this matters

AAA scale fails through drift. Validation catches drift before it becomes a
runtime mystery.

Ascentia also needs tools that compress authoring time. The user already has the
right instinct:

- Skin Forge / InterfaceArtForge for UI look generation and WBP skinning;
- Asset Factory for modular armor and weapon mesh variants;
- Landscry for landscape, world-package generation, and PCG source material.

The next tool layer should not be "AI makes game." It should be:

```text
Tool creates drafts, audits, previews, repairs, and evidence.
Designer keeps authorship and taste.
Native systems keep authority.
```

### 11.2 Accelerator tool catalog

Build bespoke tools when they remove repeated production friction. Do not build a
custom tool merely because a one-off task is annoying once. A good Ascentia tool
does at least three of these:

- generates a valid first draft from stable data;
- normalizes third-party or generated content into Ascentia conventions;
- previews gameplay consequences before PIE;
- creates validation reports Designers understand;
- writes evidence packs for the dashboard;
- makes hidden native state visible in the Editor;
- lets a human choose among variants quickly;
- records the human's choice for future calibration.

#### 11.2.1 Current tool spine

Current or documented tools already point in the right direction:

```text
Skin Forge / InterfaceArtForge
  UI skin and WidgetBlueprint look generation.

Asset Factory
  modular armor/weapon mesh variant generation.

Landscry
  prompt/world-package/landscape/PCG source generation.

Tools/remote_playtest.py
  scenario runner and runtime proof harness.

Tools/presentation_sweep.py
  presentation variant/evidence runner.

Tools/data/export_xlsx_to_csv.py
  first stage of the spreadsheet-to-data pipeline.

Tools/create_p11_bt_assets.py
  example of asset creation automation for AI assets.

Tools/build_*_arena.py and map scripts
  repeatable level/blockout construction.

UMGToolSet / NiagaraToolsets / MCP toolsets
  scriptable editor asset surgery where Python alone is too thin.
```

The gap is orchestration: these tools should feed one production dashboard and
one evidence folder structure instead of living as isolated scripts.

#### 11.2.2 Highest-value new bespoke tools

1. `EUW_AscentiaWorldDirector`

Purpose: the bridge between Landscry, World Partition, PCG, Ultra Dynamic Sky,
audio ambience, and zone identity.

Inputs:

- Landscry package;
- `PDA_WorldZone`;
- biome tags;
- time-of-day/mood tags;
- UDS/weather presets;
- PCG graph set;
- HLOD/nav/smart-object builder profiles.

Outputs:

- World Partition level or zone sublevel;
- Data Layers;
- PCG component assignments;
- UDS actor/preset bindings;
- biome ambience/music state tags;
- validation report.

Why it matters: open-world production dies if every generated zone requires a
hand checklist. Ultra Dynamic Sky should become a zone-authored mood/weather
system, not just a manually tweaked actor in the level.

2. `EUW_AscentiaPOIComposer`

Purpose: make Landscry terrain playable by placing authored POI cards into it.

Inputs:

- `PDA_POI`;
- encounter budget;
- reward budget;
- traversal requirements;
- nearby biome/road/slope/water data.

Outputs:

- POI actor cluster;
- encounter zone;
- loot/fast-travel/discovery hooks;
- map icon;
- objective/world-state bindings;
- screenshot/evidence card.

Why it matters: the world should be generated for shape and density, but authored
for player intention. POI Composer is the handshake.

3. `EUW_AscentiaEncounterForge`

Purpose: assemble combat encounters from enemy definitions and budget rules.

Inputs:

- enemy family;
- encounter budget;
- arena shape;
- patrol/idle spawn points;
- reward table;
- difficulty/world-tier profile.

Outputs:

- `PDA_EncounterDefinition`;
- placed `BP_AscentiaEncounterZone`;
- EQS/SmartObject/nav validation;
- preview of spawn waves and reward expectation.

Why it matters: encounter authoring will outnumber boss authoring. Designers need
"make three variants of this ruins ambush" faster than they need another manual
Details panel.

4. `EUW_AscentiaSkillForge`

Purpose: productize the skill-authoring pipeline already described in
`Docs/Architecture/Pipeline_SkillAuthoring.md`.

Inputs:

- skill spec;
- weapon family;
- reference animation/montage;
- frame targets;
- cost/cooldown data;
- cue profile.

Outputs:

- ability asset draft;
- montage/sections;
- damage-window, cancel-window, iframe, footstep, and cue notifies;
- `PDA_Moveset` or skill row update;
- presentation contract card;
- calibration sweep.

Why it matters: Ascentia will need many attacks, weapon arts, spells, and boss
moves. This is one of the biggest leverage tools because it converts animation
and combat feel into a repeatable authoring loop.

5. `EUW_AscentiaCueMatrix`

Purpose: one matrix for every GameplayCue, Niagara system, MetaSound, camera
profile, rumble profile, accessibility group, and budget priority.

Inputs:

- cue profiles;
- damage/status/spell/projectile/loot/boss rows;
- Niagara systems;
- MetaSound sources;
- camera feedback profiles.

Outputs:

- missing cue report;
- preview buttons;
- "fire this event at this location" debug command;
- accessibility coverage report;
- budget risk report.

Why it matters: P20 presentation will fail through missing or inconsistent
feedback long before it fails through lack of raw art.

6. `EUW_AscentiaAudioStateBoard`

Purpose: make music states, ambience, MetaSounds, mixes, settings, and
concurrency visible instead of hiding them in level Blueprints and audio
components.

Inputs:

- audio cue profiles;
- music state profiles;
- ambience and Soundscape profiles;
- Audio Modulation buses and mixes;
- Audio Gameplay Volume profiles;
- settings snapshots and presentation events.

Outputs:

- active audio state view;
- missing profile report;
- music transition proof;
- MetaSound parameter warnings;
- concurrency and settings suppressions;
- audio accessibility report.

Why it matters: Ascentia needs music and ambience to react to zones, combat,
bosses, The Below, menus, weather, and accessibility settings without becoming a
pile of fragile one-off components.

7. `EUW_AscentiaLootEconomyLab`

Purpose: simulate itemization and economy before content is cooked into the
game.

Inputs:

- loot tables;
- affix pools;
- world-tier curves;
- vendor/crafting rules;
- expected playtime/kills/POI completions.

Outputs:

- rarity distribution;
- affix frequency;
- power curve;
- currency sinks/sources;
- item-name preview;
- "too many dead drops" warnings.

Why it matters: Diablo-style itemization cannot be balanced by opening a few
chests in PIE.

8. `EUW_AscentiaCharacterCreatorLab`

Purpose: connect future custom character creation to the real save/appearance
contract.

Inputs:

- avatar presets;
- modular body parts;
- morph ranges;
- material palettes;
- origin/class data;
- starting equipment.

Outputs:

- `FAscentiaCharacterCreationDraft`;
- avatar preview;
- save payload preview;
- skeleton/physics/retarget validation;
- icon/portrait capture.

Why it matters: AssassinGirl is placeholder. The character creator has to prove
that appearance data, gameplay identity, animation, and save data agree.

8. `EUW_AscentiaSaveLab`

Purpose: inspect, migrate, fuzz, and compare save files.

Inputs:

- save fixtures;
- current schema;
- migration functions;
- character/world test scenarios.

Outputs:

- save diff;
- migration report;
- missing field warnings;
- old-save compatibility proof;
- death-loop/corpse state inspection.

Why it matters: once players have real saves, save bugs become trust bugs.

9. `EUW_AscentiaNPCStoryGraph`

Purpose: author world-state dialogue, NPC schedules, vendor states, and quest
consequences without creating a full traditional quest log.

Inputs:

- dialogue rows;
- world-state flags;
- NPC definitions;
- vendor stock;
- POI completion tags;
- time/weather conditions.

Outputs:

- graph of prerequisites and consequences;
- unreachable-line report;
- subtitle/localization export;
- NPC placement/schedule preview.

Why it matters: the current architecture is "world-state, not quest log." That
still needs tools or it becomes invisible spaghetti.

10. `EUW_AscentiaPackIngestor`

Purpose: normalize Marketplace/Fab/generative assets into Ascentia conventions.

Inputs:

- imported pack folder;
- asset type mapping;
- target skeleton/retarget profile;
- naming profile;
- material profile;
- collision/LOD policy.

Outputs:

- renamed/moved assets;
- redirector cleanup plan;
- generated thumbnails;
- retarget or material conversion tasks;
- license/source notes;
- validation report.

Why it matters: Ascentia will consume many packs and generated assets. Manual
cleanup will quietly eat weeks.

11. `EUW_AscentiaMaterialSurfaceLab`

Purpose: unify surface types, physical materials, decals, footstep sounds, impact
VFX, projectile puffs, and traversal readability.

Inputs:

- materials;
- physical materials;
- surface tags;
- cue profiles;
- PCG biome tags.

Outputs:

- surface response matrix;
- missing physical material report;
- footstep/impact preview;
- PCG biome surface coverage;
- weather material response proof;
- hazard damage/status route proof.

Why it matters: open-world feel depends on consistent surface feedback across
thousands of placed/generated meshes.

12. `EUW_AscentiaLightingMoodLab`

Purpose: turn Ultra Dynamic Sky, fog, exposure, weather, biome mood, and
encounter presentation into reusable zone presets.

Inputs:

- UDS presets;
- post-process profiles;
- biome tags;
- world-zone mood tags;
- boss/POI presentation overrides.

Outputs:

- `PDA_LightingMoodProfile`;
- level actor settings;
- screenshot turntable;
- readability warnings for fog/darkness/contrast;
- accessibility variants.

Why it matters: UDS is most valuable when it becomes a repeatable art-direction
tool, not a beautiful actor that each map tweaks differently.

13. `EUW_AscentiaBalanceArena`

Purpose: run repeated combat/economy scenarios with parameter sweeps.

Inputs:

- enemy definitions;
- player builds;
- weapon profiles;
- world-tier scaling;
- encounter definitions.

Outputs:

- time-to-kill;
- damage taken;
- resource pressure;
- deaths;
- loot earned;
- recommended tuning deltas.

Why it matters: a Soulslike ARPG needs feel and numbers tuned together.

14. `EUW_AscentiaBuildDoctor`

Purpose: one panel that answers "why will this not cook/package/play?"

Inputs:

- Data Validation;
- asset registry;
- redirectors;
- missing soft refs;
- map check;
- automation logs;
- plugin state.

Outputs:

- prioritized blocker list;
- one-click repair where safe;
- links to offending assets;
- last green build evidence.

Why it matters: production speed improves when failures become actionable in the
Editor, not buried in logs.

15. `AscentiaDirectorDashboard`

Purpose: merge the existing gap dashboard with automation evidence, feel cards,
tool reports, and phase status.

Inputs:

- `Docs/Feature_Gap_Analysis_*.md`;
- phase docs;
- remote playtest reports;
- presentation sweeps;
- validator reports;
- tool-generated evidence cards.

Outputs:

- live status by phase/system;
- native/designer/presentation/content completion;
- failing validators;
- last human verdict;
- next best production actions.

Why it matters: the dashboard should become production command, not a static
snapshot.

16. `EUW_AscentiaAtmosphereDirector`

Purpose: wrap Ultra Dynamic Sky and weather into Ascentia-authored atmosphere
profiles.

Inputs:

- UDS sky/weather actor or preset;
- `PDA_LightingMoodProfile`;
- `PDA_WeatherProfile`;
- `PDA_TimeOfDayProfile`;
- biome and mood tags;
- audio and VFX budget profiles.

Outputs:

- zone atmosphere binding;
- weather/time-of-day preview;
- screenshot proof grid;
- material/weather response checks;
- performance and readability warnings.

Why it matters: UDS is powerful, but Ascentia should not become dependent on
raw per-level actor tuning. A profile wrapper makes weather, time, mood,
materials, audio, and VFX legible to Designers and validators.

#### 11.2.3 Tool priority order

Near-term:

1. `EUW_AscentiaCueMatrix`
2. `EUW_AscentiaSkillForge`
3. `EUW_AscentiaWorldDirector`
4. `EUW_AscentiaAtmosphereDirector`
5. `EUW_AscentiaSaveLab`
6. `AscentiaDirectorDashboard`

Mid-term:

1. `EUW_AscentiaEncounterForge`
2. `EUW_AscentiaLootEconomyLab`
3. `EUW_AscentiaPOIComposer`
4. `EUW_AscentiaCharacterCreatorLab`
5. `EUW_AscentiaLightingMoodLab`
6. `EUW_AscentiaMotionInspector`
7. `EUW_AscentiaWeaponMountLab`

Later, when content volume justifies:

1. `EUW_AscentiaNPCStoryGraph`
2. `EUW_AscentiaPackIngestor`
3. `EUW_AscentiaMaterialSurfaceLab`
4. `EUW_AscentiaBalanceArena`
5. `EUW_AscentiaBuildDoctor`
6. `EUW_AscentiaRoadAndTraversalPlanner`
7. `EUW_AscentiaMapBakeLab`
8. `EUW_AscentiaModularFitLab`
9. `EUW_AscentiaLODAndCollisionDoctor`
10. `EUW_AscentiaThumbnailAndTurntableStudio`
11. `EUW_AscentiaSkinForgeBridge`
12. `EUW_AscentiaInputGlyphBinder`
13. `EUW_AscentiaLocalizationAndFontStress`
14. `EUW_AscentiaNotifyWindowDoctor`
15. `EUW_AscentiaHitboxReplay`
16. `EUW_AscentiaNPCScheduleSimulator`
17. `EUW_AscentiaWorldStateGraph`

The order is not because later tools are less useful. It is because near-term
tools sit directly on current pain: combat/presentation proof, GASP/montage
authoring, Landscry/UDS world setup, save schema confidence, and dashboard sync.

#### 11.2.4 Tool design rules

Every bespoke tool needs:

- a named owner system;
- a stable input schema;
- a stable output asset or report;
- a dry-run mode;
- undo/transaction support for editor mutations;
- a machine-readable report under `Saved/Automation/Evidence/...`;
- a Designer-readable summary;
- links to affected assets;
- a "last run" timestamp;
- no hidden gameplay authority.

Tool output should be inspectable and editable. Generated content becomes a
draft, not a black box.

#### 11.2.5 Tool product lines

Think of Ascentia tools as product lines, not scattered utilities. Each product
line should have an Editor UI, a batch/automation entry point, validation rules,
and dashboard evidence.

World and environment tools:

`EUW_AscentiaWorldDirector`

- Consumes: Landscry package, `PDA_WorldZone`, biome tags, PCG graph set,
  Data Layer policy, World Partition builder profile, atmosphere profile.
- Produces: zone import report, Data Layers, PCG components, HLOD/nav/smart
  object build tasks, map-preview card, screenshot proof.
- Implementation route: EUW for Designer orchestration, Python/MCP for import
  and builder calls, C++ editor helpers for asset registry and transaction-safe
  mutation.
- First slice: import one Landscry test zone into a sandbox World Partition map,
  attach zone identity, run PCG generation, and write one evidence card.

`EUW_AscentiaAtmosphereDirector`

- Consumes: Ultra Dynamic Sky actors or presets, `PDA_LightingMoodProfile`,
  `PDA_WeatherProfile`, `PDA_TimeOfDayProfile`, biome mood tags, audio states.
- Produces: zone atmosphere binding, weather/time screenshot grid, performance
  and readability warnings, music/ambience state preview.
- Implementation route: EUW for authoring, Blueprint component/subsystem for
  runtime profile application, C++ only for stable interfaces and validation.
- First slice: clear, overcast, rain, snow, dusk, night, and storm profiles for
  the MVP arena plus one Landscry sandbox zone.

`EUW_AscentiaWeatherTimeScenarioLab`

- Consumes: `PDA_AtmosphereProfile`, `PDA_WeatherProfile`,
  `PDA_TimeOfDayProfile`, `PDA_WeatherGameplayPolicy`, zone/biome tags, save
  fixtures, fast-travel arrival profiles, respawn anchors, and combat scenarios.
- Produces: scenario run report, transition proof, save/load proof, explicit
  gameplay opt-in warnings, screenshots, and dashboard findings.
- Implementation route: EUW for scenario selection, C++ editor validator for
  profile resolution, commandlet for repeatable dry-runs, and provider adapter
  hooks for UDS or native Unreal sky actors.
- First slice: clear/noon -> rain -> storm -> night transition in the MVP arena
  with no implicit movement, AI, spawn, or stealth side effects.

`EUW_AscentiaAtmospherePerformanceBudgetLab`

- Consumes: atmosphere/weather/time profiles, scalability buckets, device
  profiles, VFX budgets, post-process settings, fog/cloud settings, and material
  response policies.
- Produces: cloud, fog, weather VFX, post, shadow, precipitation, lightning, and
  material-response budget rows with screenshots and blocking findings.
- Implementation route: EUW for profile comparison, commandlet for repeatable
  screenshot/cost captures, and dashboard cards for profile promotion status.
- First slice: clear, rain, night, and storm budget report across two
  scalability buckets in the MVP arena.

`EUW_AscentiaPOIComposer`

- Consumes: `PDA_POI`, slope/water/road metadata, encounter and reward budgets,
  traversal requirements, map-region tags.
- Produces: placed POI cluster, discovery actor, encounter zone, loot source,
  map icon, screenshot card, unreachable/overbudget warnings.
- Implementation route: EUW plus Scriptable Tool for viewport placement handles.
- First slice: "ruins ambush," "cairn chest," and "minor shrine" templates that
  can be stamped onto generated terrain.

`EUW_AscentiaRoadAndTraversalPlanner`

- Consumes: terrain samples, POI graph, climb/drop/swim/mount affordance tags,
  enemy density rules, vista targets.
- Produces: road spline drafts, traversal gate report, slope heatmap, ledge/drop
  warnings, nav link candidates.
- Implementation route: Scriptable Tool for viewport spline edits, PCG graph
  adapters for road dressing, validation for impossible traversal.
- First slice: route from spawn to first POI with authored choke points and a
  "can return from here" check.

`EUW_AscentiaMapBakeLab`

- Consumes: World Partition cells, Data Layers, POI definitions, discovery
  actors, fast-travel points, biome masks.
- Produces: minimap/map tiles, fog-of-war regions, icon placement preview,
  collision with UI labels, map-data validation.
- Implementation route: editor capture utility plus DataAsset output.
- First slice: MVP arena map tile and one generated zone tile with icons.

Art, asset, and content-ingestion tools:

`EUW_AscentiaPackIngestor`

- Consumes: Marketplace/Fab/generative content folder, naming policy, skeleton
  mapping, material policy, collision policy, license/source metadata.
- Produces: proposed move/rename plan, redirector cleanup list, normalized
  folders, thumbnail sheet, validation report.
- Implementation route: EUW for review, Python for batch asset operations,
  Data Validation for acceptance.
- First slice: ingest one weapon or armor pack into `/Game/Ascentia/Art/Staging`
  and produce a no-mutation dry-run report.

`EUW_AscentiaModularFitLab`

- Consumes: modular armor/body meshes from Asset Factory, skeleton reference,
  socket policy, morph extremes, physics/collision profile.
- Produces: fit test report, clipping screenshot sheet, socket adjustment
  suggestions, physics asset warnings, equip-preview variants.
- Implementation route: EUW preview scene plus Geometry Script/mesh inspection
  where useful.
- First slice: AssassinGirl placeholder plus two body presets to prove the
  system is character-creator-ready.

`EUW_AscentiaWeaponMountLab`

- Consumes: weapon mesh, hand socket, sheath socket, animation pose set, trail
  profile, collision capsule.
- Produces: designer-adjustable socket offsets, grip/sheath screenshots,
  attack-trail preview, collision alignment warnings.
- Implementation route: Blueprint preview actor plus editor details widget.
- First slice: fix current sword grip/trail visibility workflow so Designers can
  tune the hand socket without C++ spelunking.

`EUW_AscentiaLODAndCollisionDoctor`

- Consumes: static meshes, skeletal meshes, Nanite/LOD policy, collision policy,
  physics asset policy, platform target.
- Produces: invalid collision list, missing LOD/Nanite decisions, high-poly
  warning report, auto-fix candidates.
- Implementation route: Data Validation plus EUW report and safe fix buttons.
- First slice: scan playable arena and report "Invalid Bodies" style issues
  before PIE.

`EUW_AscentiaThumbnailAndTurntableStudio`

- Consumes: items, armor, weapons, NPCs, bosses, materials, lighting profile.
- Produces: item icons, inventory thumbnails, character portraits, marketing
  screenshots, comparison sheets.
- Implementation route: editor capture map plus commandlet/batch script.
- First slice: generate icon/turntable proof for the first 20 inventory items.

UI and interface tools:

`EUW_AscentiaSkinForgeBridge`

- Consumes: InterfaceArtForge output, UI style tokens, CommonUI style assets,
  target WBP classes, input glyph tables.
- Produces: WBP skin application report, missing widget binding warnings,
  style variance screenshots, controller/mouse navigation proof.
- Implementation route: EUW plus UMGToolSet operations.
- First slice: inventory, loot window, HUD, and settings skin smoke test.

`EUW_AscentiaInputGlyphBinder`

- Consumes: Enhanced Input mapping contexts, Player Mappable Input Configs,
  CommonUI action rows, platform glyph tables.
- Produces: input-glyph DataTable, missing binding report, UI preview for
  keyboard/mouse/gamepad.
- Implementation route: EUW plus DataTable writer.
- First slice: prove E loot, I inventory, LMB attack, dodge, interact, and menu
  prompts all display from one source.

`EUW_AscentiaLocalizationAndFontStress`

- Consumes: localized text tables, UI screens, font assets, language profiles,
  controller glyphs.
- Produces: overflow screenshots, missing localization warnings, font fallback
  report, accessibility contrast notes.
- Implementation route: commandlet screenshot pass plus dashboard cards.
- First slice: English pseudo-localization pass for inventory and loot screens.

Combat, animation, and feel tools:

`EUW_AscentiaSkillForge`

- Consumes: skill spec, montage/animation, GameplayAbility class, cue profile,
  damage/cost/cooldown data, cancel-window policy.
- Produces: ability draft, montage section map, notify windows, cue contract
  card, balance sweep fixture.
- Implementation route: EUW authoring surface plus native validators.
- First slice: one light attack, one dodge roll, one spell, and one boss attack
  generated from the same contract shape.

`EUW_AscentiaMotionInspector`

- Consumes: dodge/attack montages, root-motion extraction, movement-mode rules,
  intent vector samples, ledge/stair test maps.
- Produces: root-motion path overlay, ledge/stair warnings, cancel availability
  chart, capsule/path comparison, "player intent vs montage direction" report.
- Implementation route: EUW preview plus runtime recorder from PIE scenarios.
- First slice: Essential Bow dodge set direction audit so F/B/L/R/BL/BR choices
  become visible and correctable.

`EUW_AscentiaNotifyWindowDoctor`

- Consumes: montages, animation notifies, GameplayCue tags, damage windows,
  cancel windows, iframe windows, audio/VFX windows.
- Produces: missing notify report, overlapping windows, per-frame event timeline,
  cue preview buttons.
- Implementation route: EUW plus animation asset inspection.
- First slice: light attack sword whoosh/trail/damage/cancel windows in one
  timeline.

`EUW_AscentiaHitboxReplay`

- Consumes: recorded attack traces, weapon sockets, target capsules, animation
  frame data, gameplay tags.
- Produces: replayable hitbox overlay, whiff/hit classification, frame timing
  card, designer notes.
- Implementation route: PIE recorder plus editor playback.
- First slice: boss attack and player light attack replay cards.

AI, encounter, and world-state tools:

`EUW_AscentiaEncounterForge`

- Consumes: enemy definitions, arena shape, spawn points, encounter budget,
  world-tier profile, reward table.
- Produces: encounter definition, placed encounter zone, spawn-wave preview,
  nav/EQS/SmartObject warnings, reward expectation.
- Implementation route: EUW plus validation and optional Functional Test.
- First slice: three arena encounter variants with no auto-spawn surprise in PIE.

`EUW_AscentiaNPCScheduleSimulator`

- Consumes: NPC definitions, world-state flags, time/weather conditions,
  Data Layers, vendor/dialogue state.
- Produces: schedule timeline, unreachable state warnings, spawned actor preview,
  dialogue/vendor state proof.
- Implementation route: EUW graph plus World State subsystem simulation.
- First slice: one campsite NPC with day/night/vendor/dialogue state changes.

`EUW_AscentiaWorldStateGraph`

- Consumes: world-state tags, POI completions, boss kills, NPC states, loot
  claims, Data Layers.
- Produces: dependency graph, orphan flag report, save payload preview,
  regression fixtures.
- Implementation route: dashboard view plus editor graph.
- First slice: current MVP arena, loot container, boss, campsite, and save state.

`EUW_AscentiaPopulationEcologyLab`

- Consumes: `PDA_WorldZone`, population profiles, species profiles, PCG
  candidate points, Smart Object definitions, StateTrees, Mass profiles,
  time/weather/world-state fixtures, density budgets.
- Produces: population source coverage, ambient route proof, Smart Object
  reservation report, Mass/StateTree budget report, save/load persistence rows.
- Implementation route: EUW plus population audit commandlets and scenario
  runner.
- First slice: Zone01 traveler, deer/crow wildlife, campsite/guard/forage Smart
  Objects, and a no-combat-authority leak proof.

`EUW_AscentiaGuidanceBoard`

- Consumes: objective world-state tags, tutorial prompts, map clues, NPC clue
  lines, memory clues, accessibility settings, spoiler bands.
- Produces: active guidance view, stale-hint warnings, replay/dismiss state,
  first-run readability report, surface coverage matrix.
- Implementation route: EUW backed by P12 guidance assets and audit commandlets.
- First slice: one MVP objective whose HUD hint, map clue, NPC clue, tutorial
  prompt, and memory clue all resolve from the same world-state truth.

Economy, save, and production-control tools:

`EUW_AscentiaLootEconomyLab`

- Consumes: loot tables, affix pools, vendor/crafting sinks, world-tier curves,
  expected playtime.
- Produces: rarity/power/currency distribution, dead-drop warnings, item-name
  samples, balance deltas.
- Implementation route: C++/Python simulator with EUW charts.
- First slice: 1-hour MVP progression simulation and 10,000 chest/kill rolls.

`EUW_AscentiaBelowRunLab`

- Consumes: Below run profiles, room sets, Level Instance room kits, modifiers,
  encounter profiles, reward profiles, transition policies, seeds.
- Produces: deterministic layout preview, room/socket validation, modifier and
  reward budget report, transition safety report, replayable run evidence.
- Implementation route: EUW plus endgame audit/simulation commandlets.
- First slice: current MVP Below entry/guardian/cache as a deterministic
  authored-room run profile.

`EUW_AscentiaSaveLab`

- Consumes: save fixtures, current schema, migration functions, character/world
  state scenarios.
- Produces: save diff, migration report, missing field warnings, last-safe
  transform proof, death-loop inspection.
- Implementation route: EUW inspector plus automation fixtures.
- First slice: current save schema vs character-creation future schema.

`EUW_AscentiaBuildDoctor`

- Consumes: Data Validation, asset registry, redirectors, missing soft refs,
  cook/package logs, plugin state, map check, editor crash notes.
- Produces: prioritized blocker list, safe repair actions, affected asset links,
  last green build evidence.
- Implementation route: Editor module and dashboard sync.
- First slice: one "open this before PIE" panel that surfaces invalid bodies,
  missing compression assets, missing cue assets, and broken Blueprint refs.

`AscentiaDirectorDashboard`

- Consumes: phase docs, validator output, playtest reports, presentation sweeps,
  tool evidence cards, human verdicts.
- Produces: live phase/status view, next action list, drift warnings, confidence
  score by system.
- Implementation route: existing dashboard plus generated JSON evidence.
- First slice: include tool report status beside existing phase progress.

#### 11.2.6 Accelerator opportunity matrix

Existing bespoke tools already cover three major production lanes:

- Skin Forge / InterfaceArtForge: UI art, WBP skinning, visual language.
- Asset Factory: armor, weapons, modular meshes, fit checks, generated variants.
- Landscry: landscape, world packages, PCG source material, world generation.

The next accelerators should fill the gaps between those lanes and the actual
gameplay/editor workflow. Do not build a giant general-purpose editor. Build
small, evidence-writing tools that make one expensive judgment cheap.

| Tool | Phase trigger | Primary pain removed | Editor surface | First slice |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `EUW_AscentiaBuildDoctor` | now / all phases | missing refs, invalid physics bodies, broken cue assets, plugin drift, PIE blockers | dockable EUW + dashboard card | one "open before PIE" report with fix links |
| `EUW_AscentiaMotionInspector` | P10/P13/P14/P15 | root-motion direction, dodge selection, ledges/stairs, cancel windows | EUW preview + PIE evidence replay | Essential Bow F/B/L/R/BL/BR dodge direction audit |
| `EUW_AscentiaNotifyWindowDoctor` | P10/P13/P20 | vanished swing SFX/trails, missing damage/cancel/iframe notifies | montage timeline EUW | light attack whoosh/trail/damage/cancel timeline |
| `EUW_AscentiaCueMatrix` | P13/P20 | GameplayCue coverage drift across impacts, status, spells, boss attacks | cue coverage EUW | every current combat event has VFX/audio/UI cue status |
| `EUW_AscentiaAudioStateBoard` | P20/P21 | music, ambience, MetaSounds, mixes, settings, and concurrency become scattered component state | audio state EUW + music/mix labs | sword whoosh plus MVPArena/Below state transitions |
| `EUW_AscentiaAtmosphereDirector` | P17/P20 | raw Ultra Dynamic Sky tuning scattered in levels | UDS adapter/profile EUW | three mood/weather profiles applied through Ascentia data |
| `EUW_AscentiaWeatherTimeScenarioLab` | P17/P20/P21 | time/weather transitions can change saves, spawns, respawns, or AI through accidental provider state | weather/time scenario EUW + commandlet | clear/rain/storm/night transition with explicit gameplay opt-in proof |
| `EUW_AscentiaAtmospherePerformanceBudgetLab` | P17/P20/P21 | clouds, fog, post, lightning, and precipitation can break performance or accessibility after art tuning | atmosphere budget EUW + dashboard card | clear/rain/night/storm cost rows per scalability bucket |
| `EUW_AscentiaWorldDirector` | P17 | Landscry, World Partition, PCG, Data Layers, atmosphere, POI identity not connected | world-zone EUW + Scriptable Tools for placement | import one Landscry zone and generate evidence |
| `EUW_AscentiaCartographyBoard` | P17/P12/P21 | map regions, discovery, compass markers, fast travel, and questless guidance can fork into separate UI facts | cartography board + map bake/marker/route labs | Zone01 region, reveal state, compass markers, and fast-travel proof |
| `EUW_AscentiaPopulationEcologyLab` | P17/P18/P20 | ambient life, wildlife, patrols, Smart Objects, Mass, and StateTrees can become unbudgeted spawn soup | population ecology EUW + scenario runner | one zone with traveler/wildlife/campsite activity proof |
| `EUW_AscentiaPackIngestor` | now / content drops | marketplace/sample packs become unmanaged Content folder debt | EUW import/audit panel | Essential Bow + GASP dependency/collision/retarget report |
| `EUW_AscentiaWeaponMountLab` | now / P13/P14 | Designer cannot tune weapon sockets, trails, hand fit, sheathe points | viewport preview EUW | sword hand offset, trail socket, sheath socket profile |
| `EUW_AscentiaMaterialSurfaceLab` | P16/P17/P20 | armor/weapon/world materials do not respond consistently to weather, impacts, footsteps | material/profile preview EUW | wetness/snow/impact response on one armor, weapon, landscape |
| `EUW_AscentiaSurfaceResponseLab` | P17/P20/P23 | Physical Materials, footsteps, impacts, decals, weather, hazards, and accessibility can fork per asset | surface response matrix + scenario labs | Stone/Mud/Snow footsteps, slash impacts, rain/snow material response, rot hazard proof |
| `EUW_AscentiaDamageFormulaLab` | P13 | armor/resistance/status/crit changes are invisible until gameplay feels wrong | curve/data preview EUW | compare 10 attacker/defender scenarios and export report |
| `EUW_AscentiaBuildPlanner` | P13/P19 | stat thresholds, equip load, passives, item requirements are hard to reason about | build simulation EUW | level 1-40 stat/equip/passive preview |
| `EUW_AscentiaLootEconomyLab` | P16 | loot tables cannot be trusted by inspection | simulator EUW + charts | 10,000 chest/kill rolls with rarity/currency distribution |
| `EUW_AscentiaBelowRunLab` | P16/P17/P18/P19/P20 | The Below can become random rooms, unsafe transitions, and untracked rewards | run lab + room-kit board + transition proof | deterministic MVP Below profile with guardian/cache proof |
| `EUW_AscentiaEncounterForge` | P11/P17/P18 | arenas and spawn logic surprise the player/Designer in PIE | encounter authoring EUW | three encounter variants, no forced MVP boss spawn |
| `EUW_AscentiaGuidanceBoard` | P12/P21 | questless objectives, tutorials, map clues, NPC clues, and memory hints drift into scattered prompts | guidance EUW + route/staleness audits | one world-state objective with HUD/map/NPC/tutorial/memory coverage |
| `EUW_AscentiaPerformanceTakeBoard` | P12/P19/P20 | facial solves, gestures, takes, and avatar-specific shot bindings can become unreviewed Sequencer clutter | performance take board + facial/gesture/avatar labs | campsite guide line with audio-driven face, gesture, look-at, and avatar binding proof |
| `EUW_AscentiaCharacterCreatorLab` | P19 | placeholder AssassinGirl path will not scale to custom character creation | character profile EUW | saveable body/identity preset with equipment preview |
| `EUW_AscentiaAvatarCosmeticBoard` | P19 | creator cosmetics, grooms, cloth, portraits, armor overlays, and PhysicsAssets can fail invisibly | cosmetic acceptance board + fit labs | stable cosmetic save tags plus body/groom/cloth/physics proof |
| `EUW_AscentiaSaveLab` | P19/P21 | save migrations and death-loop state are hard to inspect | save inspector EUW | current save vs character-creation schema diff |
| `EUW_AscentiaLocalizationAndFontStress` | P21 | UI looks correct only in English at default scale | screenshot commandlet + EUW report | pseudo-localized inventory/loot/HUD screenshots |
| `EUW_AscentiaPlatformServiceBoard` | P21 | achievements, cloud saves, overlays, provider config, and storefront claims can fail outside PIE | platform/release EUW | Null-provider proof plus achievement/cloud/storefront audit |
| `EUW_AscentiaPhotoCapturePresetLab` | P20/P21 | player screenshots, evidence captures, and store shots depend on ad hoc commands and unsafe metadata | capture preset EUW + share-safe audit | clean player preset, evidence preset, metadata scan |
| `EUW_AscentiaNetworkScenarioLab` | P22 | co-op/invasion assumptions cannot be proven by single-instance PIE | network scenario EUW + Gauntlet/UAT wrapper | two-client combat, loot, late-join, and session proof |
| `EUW_AscentiaTargetingProfileLab` | P11/P14/P15/P20 | lock-on, soft aim, reticles, weak points, and camera targeting drift per feature | targeting/camera EUW | target acquire/cycle/break plus camera collision proof |

Priority recommendation:

1. Build Doctor first, because it catches the class of "editor/PIE broke before
   design can happen" failures we have already hit.
2. Motion Inspector plus Notify Window Doctor second, because combat feel is the
   current iteration hot path and is where root motion, dodge intent, VFX, audio,
   and animation-cancel policy all collide.
3. Atmosphere Director third if Ultra Dynamic Sky is adopted, because UDS should
   become an Ascentia-authored profile provider, not a pile of raw level tweaks.
4. Pack Ingestor and Weapon Mount Lab next, because the project is already
   integrating external animation/content packs and Designer-adjustable sockets
   are a known need.
5. World Director should follow when P17 world scale starts, where Landscry, PCG,
   World Partition, POIs, Data Layers, lighting, and validation must converge.
6. Guidance Board should arrive before questless world content expands, because
   map clues, tutorials, memories, and NPC hints need a shared source of truth
   before Designers author dozens of them.
7. Population Ecology Lab should follow the first World Director slice, before
   Zone01 is filled with ambient actors, so Smart Objects, StateTrees, Mass,
   PCG candidates, and density budgets share one contract.

#### 11.2.7 Ultra Dynamic Sky integration contract

Ultra Dynamic Sky should be an art-direction provider wrapped by Ascentia data.
The project should not scatter raw UDS tuning across levels with no game-facing
contract.

Native/runtime contract:

```text
UAscentiaAtmosphereSubsystem
  ApplyAtmosphereProfile(FGameplayTagContainer ContextTags)
  ApplyWeatherProfile(PDA_WeatherProfile)
  ApplyTimeOfDayProfile(PDA_TimeOfDayProfile)
  PreviewAtmosphereProfile(PDA_LightingMoodProfile)
  OnAtmosphereStateChanged

UAscentiaAtmosphereComponent
  optional placed-world adapter for zones, arenas, dungeons, and POIs

IAscentiaAtmosphereProvider
  interface implemented by the UDS adapter and any future sky/weather provider
```

Designer data:

```text
PDA_LightingMoodProfile
  MoodTag
  TimeOfDayProfile
  WeatherProfile
  PostProcessProfile
  ExposurePolicy
  FogPolicy
  AccessibilityVariant
  ScreenshotProofSet

PDA_WeatherProfile
  WeatherTag
  UDSWeatherPreset
  PrecipitationIntensity
  WindIntensity
  SurfaceWetnessPolicy
  AudioStateTag
  NiagaraBudgetProfile
  GameplayReadabilityRisk

PDA_TimeOfDayProfile
  TimeTag
  UDSTimeOfDay
  DawnDuskPolicy
  SunMoonPolicy
  EncounterOverridePolicy
```

Tags:

```text
Atmosphere.Mood.Dread
Atmosphere.Mood.Sacred
Atmosphere.Mood.Stormbound
Weather.Clear
Weather.Rain.Light
Weather.Rain.Heavy
Weather.Snow.Light
Weather.Storm.Lightning
Time.Dawn
Time.Noon
Time.Dusk
Time.Night
Biome.Forest
Biome.Necropolis
Biome.Highlands
```

Tooling:

- `EUW_AscentiaAtmosphereDirector` owns profile creation and preview.
- `EUW_AscentiaWorldDirector` binds atmosphere profiles to zones.
- `EUW_AscentiaLightingMoodLab` compares screenshots across profiles and catches
  readability/accessibility/performance risks.
- `EUW_AscentiaMaterialSurfaceLab` verifies weather material response on
  landscape, meshes, puddles, snow, rain streaks, footstep decals, and impact
  cues.
- `AscentiaDirectorDashboard` shows the last atmosphere proof for each zone.

System connections:

- Audio: weather and biome profiles drive ambience/music state tags.
- VFX: precipitation, wind debris, lightning, breath, puddles, and snow should
  map to Niagara/cue budget profiles.
- Materials: UDS/UDW material effects must be normalized through Ascentia
  surface policies so wetness/snow is consistent across assets and packs.
- AI/perception: only expose weather as gameplay-affecting if design explicitly
  opts in. If heavy rain changes perception, it must be visible as a profile
  rule and validated.
- Traversal: weather can affect readability and surface presentation, but must
  not silently change movement friction unless a gameplay profile says so.
- Save/world state: time/weather state can be saved for deterministic world
  moments, but ambient cycling should remain a controllable design decision.

Proof gates:

1. Screenshot matrix for each profile at desktop and target low settings.
2. PIE smoke test that changes time/weather through Ascentia profiles, not raw
   UDS actor tweaks.
3. Performance report with Niagara/weather budgets.
4. Readability pass for fog, darkness, precipitation, and combat silhouettes.
5. Surface response pass for landscape, modular meshes, weapons, armor, and
   footstep/impact effects.

#### 11.2.8 Tool implementation decision tree

Use this route before building any new production tool:

```text
Need a rich Designer panel?
  -> Editor Utility Widget.

Need modal viewport interaction such as paint, place, drag, sculpt, measure?
  -> Scriptable Tool or editor mode.

Need repeatable batch import, report generation, or MCP-controlled operations?
  -> Python editor script, with an EUW button if Designers need it.

Need stable reflection, asset validation, undo transactions, details
customization, or access to editor internals?
  -> AscentiaEditor C++ module.

Need CI/cook/package proof?
  -> Commandlet or Automation Test.

Need live production status across docs, tools, and playtests?
  -> Dashboard evidence JSON plus human verdict.
```

This keeps tools visible where Designers need them and native where production
authority belongs.

#### 11.2.9 Tool anti-patterns

Avoid these:

- "Generate final gameplay content" buttons that leave no editable DataAsset,
  Blueprint, DataTable, or report.
- One-off Python scripts that mutate assets without dry-run mode or evidence.
- Raw third-party actor settings used as game architecture.
- Tool outputs that bypass Data Validation.
- Hidden defaults that Designers cannot inspect in the Editor.
- Tools tied to hard-coded Content paths instead of tags, Primary Asset IDs,
  settings assets, or selected roots.
- Screenshot-only proof with no machine-readable report.
- Dashboard metrics that cannot link back to an asset or validator.

#### 11.2.10 Build, buy, or bridge

Ascentia should not build a bespoke tool when a strong marketplace/plugin or
engine feature already solves the hard part. The house rule:

```text
Buy the commodity.
Bridge the provider.
Build the Ascentia-specific contract.
Validate the result.
```

Decision matrix:

| Need | Preferred route | Why |
|---|---|---|
| Sky, clouds, weather, time of day | Buy/bridge Ultra Dynamic Sky or equivalent | The hard visual system already exists; Ascentia needs mood/weather profiles, not a bespoke sky renderer. |
| Landscape/world source generation | Bridge Landscry into World Partition/PCG | Generated terrain is source material; Ascentia must own zone identity, POIs, Data Layers, nav, and proof. |
| Modular mesh generation | Bridge Asset Factory | Generated meshes need fit, socket, LOD, collision, material, and source/license validation. |
| UI visual skinning | Bridge Skin Forge / InterfaceArtForge | Art direction can be generated quickly, but WidgetBlueprint bindings, CommonUI behavior, localization, and navigation need validation. |
| Designer dock panels | Build EUWs | They are visible, fast to iterate, and live in the Editor where Designers work. |
| Viewport placement/painting/measuring | Build Scriptable Tools | Gizmos, hit-testing, property sets, and viewport feedback matter more than a docked form. |
| Batch import/report/repair | Build Python/MCP scripts behind EUW buttons | Python is ideal for editor automation, but reports and dry-run modes are mandatory. |
| Stable validation/invariants | Build `AscentiaEditor` C++ validators | C++ should own rules that block cooked builds or touch protected runtime contracts. |
| Packaged proof | Build commandlets/automation/Gauntlet-style scenarios | PIE can lie; cooked builds need their own evidence. |

Bridge assets should always have an adapter seam:

```text
ThirdPartyProvider
  raw plugin/asset actor, preset, graph, file, or generated package

AscentiaProfile
  stable DataAsset/DataTable row that Designers tune

AscentiaAdapter
  runtime/editor class that applies profile values to the provider

AscentiaValidator
  checks profile/provider compatibility

AscentiaEvidence
  screenshot/report/log proving the bridge still works
```

This avoids locking the game architecture to any one plugin. UDS can be replaced
later if necessary; `PDA_LightingMoodProfile` and `IAscentiaAtmosphereProvider`
remain the game contract.

#### 11.2.11 Tool maturity ladder

Every accelerator should move through explicit stages. Do not let prototype
scripts silently become production infrastructure.

```text
Idea
  named pain, owner system, first slice, expected saved time

Prototype
  manual trigger, safe test folder, no production mutation by default

DryRun
  reads real project data, writes report, links affected assets

AssistedMutation
  Designer reviews proposed changes, tool uses transactions/undo

ValidatedMutation
  tool output feeds Data Validation and Dashboard evidence

Batch/CI
  commandlet or script can run unattended and fail a gate

ProductionOwned
  docs, ownership, schema version, migration policy, test fixtures
```

Tool metadata:

```text
PDA_ToolDescriptor

  Category
  OwnerSystem
  OwnerPhase
  ToolType

  MutatesAssets

```

`EUW_AscentiaBuildDoctor` should eventually list all registered tool descriptors,
their last run, last failure, evidence path, and whether their validators are
blocking any phase gate.

Descriptor rules:

- A tool without `PDA_ToolDescriptor` is experimental and cannot block a phase
  gate.
- A mutating tool must expose dry-run first, produce `FAscentiaToolMutationPlan`,
  and require a Designer or automation policy to approve the plan.
- A tool that touches third-party assets must attach a provenance record and
  plugin/provider profile.
- A tool that touches gameplay-facing content must surface its input and output
  in an EUW, DataAsset, DataTable, or Details panel. A Python script alone is
  not a production surface.
- A tool that opens PIE or talks to MCP must use the editor session guard and
  record the editor PID, map, project path, and active automation owner.

Every tool report should share a header so the Dashboard can ingest results

Evidence folder convention:

```text
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/Tools/{ToolId}/{RunId}/
  report.json
  summary.md
  assets.csv
  warnings.csv
  screenshots/
  thumbnails/
  logs/
  choices.json
```

Human taste still matters. A tool can measure that a sword trail fired; a
Designer still decides whether the arc feels right. `choices.json` preserves
that decision so future generated variants can learn which direction was chosen
without pretending the machine made the aesthetic call.

#### 11.2.13 First sprint tool schemas and proof reports

The first sprint should establish a reusable tool contract by solving current
pain: missing packages, invalid physics bodies, invisible animation/audio/VFX
notifies, root-motion ambiguity, dodge intent, and hidden socket tuning.

The first sprint is considered successful when these tools can run in dry-run
mode, write reports, link affected assets, and show the result in the Editor
without requiring someone to read raw logs or native code.

#### 11.2.14 First sprint EUW layouts and repair workflows

The first tooling sprint should feel like a native part of the Unreal Editor:
dockable panels, selected-asset workflows, Details views for structured data,
explicit run buttons, repair plans, and links back to the affected assets. The
underlying implementation can call C++, Python, Data Validation, Asset Registry,
Automation, or MCP, but the Designer should see one coherent tool.

Shared EUW layout:

```text
Header strip
  Tool name
  Tool descriptor asset
  owner phase/system
  last run status
  dry-run/mutation mode
  selected scope
  scenario profile
  Run Dry Scan
  Generate Repair Plan
  Apply Approved Repairs
  Open Evidence

Left rail
  scope picker
  saved filters
  severity filters
  owner filters
  asset root filters
  phase gate filters

Center table
  findings, reports, scenarios, or assets
  severity
  asset path
  issue category
  Designer fix
  suggested repair
  blocking gate
  last seen

Right panel
  Details View for selected finding/report/profile
  asset preview
  before/after values
  related assets
  repair explanation
  validation messages

Bottom drawer
  run log
  evidence artifacts
  screenshots
  CSV exports
  human verdict
  dashboard card preview
```

Implementation rule: every panel should drive a transient UObject or DataAsset
view model so the same data can be shown in a Details View, serialized into a
report, and validated. Avoid one-off widget state that cannot be saved or
tested.

Shared button flow:

```text
Select scope
  -> Validate scope can be scanned
  -> Run Dry Scan
  -> Write report.json
  -> Populate findings table
  -> Designer reviews findings
  -> Generate Repair Plan
  -> Designer checks approved repair rows
  -> Tool verifies no PIE/dirty-package/session conflict
  -> Apply transaction-wrapped repairs
  -> Run affected validators
  -> Write post-repair evidence
  -> Dashboard updates phase gate card
```

Safe repair contract:

- `Run Dry Scan` never mutates assets.
- `Generate Repair Plan` writes `FAscentiaToolMutationPlan`.
- `Apply Approved Repairs` requires checked repair rows, not a blanket "fix all"
  action.
- Mutating repairs use Unreal transactions so Undo/Redo can restore the prior
  state where the API supports it.
- Repairs that cannot be undone must say so in the row and require a second
  confirmation.
- The tool must check dirty packages, active PIE, source-control checkout state,
  and active MCP owner before mutation.
- The tool must save a pre-repair report and a post-repair report.
- High-risk repairs open the asset or profile at the relevant field instead of
  applying automatically.

`EUW_AscentiaBuildDoctor`

Purpose: "Can I safely press PIE or package this build?"

Panel layout:

```text
Summary cards
  PIE blockers
  cook blockers
  invalid physics assets
  Blueprint compile failures
  missing GameplayCue assets
  plugin drift
  recent log pattern hits

Tabs
  Scope
  Findings
  Repair Plan
  Validators
  Recent Logs
  Evidence
```

Primary buttons:

- `Scan Selected Assets`
- `Scan Current Map`
- `Scan MVP Arena`
- `Scan Project Essentials`
- `Run Data Validation`
- `Compile Blueprint Scope`
- `Parse Recent Logs`
- `Generate Repair Plan`
- `Apply Approved Low-Risk Repairs`
- `Open Last Green Evidence`

Safe repair examples:

- fix redirectors in approved folders;
- re-save assets with stale derived data after validation passes;
- assign approved default collision profile where policy is explicit;
- open PhysicsAsset editor for invalid bodies instead of silently generating
  collision;
- open GameplayCue Matrix for missing cue assets instead of inventing cues;
- open plugin profile when module/version drift is detected.

Validators:

```text
VLD_Ascentia_BuildDoctorReport
VLD_Ascentia_BlueprintCompileState
VLD_Ascentia_PhysicsAssetBodies
VLD_Ascentia_GameplayCueReferences
VLD_Ascentia_PluginState
VLD_Ascentia_LogFailurePatterns
```

Phase gates:

- P7/P8 combat smoke cannot close with Blueprint compile failures.
- P20 presentation cannot close with missing GameplayCue or Niagara/audio cue
  assets.
- P21 package proof cannot close with Build Doctor cook blockers.

`EUW_AscentiaDependencyDoctor`

Purpose: "Which references are broken before the loader finds them at runtime?"

Panel layout:

```text
Scope tree
  /Game/Ascentia
  GASP folders
  Essential Bow folders
  AssassinGirl placeholder folders
  UDS/plugin content references
  selected roots

Reference graph
  referencer asset
  missing package
  missing object
  expected disk path
  reference type
  mount point
  probable source

Repair panel
  replace reference
  remove reference
  restore package from source
  mark external plugin dependency
  open referencer
```

Primary buttons:

- `Scan Asset Roots`
- `Scan Selected Referencers`
- `Scan Animation Dependencies`
- `Scan Plugin Content Dependencies`
- `Build Missing Package Report`
- `Open Referencer`
- `Open Replacement Picker`
- `Write Content Provenance Rows`

Safe repair examples:

- replace a broken soft reference only after the Designer chooses the target;
- remove an optional soft reference only when the owning profile marks it
  optional;
- mark missing plugin content as an explicit third-party dependency;
- create a restore task for missing assets rather than fabricating an asset with
  the same name;
- fix redirectors only inside approved project roots.

Validators:

```text
VLD_Ascentia_MissingPackages
VLD_Ascentia_BrokenSoftReferences
VLD_Ascentia_BrokenHardReferences
VLD_Ascentia_PluginContentReferences
VLD_Ascentia_ContentProvenance
```

The missing `SandboxAnimCurveCompressionSettings` issue should become a row with
referencer, expected disk path, mount point, and an explicit "restore, replace,
or remove reference" choice.

`EUW_AscentiaMotionInspector`

Purpose: "What motion did the player ask for, what motion played, and where did
the capsule actually go?"

Panel layout:

```text
Scenario controls
  character archetype
  montage/section
  motion profile
  floor fixture
  ledge fixture
  stair fixture
  network mode
  input intent preset

Replay viewport
  root path
  capsule path
  floor contacts
  ledge exit frame
  selected direction slot
  expected direction slot
  cancel windows

Timeline
  root-motion distance
  capsule distance
  movement mode
  input vector
  chosen motion slot
  notifies
  contact state
```

Primary buttons:

- `Record PIE Intent`
- `Run Fixture Replay`
- `Compare Direction Slots`
- `Export Motion CSV`
- `Open Motion Policy`
- `Open Montage`
- `Open Root Motion Policy Lab`

Safe repair examples:

- update `PDA_DodgeSet` directional slot mapping after Designer approval;
- update intent dead-zone/minimum rules in the motion policy;
- add a scenario row for a failed ledge/stair case;
- never rewrite montage root motion automatically.

Validators:

```text
VLD_Ascentia_MotionScenarioProfiles
VLD_Ascentia_DodgeDirectionSlots
VLD_Ascentia_RootMotionPolicies
VLD_Ascentia_CancelWindowPolicies
```

This panel is where "BR plays BL" becomes objective: input vector, expected slot,
selected slot, montage, and resulting capsule path appear in the same evidence
row.

`EUW_AscentiaRootMotionPolicyLab`

Purpose: "How should root motion, player intent, stairs, ledges, collision, and
animation cancel interact?"

Panel layout:

```text
Policy editor
  motion policy asset
  montage family
  root-motion mode
  floor projection mode
  ledge policy
  stair tolerance
  collision slide policy
  cancel policy
  network policy

Comparison grid
  current policy
  proposed policy
  fixture result
  designer verdict
  risk note
```

Primary buttons:

- `Preview Current Policy`
- `Preview Proposed Policy`
- `Duplicate Policy Variant`
- `Run Ledge Fixture`
- `Run Stair Fixture`
- `Run Wall Slide Fixture`
- `Accept Variant`

Safe repair examples:

- create a new policy variant instead of overwriting the current policy;
- mark a policy as prototype or production;
- copy accepted values into the production policy only through a mutation plan;
- add failed fixture evidence to the policy notes.

Validators:

```text
VLD_Ascentia_RootMotionPolicyCoverage
VLD_Ascentia_StairFixtureResults
VLD_Ascentia_LedgeFixtureResults
VLD_Ascentia_NetworkMotionPolicies
```

`EUW_AscentiaNotifyWindowDoctor`

Purpose: "Do attacks and skills have the required damage, trail, audio, cue,
iframe, and cancel windows?"

Panel layout:

```text
Montage list
  weapon family
  action
  montage
  section
  required notify set
  missing windows

Timeline preview
  damage window
  trail window
  swing audio window
  cancel window
  iframe window
  GameplayCue window

Repair panel
  add notify from template
  shift window
  copy windows from reference montage
  open montage at frame
```

Primary buttons:

- `Audit Moveset`
- `Audit Selected Montages`
- `Preview Notify Timeline`
- `Compare To Reference Attack`
- `Generate Notify Repair Plan`
- `Apply Approved Notify Repairs`
- `Open Cue Matrix`

Safe repair examples:

- add notifies only from named templates;
- shift windows only inside allowed frame ranges;
- copy from a reference montage only when skeleton/action family matches;
- open montage for manual correction when timing would cross a restricted
  window.

Validators:

```text
VLD_Ascentia_MontageNotifyWindows
VLD_Ascentia_AttackWindowProfiles
VLD_Ascentia_CancelWindowProfiles
VLD_Ascentia_AudioNotifyCoverage
VLD_Ascentia_NiagaraNotifyCoverage
```

`EUW_AscentiaWeaponMountLab`

Purpose: "Can a Designer tune weapon grip, sheath, trail sockets, and collision
alignment without spelunking through components?"

Panel layout:

```text
Preview controls
  character preview
  body preset
  weapon mesh
  hand socket
  sheath socket
  pose set
  trail profile
  collision profile

Viewport
  idle pose
  attack pose
  roll pose
  sheath pose
  trail socket preview
  collision preview

Adjustment details
  selected socket offset
  pose-specific note
  save to socket profile
  screenshot row
```

Primary buttons:

- `Load Weapon Profile`
- `Preview Grip`
- `Preview Sheath`
- `Preview Attack Trail`
- `Capture Comparison Sheet`
- `Save Socket Profile Variant`
- `Apply Approved Socket Profile`

Safe repair examples:

- save offsets to `PDA_WeaponSocketProfile`, not hidden component defaults;
- keep per-weapon and per-body-preset overrides explicit;
- require screenshot evidence before accepting a production socket profile;
- never overwrite the skeleton socket directly unless a skeleton-owner profile
  permits it.

Validators:

```text
VLD_Ascentia_WeaponSocketProfiles
VLD_Ascentia_WeaponMountPreviewCoverage
VLD_Ascentia_TrailSocketCoverage
VLD_Ascentia_ModularFitSocketCompatibility
```

```text
Details
```

`EUW_AscentiaThirdPartyPluginRiskPanel`

Purpose: "Which external systems are we relying on, and what is Ascentia's
replacement seam?"

Panel layout:

```text
Plugin matrix
  plugin
  version
  enabled modules
  runtime dependency
  editor dependency
  shipping risk
  adapter class
  replacement seam
  last validated engine version

Risk details
  broken exposure
  missing adapter
  unsupported engine version
  direct raw-provider usage

```

Primary buttons:

- `Scan Enabled Plugins`
- `Validate Bridge Profiles`
- `Find Raw Provider Usage`

- `Open Provider Profile`

Validators:

```text
VLD_Ascentia_ThirdPartyPluginRisks
VLD_Ascentia_BridgeProviders
VLD_Ascentia_RawProviderUsage
VLD_Ascentia_PluginVersionCompatibility
```

For Ultra Dynamic Sky specifically, the risk panel should verify that levels use
Ascentia atmosphere profiles/adapters for production content. Raw UDS actor
tweaks can exist in sandbox maps, but production zones should route mood,
weather, time, material response, audio state, and VFX budget through Ascentia
profiles.

#### 11.2.15 First sprint implementation map

The repository already has an editor-only module:

```text
Ascentia.uproject
  Modules
    Ascentia
      Type: Runtime
    AscentiaEditor
      Type: Editor
      LoadingPhase: PreDefault

Source/AscentiaEditor/
  AscentiaEditor.Build.cs
  Private/AscentiaEditorModule.cpp
```

That is the correct home for tooling invariants, validators, report writers,
asset-registry scans, transaction helpers, source-control checks, commandlet
entry points, and Blueprint-callable editor facades. Runtime systems should not
know that Build Doctor, Dependency Doctor, or MCP Guard exist.

Proposed module folders:

```text
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Tooling/
  AscentiaToolTypes.h
  AscentiaToolEvidenceTypes.h
  AscentiaToolDescriptor.h
  AscentiaToolSubsystem.h
  AscentiaToolReportWriter.h
  AscentiaToolRepairTypes.h

Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Validation/
  AscentiaEditorValidatorBase.h
  VLD_Ascentia_BuildDoctorReport.h
  VLD_Ascentia_MissingPackages.h
  VLD_Ascentia_MontageNotifyWindows.h
  VLD_Ascentia_WeaponSocketProfiles.h
  VLD_Ascentia_ToolDescriptors.h

Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Automation/
  AscentiaEditorSessionGuard.h
  AscentiaSourceControlGate.h
  AscentiaScopedAssetMutation.h
  AscentiaCommandletTypes.h

Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Tooling/
  AscentiaToolSubsystem.cpp
  AscentiaToolReportWriter.cpp
  AscentiaToolRegistry.cpp
  AscentiaToolEvidenceStore.cpp

Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Doctors/
  AscentiaBuildDoctor.cpp
  AscentiaDependencyDoctor.cpp
  AscentiaNotifyWindowDoctor.cpp
  AscentiaWeaponMountLab.cpp
  AscentiaMotionInspector.cpp
  AscentiaRootMotionPolicyLab.cpp
  AscentiaContentProvenanceScanner.cpp
  AscentiaThirdPartyPluginRiskScanner.cpp

Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Commandlets/
  AscentiaRunToolCommandlet.cpp
  AscentiaValidateToolRegistryCommandlet.cpp
  AscentiaDependencyDoctorCommandlet.cpp
  AscentiaBuildDoctorCommandlet.cpp
```

The current `AscentiaEditor.Build.cs` only depends on core engine modules and
IKRig modules. The first tooling sprint should expand dependencies in two
stages:

```text
Stage 1 - registry, validation, reports
  UnrealEd
  AssetRegistry
  DataValidation
  Projects
  SourceControl
  Json
  JsonUtilities
  EditorScriptingUtilities

Stage 2 - animation and preview tooling
  AnimGraph
  AnimationEditor
  Persona
  Sequencer
  LevelEditor
  Slate
  SlateCore
  UMG
  UMGEditor

Stage 3 - optional bridge scanners
  Niagara
  NiagaraEditor
  GameplayAbilities
  GameplayTags
  ToolMenus
  EditorSubsystem
```

Keep the runtime `Ascentia` module free of editor-only dependencies. If a shared
struct must be visible to runtime and editor, place the runtime-safe data shape
in `Source/Ascentia/Public/...` and keep scan/repair/report behavior in
`AscentiaEditor`.

Core classes and ownership:

```text
UAscentiaToolSubsystem : UEditorSubsystem
  load tool descriptors
  run dry scans
  request repair plans
  dispatch tool reports
  expose BlueprintCallable editor-only functions for EUWs

UAscentiaToolDescriptor : UPrimaryDataAsset
  source asset for `PDA_ToolDescriptor_*`
  owns tool identity, phase, evidence profile, repair policy, UI asset,
  validator tags, script/commandlet route, and maturity state

UAscentiaToolEvidenceProfile : UPrimaryDataAsset
  owns required report artifacts, screenshot/CSV/log expectations,
  retention policy, and human verdict requirements

UAscentiaEditorScenarioProfile : UPrimaryDataAsset
  owns map, asset roots, PIE policy, dirty-package policy, timeout,
  and success criteria for a tool run

UAscentiaThirdPartyPluginProfile : UPrimaryDataAsset
  owns plugin provider, version, runtime/editor dependency, adapter class,
  shipping risk, source/license notes, and replacement seam

UAscentiaToolReportWriter : UObject
  writes report.json, summary.md, assets.csv, warnings.csv,
  screenshots manifest, choices.json, and dashboard card JSON

UAscentiaToolEvidenceStore : UObject
  indexes `Saved/AscentiaEvidence/Tools/...`
  supports latest run, compare runs, retention, and dashboard ingest

UAscentiaToolRepairPlanner : UObject
  builds `FAscentiaToolMutationPlan`
  assigns repair risk
  filters repairs by tool policy and user approval

FAscentiaScopedAssetMutation
  wraps `FScopedTransaction`
  calls Modify on edited assets
  records before/after values
  checks source-control state before mutation
  emits post-repair evidence

FAscentiaSourceControlGate
  resolves package filenames
  queries source-control state
  requests checkout/add when policy allows
  blocks mutation when files are locked, read-only, or unmanaged

FAscentiaEditorSessionGuard
  verifies project path, editor PID, loaded map, active PIE state,
  dirty packages, MCP owner, lock file, heartbeat, and timeout
```

Blueprint asset paths:

```text
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Tools/Descriptors/
  PDA_ToolDescriptor_BuildDoctor
  PDA_ToolDescriptor_DependencyDoctor
  PDA_ToolDescriptor_MotionInspector
  PDA_ToolDescriptor_RootMotionPolicyLab
  PDA_ToolDescriptor_NotifyWindowDoctor
  PDA_ToolDescriptor_WeaponMountLab
  PDA_ToolDescriptor_ToolRegistry
  PDA_ToolDescriptor_ToolEvidenceBrowser
  PDA_ToolDescriptor_MCPAutomationGuard
  PDA_ToolDescriptor_ContentProvenanceBrowser
  PDA_ToolDescriptor_ThirdPartyPluginRiskPanel

  EUW_AscentiaNetworkScenarioLab

  EUW_AscentiaComplianceReleaseGate
```

EUWs should be thin orchestration surfaces. They call
`UAscentiaToolSubsystem` Blueprint functions, bind to view-model objects, and
open assets/evidence. They should not contain the scanner logic.

Commandlet and script entry points:

```text
UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe Ascentia.uproject -run=AscentiaRunTool
  -ToolId=BuildDoctor
  -Scenario=MVPArena
  -DryRun
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/Tools

UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe Ascentia.uproject -run=AscentiaRunTool
  -ToolId=DependencyDoctor
  -AssetRoot=/Game/Characters
  -DryRun

UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe Ascentia.uproject -run=AscentiaValidateToolRegistry
  -FailOnMissingEvidenceProfile
  -FailOnDuplicateToolId

Tools/run_ascentia_tool.ps1
  wraps UnrealEditor-Cmd invocation
  writes process log
  normalizes exit code
  copies report summary to dashboard ingest folder

Tools/editor_session_guard.py
  only for full-editor/MCP sessions
  writes lock and heartbeat
  refuses mutation when another Ascentia editor owns automation

Tools/collect_tool_evidence.ps1
  gathers report.json, summary.md, CSVs, screenshots, and logs
  updates dashboard card cache
```

Use commandlets for read-only scans, CI/package gates, registry validation, and
batch reports. Use full-editor EUWs for Designer review, previews, selected
assets, and transaction-wrapped mutation.

Report writer contract:

```text
FAscentiaToolReportWriteRequest
  Header
  EvidenceRoot
  ReportObject
  SummaryMarkdown
  AssetRows
  WarningRows
  ScreenshotPaths
  LogExcerptPaths
  HumanVerdict
  DashboardCard

FAscentiaToolReportWriteResult
  ReportJsonPath
  SummaryMarkdownPath
  AssetsCsvPath
  WarningsCsvPath
  ChoicesJsonPath
  DashboardCardPath
  WriteErrors
```

All reports should be deterministic enough to diff: stable field order, stable
tool IDs, stable asset paths, and sorted finding rows. Human notes can remain
free text, but machine rows should not depend on selection order.

First implementation tickets:

```text
TOOL-001 Create AscentiaEditor tooling folder structure and Build.cs deps
  acceptance: module compiles, runtime module unchanged, no editor deps leak

TOOL-002 Add core tool structs and `UAscentiaToolSubsystem`
  acceptance: EUW can load registered `PDA_ToolDescriptor_*` assets

TOOL-003 Add `UAscentiaToolReportWriter`
  acceptance: writes report.json, summary.md, assets.csv, warnings.csv,
  choices.json, and dashboard card for a fake tool run

TOOL-004 Add `FAscentiaEditorSessionGuard`
  acceptance: detects project path, editor PID, loaded map, active PIE,
  dirty packages, lock owner, and heartbeat state

TOOL-005 Add `FAscentiaScopedAssetMutation` and source-control gate
  acceptance: dry-run produces mutation plan; approved mutation uses
  transaction and refuses locked/read-only packages

TOOL-006 Create `EUW_AscentiaToolRegistry`
  acceptance: lists descriptors, last evidence, maturity, blocking gates,
  and missing evidence profiles

TOOL-007 Create `EUW_AscentiaToolEvidenceBrowser`
  acceptance: browses report history and opens artifacts from
  `Saved/AscentiaEvidence/Tools`

TOOL-008 Build Doctor dry-run
  acceptance: scans current map/selected assets, Blueprint compile status,
  invalid physics assets, plugin state, recent logs, and writes report

TOOL-009 Dependency Doctor dry-run
  acceptance: reports broken soft/hard refs, redirectors, missing packages,
  plugin-content refs, and expected disk paths

TOOL-010 Notify Window Doctor dry-run
  acceptance: selected montage audit reports missing damage/trail/audio/cue/
  cancel/iframe windows

TOOL-011 Weapon Mount Lab preview
  acceptance: Designer can preview grip, sheath, trail sockets, save a socket
  profile variant, and capture comparison screenshots

TOOL-012 Motion Inspector fixture replay
  acceptance: records or replays input intent, selected direction slot,
  root path, capsule path, floor contacts, stair/ledge results, and CSV output

TOOL-013 MCP Automation Guard EUW
  acceptance: visible lock/heartbeat panel refuses mutation when active PIE,
  dirty-package policy, wrong project, or another owner is detected

TOOL-014 Content Provenance Browser dry-run

  source notes

TOOL-015 Third Party Plugin Risk Panel dry-run

TOOL-016 Narrative Graph dry-run

TOOL-017 Cinematic Binder dry-run

TOOL-018 VO/Subtitle/Localization audit dry-run

TOOL-019 Playtest Recorder dry-run

TOOL-020 Crash Triage Panel dry-run

  acceptance: old saves, current saves, cloud-save fixtures, settings profiles,
  achievement/stat facts, and demo/full fixture states are tested against the
  new content release

  acceptance: mount success, missing entitlement, missing chunk, hash mismatch,

  acceptance: patch-note claims map to regression scenario IDs, evidence rows,

  acceptance: save backup, migration risk, previous content version, player
  messaging, and support-bundle policy are visible

TOOL-109 DLC Readiness Checklist dry-run
  acceptance: release tag, chunk plan, save/loc compatibility, entitlement
  stance, platform/store claims, performance, and dashboard blockers are checked

TOOL-110 External Content Registry dry-run
  acceptance: external source records, license policy, purchase/version date,
  staging roots, production roots, owner, approval state, shipped usage, and
  evidence are visible

TOOL-111 License Compliance Board dry-run
  acceptance: commercial use, modification, collaborator sharing, source
  repository storage, standalone redistribution risk, attribution, NoAI,
  reference-only restriction, and shipping review are reported

TOOL-112 Plugin Descriptor Doctor dry-run
  acceptance: `.uplugin` descriptor fields, module compatibility, target
  platforms, dependencies, docs/support links, raw provider use, adapter
  coverage, and version drift are scanned

TOOL-113 External Asset Quarantine dry-run
  acceptance: newly imported folders show naming, layout, asset classes,
  skeleton/material/LOD/collision policy, duplicate source, license state, and
  dangerous references before promotion

TOOL-114 External Upgrade Planner dry-run
  acceptance: current/candidate versions, changed assets/modules, required
  regression scenarios, rollback stance, license recheck, and owner sign-off are
  visible

TOOL-115 Attribution And Credits Board dry-run
  acceptance: CC-BY and vendor credit rows, soundtrack/audio/voice credit needs,
  screenshot/store attribution, and final credits export are generated

TOOL-116 NoAI And Generated Content Audit dry-run
  acceptance: generated content provenance, NoAI policy, reference-only inputs,
  human likeness/voice/trademark/music flags, and review blockers are reported

TOOL-117 External Content Promotion Gate dry-run
  acceptance: staged-to-production promotion plan, source records, license
  policy, acceptance state, redirector risk, source-control state, and package
  blockers are visible

TOOL-118 Workspace Doctor dry-run
  acceptance: project root, source-control provider, cloud-sync risk, path
  length, engine/plugin versions, dirty packages, active editors, and
  team-authority readiness are reported

TOOL-119 Changelist Composer dry-run
  acceptance: phase tags, system tags, changed files, binary counts, evidence
  links, review requests, package impact, save impact, Designer verdict, and
  rollback notes are captured

TOOL-120 OFPA Changelist Validator dry-run
  acceptance: external actor files resolve to readable levels/actors/Data
  Layers/World Partition cells and partial-submit or dangling-reference risks
  become blockers

TOOL-121 Asset Lock Board dry-run
  acceptance: checked-out assets, stale locks, writable-without-checkout
  packages, not-at-head packages, lock owners, and binary merge risk are visible

TOOL-122 Review Gate Board dry-run
  acceptance: code review, asset review, automation, package, Designer, waiver,
  and evidence rows produce one final change verdict

TOOL-123 Verified Sync Board dry-run
  acceptance: green revision/build identity, smoke-test state, compatible
  engine/plugin versions, known blockers, DDC notes, and artifact links are
  visible to Designers

TOOL-124 Branch Stream Map dry-run
  acceptance: branch/stream purpose, parent branch, allowed content roots,
  allowed code modules, freeze policy, release channel, and required merge
  gates are reported

TOOL-125 Redirector And Move Planner dry-run
  acceptance: selected asset move/rename/fixup plan lists affected references,
  redirector risk, source-control state, transaction policy, and approval rows

TOOL-126 Zone Delivery Board dry-run
  acceptance: zone milestone, owner, blockers, required evidence, latest route
  proof, streaming/nav/HLOD state, and next gate are visible

TOOL-127 Blockout Playtest Lab dry-run
  acceptance: route anchors, travel time, camera issues, fall/death events,
  combat pocket outcomes, guidance state, screenshots, and human notes are
  captured

TOOL-128 World Partition Proof Board dry-run
  acceptance: runtime grid, cells, loaded regions, Location Volumes, streaming
  sources, Data Layers, minimap, HLOD, cook state, and debug rows are visible

TOOL-129 Zone Nav Traversal Gate dry-run
  acceptance: player anchors, AI nav, Smart Objects, nav links, ledge/water
  exceptions, combat-pocket access, and recovery anchors are reported

TOOL-130 Zone HLOD Budget Board dry-run
  acceptance: HLOD layers, stale builds, source actor drift, draw/triangle/
  material cost, and scalability variants are visible

TOOL-131 PCG Provenance Gate dry-run
  acceptance: Landscry source package, PCG graph, seed, generated roots,
  accepted/rejected outputs, promotion state, and findings are reported

TOOL-132 Zone Performance Trace Board dry-run
  acceptance: Insights trace links, route markers, Timing/Memory/Asset Loading/
  Cooking summaries, and blocking budget rows are visible

TOOL-133 Zone Signoff Board dry-run
  acceptance: milestone verdicts for design, engineering, QA, audio, lighting,
  encounter, accessibility, performance, and release are captured

TOOL-134 Ratings Evidence Board dry-run
  acceptance: rating content signals, source assets, evidence clips/screenshots,
  DLC/update deltas, interactive elements, descriptors, and submission readiness
  are visible

TOOL-135 Privacy Data Map dry-run
  acceptance: collected data elements, runtime sources, providers, purpose,
  retention, opt-out/delete/export behavior, player-visible settings, and owners
  are reported

TOOL-136 Store Claim Compliance Board dry-run
  acceptance: public claims by surface, evidence, freshness, region,
  localization keys, screenshots/trailers, approval state, and blockers are
  visible

TOOL-137 Legal Text And Credits Board dry-run
  acceptance: EULA/privacy/support notices, third-party attribution, soundtrack,
  VO, vendor credits, locale coverage, counsel-review status, and output paths
  are reported

TOOL-138 Age Gate And Regional Availability Lab dry-run
  acceptance: storefront, territory, rating authority, age-gate state, valid
  rating presence, Steam Germany state, regional visibility, and publish
  blockers are visible

TOOL-139 Player Data Request Simulator dry-run
  acceptance: access, export, delete, correct, opt-out, limit-sensitive, and
  support-bundle scenarios produce evidence against privacy data map rows

TOOL-140 Marketing Asset Truth Audit dry-run
  acceptance: screenshots, trailers, GIFs, capsules, press-kit copy, and store
  text are checked against shipped features, rating signals, accessibility
  claims, and localization obligations

TOOL-141 Compliance Release Gate dry-run
  acceptance: missing rating evidence, unowned privacy data, unreviewed public
  claims, incomplete credits, regional-age-gate risks, and player-data-request
  failures become release blockers
```

The first milestone is not "all tools complete." It is "one boring tool
pipeline works end to end": descriptor -> EUW -> dry scan -> report writer ->
evidence browser -> dashboard card -> validator -> optional approved repair.
Build Doctor or Dependency Doctor is the best first vertical slice because it
directly addresses the recurring PIE/package failures.

#### 11.2.16 Starter package for TOOL-001 through TOOL-005

The first implementation package should stop before Build Doctor becomes a
feature. Its job is to create the reusable spine that every later tool uses.

`TOOL-001` file stubs:
