```text
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Tests/AscentiaScopedAssetMutationTest.cpp
```

`TOOL-001` module dependency change:

```csharp
PrivateDependencyModuleNames.AddRange(new string[]
{
    "Core",
    "CoreUObject",
    "Engine",
    "UnrealEd",
    "AssetRegistry",
    "DataValidation",
    "Projects",
    "SourceControl",
    "Json",
    "JsonUtilities",
    "EditorSubsystem",
    "EditorScriptingUtilities",
    "IKRig",
    "IKRigDeveloper"
});
```

Acceptance:

- `AscentiaEditor` compiles with the new folders.
- `Ascentia` runtime module remains unchanged.
- No editor-only headers are included from runtime code.
- `AscentiaEditorModule.cpp` registers no heavyweight startup behavior beyond
  module/tool menu initialization.

`TOOL-002` reflected data and subsystem signatures:

```cpp
UENUM(BlueprintType)
enum class EAscentiaToolRunMode : uint8
{
    DryRun,
    GenerateRepairPlan,
    ApplyApprovedRepairs
};

UENUM(BlueprintType)
enum class EAscentiaToolRunResult : uint8
{
    NotRun,
    Passed,
    Warning,
    Failed,
    Blocked
};

USTRUCT(BlueprintType)
struct FAscentiaToolRunRequest
{
    GENERATED_BODY()

    UPROPERTY(EditAnywhere, BlueprintReadWrite, Category="Ascentia|Tools")
    FName ToolId;

    UPROPERTY(EditAnywhere, BlueprintReadWrite, Category="Ascentia|Tools")
    EAscentiaToolRunMode RunMode = EAscentiaToolRunMode::DryRun;

    UPROPERTY(EditAnywhere, BlueprintReadWrite, Category="Ascentia|Tools")
    TArray<FSoftObjectPath> AssetScope;

    UPROPERTY(EditAnywhere, BlueprintReadWrite, Category="Ascentia|Tools")
    FSoftObjectPath MapScope;

    UPROPERTY(EditAnywhere, BlueprintReadWrite, Category="Ascentia|Tools")
    bool bAllowPIE = false;

    UPROPERTY(EditAnywhere, BlueprintReadWrite, Category="Ascentia|Tools")
    bool bAllowMutation = false;

    UPROPERTY(EditAnywhere, BlueprintReadWrite, Category="Ascentia|Tools")
    FString ReportRoot;
};

UCLASS(BlueprintType)
class UAscentiaToolDescriptor : public UPrimaryDataAsset
{
    GENERATED_BODY()

public:
    UPROPERTY(EditDefaultsOnly, BlueprintReadOnly, Category="Ascentia|Tools")
    FName ToolId;

    UPROPERTY(EditDefaultsOnly, BlueprintReadOnly, Category="Ascentia|Tools")
    FText DisplayName;

    UPROPERTY(EditDefaultsOnly, BlueprintReadOnly, Category="Ascentia|Tools")
    FName OwnerPhase;

    UPROPERTY(EditDefaultsOnly, BlueprintReadOnly, Category="Ascentia|Tools")
    bool bSupportsDryRun = true;

    UPROPERTY(EditDefaultsOnly, BlueprintReadOnly, Category="Ascentia|Tools")
    bool bMutatesAssets = false;

    UPROPERTY(EditDefaultsOnly, BlueprintReadOnly, Category="Ascentia|Tools")
    TSoftObjectPtr<UEditorUtilityWidgetBlueprint> EditorWidget;
};

UCLASS()
class UAscentiaToolSubsystem : public UEditorSubsystem
{
    GENERATED_BODY()

public:
    UFUNCTION(BlueprintCallable, Category="Ascentia|Tools")
    void RefreshToolRegistry();

    UFUNCTION(BlueprintCallable, Category="Ascentia|Tools")
    TArray<UAscentiaToolDescriptor*> GetRegisteredTools() const;

    UFUNCTION(BlueprintCallable, Category="Ascentia|Tools")
    bool BuildRunRequestFromDescriptor(
        UAscentiaToolDescriptor* Descriptor,
        FAscentiaToolRunRequest& OutRequest) const;

    UFUNCTION(BlueprintCallable, Category="Ascentia|Tools")
    FAscentiaToolRunResult RunToolDryScan(
        const FAscentiaToolRunRequest& Request);

    UFUNCTION(BlueprintCallable, Category="Ascentia|Tools")
    bool OpenEvidenceFolder(const FName ToolId, const FString& RunId) const;
};
```

Signature rules:

- Blueprint-callable subsystem functions return simple structs and object refs
  that EUWs can bind to.
- Long-running work should be async later, but the first slice can be blocking
  if reports are small.
- `RunToolDryScan` must reject mutation requests unless the descriptor and
  repair policy allow mutation.
- Every `UPROPERTY` shown in EUWs needs a category and concise tooltip in the
  final code.

`TOOL-003` report writer signatures and example output:

```cpp
UCLASS()
class UAscentiaToolReportWriter : public UObject
{
    GENERATED_BODY()

public:
    bool WriteToolReport(
        const FAscentiaToolReportWriteRequest& Request,
        FAscentiaToolReportWriteResult& OutResult) const;

    bool WriteSummaryMarkdown(
        const FAscentiaToolReportWriteRequest& Request,
        FString& OutSummaryPath) const;

    bool WriteCsvArtifacts(
        const FAscentiaToolReportWriteRequest& Request,
        FString& OutAssetsCsvPath,
        FString& OutWarningsCsvPath) const;

    bool WriteDashboardCard(
        const FAscentiaToolDashboardCard& Card,
        FString& OutDashboardCardPath) const;
};
```

Example dry-run report:

```json
{
  "header": {
    "toolId": "BuildDoctor",
    "toolVersion": "0.1.0",
    "reportSchemaVersion": "1",
    "projectName": "Ascentia",
    "engineVersion": "5.8",
    "runId": "2026-06-15T231500Z_BuildDoctor",
    "runStartedUtc": "2026-06-15T23:15:00Z",
    "runEndedUtc": "2026-06-15T23:15:12Z",
    "userOrAutomation": "EditorUser",
    "mapOrAssetScope": "MVPArena",
    "result": "Warning",
    "errorCount": 0,
    "warningCount": 2
  },
  "findings": [
    {
      "findingId": "BD-0001",
      "severity": "Warning",
      "category": "PhysicsAsset",
      "assetPath": "/Game/Characters/AssassinGirl/SK_AssassinGirl",
      "message": "Physics asset contains invalid bodies.",
      "designerFix": "Open the PhysicsAsset and add or repair root/body collision.",
      "suggestedRepairId": "OpenPhysicsAsset",

      "blocksCook": false
    }
  ],

    "assetsCsv": "assets.csv",
    "warningsCsv": "warnings.csv",
    "dashboardCard": "dashboard_card.json"
  }
}
```

Report writer acceptance tests:

- creates the evidence directory when missing;
- writes deterministic JSON with stable sorted finding rows;
- writes CSV headers even with zero findings;
- refuses to write outside the configured evidence root;
- writes a dashboard card that can be loaded by the evidence browser.

`TOOL-004` session guard signatures:

```cpp
struct FAscentiaEditorSessionSnapshot
{
    FString ProjectPath;
    int32 EditorProcessId = 0;
    FString LoadedMapPackage;
    bool bIsPIEActive = false;
    int32 DirtyPackageCount = 0;
    FString AutomationOwner;
    FString LockFilePath;
    FDateTime LastHeartbeatUtc;
};

struct FAscentiaEditorSessionGuardResult
{
    bool bAllowed = false;
    TArray<FText> BlockingReasons;
    FAscentiaEditorSessionSnapshot Snapshot;
};

class FAscentiaEditorSessionGuard
{
public:
    FAscentiaEditorSessionGuardResult VerifyReadOnlyRun() const;
    FAscentiaEditorSessionGuardResult VerifyMutationRun() const;
    bool AcquireAutomationLock(const FString& Owner, FString& OutLockPath);
    bool WriteHeartbeat(const FString& Owner);
    void ReleaseAutomationLock(const FString& Owner);
};
```

Session manifest:

```json
{
  "projectPath": "D:/Ascentia/repos/game/Ascentia.uproject",
  "editorProcessId": 12345,
  "loadedMapPackage": "/Game/Ascentia/Maps/MVP_Arena",
  "automationOwner": "EUW_AscentiaBuildDoctor",
  "acquiredUtc": "2026-06-15T23:20:00Z",
  "lastHeartbeatUtc": "2026-06-15T23:20:10Z",
  "allowMutation": false,
  "allowPIE": false
}
```

Session guard acceptance tests:

- read-only scans are allowed with dirty packages when the tool policy permits;
- mutation scans are blocked during active PIE;
- mutation scans are blocked when another owner holds the lock;
- wrong project path is blocked;
- stale heartbeat can be reported as stale but should require explicit takeover;
- every blocked result has Designer-readable reasons.

`TOOL-005` mutation/source-control signatures:

```cpp
struct FAscentiaSourceControlCheck
{
    FString PackageName;
    FString Filename;
    bool bExistsOnDisk = false;
    bool bIsReadOnly = false;
    bool bIsCheckedOut = false;
    bool bCanCheckout = false;
    bool bIsLockedByOtherUser = false;
    FText DesignerMessage;
};

class FAscentiaSourceControlGate
{
public:
    bool CheckPackagesForMutation(
        const TArray<UPackage*>& Packages,
        TArray<FAscentiaSourceControlCheck>& OutChecks) const;

    bool TryCheckoutPackages(
        const TArray<UPackage*>& Packages,
        TArray<FText>& OutErrors) const;
};

class FAscentiaScopedAssetMutation
{
public:
    FAscentiaScopedAssetMutation(
        const FText& TransactionLabel,
        const FAscentiaToolMutationPlan& MutationPlan);

    bool CanApply(TArray<FText>& OutErrors) const;
    bool MarkObjectForEdit(UObject* Object);
    void RecordBeforeAfter(
        UObject* Object,
        const FString& FieldName,
        const FString& BeforeValue,
        const FString& AfterValue);
    void Commit();
    void Cancel();
};
```

Mutation acceptance tests:

- dry-run creates a mutation plan without calling `Modify`;
- approved mutation starts one named transaction;
- edited objects are marked modified before value changes;
- source-control locked/read-only packages block mutation;
- cancellation leaves no package dirty when no changes were committed;
- high-risk repair rows cannot be applied by the low-risk helper.

Commandlet argument names for the starter package:

```text
-ToolId=BuildDoctor
-Scenario=MVPArena
-AssetRoot=/Game/Ascentia
-Map=/Game/Ascentia/Maps/MVP_Arena
-DryRun
-GenerateRepairPlan
-ApplyApprovedRepairs
-ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/Tools
-RunId=ManualOrCIProvidedRunId
-FailOnWarnings
-FailOnErrors
-JsonOnly
```

Commandlet acceptance:

- missing `-ToolId` returns a nonzero exit code and help text;
- `-DryRun` never mutates assets;
- `-ReportRoot` is normalized under the project or explicitly configured root;
- `-FailOnErrors` maps report errors to process failure;
- commandlet logs the final `report.json` path.

Recommended automation test names:

```text
Ascentia.Tools.ReportWriter.WritesDeterministicReport
Ascentia.Tools.ReportWriter.BlocksOutsideEvidenceRoot
Ascentia.Tools.SessionGuard.BlocksMutationDuringPIE
Ascentia.Tools.SessionGuard.BlocksWrongProject
Ascentia.Tools.SourceControlGate.BlocksLockedPackages
Ascentia.Tools.ScopedMutation.DryRunDoesNotModify
Ascentia.Tools.ToolSubsystem.LoadsDescriptors
Ascentia.Tools.Commandlet.RequiresToolId
```

First implementation stop line: after TOOL-001 through TOOL-005, a fake
`BuildDoctor` run should be able to load a descriptor, create a dry-run request,
write deterministic evidence, appear in the evidence browser, and prove that
mutation is blocked unless the session guard, source-control gate, descriptor,
and repair policy all agree.

#### 11.2.17 MCP and Python operating rules

The Python crash history and the two-editor MCP issue mean automation needs
guardrails. MCP is useful, especially now that Unreal has a first-party MCP
path, but Ascentia should treat editor automation as a single-owner session.

Rules:

- Only one Ascentia editor instance should own MCP/tool automation at a time.
- Tools must identify the project path, map, and editor PID before mutating
  assets.
- Python scripts should prefer commandlet/headless runs for batch reports and
  full-editor runs only when level/editor context is required.
- Python must use Unreal asset APIs for asset movement/creation/renaming, not
  raw filesystem moves.
- Any mutating script needs dry-run mode, evidence output, and a staging folder.
- Long-running editor automation should write heartbeat/status files so the
  Dashboard can distinguish "running" from "hung."
- If the tool opens PIE, it should record whether PIE ended cleanly and collect
  log excerpts for warnings/errors.
- Tool scripts should never assume the editor is idle; check selected assets,
  loaded map, active PIE state, and dirty packages before proceeding.

Suggested helper:

```text
Tools/editor_session_guard.py
  acquire_ascentia_editor_lock
  verify_project_path
  verify_single_mcp_owner
  verify_editor_pid
  verify_loaded_map
  verify_no_active_pie
  verify_dirty_package_policy
  write_session_manifest
  write_heartbeat
  release_lock
```

This is not bureaucracy. It is how we keep automation from writing to the wrong
editor or mutating assets during another tool's session.

#### 11.2.18 Additional accelerator candidates

These are not all immediate builds. They are good candidates because they sit
between existing bespoke tools and the production plan.

`EUW_AscentiaDependencyDoctor`

- Finds missing packages, deleted soft references, broken redirectors, and
  package dependencies like the missing animation compression settings issue.
- First slice: scan animation, character, and GASP folders for missing package
  dependencies before PIE.

`EUW_AscentiaRetargetBatchDoctor`

- Audits imported animation packs for skeleton, IK Rig, IK Retargeter, root
  motion, montage sections, compression settings, additive flags, and notify
  tracks.
- First slice: Essential Bow plus GASP animation dependency report.

`EUW_AscentiaRootMotionPolicyLab`

- Lets Designers compare extracted root path, capsule path, intent vector,
  floor projection, ledge behavior, stair behavior, cancel window, and movement
  mode.
- First slice: dodge roll and light attack root-motion profiles.

`EUW_AscentiaNiagaraVisibilityDoctor`

- Spawns GameplayCue/Niagara systems in a controlled preview map and reports
  activation, bounds, material visibility, culling, pooling, and scalability
  suppression.
- First slice: sword swing trail and impact cue visibility proof.

`EUW_AscentiaAudioNotifyAuditor`

- Cross-checks montage notify windows, MetaSound/SoundCue references,
  concurrency, attenuation, and current settings volumes.
- First slice: light attack sword-move sound restored and proven mid-swing.

`EUW_AscentiaInputIntentRecorder`

- Records move/look/dodge/attack intent vectors during PIE and overlays them
  against chosen motion requests.
- First slice: strafing plus BR/BL dodge direction evidence.

`EUW_AscentiaArenaScenarioSwitcher`

- Toggles placed bosses, encounter spawners, loot fixtures, music, weather, and
  debug actors through named scenario profiles.
- First slice: "PC alone," "placed boss only," "loot UI," and "combat smoke"
  arena modes without changing GameMode.

`EUW_AscentiaContentProvenanceBrowser`

- Shows where an asset came from: pack, generated tool, manual authoring, license
  note, source path, normalized destination, validators passed, and accepted
  evidence.
- First slice: GASP, Essential Bow, AssassinGirl, UDS, and first Asset Factory
  outputs.

`EUW_AscentiaDesignerTuningSurfaceAudit`

- Reports gameplay values that are only editable in C++ defaults or obscure
  Blueprint internals and proposes DataAsset/Details-panel surfacing.
- First slice: weapon socket offsets, dodge cancel timing, breath regen, attack
  costs, arena boss spawn policy.

`EUW_AscentiaThirdPartyPluginRiskPanel`

- Tracks plugin/version, enabled modules, runtime/editor dependency, shipping
  risk, source availability, Blueprint/C++ exposure, and replacement seam.
- First slice: UDS, GASP, Essential Bow, Mover/NetworkPrediction, CommonUI,
  PCG, and Python/MCP plugin status.

#### 11.2.19 Tool roadmap recommendation

Given the current project pain and future custom-character/open-world scope, the
next concrete tooling sprint should be:

1. `EUW_AscentiaBuildDoctor` plus `EUW_AscentiaDependencyDoctor`:
   catch missing packages, invalid bodies, broken cues, plugin drift, and asset
   dependency failures before PIE.
2. `EUW_AscentiaMotionInspector`, `EUW_AscentiaRootMotionPolicyLab`, and
   `EUW_AscentiaInputIntentRecorder`:
   make dodge, attack lock, ledge/stair behavior, and player intent visible.
3. `EUW_AscentiaNotifyWindowDoctor`, `EUW_AscentiaNiagaraVisibilityDoctor`, and
   `EUW_AscentiaAudioNotifyAuditor`:
   keep sword trails, swing audio, damage windows, cancel windows, and cues from
   vanishing during animation iteration.
4. `EUW_AscentiaWeaponMountLab` and `EUW_AscentiaDesignerTuningSurfaceAudit`:
   move socket/cost/cancel/tuning values into obvious Designer surfaces.
5. `EUW_AscentiaAtmosphereDirector` and UDS adapter:
   adopt UDS only through Ascentia profiles, screenshot proof, and material/
   weather response validation.
6. `EUW_AscentiaWorldDirector` and `EUW_AscentiaContentProvenanceBrowser`:
   bring Landscry, PCG, World Partition, POIs, Data Layers, generated assets,
   and source provenance into one visible production lane.

The first sprint should be boring and brutally useful: Build Doctor, Dependency
Doctor, Motion Inspector, Notify Window Doctor, Weapon Mount Lab. Those five
turn the current recurring "why did this break in PIE?" loop into visible,
actionable reports.

### 11.3 Validation tiers

1. Native `IsDataValid` on custom asset classes.
2. `UEditorValidatorBase` validators for cross-asset rules.
3. Commandlet validation in CI:
   `UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe Ascentia.uproject -run=DataValidation`
4. Custom content completeness reports.
5. Remote playtest scenarios for runtime behavior.

Each validator should produce messages Designers understand. "Invalid pointer"
is not acceptable. "CairnGreatsword has WeaponFamily Greatsword but no
Greatsword light attack montage set" is useful.

## 12. Designer Surface Catalog

### 12.1 Purpose

Designer-facing surfaces are not decoration. They are production architecture.
If Ascentia expects many weapons, enemies, bosses, zones, quests, settings,
items, abilities, and presentation variants, Designers need stable Editor
objects, clear categories, validation messages, preview tools, and safe
Blueprint extension points.

The rule:

```text
C++ owns invariants.
Data owns content.
Blueprint owns composition and presentation.
Editor tools own inspection, sync, preview, and repair.
```

When a feature lands without a Designer surface, mark it as native scaffold, not
production-ready.

### 12.2 UPROPERTY and UFUNCTION exposure rules

Use these as defaults:

```text
EditDefaultsOnly
  class defaults on Blueprint assets, DataAssets, and archetypes

EditAnywhere
  placed-world authoring, encounter zones, actors that Designers configure per instance

BlueprintReadOnly
  telemetry and authoritative state that Blueprints may display but not mutate

BlueprintReadWrite
  presentation-only or editor-only values where mutation is intended

BlueprintCallable
  safe command surfaces with validation and predictable side effects

BlueprintPure
  read-only queries, preview data, and view-model accessors

BlueprintImplementableEvent
  presentation hooks where C++ emits a fact and Blueprint chooses the look/sound

BlueprintAssignable
  UI/presentation delegates that multiple widgets or systems may listen to
```

Every exposed field needs:

- Category using `Ascentia|System|SubSystem`;
- Tooltip for non-obvious gameplay meaning;
- clamp metadata for numeric tuning;
- units metadata where relevant;
- soft reference filters where possible;
- stable names that read well in Details panels.

No Designer should have to know C++ member history to understand a Details panel.

### 12.3 Core folder and naming catalog

```text
/Game/Ascentia/Design/
  DataTables/
    DT_Items_*
    DT_WeaponTypes
    DT_Affixes
    DT_LootTables
    DT_Skills

```

Use Epic-style prefixes consistently, but Ascentia-specific semantic names
matter more than blindly mirroring samples. Searchability in the Content Browser
is the goal.

Each base should expose only safe tuning and presentation hooks. Native
subclasses own authority; Blueprint subclasses own content assembly.

Editor Utility Blueprints or scripted actions for one-shot repair tasks. Use
Blueprint Function Libraries for shared utility nodes.


Runtime UI should receive view models or events. It should not assemble gameplay
truth by crawling actors or parsing display text. Avoid tick-heavy bindings for
high-frequency UI. Use reusable User Widgets for repeated parts such as item
rows, stat lines, resource bars, and prompt rows.

### 12.9 GameplayCue and presentation catalog

```text
GameplayCue.Ascentia.Attack.Swing.*
GameplayCue.Ascentia.Attack.Impact.*
GameplayCue.Ascentia.Block.*
GameplayCue.Ascentia.Parry.*
GameplayCue.Ascentia.Dodge.*
GameplayCue.Ascentia.Status.*
GameplayCue.Ascentia.Spell.*
GameplayCue.Ascentia.Loot.*
GameplayCue.Ascentia.Boss.*
GameplayCue.Ascentia.World.*
```

GameplayCues are cosmetic and authorable. They should be covered by validation:
if a damage school, status payload, ability, boss move, or item says it emits a
cue, the cue class or cue route must exist.

### 12.10 Validation surface catalog

Each major system needs two outputs:

1. Machine-readable report under `Saved/Automation/Evidence/.../report.json`.
2. Designer-readable Editor panel or validation message.

```text
VLD_Ascentia_SupportBundleProfiles
VLD_Ascentia_ToolDescriptors
VLD_Ascentia_ToolEvidence
VLD_Ascentia_ToolRegistration
VLD_Ascentia_ToolRepairPolicies
VLD_Ascentia_BuildDoctorReport
VLD_Ascentia_BlueprintCompileState
VLD_Ascentia_PhysicsAssetBodies
VLD_Ascentia_GameplayCueReferences
VLD_Ascentia_PluginState
VLD_Ascentia_LogFailurePatterns
VLD_Ascentia_MissingPackages
VLD_Ascentia_BrokenSoftReferences
VLD_Ascentia_BrokenHardReferences
VLD_Ascentia_PluginContentReferences
VLD_Ascentia_MotionScenarioProfiles
VLD_Ascentia_DodgeDirectionSlots
VLD_Ascentia_RootMotionPolicyCoverage
VLD_Ascentia_StairFixtureResults
VLD_Ascentia_LedgeFixtureResults
VLD_Ascentia_MontageNotifyWindows
VLD_Ascentia_AudioNotifyCoverage
VLD_Ascentia_NiagaraNotifyCoverage
VLD_Ascentia_WeaponSocketProfiles
VLD_Ascentia_TrailSocketCoverage
VLD_Ascentia_ToolEvidenceRetention
VLD_Ascentia_MCPAutomationOwnership
VLD_Ascentia_RawProviderUsage
VLD_Ascentia_AutomationSessionPolicies
VLD_Ascentia_BridgeProviders
VLD_Ascentia_ContentProvenance
VLD_Ascentia_ThirdPartyPluginRisks
VLD_Ascentia_EditorScenarioProfiles
VLD_Ascentia_NetworkAuthority
VLD_Ascentia_NetworkScenarios
VLD_Ascentia_AbilityNetworkPolicies
VLD_Ascentia_MotionNetworkPolicies
VLD_Ascentia_MultiplayerLootPolicies
VLD_Ascentia_ExploitSensitivePaths
```

The validator should tell a Designer the fix, not merely the failure.

### 12.11 Blueprint obscurity rule

When a feature ships, ask:

- Can a Designer find the asset that owns it?
- Can a Designer tune the values without compiling?
- Can a Designer preview the result?
- Can a Designer see why validation failed?
- Can a Designer tell whether a value came from fallback, row, asset, or runtime
  instance?
- Can a Designer bind presentation without changing authority?
- Can a playtest prove the Blueprint surface still works?

If the answer is no, the feature is not finished. It may be a good native slice,
but it is not production-ready Ascentia.

### 12.12 Designer surface governance starter package

This chapter should not remain only a catalog. Ascentia needs a repeatable way
to prove that every native system has the right Designer surface, validation,
preview path, report output, and Blueprint boundary. Treat this as a
cross-phase governance package named `SURF`, because it applies to P11 through
P23 and every bespoke tool.

Reference posture:

- Use Editor Utility Widgets for dockable, report-driven workflows where
  Designers browse, compare, validate, preview, and open related assets.
- Use Scriptable Tools for interactive viewport placement, painting, measuring,
  road/POI layout, anchor placement, and any workflow where hit-testing and
  gizmos matter more than a docked form.
- Use Details panel metadata and customizations when a property needs better
  ordering, edit conditions, units, buttons, inline warnings, or structured
  Slate UI.
- Use Data Validation for asset correctness gates and commandlet/package
  checks. Validators should emit Designer-readable repair guidance and
  machine-readable report rows.
- Keep unsafe mutation behind dry-run, source-control checks, transactions, and
  evidence. A one-click repair without a preview report is a production risk.

Runtime/editor ownership split:

```text
Source/Ascentia/Public/DesignerSurface/AscentiaDesignerSurfaceTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/DesignerSurface/AscentiaDesignerSurfaceRegistry.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/DesignerSurface/AscentiaDesignerSurfaceReportTypes.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/DesignerSurface/AscentiaDesignerSurfaceAudit.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/DesignerSurface/AscentiaDesignerSurfaceValidation.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/DesignerSurface/AscentiaDetailsPanelPolicy.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/DesignerSurface/AscentiaDesignerSurfaceAudit.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/DesignerSurface/AscentiaDesignerSurfaceValidation.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/DesignerSurface/AscentiaDetailsPanelPolicy.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Commandlets/AscentiaDesignerSurfaceAuditCommandlet.cpp
```

Core assets:

```text
PDA_DesignerSurfaceRegistry_MVP
PDA_DesignerSurfacePolicy_Default
PDA_DesignerSurfacePhaseGate_P11
PDA_DesignerSurfacePhaseGate_P12
PDA_DesignerSurfacePhaseGate_P13
PDA_DesignerSurfacePhaseGate_P14
PDA_DesignerSurfacePhaseGate_P15
PDA_DesignerSurfacePhaseGate_P16
PDA_DesignerSurfacePhaseGate_P17
PDA_DesignerSurfacePhaseGate_P18
PDA_DesignerSurfacePhaseGate_P19
PDA_DesignerSurfacePhaseGate_P20
PDA_DesignerSurfacePhaseGate_P21
PDA_DesignerSurfacePhaseGate_P22
PDA_DesignerSurfacePhaseGate_P23
```

Surface registry row:

```text
FAscentiaDesignerSurfaceEntry
  SurfaceTag
  PhaseTag
  OwningSystemTag
  SurfaceKind
  AssetPath
  NativeClass
  BlueprintBase
  DataAssetType
  DataTableType
  EditorWidget
  ScriptableTool
  ValidatorName
  EvidencePath
  DashboardCardTag

  MutatesAssets
  SupportsDryRun
  SupportsUndoTransaction
  SupportsSourceControlCheck
  DesignerOwner
  EngineeringOwner
  StatusTag
```

Surface kinds:

```text
Ascentia.Surface.DataAsset
Ascentia.Surface.DataTable
Ascentia.Surface.BlueprintBase
Ascentia.Surface.EditorUtilityWidget
Ascentia.Surface.ScriptableTool
Ascentia.Surface.DetailsPanel
Ascentia.Surface.RuntimeWidget
Ascentia.Surface.Validator
Ascentia.Surface.Commandlet
Ascentia.Surface.Report
Ascentia.Surface.DashboardCard
```

Coverage report:

```text
FAscentiaDesignerSurfaceCoverageReport
  RunId
  PhaseTag
  SurfacesExpected
  SurfacesFound
  MissingAssets
  MissingValidators
  MissingEvidenceReports
  MissingPreviewTools
  MissingBlueprintBases
  NativeOnlyFeatures
  UnsafeMutationTools
  BadDetailsMetadata
  StaleCatalogEntries
  NewlyDiscoveredUnregisteredAssets
  BlockingFailures
  Warnings
```

Every phase package should have a coverage row. Example:

```text
P11 Combat Motion
  Required surfaces:
    PDA_CombatMotionProfile_*
    PDA_DodgeSet_*
    EUW_AscentiaMotionInspector
    EUW_AscentiaRootMotionPolicyLab
    VLD_Ascentia_CombatMotion
    Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P11/CombatMotion/{RunId}/report.json
  Blocking native-only features:
    dodge direction mapping hidden only in C++
    root-motion fallback hidden only in logs
```

Details panel policy:

```text
FAscentiaDetailsPanelFieldPolicy
  FieldName
  Category
  TooltipRequired
  Units
  ClampMin
  ClampMax
  EditCondition
  AllowedClasses
  AssetBundles
  DesignerFacingName
  MutabilityTag
  ValidationRuleTag
```

Rules:

- Designer-facing numeric fields need units or range hints.
- Motion, damage, economy, and performance values need clamps.
- Soft object/class references need allowed-class metadata where possible.
- Fields with side effects need clear mutability tags and validation warnings.
- Prototype-only fields must say so in category or tooltip.
- Runtime telemetry should be `BlueprintReadOnly` unless mutation is explicitly
  part of the surface.
- Editor-only repair actions must use transactions and source-control checks.

EUW governance:

```text
EUW_AscentiaDesignerSurfaceAudit
  Loads the surface registry, scans assets/classes, compares expected vs found
  surfaces, opens missing asset paths, and writes a coverage report.

EUW_AscentiaDesignerTuningSurfaceAudit
  Finds reflected properties in native classes/Blueprints that are likely
  Designer-facing but lack category, tooltip, units, clamp metadata, validation,
  or DataAsset surfacing.

EUW_AscentiaSurfacePhaseGate
  Shows phase readiness by surface: native class, DataAsset/DataTable,
  Blueprint base, EUW, validator, report, dashboard card, and latest evidence.

EUW_AscentiaDetailsPanelPolicyPreview
  Opens selected classes/assets and displays Details metadata policy failures
  without requiring Designers to read C++ headers.
```

Commandlet:

```text
AscentiaDesignerSurfaceAudit
  -Registry=/Game/Ascentia/Design/Tools/PDA_DesignerSurfaceRegistry_MVP
  -Phase=P11
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/DesignerSurfaces
  -FailOnNativeOnlyPhaseGate
```

Validation:

```text
VLD_Ascentia_DesignerSurfaceRegistry
VLD_Ascentia_DesignerSurfaceCoverage
VLD_Ascentia_DetailsMetadata
VLD_Ascentia_EditorUtilityWidgets
VLD_Ascentia_ScriptableTools
VLD_Ascentia_UnsafeEditorMutation
VLD_Ascentia_NativeOnlyPhaseFeatures
```

Machine report path:

```text
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/DesignerSurfaces/{RunId}/coverage_report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/DesignerSurfaces/{RunId}/details_metadata.csv
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/DesignerSurfaces/{RunId}/native_only_features.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/DesignerSurfaces/{RunId}/unsafe_mutation_tools.json
```

Proof matrix:

```text
DesignerSurface_P11_CombatMotion_Coverage
DesignerSurface_P14_WeaponBreadth_Coverage
DesignerSurface_P17_WorldZone_Coverage
DesignerSurface_P18_AI_Coverage
DesignerSurface_P19_SaveIdentity_Coverage
DesignerSurface_P20_Presentation_Coverage
DesignerSurface_P23_ContentData_Coverage
DetailsMetadata_NoUncategorizedDesignerFields
EditorMutation_AllRepairToolsHaveDryRun
NativeOnly_NoPhaseGateBlockingFeatures
```

SURF starter tickets:

- SURF-001: Add `PDA_DesignerSurfaceRegistry_MVP`, surface entry structs, and
  first registry rows for P11, P14, P17, P18, P19, P20, and P23.
- SURF-002: Add `AscentiaDesignerSurfaceAudit` commandlet and coverage report
  writer.
- SURF-003: Add `EUW_AscentiaDesignerSurfaceAudit` and phase-gate view.
- SURF-004: Add Details metadata policy scan for native classes and Blueprint
  Class Defaults that are marked Designer-facing.
- SURF-005: Add unsafe mutation audit for EUWs, Scriptable Tools, Python tools,
  and repair buttons.
- SURF-006: Add validators for registry coverage, missing assets, missing
  validators, missing reports, native-only features, and stale catalog entries.
- SURF-007: Add dashboard card output showing Designer-surface coverage by
  phase.
- SURF-008: Add CI/package gate mode that fails only on phase-gate surfaces,
  while reporting future-phase gaps as warnings.

Recommended implementation order:

1. SURF-001 and SURF-002 first. The registry and report make the catalog
   actionable.
2. SURF-003 then gives Designers and Technical Designers a navigable view.
3. SURF-004 catches the "buried in C++ obscurity" failure mode directly.
4. SURF-005 protects the growing tool ecosystem from unsafe repair buttons.
5. SURF-006 through SURF-008 connect the surface report to validation,
   dashboard, and package gates.

## 13. Progression, Save, Death Loop, and Character Identity

### 13.1 Player-facing goal

P19 is not "add a save file" or "make death respawn." It is the phase where the
player's character becomes a long-term identity that can survive:

- character creation;
- sessions and patch versions;
- death and recovery;
- respec;
- world cycle changes;
- future class/origin changes;
- eventual platform/user save rules;
- eventual multiplayer discipline.

The player promise is simple:

```text
My character is mine.
The game remembers what should matter.
Death has drama without corrupting that memory.
Respec and NG+ are explicit choices, not accidental side effects.
```

Everything in this chapter exists to protect that promise.

### 13.2 Current Ascentia state

The MVP already has a useful native progression/save slice:

- `UAscentiaProgressionComponent` tracks:
  - `CharacterLevel`;
  - `Stones`;
  - `UnspentStatPoints`;
  - per-primary-stat investment counts;
  - stat investment/refund telemetry;
  - threshold passive tags and tier telemetry.
- `UAscentiaSaveGame` currently stores:
  - `SaveVersion`;
  - `PlayerDisplayName`;
  - `SavedAtUtc`;
  - level, stones, and unspent points;
  - inventory item stacks;
  - equipment entries;
  - world tier state;
  - map discovery state;
  - completed POI tags and resettable loot claims.
- `UAscentiaSaveSubsystem` can:
  - build a save object from a character;
  - apply a save object to a character;
  - expose three MVP slot summaries;
  - save and load through `UGameplayStatics::SaveGameToSlot` and
    `LoadGameFromSlot`.
- `AAscentiaCampsiteActor` currently:
  - restores health, Breath, Eth, and potion charges;
  - saves the active slot;
  - asks the game mode to reset the MVP encounter.
- `AAscentiaGameMode` handles delayed player respawn and re-applies active save
  data when the active slot exists.

The current gaps are important:

- per-stat investment counts are native but not yet persisted;
- active threshold passive state is derivable but the source facts that produce
  it must be saved;
- there is no stable character GUID;
- there is no explicit character creation identity;
- there is no appearance/custom-character save payload;
- there is no origin/class/archetype data contract beyond a placeholder
  `ArchetypeTag`;
- there is no death penalty state;
- there is no corpse-run or recoverable-currency state;
- there is no last-safe respawn anchor contract;
- there is no save migration path;
- there is no per-map/player transform header;
- there is no async save/load pipeline;
- there is no save compatibility validation report;
- placed-world actor state is not yet saved through the stable world-actor
  identity planned in section 8.8.

That means the current save is an MVP checkpoint, not a production character
identity system.

### 13.3 Production save principles

P19 must adopt these rules before content volume grows:

1. Save source facts, not display output.
2. Save stable identifiers, not actor pointers.
3. Save versioned structs, not loose fields spread across code.
4. Save local-player identity and slot identity together.
5. Save enough header data to render a slot without loading the whole world.
6. Derive transient state on load.
7. Migrate old saves deliberately.
8. Treat save/load as asynchronous at production scale.
9. Treat death as a state transition, not a save corruption event.
10. Treat character creation as identity data plus presentation data, not as
    "the pawn class."

The distinction between source facts and derived state matters. For example,
active threshold passive tags can be recomputed from invested stat counts and
passive rules. The save should persist invested stat counts and the passive-rule
version; load should recompute the active tags and report migration differences.

### 13.4 Save schema target

P19 should replace the flat MVP save with explicit nested structs:

```text
UAscentiaSaveGame
  Header: FAscentiaSaveHeader
  CharacterIdentity: FAscentiaCharacterIdentityState
  CharacterProgression: FAscentiaCharacterProgressionState
  CharacterAppearance: FAscentiaCharacterAppearanceState
  Inventory: FAscentiaInventorySaveState
  Equipment: FAscentiaEquipmentSaveState
  Resources: FAscentiaResourceSaveState
  World: FAscentiaWorldSaveState
  Death: FAscentiaDeathLoopState
  Cycle: FAscentiaWorldCycleState
  SettingsSnapshot: optional compatibility metadata only
```

Header:

```text
FAscentiaSaveHeader
  SaveVersion
  BuildVersion
  CreatedAtUtc
  SavedAtUtc
  SlotName
  SlotIndex
  LocalUserIndex
  CharacterGuid
  CharacterDisplayName
  CharacterLevel
  PlayTimeSeconds
  MapAssetTag
  WorldZoneTag
  LastSafeAnchorTag
  LastKnownTransform
  HighestUnlockedWorldTier
  bHasRecoverableDeathState
  SummaryRightHandItemTag
  SummaryIconOrThumbnail
```

Character identity:

```text
FAscentiaCharacterIdentityState
  CharacterGuid
  DisplayName
  OriginTag
  ClassTag
  ArchetypeTag
  BodyPresetTag
  VoiceTag
  PronounTag
  CreationVersion
  CreatedAtUtc
```

Appearance:

```text
FAscentiaCharacterAppearanceState
  BodyPresetTag
  HeadPresetTag
  HairPresetTag
  SkinToneId
  HairColorId
  EyeColorId
  MorphSliderValues
  CosmeticEquipmentOverrides
```

This is where the placeholder AssassinGirl decision connects to the future.
AssassinGirl should remain an avatar preset or temporary visual override. The
save contract should never assume that a production character is "the
AssassinGirl pawn." The pawn is a runtime vehicle; the character identity is
data.

Progression:

```text
FAscentiaCharacterProgressionState
  CharacterLevel
  Stones
  UnspentStatPoints
  InvestedConstitution
  InvestedEndurance
  InvestedFocus
  InvestedStrength
  InvestedAgility
  InvestedIntelligence
  PassiveRuleVersion
  ExplicitUnlockedPassiveTags
  KnownAbilitySetIds
  KnownSpellIds
  RespecCount
  LastRespecAtUtc
```

`ExplicitUnlockedPassiveTags` should be used only for passives that are not
derivable from stat thresholds. Threshold passives should be recomputed from
invested stats on load.

World:

```text
FAscentiaWorldSaveState
  WorldTierState
  MapDiscoveryState
  CompletedPOIs
  ResettableLootClaims
  WorldActorStates
  ObjectiveState
  FastTravelState
  CampsiteState
```

`WorldActorStates` should use the stable identity pattern from section 8.8:

```text
WorldActorId
ActorClassTag
WorldZoneTag
DataLayerTag
SavePolicy
PayloadVersion
PayloadBytesOrStruct
```

No save payload should contain raw object pointers, display names as identity,
or location-only identity for placed actors.

Save schema profile:

```text
PDA_SaveSchemaProfile
  CurrentSaveVersion
  MinimumReadableVersion
  SlotHeaderPolicy
  MigrationChain
  SaveFragmentPolicies
  DeprecatedFieldPolicies
  ContentRedirectManifest
  ValidationProfile
```

Save fragment header:

```text
FAscentiaSaveFragmentHeader
  FragmentTag
  FragmentVersion
  OwnerSystemTag
  ContentSchemaVersion
  CreatedByBuildVersion
  PayloadHash
```

Save validation result:

```text
FAscentiaSaveValidationResult
  SlotName
  SaveVersion
  CharacterGuid
  HeaderValid
  FragmentResults
  MissingContentTags
  DeprecatedContentTags
  DefaultedFields
  BlockingErrors
  RecoverableWarnings
```

Slot summary:

```text
FAscentiaSaveSlotSummary
  SlotName
  SlotIndex
  LocalUserIndex
  CharacterGuid
  CharacterDisplayName
  OriginTag
  ClassTag
  CharacterLevel
  PlayTimeSeconds
  SavedAtUtc
  MapAssetTag
  WorldZoneTag
  SummaryEquipmentTags
  bHasRecoverableDeathState
  bRequiresMigration
  bIsCorruptOrUnreadable
```

Slot summaries are UI-facing and cheap to load. The full save payload is
gameplay-facing and may require migration, validation, content resolution, and
world load readiness.

### 13.5 Save ownership and lifecycle

Production save ownership should be:

```text
UAscentiaSaveSubsystem
  owns save slot discovery, async IO, schema migration, save validation

UAscentiaCharacterSaveFacade
  gathers character-owned save fragments from PlayerState/components

UAscentiaWorldSaveFacade
  gathers world-owned save fragments from subsystems and saveable actors

UAscentiaSaveGame
  stores data only; no gameplay logic
```

At save time:

1. Pause save-sensitive mutation or take a snapshot lock.
2. Gather character identity/progression/inventory/equipment/resource state.
3. Gather world state from world subsystems and saveable actor components.
4. Validate the in-memory save object.
5. Write through async save for production paths.
6. Emit slot header cache and telemetry.
7. Notify UI only after success/failure is known.

At load time:

1. Load the slot by explicit slot and local user.
2. Validate `SaveVersion`.
3. Migrate through every intermediate version.
4. Validate migrated data.
5. Spawn or possess the correct runtime pawn class for the current build.
6. Apply identity/appearance before presentation begins.
7. Apply progression/inventory/equipment.
8. Recompute derived attributes, passives, abilities, and HUD view models.
9. Apply world state after the target map/zone is loaded.
10. Restore resources according to load reason.

Load reason matters:

```text
EAscentiaLoadReason
  MainMenuLoad
  CampsiteRest
  PlayerDeathRespawn
  SeamlessTravel
  DebugCommand
  Automation
```

A death respawn may restore resources and place the player at an anchor while
preserving a recoverable death state. A main-menu load should restore the exact
last persistent state. A debug load may skip presentation.

Async request/result:

```text
FAscentiaSaveRequest
  RequestId
  SlotName
  LocalUserIndex
  SaveReason
  SavePolicyTag
  bWriteSlotSummary
  bValidateBeforeWrite
  bBlockSaveSensitiveMutation

FAscentiaSaveResult
  RequestId
  SlotName
  bSucceeded
  FailureReasonTag
  ValidationResult
  WroteSlotSummary
  DurationMs
  EvidenceId

FAscentiaLoadRequest
  RequestId
  SlotName
  LocalUserIndex
  LoadReason
  TargetMapPolicy
  bAllowMigration
  bValidateAfterMigration

FAscentiaLoadResult
  RequestId
  SlotName
  bSucceeded
  FailureReasonTag
  LoadedSaveVersion
  MigratedToVersion
  ValidationResult
  AppliedFragments
  DurationMs
  EvidenceId
```

Dirty-state routing:

```text
FAscentiaSaveDirtyEvent
  DirtyTag
  SourceSystemTag
  SourceObjectGuidOrActorId
  SaveReasonHint
  RequiresImmediateWrite
  CoalescingWindowSeconds
```

Rules:

- inventory, equipment, death state, world-state changes, and character creation
  all emit dirty events instead of calling save directly;
- campsites and shell UI request saves through `UAscentiaSaveSubsystem`;
- autosave policy should be data-authored and visible in `EUW_AscentiaSaveLab`;
- save-sensitive transactions should either finish before snapshot or be
  rejected while a snapshot lock is active;
- load failure needs a user-facing error path owned by P21 shell, but P19 owns
  the failure tags and validation details.

### 13.6 Death loop contract

Ascentia should make the P19 death-loop choice explicit. The recommended default
for the current Soulslike/ARPG direction is:

```text
One recoverable dropped-currency state at a time.
Death drops recoverable Stones at the death location or a nearby safe proxy.
A second unrecovered death consumes the previous recoverable state.
Permanent progression, inventory, map discovery, and world unlocks remain.
Respawn returns to the last safe anchor.
```

Death state:

```text
FAscentiaDeathLoopState
  bHasRecoverableDeathState
  LostCurrencyAmount
  CurrencyTag
  DeathWorldActorId
  DeathMapAssetTag
  DeathWorldZoneTag
  DeathTransform
  RecoveryProxyTransform
  LastSafeAnchorTag
  KillerTag
  DeathTimeUtc
  RecoveryPolicyTag
  DeathSequenceNumber
```

Runtime actors:

```text
BP_AscentiaDeathEcho
  visual marker / interactable pickup / minimap ping source

UAscentiaDeathLoopSubsystem
  creates, saves, restores, replaces, and consumes death echo state

PDA_DeathPenaltyProfile_*
  authoring asset for dropped resource, percentage, caps, boss-room behavior,
  unreachable-location repair, and UI/audio presentation
```

Death lifecycle:

1. Combat determines death through health/attribute authority.
2. Pawn latches death once.
3. Gameplay input is blocked.
4. Death presentation begins.
5. Death loop subsystem calculates penalty.
6. Existing unrecovered death echo is resolved according to policy.
7. New death state is saved before respawn if the design requires persistence
   through quit.
8. Player respawns at the last safe anchor.
9. Campsite/anchor restore policy restores health/resources.
10. World encounter reset policy runs.
11. UI tells the player what was lost and where recovery is.

Recovery lifecycle:

1. Player reaches the death echo.
2. Interact or proximity policy validates ownership.
3. Stones are restored.
4. Death state is cleared.
5. Save is updated.
6. Presentation confirms recovery.

Unreachable death protection:

- if death happened in a kill volume, deep fall, boss transition, streamed-out
  proxy, or nav-invalid area, place the echo at a recovery proxy:
  - last valid floor;
  - boss fog entrance;
  - last safe anchor;
  - nearest authored `BP_AscentiaDeathEchoProxy`.

This must be deterministic and logged. "The player lost currency because the
marker spawned under the world" is a production failure, not a tuning issue.

Death echo placement:

```text
FAscentiaDeathEchoPlacementRequest
  CharacterGuid
  DeathTransform
  DeathMapAssetTag
  DeathWorldZoneTag
  LastSafeAnchorTag
  KillerTag
  DeathCauseTag
  BossArenaTag
  NavProjectionPolicy
  ProxyPolicy

FAscentiaDeathEchoPlacementResult
  PlacementTransform
  PlacementPolicyTag
  UsedProxyActorId
  UsedLastSafeAnchor
  NavProjectionSucceeded
  StreamingSafe
  FailureReasonTag
  DebugTrace
```

Death-loop report:

```text
FAscentiaDeathLoopReport
  RunId
  ScenarioTag
  DeathStateBefore
  PlacementResult
  RespawnAnchor
  EncounterResetResults
  RecoveryResult
  SecondDeathResult
  SaveLoadResult
  Errors[]
  Warnings[]
```

`EUW_AscentiaDeathLoopSimulator` should let Designers drop sample death points
in a map and preview where the echo will land. This catches ledge, stair, boss
fog, streamed-out, and kill-volume problems before a playtest loses currency in
an unfair place.

### 13.7 Respec contract

Respec is a progression system, not a debug refund button.

Data:

```text
PDA_RespecProfile_*
  CurrencyTag
  BaseCost
  CostCurveByLevel
  CostCurveByRespecCount
  MinimumAnchorTag
  AllowedStats
  bRefundThresholdPassives
  bRefundSkillUnlocks
  bRequireConfirmation
```

UI:

```text
WBP_StatAllocationScreen
  current values
  preview values
  derived stat deltas
  passive breakpoints gained/lost
  cost
  confirmation
```

Native flow:

1. Build a preview transaction.
2. Validate currency and rules.
3. Show derived deltas and passive changes.
4. Confirm.
5. Spend currency.
6. Apply stat changes.
7. Recompute attributes/passives/ability grants.
8. Save source facts.
9. Emit telemetry and presentation.

Do not let UI call six independent stat mutations and hope the result is valid.
Use a transaction object:

```text
FAscentiaStatRespecTransaction
  RequestedRefunds
  RequestedInvestments
  Cost
  ResultingInvestedStats
  ResultingUnspentPoints
  GainedPassiveTags
  LostPassiveTags
  ValidationErrors
```

Respec preview result:

```text
FAscentiaRespecPreviewResult
  ProfileTag
  RequestedTransaction
  CurrentStats
  PreviewStats
  CurrentDerivedAttributes
  PreviewDerivedAttributes
  GainedPassiveTags
  LostPassiveTags
  RemovedAbilityTags
  GrantedAbilityTags
  CostBreakdown
  FailureReasons
  ConfirmationPolicy
```

Respec report:

```text
FAscentiaRespecReport
  RunId
  ProfileTag
  ScenarioTag
  PreviewResult
  CommitResult
  SaveResult
  ReloadVerification
  Errors[]
  Warnings[]
```

`EUW_AscentiaRespecPlanner` should share math with the P13 build planner. Respec
is where build math, item requirements, stat thresholds, ability grants, and
save facts meet; if it is only a stat refund screen, it will break the first
time a passive or item requirement has consequences.

### 13.8 Character creation contract

Character creation must create data before it creates a pawn.

Data:

```text
PDA_Origin_*
  OriginTag
  DisplayName
  DescriptionTextKey
  StartingStatAdjustments
  StartingItemSetId
  StartingAbilitySetId
  IntroSpawnTag
  TutorialFlags

PDA_ClassArchetype_*
  ClassTag
  DisplayName
  StartingAttributeSpread
  StartingAbilitySetId
  RecommendedWeaponTags
  PresentationPoseSet

PDA_AvatarPreset_*
  BodyPresetTag
  SkeletalMesh
  AnimRetargetProfile
  GroomOptions
  DefaultMaterials
  PhysicsAsset
```

Creation flow:

1. Player selects origin/class/appearance.
2. UI creates a preview-only `FAscentiaCharacterCreationDraft`.
3. Validation checks required tags, assets, and starting inventory.
4. Confirm creates a `CharacterGuid`.
5. Save slot is initialized with identity, appearance, progression, inventory,
   and starting world state.
6. The runtime pawn is spawned from current platform/game-mode rules.
7. Appearance is applied from saved identity.

The custom character creator should not author gameplay by swapping pawn
classes. It should author a character identity that the current player pawn can
render and that future multiplayer can replicate safely.

Creation validation:

```text
PDA_CharacterCreationRules
  AllowedOriginTags
  AllowedClassTags
  AllowedAvatarPresetTags
  NameValidationPolicy
  StartingWorldStateProfile
  StartingInventoryPolicy
  StartingAbilityPolicy
  SaveSlotPolicy
  PreviewMap
```

Starting loadout:

```text
PDA_StartingLoadoutProfile
  LoadoutTag
  OriginTags
  ClassTags
  StartingItems
  StartingEquipment
  StartingAbilities
  StartingSpells
  StartingCurrencies
  TutorialFlags
```

Character creation validation result:

```text
FAscentiaCharacterCreationValidationResult
  Draft
  NameResult
  OriginResult
  ClassResult
  AvatarResult
  StartingLoadoutResult
  SaveSlotResult
  BlockingErrors
  Warnings
```

Creator report:

```text
FAscentiaCharacterCreationReport
  RunId
  DraftsTested
  SuccessfulCommits
  FailedCommits
  OriginCoverage
  ClassCoverage
  AvatarCoverage
  StartingLoadoutCoverage
  SaveReloadResults
  Errors[]
  Warnings[]
```

The creator should be able to create a valid character while the current runtime
pawn class remains unchanged. That is the practical test that the system has
escaped the placeholder pawn trap.

### 13.9 NG+ and world cycle contract

NG+ is a world-cycle transformation over a stable character.

Data:

```text
FAscentiaWorldCycleState
  CycleIndex
  CycleProfileTag
  StartedAtUtc
  RetainedProgressionTags
  ResetWorldStateTags
  ScalingProfileTag

PDA_WorldCycleProfile_*
  EnemyScalingCurve
  LootScalingCurve
  RetainedStateRules
  ResetStateRules
  NewRewards
  NewEnemyRules
```

Rules:

- character identity persists;
- level/stats/inventory policy is explicit;
- map discovery policy is explicit;
- boss/POI reset policy is explicit;
- unlocks that affect build identity should be retained unless design says
  otherwise;
- world-state resets should be tag-driven and auditable.

Verification needs a cycle transition test, not just a level-scaling unit test.

Cycle transition:

```text
FAscentiaWorldCycleTransitionRequest
  CharacterGuid
  CurrentCycleIndex
  TargetCycleProfileTag
  ConfirmedByPlayer
  RetainOverrideTags
  ResetOverrideTags

FAscentiaWorldCycleTransitionResult
  NewCycleState
  RetainedStateTags
  ResetStateTags
  ResetBossTags
  ResetPOITags
  NewRewardsGranted
  SaveResult
  FailureReasonTag
```

Cycle report:

```text
FAscentiaWorldCycleReport
  RunId
  CycleProfileTag
  RetainResetMatrix
  BossResetResults
  POIResetResults
  LootScalingResults
  EnemyScalingResults
  SaveReloadResults
  Errors[]
  Warnings[]
```

Do not implement NG+ until the first zone, boss roster, and reward economy are
stable enough to make reset/retain rules meaningful. The data contract can
exist early; the feature should not quietly become a debug map reset button.

### 13.10 Designer surfaces

P19 needs these Editor surfaces:

```text
PDA_DeathPenaltyProfile_*
PDA_DeathEchoPlacementProfile_*
PDA_RespecProfile_*
PDA_Origin_*
PDA_ClassArchetype_*
PDA_AvatarPreset_*
PDA_CharacterCreationRules_*
PDA_StartingLoadoutProfile_*
PDA_SaveSchemaProfile_*
PDA_SaveValidationProfile_*
PDA_SaveMigrationProfile_*
PDA_WorldCycleProfile_*
BP_AscentiaCampsite
BP_AscentiaDeathEcho
BP_AscentiaDeathEchoProxy
WBP_CharacterCreation
WBP_StatAllocation
WBP_DeathRecoveryNotice
WBP_SaveSlotScreen
WBP_NGPlusPrompt
EUW_AscentiaSaveInspector
EUW_AscentiaSaveLab

EUW_AscentiaProgressionBalancePreview
EUW_AscentiaCharacterCreationValidator
EUW_AscentiaCharacterCreatorLab
EUW_AscentiaAvatarAssemblyPreview
EUW_AscentiaDeathEchoPlacementPreview
EUW_AscentiaDeathLoopSimulator
EUW_AscentiaRespecPlanner
EUW_AscentiaWorldCyclePreview
```

Designer-facing validation examples:

- "Origin.GutterSaint grants Item.AshDagger, but that item is missing from the
  item registry."
- "AvatarPreset.AssassinGirlPlaceholder has no compatible physics asset for hit
  reactions."
- "RespecProfile.CampsiteRespec references Currency.Stones but has no maximum
  cost cap."
- "SaveVersion 3 migration does not populate InvestedStrength."
- "DeathPenaltyProfile.Soulslike requires a death echo class but none is set."
- "StartingLoadout.Rogue grants Item.AshDagger but the item is not allowed for
  the selected class."
- "SaveSchemaProfile.Current has no migration path from the earliest supported
  version."

### 13.11 Modular character and avatar architecture

The production character creator should not grow out of the current
VisualOverride costume arrangement. That setup is acceptable as a migration
bridge, but the production model needs a character identity layer, an avatar
assembly layer, and a runtime pawn layer.

```text
Character identity
  -> saved origin/class/appearance/build identity

Avatar assembly
  -> modular body parts, materials, grooms, physics, animation compatibility

Runtime pawn
  -> movement, GAS, combat, camera, inventory, interaction, replication
```

Unreal's modular-character options imply three Ascentia tiers:

- Leader Pose Component: quickest path for interchangeable body/clothing pieces,
  good for the first creator because the main body drives animation;
- Copy Pose from Mesh: useful when a part needs its own physics or animation
  behavior, but heavier;
- Skeletal Mesh Merge: useful for optimized final assembled characters, but
  costs setup complexity and limits morph-target flexibility.

Ascentia recommendation:

1. Use a leader-pose modular Blueprint for creator iteration and armor/body part
   swaps.
2. Keep Copy Pose available for hair, cloth, physics-heavy parts, or exceptional
   armor.
3. Add a mesh-merge bake path later for performance-critical crowds, companions,
   or final player-body optimization.

Data:

```text
PDA_AvatarPreset
  AvatarPresetTag
  DisplayName
  BaseSkeleton
  LeaderMesh
  DefaultBodyPartSet
  DefaultMaterialPalette
  AnimRetargetProfile
  PhysicsProfile
  CollisionProfile
  CameraProbePolicy
  PreviewPoseSet
  ValidationNotes

PDA_ModularBodyPartSet
  BodySetTag
  RequiredSkeleton
  HeadMeshes
  TorsoMeshes
  ArmMeshes
  LegMeshes
  HandMeshes
  FootMeshes
  HairGrooms
  ClothPieces
  CompatibleMaterialPalettes
  ExclusionRules

PDA_AppearanceMaterialPalette
  PaletteTag
  SkinToneOptions
  HairColorOptions
  EyeColorOptions
  TattooOptions
  ScarOptions
  MaterialParameterSets
```

Runtime state:

```text
FAscentiaAvatarAssemblyState
  AvatarPresetTag
  BodyPartSelections
  MaterialPaletteTag
  ScalarMorphValues
  ColorSelections
  CosmeticOverrides
  EquipmentVisualPolicy
```

Assembly request/result:

```text
FAscentiaAvatarAssemblyRequest
  CharacterGuid
  AvatarPresetTag
  BodyPartSelections
  MaterialPaletteTag
  MorphValues
  EquipmentVisuals
  PreviewOrRuntime
  PerformancePolicy

FAscentiaAvatarAssemblyResult
  AssembledComponents
  LeaderPoseAssignments
  CopyPoseAssignments
  MeshMergeCandidate
  AppliedMaterials
  AppliedMorphs
  PhysicsAssets
  SocketCompatibility
  CameraProbeResult
  Errors[]
  Warnings[]
```

Compatibility report:

```text
FAscentiaAvatarCompatibilityReport
  RunId
  AvatarPresetTag
  BodyPartSetTag
  SkeletonMatch
  RetargetProfileMatch
  PhysicsCompatibility
  SocketCompatibility
  EquipmentOverlayResults
  AnimationPreviewResults
  CameraProbeFindings
  PerformanceEstimate
```

Designer rules:

- AssassinGirl becomes `PDA_AvatarPreset_AssassinGirlPlaceholder`, not a hidden
  hard dependency.
- Every body part declares skeleton, physics, socket, material, and animation
  compatibility.
- Character creator preview uses the same assembly path as runtime.
- Save data stores tags and parameter values, not direct mesh pointers.
- Equipment visuals and body customization must share the same socket/profile
  vocabulary used by P14 weapon and armor presentation.
- `EUW_AscentiaAvatarAssemblyPreview` should test the same avatar against idle,
  locomotion, dodge, light attack, spell cast, death, revive, inventory preview,
  and dialogue pose sets.
- Avatar assembly reports should flag invalid physics bodies, missing sockets,
  camera intersection risk, retarget mismatch, and body-part material parameter
  drift.

### 13.12 Character creation authoring flow

Character creation should be authored as a transaction with validation.

Draft state:

```text
FAscentiaCharacterCreationDraft
  DisplayName
  OriginTag
  ClassTag
  AvatarAssemblyState
  StartingStatPreview
  StartingInventoryPreview
  StartingAbilityPreview
  ValidationErrors
```

Confirm transaction:

```text
FAscentiaCharacterCreationCommit
  NewCharacterGuid
  SlotName
  IdentityState
  AppearanceState
  ProgressionState
  InventoryState
  EquipmentState
  WorldStartState
  CreatedAtUtc
```

Creation UI:

```text
WBP_AscentiaCharacterCreation
  -> WBP_AscentiaOriginSelect
  -> WBP_AscentiaClassSelect
  -> WBP_AscentiaAppearanceEditor
  -> WBP_AscentiaStartingLoadoutPreview
  -> WBP_AscentiaCreationConfirm
```

Editor tooling:

- `EUW_AscentiaCharacterCreatorLab` previews origins, classes, avatar presets,
  starting loadouts, and save payload.
- `EUW_AscentiaAvatarAssemblyPreview` tests body-part combinations and equipment
  overlays.
- `EUW_AscentiaAppearancePresetValidator` catches missing skeleton, invalid
  material parameter, missing groom binding, physics asset mismatch, and camera
  probe occlusion risk.

Proof must include both Designer-preview and runtime-pawn paths. A creator that
only renders in UI but spawns the wrong pawn is not done.

Authoring report:

```text
FAscentiaCharacterCreatorAuthoringReport
  RunId
  OriginRowsChecked
  ClassRowsChecked
  AvatarPresetsChecked
  StartingLoadoutsChecked
  AppearanceOptionsChecked
  DraftValidationResults
  CommitResults
  SaveReloadResults
  RuntimeSpawnResults
  Errors[]
  Warnings[]
```

Evidence path:

```text
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P19/CharacterCreation/{RunId}/report.json
```

### 13.13 Save migration and old-save fixtures

Production save work is incomplete without migration fixtures.

Migration data:

```text
FAscentiaSaveMigrationStep
  FromVersion
  ToVersion
  MigrationTag
  Description
  RequiredBeforeTags
  DeprecatedFields
  AddedFields
  DefaultingPolicy
  ValidationPolicy
```

Fixture folder:

```text
Tools/save_fixtures/
  v1_mvp_basic.sav.json
  v2_stats_invested.sav.json
  v3_character_identity.sav.json
  v4_death_echo.sav.json
```

Report:

```text
Saved/Automation/Evidence/save_migration/<timestamp>/report.json
  FixturesLoaded
  FixturesMigrated
  VersionPath
  DefaultedFields
  DeprecatedFieldsRemoved
  ValidationErrors
  RoundTripHash
```

Rules:

- every save version change adds at least one fixture;
- migration must be stepwise, not a pile of "if old version then patch many
  things";
- missing fields get explicit defaulting policy;
- removed content tags use the content migration manifest from section 23;
- slot headers are regenerated after migration;
- migration failures show a user-facing recovery/error path, not only a log.

Migration commandlet/report:

```text
UAscentiaSaveMigrationCommandlet
  InputFixturePath
  OutputReportPath
  TargetVersion
  bRoundTrip
  bWriteMigratedFixture

FAscentiaSaveMigrationReport
  RunId
  TargetVersion
  FixturesLoaded
  FixturesMigrated
  FailedFixtures
  MigrationSteps
  DefaultedFields
  DeprecatedFieldsRemoved
  ContentRedirectsApplied
  RoundTripHashes
  Errors[]
  Warnings[]
```

`EUW_AscentiaSaveMigrationLab` should call the same migration path as runtime
load. A beautiful editor-only migration preview is not proof unless it exercises
the real subsystem.

### 13.14 Death-loop UX and recovery readability

Death penalty mechanics are only good if the player understands them.

Presentation data:

```text
PDA_DeathPenaltyProfile
  LostCurrencyTag
  LostCurrencyPercent
  MaxLostCurrency
  DropPolicy
  EchoActorClass
  EchoMapMarkerPolicy
  RecoveryInteractionPolicy
  SecondDeathPolicy
  BossArenaProxyPolicy
  DeathMessageText
  RecoveryMessageText
  UIIcon
  CueProfile
```

UX surfaces:

```text
WBP_AscentiaDeathRecoveryNotice
WBP_AscentiaDeathEchoMarker
WBP_AscentiaCompassDeathEchoIndicator
WBP_AscentiaMapDeathEchoMarker
```

Rules:

- after death, the player sees what was lost and where recovery begins;
- the death echo should appear in map/compass only when that fits Ascentia's
  intended discovery language;
- if the echo is above/below the player, verticality must be readable;
- in boss arenas, the echo should not force the player through active fog-wall
  state in a way that soft-locks recovery;
- recovery is a short, satisfying confirmation, not an inventory transaction.

The FromSoft rune/souls pattern is useful because it creates tension without
destroying permanent character identity: carried currency is at risk, while
level, equipment, world progression, and learned knowledge remain. Ascentia can
adapt the same emotional shape while making proxy placement and UI readability
more explicit.

Death-loop UX report:

```text
FAscentiaDeathLoopUXReport
  RunId
  Scenarios
  LostCurrencyNoticeShown
  MapMarkerPolicyResults
  CompassMarkerPolicyResults
  VerticalityReadabilityFindings
  BossFogRecoveryFindings
  RecoveryCueResults
  TextKeyFindings
```

This report belongs with P20 presentation eventually, but P19 owns whether the
death-loop facts are exposed. Presentation cannot fix missing facts.

### 13.15 P19 tooling and validation matrix

Editor tools:

```text
EUW_AscentiaSaveInspector
  inspect slot headers, nested structs, version, identity, death state,
  world state, inventory, and migration status

EUW_AscentiaSaveLab
  run save/load scenarios, inspect dirty events, compare slot summaries, and
  open evidence from the latest proof

EUW_AscentiaSaveMigrationLab
  run fixtures, show defaulted fields, compare pre/post save summaries

EUW_AscentiaSaveFixtureReport
  list fixtures by version, expected migration path, round-trip hash, and
  blocking validation failures

EUW_AscentiaDeathLoopSimulator
  simulate death location, proxy placement, second-death policy, recovery

EUW_AscentiaDeathEchoPlacementPreview
  preview proxy placement in the selected map for falls, boss arenas, kill
  volumes, and streamed-out areas

EUW_AscentiaRespecPlanner
  preview stat transactions, passive deltas, costs, and save result

EUW_AscentiaWorldCyclePreview
  preview NG+ retained/reset tags, scaling, rewards, and boss/POI resets

EUW_AscentiaCharacterCreatorLab
  preview origin/class/avatar/loadout combinations and commit draft save payloads

EUW_AscentiaAvatarAssemblyPreview
  test body parts, materials, grooms, physics assets, retarget profile,
  equipment overlays, camera probes, and animation pose sets
```

Validators:

```text
VLD_Ascentia_SaveSchema
VLD_Ascentia_SaveMigrations
VLD_Ascentia_SaveValidationProfiles
VLD_Ascentia_DeathPenaltyProfiles
VLD_Ascentia_DeathEchoPlacementProfiles
VLD_Ascentia_RespecProfiles
VLD_Ascentia_Origins
VLD_Ascentia_ClassArchetypes
VLD_Ascentia_AvatarPresets
VLD_Ascentia_AvatarAssemblyProfiles
VLD_Ascentia_CharacterCreationRules
VLD_Ascentia_StartingLoadouts
VLD_Ascentia_ModularCharacters
VLD_Ascentia_WorldCycleProfiles
```

Machine proof:

- every save fixture migrates;
- character creation creates a slot;
- appearance survives save/load;
- stat investment and threshold passives survive save/load;
- respec applies as one transaction;
- death echo persists through quit/reload;
- second death resolves old echo by policy;
- NG+ resets and retains the correct tags.

Evidence folders:

```text
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P19/SaveSchema/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P19/SaveMigration/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P19/CharacterCreation/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P19/AvatarAssembly/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P19/DeathLoop/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P19/Respec/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P19/WorldCycle/{RunId}/report.json
```

Required report structs:

```text
FAscentiaSaveValidationReport
FAscentiaSaveMigrationReport
FAscentiaCharacterCreationReport
FAscentiaAvatarCompatibilityReport
FAscentiaDeathLoopReport
FAscentiaDeathLoopUXReport
FAscentiaRespecReport
FAscentiaWorldCycleReport
```

### 13.16 P19 proof gates

Minimum proof:

```text
Tools/remote_playtest.py character_creation
  creates a new slot
  applies origin/class/appearance
  reloads and proves identity persisted
  proves runtime pawn uses the saved avatar assembly path

Tools/remote_playtest.py save_schema_v2
  invests stats
  unlocks threshold passives
  saves
  reloads
  proves invested stats and derived passives match

Tools/remote_playtest.py death_loop
  dies with Stones
  respawns at anchor
  proves recoverable echo exists
  proves proxy placement is valid
  recovers Stones
  saves cleared death state

Tools/remote_playtest.py death_loop_replace
  dies twice without recovery
  proves first echo is consumed/replaced by policy

Tools/remote_playtest.py respec
  previews stat refund/investment
  confirms transaction
  proves attributes/passives/save state update

Tools/remote_playtest.py ng_plus_cycle
  completes cycle gate
  starts next cycle
  proves retained and reset state policies
```

Editor validation:

```text
VLD_Ascentia_SaveSchema
VLD_Ascentia_SaveMigrations
VLD_Ascentia_SaveValidationProfiles
VLD_Ascentia_Origins
VLD_Ascentia_ClassArchetypes
VLD_Ascentia_AvatarPresets
VLD_Ascentia_AvatarAssemblyProfiles
VLD_Ascentia_CharacterCreationRules
VLD_Ascentia_StartingLoadouts
VLD_Ascentia_ModularCharacters
VLD_Ascentia_DeathEchoPlacementProfiles
VLD_Ascentia_DeathPenaltyProfiles
VLD_Ascentia_RespecProfiles
VLD_Ascentia_WorldCycleProfiles
```

The P19 "done" claim requires at least one old save fixture migrated through the
current migration path. A save system without old-save fixtures is unproven.

Remote proof IDs:

```text
save_slot_summary
save_async_roundtrip
save_migration_fixture
character_creation_commit
avatar_assembly_runtime
death_echo_proxy
death_loop_recovery
death_loop_replace
respec_transaction
world_cycle_transition
```

### 13.17 P19 starter implementation package

P19 should begin by turning the current MVP save checkpoint into a versioned
character-identity system. The first slice should not try to build the whole
character creator or NG+ loop. It should prove that Ascentia can create one
stable character identity, write and load it asynchronously, migrate at least one
old fixture, assemble the runtime avatar through data, and preserve death-loop
facts through save/load.

Reference posture:

- Use `USaveGame` as the serialized payload, but keep gameplay logic in
  subsystems/facades. A save object should be data, not a gameplay service.
- Prefer async save/load requests for production paths. Blocking save/load is
  acceptable for narrow debug tools and tiny MVP tests, but not as the long-term
  player-facing path.
- Always pair slot name with local user identity. Even if the first SKU is
  single-player PC, the schema should not assume that "slot 1" means one global
  human forever.
- Save stable tags, GUIDs, versions, and source facts. Recompute attributes,
  passives, UI view models, and presentation state after load.
- Treat modular avatar assembly as data-driven presentation over a saved
  character identity. AssassinGirl is a preset/placeholder, not the save schema.
- Use Leader Pose or Copy Pose for early modular proofs, but record when a preset
  should become a merged skeletal mesh path for performance, morph, physics, or
  equipment-overlay reasons.

Runtime module layout:

```text
Source/Ascentia/Public/Save/AscentiaSaveTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Save/AscentiaSaveSubsystem.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Save/AscentiaSaveFacades.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Save/AscentiaSaveMigrationTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Save/AscentiaSaveValidationTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Progression/AscentiaCharacterIdentityTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Progression/AscentiaCharacterCreationTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Progression/AscentiaRespecTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Death/AscentiaDeathLoopTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Death/AscentiaDeathLoopSubsystem.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Characters/AscentiaAvatarAssemblyTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Characters/AscentiaAvatarAssemblySubsystem.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/World/AscentiaWorldCycleTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Private/Save/AscentiaSaveSubsystem.cpp
Source/Ascentia/Private/Save/AscentiaSaveFacades.cpp
Source/Ascentia/Private/Save/AscentiaSaveMigration.cpp
Source/Ascentia/Private/Progression/AscentiaCharacterCreationTypes.cpp
Source/Ascentia/Private/Death/AscentiaDeathLoopSubsystem.cpp
Source/Ascentia/Private/Characters/AscentiaAvatarAssemblySubsystem.cpp
```

Editor module layout:

```text
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Save/AscentiaSaveSchemaValidation.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Save/AscentiaSaveFixtureValidation.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Progression/AscentiaCharacterCreationValidation.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Characters/AscentiaAvatarAssemblyValidation.h

Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Save/AscentiaSaveFixtureValidation.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Progression/AscentiaCharacterCreationValidation.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Characters/AscentiaAvatarAssemblyValidation.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Death/AscentiaDeathLoopValidation.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/World/AscentiaWorldCycleValidation.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Commandlets/AscentiaSaveMigrationCommandlet.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Commandlets/AscentiaSaveSchemaAuditCommandlet.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Commandlets/AscentiaCharacterCreationAuditCommandlet.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Commandlets/AscentiaDeathLoopAuditCommandlet.cpp
```

Tests and proof helpers:

```text
Source/AscentiaTests/Private/Save/AscentiaSaveSchemaTests.cpp
Source/AscentiaTests/Private/Save/AscentiaSaveMigrationTests.cpp
Source/AscentiaTests/Private/Save/AscentiaAsyncSaveRoundtripTests.cpp
Source/AscentiaTests/Private/Progression/AscentiaCharacterCreationTests.cpp
Source/AscentiaTests/Private/Progression/AscentiaRespecTransactionTests.cpp
Source/AscentiaTests/Private/Death/AscentiaDeathLoopTests.cpp
Source/AscentiaTests/Private/Characters/AscentiaAvatarAssemblyTests.cpp
Tools/save_fixtures/v1_mvp_basic.sav.json
Tools/save_fixtures/v2_progression_stats.sav.json
Tools/save_fixtures/v3_character_identity.sav.json
Tools/save_fixtures/v4_death_echo.sav.json
Tools/remote_playtest.py scenarios: save_async_roundtrip,
  save_migration_fixture, character_creation_commit, avatar_assembly_runtime,
  death_loop_recovery, death_loop_replace, respec_transaction,
  world_cycle_transition
```

The first native ownership split:

```text
UAscentiaSaveSubsystem : UGameInstanceSubsystem
  Owns slot discovery, async request queue, snapshot locks, migration,
  validation, slot summaries, evidence, and failure tags.

UAscentiaCharacterSaveFacade : UObject or UGameInstanceSubsystem helper
  Gathers identity, appearance, progression, inventory, equipment, resources,
  spellbook, and build-state fragments from the possessed character path.

UAscentiaWorldSaveFacade : UWorldSubsystem helper
  Gathers world-state fragments, stable world-actor records, objectives,
  discovery, fast travel, encounters, and resettable rewards.

UAscentiaDeathLoopSubsystem : UWorldSubsystem
  Owns death echo creation, proxy placement, recovery, second-death policy,
  save fragment writes, and death-loop evidence.

UAscentiaAvatarAssemblySubsystem : UGameInstanceSubsystem
  Resolves saved appearance/identity plus equipment into a runtime avatar
  assembly request. It selects Leader Pose, Copy Pose, or merged mesh policy
  from data rather than hardcoding the current pawn costume setup.
```

Starter content folders:

```text
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Save/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/CharacterCreation/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Avatar/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Death/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Respec/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/WorldCycle/
/Game/Ascentia/UI/CharacterCreation/
/Game/Ascentia/UI/Save/
/Game/Ascentia/UI/Death/
/Game/Ascentia/Blueprints/Death/
/Game/Ascentia/Editor/Save/
```

Starter assets:

```text
PDA_SaveSchemaProfile_MVP
PDA_SaveValidationProfile_MVP
PDA_SaveMigrationProfile_MVP
PDA_Origin_Wanderer_MVP
PDA_ClassArchetype_Adventurer_MVP

PDA_AvatarAssemblyProfile_GASP_Prototype
```

`UAscentiaSaveSubsystem` should serialize requests. Do not allow overlapping
load/save operations unless the platform policy says it is safe. The subsystem
should emit a clear failure tag if a request is rejected because another
operation is active.

P19 starter tickets:

- P19-001: Add versioned save schema structs, slot header, slot summary, and
  `PDA_SaveSchemaProfile_MVP`.
- P19-002: Convert `UAscentiaSaveSubsystem` to a request/result facade with
  async save/load support, local user fields, snapshot lock state, and dirty
  event routing while preserving current campsite save/load behavior.
- P19-003: Add migration steps, fixture folder, `AscentiaSaveMigration`
  commandlet, and one MVP v1 fixture migration proof.
- P19-004: Add character identity, appearance, avatar assembly state, first
  origin/class/avatar/loadout assets, and `CharacterCreation_Commit_NewSlot`
  proof.
- P19-005: Add `UAscentiaAvatarAssemblySubsystem`, prototype AssassinGirl avatar
  preset, GASP VisualOverride assembly policy report, and runtime pawn proof.
- P19-006: Add death-loop subsystem, dropped-stones profile, death echo actor,
  proxy placement request/result, ordinary death recovery proof, and save/reload
  death-state proof.
- P19-007: Add second-death policy, kill-volume/boss-arena proxy proofs, and
  `EUW_AscentiaDeathLoopSimulator`.
- P19-008: Add respec request/preview/commit transaction, campsite profile, and
  passive recompute proof.
- P19-009: Add world-cycle profile preview, retained/reset state report, and
  first NG+ policy proof.
- P19-010: Wire SaveLab, migration lab, avatar preview, death-loop simulator,
  reports, evidence folders, dashboard cards, and package gates.

Recommended implementation order:

1. P19-001 through P19-003 first. Schema, async request routing, and migration
   fixtures are the backbone; character creator work should not pile onto the
   flat MVP save.
2. P19-004 and P19-005 next. These separate saved character identity from the
   current GASP/AssassinGirl presentation path without breaking the prototype.
3. P19-006 and P19-007 then make death meaningful and persistent, with proxy
   placement proof before tuning punishment severity.
4. P19-008 should happen after source stat facts are safely saved and migrated.
5. P19-009 is last because NG+ is a policy layer over world, content, death,
   rewards, and progression.
6. P19-010 closes the loop with Designer tools and evidence.

### 13.18 Character creator cosmetics, grooms, cloth, physics, and portrait package

This package is the production acceptance layer for creator cosmetics. Section
13.17 owns save identity and avatar assembly; this section owns whether body
presets, morphs, modular armor, hair, cloth, physics assets, portraits, and
cosmetic overlays are shippable content that Designers can inspect and fix.

The goal is not to rebuild character creation twice. It is to prevent the future
custom creator from becoming the same kind of hidden dependency problem that
already hit animation compression settings, attack notifies, weapon sockets, and
the GASP costume path.

Engine-facing posture:

- Modular characters should keep the section 13.17 assembly tiers: Leader Pose
  for the first low-risk creator proof, Copy Pose where a child mesh needs
  additional bones or physics behavior, and merged skeletal meshes for later
  performance or morph-heavy outfits. The selected tier must be authored data,
  not an invisible Blueprint convention.
- Hair should use explicit Groom profiles. A hair option is not accepted until
  it has a binding policy, material/color policy, LOD/scalability policy,
  simulation toggle, close-camera preview, and a fallback decision for platforms
  or scalability levels where strands are too expensive.
- Simulated garments should use explicit cloth profiles. A cloth item is not
  accepted until its Chaos Cloth source, collision body coverage, budget,
  scalability behavior, and combat-readability verdict are visible.
- PhysicsAsset coverage is part of creator acceptance, not a late combat bug.
  Missing root/body coverage, invalid bodies, broken constraints, or collision
  disabled on playable avatar bodies should block promotion.
- Portraits and icons must be generated from the same resolved avatar cosmetic
  state used at runtime. A portrait built from a hand-assembled preview pawn is
  evidence debt.
