Save-state extension:

```text
FAscentiaAvatarCosmeticState
  BodyPresetTag
  MorphValues
  MaterialPaletteTag
  CosmeticPartTags
  GroomTags
  ClothTags
  TattooScarMakeupTags
  PortraitId
  Version
```

The save stores stable tags and slider values. It should not store direct mesh,
Groom, cloth, material, or portrait asset pointers. Runtime assembly resolves
the current content from those tags, then writes evidence if the content no
longer matches the saved state.

Acceptance rules:

- Every creator part has skeleton, retarget, physics, socket, material, and
  save-tag compatibility.
- Every body preset has a morph profile with ranges, defaults, clamp policy,
  and at least one animation/locomotion preview.
- Every groom has binding, LOD/scalability, color/material parameters,
  simulation policy, close-camera preview, and fallback policy.
- Every cloth item has a source asset, simulation policy, PhysicsAsset collision
  compatibility, budget, scalability behavior, and combat-readability verdict.
- Every playable avatar PhysicsAsset has root/body coverage for collisions,
  hit reactions, cloth collision, and root-motion floor checks. Invalid bodies
  block promotion.
- Tattoos, scars, makeup, skin details, and body paint use parameterized
  material layers or approved overlays. Do not create one-off material
  duplicates for every appearance choice.
- Equipment overlays resolve clipping through compatibility rules, hide masks,
  or part replacement policy. Clipping should not be a "Designer will remember"
  problem.
- Portrait capture uses the same avatar cosmetic state and assembly subsystem
  as runtime. A portrait proof should include save/load roundtrip, not just a
  render target screenshot.
- Cosmetic display names, slot names, and unlock text use stable localization
  keys. Creator data should not bake player-facing text into asset names.

Designer tools:

```text
EUW_AscentiaAvatarCosmeticBoard
EUW_AscentiaBodyMorphLab
EUW_AscentiaGroomFitLab
EUW_AscentiaClothFitLab
EUW_AscentiaPhysicsAssetBodyDoctor
EUW_AscentiaPortraitCaptureLab
EUW_AscentiaCosmeticCompatibilityMatrix
```

`EUW_AscentiaAvatarCosmeticBoard` is the owner-facing production board. It
lists every creator part, current promotion state, missing dependencies,
save-tag stability, assembly policy, budget cost, and the last proof run.

`EUW_AscentiaBodyMorphLab` previews body presets, morph sliders, armor overlays,
weapon sockets, locomotion, attacks, dodge, stairs, and ledge cases. It should
show clipping verdicts and morph channels that exceed accepted ranges.

`EUW_AscentiaGroomFitLab` previews each hair option on every accepted head/body
profile, toggles simulation/scalability, captures close-camera screenshots, and
reports missing bindings or fallback debt.

`EUW_AscentiaClothFitLab` previews garment simulation against locomotion,
attacks, dodge, stairs, ledges, hit reactions, and camera distances. It should
show cloth budget and collision body dependencies.

`EUW_AscentiaPhysicsAssetBodyDoctor` scans PhysicsAssets for missing root/body
coverage, disabled collision, invalid bodies, suspect constraints, and cloth or
hit-reaction body gaps. This is the direct prevention tool for the "Invalid
Bodies" warning class already seen in PIE.

`EUW_AscentiaPortraitCaptureLab` assembles a runtime avatar from a saved
cosmetic state, captures portraits/icons through approved profiles, writes
output assets, and records the resolved avatar hash.

```text
AscentiaAvatarCosmeticAudit

  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P19/AvatarCosmetics
```

P19C starter tickets:

- P19C-001: Add cosmetic state/profile structs, profile assets, and save-state
  extension using stable tags and versioned slider values.
- P19C-002: Add body preset and morph-channel validation, `EUW_AscentiaBodyMorphLab`,
  and `BodyMorph_MinMax_NoClipping` proof.
- P19C-003: Add Groom profile validation, `EUW_AscentiaGroomFitLab`, binding
  audit, scalability/fallback audit, and default hair proof.
- P19C-004: Add cloth simulation profile validation, `EUW_AscentiaClothFitLab`,
  collision/budget audit, and combat-readability proof.

  `EUW_AscentiaPhysicsAssetBodyDoctor`, and package gate for invalid playable
  avatar bodies.

  and armor overlay proof across the first body preset.
- P19C-007: Add portrait capture request/result path,
  `EUW_AscentiaPortraitCaptureLab`, and runtime-avatar portrait proof.
- P19C-008: Add Avatar Cosmetic Board, commandlets, evidence folders, dashboard
  cards, and package gates.

Recommended implementation order:

1. P19C-001 and P19C-002 first. The creator needs a stable cosmetic payload and
   body/morph rules before content volume grows.
2. P19C-005 should happen early because invalid PhysicsAssets have already
   appeared in PIE, and grooms/cloth/combat all depend on trustworthy bodies.
3. P19C-003 and P19C-004 then prove hair and cloth as explicit production
   assets rather than one-off visuals.
4. P19C-006 connects armor, body, hide masks, sockets, and clipping policy.
5. P19C-007 and P19C-008 close the loop with portraits, boards, commandlets,
   package gates, and dashboard evidence.

### 13.19 Campsites, checkpoints, rest/reset, respawn anchors, and world recovery package

The current MVP campsite already saves, restores resources, and resets the
arena. Production needs that loop to become a named system. In a Soulslike ARPG,
resting is not only a heal button. It is a world transaction: save, refill,
reset, respawn, travel, death echo, boss persistence, NPC schedule, music state,
map state, and streaming readiness all agree or the world feels arbitrary.

Primary references:

- Unreal SaveGame documentation recommends asynchronous saving for meaningful
  game data because synchronous disk work can hitch and create platform risk:
  <https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/saving-and-loading-your-game-in-unreal-engine>
- The Blueprint async save node documents `AsyncSaveGameToSlot` and its
  completion result:
  <https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/BlueprintAPI/SaveGame/AsyncSaveGametoSlot>
- World Partition streams grid cells from streaming sources such as the player,
  which makes respawn, fast travel, Below return, and boss-arena recovery a
  streaming contract:
  <https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/world-partition-in-unreal-engine>
- Data Layers can organize actors at editor and runtime, so rest/reset policy
  must say which layers, actors, and world-state rows change:
  <https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/world-partition---data-layers-in-unreal-engine>
- GAS Gameplay Effects are the right path for resource restoration, flask
  refill, temporary buff clearing, rest debuffs, and death penalties:
  <https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/gameplay-effects-for-the-gameplay-ability-system-in-unreal-engine>
- Gameplay Tags remain the shared identity language for checkpoints, rest
  policies, respawn anchors, reset groups, and death echo state:
  <https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/using-gameplay-tags-in-unreal-engine>

```text
  ResourceRestoreProfileTag
  EncounterResetProfileTag
  WorldResetProfileTag
  DeathEchoPolicyTag

  bRequiresSafeState
  bBlocksDuringCombat

FAscentiaCheckpointProfile
  CheckpointTag
  DisplayNameKey
  RegionTag
  WorldLocation
  RespawnAnchorTag
  FastTravelAnchorTag

  AttunementPolicy
  SaveSlotPolicy
  MapMarkerPolicy

FAscentiaRespawnAnchor
  AnchorTag

  CollisionClearancePolicy

  StatusClearPolicy
  TemporaryBuffClearPolicy

FAscentiaEncounterResetProfile
  ProfileTag
  ResetNonBossEnemies
  ResetAmbientPopulation
  ResetLootContainers
  ResetProjectilesAndHazards

  PreserveBossDefeat
  EncounterGroupRules

FAscentiaWorldResetProfile
  ProfileTag
  DataLayerRequests
  WorldStateTagsToSet
  WorldStateTagsToClear
  RuntimeActorsToDestroy
  RuntimeActorsToRespawn
  NPCSchedulePolicy
  WeatherTimePolicy
  AudioStatePolicy

FAscentiaRestRequest
  RequestId
  Instigator
  CheckpointTag
  RestReason
  RequestedSaveSlot
  bAllowEncounterReset
  bAllowFastTravelUnlock
  ContextTags

FAscentiaRestResult
  RequestId
  bSucceeded
  SaveResult
  ResourceRows
  EncounterResetRows
  WorldResetRows
  RespawnAnchor
  PresentationEvents
  BlockingFindings

FAscentiaFastTravelArrivalRequest
  SourceCheckpointTag
  DestinationCheckpointTag
  DestinationRespawnAnchorTag
  RequiredStreamingCells
  ArrivalResourcePolicy
  ArrivalPresentationPolicy

FAscentiaWorldRecoveryReport
  RunId
  CheckpointRows
  RespawnAnchorRows
  RestRows
  EncounterResetRows
  FastTravelArrivalRows
  DeathEchoRows
  StreamingRows
  BlockingFindings
```

Runtime policy:

- `UAscentiaRestRecoverySubsystem` owns the transaction. Campsite actors,
  fast-travel points, death respawn, Below exits, boss fog walls, and shell load
  screens request recovery; they do not each implement their own reset logic.
- Rest requests must be server-authoritative even in single-player architecture
  terms. They validate safe state, combat lockout, checkpoint identity, save slot,
  world-state requirements, and streaming readiness before mutating state.
- Resource restoration applies through GAS Gameplay Effects and profile data.
  Health, Breath, Eth, flask charges, status clears, buff clears, and rest-only
  penalties should be visible rows, not hidden character functions.
- Encounter reset is profile-driven. Trash enemies, ambient actors, hazards,
  loot containers, unique elites, boss defeat, Below rooms, and reward pickups
  each state whether rest resets, preserves, respawns, or consumes them.
- Respawn anchors are distinct from campsites. A boss-adjacent respawn point may
  be legal without being a full rest/save/fast-travel service.
- Fast travel is a recovery request plus streaming preload. Destination cells,
  Data Layers, spawn clearance, camera restore, music state, and arrival save
  policy must be proven before the teleport happens.
- Death echo and dropped-currency state is resolved during recovery. The second
  death policy, proxy placement, kill-volume fallback, and boss-arena proxy
  volumes must be authored data.
- Rest can update NPC schedules, world-state tags, weather/time, and audio, but
  every side effect must appear in the recovery result and evidence.
- Save/load ordering: load save -> restore world state -> stream destination ->
  spawn/possess pawn -> apply resources/death state -> evaluate UI/NPC/map.
- Package builds should block on any checkpoint, fast-travel point, boss arena,
  Below exit, or death respawn path that lacks a valid anchor, safe floor,
  streaming proof, and save/load proof.

Designer-facing tools:

```text
EUW_AscentiaRestCheckpointBoard
  inspect checkpoints, campsite profiles, rest policies, attunement, map
  markers, save slots, resource restore, side effects, and latest evidence

EUW_AscentiaRespawnAnchorLab
  preview respawn anchors, safe floor, camera restore, collision clearance,
  death echo proxy placement, fallback anchors, and streaming source readiness

EUW_AscentiaWorldResetScenarioLab
  simulate rest, death, reload, boss defeat, Below return, NG+ reset, and world
  state reset outcomes across encounters, actors, rewards, NPCs, and Data Layers

EUW_AscentiaFastTravelArrivalLab
  validate source/destination checkpoints, attunement, preload, arrival camera,
  resource policy, music/presentation, map marker state, and save-before-travel
```

Evidence folders:

```text
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P19/RestRecovery/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P19/Checkpoints/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P19/RespawnAnchors/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P19/WorldReset/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P19/FastTravelArrival/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P19/DeathEchoRecovery/{RunId}/report.json
```

Commandlet contracts:

```text
UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe Ascentia.uproject -run=AscentiaRestRecoveryAudit
  -Policy=/Game/Ascentia/Design/Progression/PDA_RestRecoveryPolicy_MVP
  -Map=/Game/Ascentia/Maps/MVP_Arena
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P19/RestRecovery

UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe Ascentia.uproject -run=AscentiaRespawnAnchorScenario
  -Profile=/Game/Ascentia/Design/Progression/PDA_RespawnAnchorProfile_MVP
  -Map=/Game/Ascentia/Maps/MVP_Arena
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P19/RespawnAnchors

UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe Ascentia.uproject -run=AscentiaWorldResetScenario
  -Profile=/Game/Ascentia/Design/Progression/PDA_WorldResetProfile_MVP
  -Scenario=RestDeathReloadBossDefeatBelowReturn
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P19/WorldReset

UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe Ascentia.uproject -run=AscentiaFastTravelArrivalScenario
  -Profile=/Game/Ascentia/Design/Progression/PDA_FastTravelArrivalProfile_MVP
  -Source=Campsite_MVP
  -Destination=FastTravel_MVP
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P19/FastTravelArrival
```

Starter proof matrix:

```text
rest_campsite_restores_resources_through_GAS
  spend Health/Breath/Eth/flasks/status, rest, verify Gameplay Effects restore
  or clear according to `FAscentiaResourceRestoreProfile`

rest_campsite_saves_async_and_reports_result
  rest at campsite, verify async save request/result, slot summary update,
  save-dirty reason, and no hidden synchronous save dependency

rest_encounter_reset_preserves_boss_defeat
  defeat trash and boss, rest, verify trash reset and boss defeat persists
  according to encounter reset profile

rest_respawn_anchor_safe_floor_and_camera
  die near wall/stairs/ledge, respawn at anchor, verify safe floor, collision
  clearance, camera restore, and no mesh/camera intersection

rest_death_echo_proxy_boss_and_kill_volume
  die in boss arena and kill volume, verify death echo proxy placement and
  recovery route are authored and save-persistent

rest_fast_travel_preloads_destination
  fast travel after attunement, verify destination streaming/Data Layers are
  ready before pawn placement and presentation handoff

rest_world_state_side_effects_visible
  rest changes NPC schedule, weather/time, or reset tags only through explicit
  world reset profile rows and evidence

rest_below_return_valid_anchor_and_rewards
  return from Below, verify reward transaction, destination anchor, resource
  policy, save/load state, and no duplicate cache reward

rest_save_load_roundtrip_checkpoint_state
  save at campsite, quit/load, verify checkpoint, resources, inventory, world
  state, map discovery, death echo, and encounter reset policy agree
```

Implementation tickets:

```text
P19R-001 Rest recovery policy and subsystem facade
  Add `FAscentiaRestRecoveryPolicy`, `FAscentiaRestRequest`,
  `FAscentiaRestResult`, `UAscentiaRestRecoverySubsystem`, and campsite request
  routing without bypassing the save/death-loop subsystem.

P19R-002 Checkpoint and respawn anchor profiles
  Add checkpoint profiles, respawn anchor components, safe-floor/camera/collision
  validation, fallback anchors, boss-adjacent anchor policy, and Designer
  Respawn Anchor Lab.

P19R-003 Resource restore and rest-side Gameplay Effects
  Add resource restore profiles, rest-applied/removed Gameplay Effects, flask
  refill policy, status clear policy, and proof that restores go through GAS.

P19R-004 Encounter/world reset profile bridge
  Add encounter reset profiles, Data Layer/world-state side-effect rows, trash/
  elite/boss/reward reset policy, and World Reset Scenario Lab.

P19R-005 Fast travel and Below arrival recovery
  Add fast-travel arrival requests, destination preload checks, Below return
  anchor proof, arrival presentation, duplicate reward guards, and map marker
  sync.

P19R-006 Death echo recovery integration
  Add death echo proxy policy, kill-volume/boss-arena proxy fixtures, second
  death policy hooks, and save/load proof.

P19R-007 Rest/checkpoint Designer boards and dashboard gates
  Add Rest Checkpoint Board, Respawn Anchor Lab, World Reset Scenario Lab,
  Fast Travel Arrival Lab, dashboard cards, commandlets, and package blockers
  for unsafe anchors, unprofiled reset side effects, and missing streaming proof.
```

Recommended implementation order:

1. Build P19R-001 and P19R-002 immediately after the save subsystem spine.
2. Add P19R-003 before broader flask, Eth, status, or rest-buff content.
3. Add P19R-004 before encounter and world-life content assumes rest behavior.
4. Add P19R-005 before fast travel or Below return becomes a progression tool.
5. Add P19R-006 before death echo tuning or boss kill-volume content expands.
6. Add P19R-007 before milestone maps claim checkpoint or recovery readiness.

## 14. Presentation, Feedback, Audio, VFX, and Performance

### 14.1 Player-facing goal

P20 is where Ascentia stops proving that systems technically work and starts
proving that the whole game speaks a coherent sensory language.

The player should feel:

- impacts have weight;
- dodges, attacks, spells, arrows, throwables, blocks, status effects, loot, and
  boss phases are readable;
- camera/audio/VFX react without becoming noisy;
- settings can reduce intensity without losing information;
- performance remains stable even in dense combat.

P20 should not be written as "add more particles." It should be written as a
presentation operating system.

### 14.2 Current Ascentia state

Ascentia already has useful presentation plumbing:

- `UAscentiaCombatComponent` emits action, hit, damage, status, phase, target,
  and feedback telemetry for many verbs.
- `UAscentiaAvatarComponent` owns the portable melee contact-sweep feel layer
  for the GASP pawn:
  - weapon trail component;
  - swing-tracking window;
  - blade speed thresholds;
  - swing whoosh;
  - hit reaction physics blend;
  - BlueprintPure trail/audio diagnostics.
- The default weapon trail path is:
  `/Game/Ascentia/Design/FX/NS_WeaponTrail.NS_WeaponTrail`.
- The default swing whoosh is a `USoundBase` cue from the action-sounds pack.
- `AAscentiaAmbientMusicActor` can auto-start looping level music and stop the
  game-mode ambient track.
- `AAscentiaPlayerController` and native UI classes have MVP HUD, inventory,
  loot, save-menu, target, objective, and feedback surfaces.
- Corpse loot already has Niagara presentation through
  `UAscentiaCorpseLootComponent`.
- The docs already name a large set of settings candidates: camera shake,
  rumble, damage-number visibility/size, impact VFX intensity, projectile trail
  readability, status VFX, boss music intensity, fog-wall readability, and more.

The gaps:

- no unified presentation event bus;
- no canonical cue profile data;
- no GameplayCue coverage validation;
- no Niagara Data Channel strategy for high-volume events;
- no audio state manager;
- no MetaSound authoring contract;
- no camera feedback manager;
- no accessibility intensity scaling pipeline;
- no presentation performance budgets;
- no final UMG view-model architecture;
- no global "presentation proof" scenario.

### 14.3 Presentation event contract

Gameplay systems should emit facts, not presentation decisions.

Use a native event shape:

```text
FAscentiaPresentationEvent
  EventTag
  SourceActor
  TargetActor
  InstigatorActor
  WorldLocation
  WorldNormal
  Magnitude
  PoiseMagnitude
  DamageSchoolTag
  StatusTag
  AbilityTag
  ItemTag
  SurfaceTag
  GameplayCueTag
  PresentationProfileTag
  bBlocked
  bCritical
  bKillingBlow
  SequenceId
```

Native systems emit:

```text
Ascentia.Presentation.Attack.Swing
Ascentia.Presentation.Attack.Impact
Ascentia.Presentation.Block.Success
Ascentia.Presentation.GuardBreak
Ascentia.Presentation.Parry
Ascentia.Presentation.Dodge
Ascentia.Presentation.Status.Buildup
Ascentia.Presentation.Status.Trigger
Ascentia.Presentation.Spell.Cast
Ascentia.Presentation.Spell.Impact
Ascentia.Presentation.Projectile.Fire
Ascentia.Presentation.Projectile.Impact
Ascentia.Presentation.Loot.Drop
Ascentia.Presentation.Boss.Phase
Ascentia.Presentation.World.Discovery
Ascentia.Presentation.Death.Player
Ascentia.Presentation.Death.Recovery
```

Presentation systems consume and route:

```text
UAscentiaPresentationSubsystem
  routes events to cues, audio, camera, rumble, UI, and telemetry

UAscentiaCueRouter
  resolves GameplayCue tags and CueProfile overrides

UAscentiaAudioStateSubsystem
  resolves one-shots, loops, mixes, music state, stingers

UAscentiaCameraFeedbackSubsystem
  resolves shakes, FOV kicks, camera lag profiles

UAscentiaVFXBudgetSubsystem
  enforces active Niagara and decal budgets

UAscentiaPresentationSettingsSubsystem
  applies accessibility and intensity multipliers
```

The combat component should not know which Niagara system represents a slash on
wet stone. It should know the hit facts. The cue/profile layer decides the look.

Route request/result:

```text
FAscentiaPresentationRouteRequest
  Event
  LocalViewer
  NetworkRole
  PlatformProfile
  PresentationSettingsSnapshot
  BudgetSnapshot
  bAllowCamera
  bAllowRumble
  bAllowAudio
  bAllowVFX
  bAllowUI

FAscentiaPresentationRouteResult
  EventTag
  SequenceId
  RoutedGameplayCue
  ResolvedCueProfile
  SpawnedNiagaraRefs
  WrittenNiagaraDataChannels
  PlayedAudioRefs
  AppliedCameraFeedback
  AppliedRumble
  SpawnedUIEvents
  SuppressedByBudget
  SuppressedBySettings
  MissingRouteReasons
  EvidenceId
```

Presentation event report:

```text
FAscentiaPresentationEventReport
  RunId
  EventCountByTag
  RouteResults
  MissingCueRoutes
  BudgetSuppressions
  SettingsSuppressions
  NetworkVisibilityFindings
  Errors[]
  Warnings[]
```

The event report is the answer to "why did my light attack sound vanish?" It
should show whether the notify fired, whether the event reached the router,
which cue/audio profile resolved, and whether budget/settings/concurrency
suppressed it.

### 14.4 Cue profile data

Data assets:

```text
PDA_CueProfile_*
  EventTag
  GameplayCueTag
  NiagaraSystem
  NiagaraDataChannelTag
  Sound
  MetaSoundSource
  CameraFeedbackProfile
  RumbleProfile
  DamageNumberStyle
  DecalProfile
  Priority
  CooldownSeconds
  MaxInstances
  AccessibilityGroupTag

PDA_ImpactFeedback_*
  DamageSchoolTag
  SurfaceTag
  BlockStateTag
  NiagaraSystem
  Sound
  CameraFeedbackProfile
  HitStopProfile
  DecalMaterial
```

GameplayCue taxonomy:

```text
GameplayCue.Ascentia.Attack.Swing.Light
GameplayCue.Ascentia.Attack.Swing.Heavy
GameplayCue.Ascentia.Attack.Impact.Slash
GameplayCue.Ascentia.Attack.Impact.Strike
GameplayCue.Ascentia.Block.Shield
GameplayCue.Ascentia.GuardBreak
GameplayCue.Ascentia.Parry
GameplayCue.Ascentia.Dodge.Roll
GameplayCue.Ascentia.Status.Poison.Buildup
GameplayCue.Ascentia.Status.Poison.Trigger
GameplayCue.Ascentia.Spell.EtherSpark.Cast
GameplayCue.Ascentia.Spell.EtherSpark.Impact
GameplayCue.Ascentia.Projectile.Arrow.Fire
GameplayCue.Ascentia.Projectile.Arrow.Impact
GameplayCue.Ascentia.Loot.RarityBeam
GameplayCue.Ascentia.Boss.Phase.Enraged
GameplayCue.Ascentia.World.Discovery
GameplayCue.Ascentia.Player.Death
```

Rules:

- every event can name a fallback cue;
- every content row can override via profile;
- missing cue is a validation error, not silent absence;
- cue execution is cosmetic and must not change gameplay truth;
- cue spam is budgeted and rate-limited.

Cue route table:

```text
DT_PresentationCueRoutes
  EventTag
  DamageSchoolTag
  SurfaceTag
  StatusTag
  AbilityTag
  ItemFamilyTag
  DefaultCueProfile
  FallbackCueProfile
  Priority
  RequiredForMilestone
```

Cue coverage report:

```text
FAscentiaCueCoverageReport
  RunId
  RoutesChecked
  CueProfilesChecked
  GameplayCueAssetsChecked
  MissingRoutes
  MissingCueAssets
  MissingSurfaceVariants
  MissingAccessibilityGroups
  MissingBudgetProfiles
  InvalidFallbacks
```

GameplayCue execution policy:

```text
PDA_GameplayCueExecutionPolicy
  CueTag
  ExecutionAuthority
  ReplicationPolicy
  PredictionPolicy
  LocalOnlyCosmetic
  RequiredPayloadFields
  FallbackOnMissingTarget
```

GameplayCue is a good fit for GAS-owned presentation, especially when effects
need network-aware cosmetic routing. Ascentia should still keep profile data
outside the cue Blueprint when Designers need per-surface, per-school, or
accessibility variants without making duplicate cue classes.

### 14.5 Niagara and VFX architecture

Use Niagara for:

- weapon trails;
- hit sparks and blood;
- status buildup/trigger VFX;
- spell casts and impacts;
- projectile trails;
- loot beams and motes;
- fog walls;
- boss phase effects;
- environmental ambience.

Use Niagara Data Channels for high-volume events:

```text
NDC_AscentiaImpactEvents
  Location
  Normal
  DamageSchool
  SurfaceType
  Magnitude
  Color
  Lifetime

NDC_AscentiaFootstepEvents
  Location
  Foot
  SurfaceType
  Speed
  CharacterWeight

NDC_AscentiaStatusEvents
  Location
  StatusTag
  Intensity
  Radius

NDC_AscentiaLootEvents
  Location
  RarityTag
  BeamHeight
  PulseSeed
```

Ordinary Blueprint-spawned Niagara systems are fine for rare, authored events.
Data Channels should be used when many similar events occur and a shared
simulation can read a stream of payloads.

VFX budgets:

```text
MaxActiveImpactBursts
MaxActiveLootBeams
MaxActiveStatusLoopsPerActor
MaxDecalsPerCombatArea
MaxProjectileTrails
CullDistanceByCuePriority
LowIntensityAccessibilityScale
```

Validation:

- Niagara asset exists;
- user parameters match profile expectations;
- cue profile does not reference editor-only assets;
- data-channel payload layout matches writer code;
- LOD/cull settings exist for expensive systems;
- accessibility group is assigned.

Debug rule learned from the current Niagara trail work: trust component state,
asset refs, and Niagara debug output before trusting a single screenshot. The
presentation sweep should dump component existence, asset path, activation
count, active state, and user parameter values.

Niagara payload contracts:

```text
FAscentiaNiagaraImpactPayload
  Location
  Normal
  Tangent
  DamageSchoolTag
  SurfaceTag
  Magnitude
  Color
  Lifetime
  SourceActorId

FAscentiaNiagaraStatusPayload
  Location
  StatusTag
  Intensity
  Radius
  Duration
  SourceActorId

FAscentiaNiagaraLootPayload
  Location
  RarityTag
  BeamHeight
  PulseSeed
  ItemTag
```

Data Channel writer report:

```text
FAscentiaNiagaraDataChannelReport
  RunId
  ChannelTag
  PayloadsWritten
  PayloadsRejected
  ReaderSystems
  LayoutMismatches
  MissingUserParameters
  TickGroupLatencyFindings
  BudgetSuppressions
```

Visibility report:

```text
FAscentiaNiagaraVisibilityReport
  RunId
  CueProfile
  NiagaraSystem
  SpawnCount
  ActivationCount
  ActiveDuration
  BoundsFindings
  CullDistanceFindings
  RendererFindings
  ScreenshotPaths
  Errors[]
  Warnings[]
```

`EUW_AscentiaNiagaraVisibilityDoctor` should preview a cue profile in a controlled
map, set required user parameters, check bounds/culling/renderer state, and
export a report. This is exactly the tool family that prevents "the trail exists
but is invisible" from becoming a mystery.

### 14.6 Audio and music architecture

Current `USoundBase` one-shots are acceptable MVP proof. P20 target:

```text
UAscentiaAudioStateSubsystem
  OneShotRouter
  LoopRouter
  MusicStateStack
  StingerQueue
  MixStateController
  AccessibilityVolumeScales

PDA_AudioState_*
  StateTag
  MusicSource
  CombatIntensity
  ExplorationLayer
  BossLayer
  StingerSet
  FadeInSeconds
  FadeOutSeconds
  Priority

PDA_AudioCueProfile_*
  EventTag
  SoundCue
  MetaSoundSource
  VolumeRange
  PitchRange
  CooldownSeconds
  MaxConcurrent
  Attenuation
  BusOrMixTag
```

MetaSounds should be used where designers need:

- procedural variation;
- parameterized weapon whooshes;
- status-loop modulation;
- spell charge/release behavior;
- adaptive ambience;
- boss music layers;
- biome wind/weather beds.

Music state should be tag-driven:

```text
Music.Exploration.Zone01
Music.Combat.Minor
Music.Combat.Elite
Music.Boss.Opening
Music.Boss.Enraged
Music.Below.Cathedral
```

The level-placed `BP_MVPArenaMusic` is a good Designer surface proof. P20 should
turn that idea into a state system so music responds to zones, encounters, boss
phases, death, menus, and settings without changing game modes.

Audio event request/result:

```text
FAscentiaAudioEventRequest
  EventTag
  SourceActor
  WorldLocation
  AudioCueProfileTag
  Intensity
  SurfaceTag
  MusicStateTag
  ListenerContext

FAscentiaAudioEventResult
  EventTag
  ResolvedProfile
  PlayedSound
  PlayedMetaSound
  AppliedParameters
  ConcurrencyDecision
  MixState
  SuppressedBySettings
  FailureReasonTag
```

Music state transition:

```text
FAscentiaMusicStateTransition
  PreviousStateTag
  NextStateTag
  RequestSourceTag
  Priority
  FadeOutSeconds
  FadeInSeconds
  StingerTag
  bInterruptsLowerPriority
```

Audio report:

```text
FAscentiaAudioPresentationReport
  RunId
  OneShotsRequested
  OneShotsPlayed
  ConcurrencySuppressions
  MissingMetaSoundParameters
  MusicTransitions
  MixStateChanges
  VolumeSettingResults
  Errors[]
  Warnings[]
```

Sound concurrency is a design tool, not just an engine checkbox. Combat whooshes,
impact layers, UI sounds, ambience, and boss stingers need different concurrency
groups so dense combat stays readable instead of becoming either silence or mud.

### 14.7 Camera, rumble, and hit-stop

Feedback needs profiles, not scattered constants:

```text
PDA_CameraFeedback_*
  CameraShakeClass
  SourceRadius
  Falloff
  IntensityScale
  DurationScale
  FOVKick
  CameraLagOverride
  AccessibilityGroupTag

PDA_RumbleProfile_*
  GamepadEffect
  LeftMotorScale
  RightMotorScale
  Duration
  AccessibilityGroupTag

PDA_HitStopProfile_*
  Duration
  TimeDilation
  AffectedActors
  bSkipForMultiplayer
  AccessibilityGroupTag
```

Rules:

- light hits should be readable but not disruptive;
- heavy/charged/guard-break/critical hits can layer camera, rumble, and hit-stop;
- boss attacks can use Camera Shake Sources for proximity-aware feedback;
- accessibility settings scale or disable camera shake, rumble, flash, and
  hit-stop independently;
- camera feedback never determines hit truth.

Feedback request/result:

```text
FAscentiaCameraFeedbackRequest
  EventTag
  SourceLocation
  TargetActor
  CameraFeedbackProfile
  Magnitude
  DistanceToCamera
  AccessibilityScale

FAscentiaCameraFeedbackResult
  EventTag
  Profile
  AppliedShake
  AppliedFOVKick
  AppliedCameraLag
  SuppressedByDistance
  SuppressedBySettings
  SuppressedByRateLimit

FAscentiaHitStopResult
  EventTag
  Profile
  AppliedDuration
  AffectedActors
  SkippedForNetworkPolicy
  SuppressedBySettings
```

Camera/rumble report:

```text
FAscentiaFeedbackPresentationReport
  RunId
  CameraEvents
  RumbleEvents
  HitStopEvents
  AccessibilityScaleResults
  RateLimitFindings
  DiscomfortRiskFindings
```

### 14.8 Animation presentation architecture

P20 owns the polish layer around the motion system, while section 6 owns the
gameplay contract.

Animation presentation assets:

```text
PDA_AnimationSet_Player
  LocomotionDatabase
  CombatIdleSet
  TurnInPlaceSet
  StartStopSet
  DodgeSet
  HitReactionSet
  AdditiveFlinchSet
  FootIKProfile
  NotifyAuditProfile

PDA_MontagePresentationProfile_*
  Montage
  RequiredNotifies
  CueWindows
  MotionWarpWindows
  CameraWindows
  AudioWindows
  CancelWindows
```

P20 should validate:

- attack montages have strike-window notifies;
- dodge montages have root motion where policy requires it;
- footsteps have surface-aware notify policy;
- weapon trails have start/end or speed-driven fallback;
- hit reactions have physics bodies or additive fallback;
- boss phase montages emit presentation events;
- motion matching databases have coverage for expected locomotion states.

GASP should remain the locomotion presentation layer. Combat code should keep
selecting intent, requests, and gameplay windows; animation assets should make
those requests look authored.

Notify audit contract:

```text
FAscentiaAnimationPresentationAudit
  MontageOrSequence
  RequiredNotifyWindows
  FoundNotifyWindows
  MissingSwingAudioWindows
  MissingTrailWindows
  MissingGameplayCueMarkers
  MissingFootstepSurfacePolicy
  MissingHitReactionFallback
  RootMotionPolicyMatch
  Errors[]
  Warnings[]
```

Animation presentation report:

```text
FAscentiaAnimationPresentationReport
  RunId
  AssetsAudited
  NotifyAuditResults
  RootMotionPolicyFindings
  FootstepPolicyFindings
  HitReactionFindings
  BossPhasePresentationFindings
```

`EUW_AscentiaAnimationNotifyAudit` should show the timeline in Designer terms:
swing audio, trail visibility, damage, cancel, camera, rumble, footstep, and
GameplayCue markers. It should not require reading animation notify class names
to know whether a montage is production-ready.

### 14.9 UI, settings, and accessibility presentation

P20 UI should move toward CommonUI + view-model style:

```text
UAscentiaHUDViewModel
  Health
  Breath
  Eth
  PotionCharges
  TargetHealth
  TargetPoise
  StatusPayloads
  DamageNumberEvents

UAscentiaInventoryViewModel
  Items
  Equipment
  SelectedItem
  Comparison
  SortMode

UAscentiaPresentationSettingsViewModel
  CameraShakeScale
  RumbleScale
  VFXIntensity
  DamageNumberMode
  StatusReadabilityMode
  ProjectileTrailIntensity
  MusicVolume
  SFXVolume
```

Rules:

- gameplay systems push events/view-model changes;
- widgets do not poll every actor every tick;
- CommonUI handles modal focus and layered input;
- Enhanced Input PMI handles remapping presets;
- input glyphs come from data, not hard-coded strings;
- readability settings affect VFX/UI/audio profiles, not gameplay truth.

Accessibility groups:

```text
Accessibility.CameraShake
Accessibility.Rumble
Accessibility.Flash
Accessibility.Blood
Accessibility.DamageNumbers
Accessibility.StatusReadability
Accessibility.ProjectileTrails
Accessibility.LootReadability
Accessibility.BossPresentation
```

Each presentation profile should identify which group scales it.

Presentation settings snapshot:

```text
FAscentiaPresentationSettingsSnapshot
  CameraShakeScale
  RumbleScale
  FlashIntensityScale
  BloodVFXScale
  ImpactVFXScale
  ProjectileTrailScale
  StatusReadabilityMode
  DamageNumberMode
  LootBeamMode
  BossPresentationIntensity
  MusicIntensityScale
```

Settings invariance proof:

```text
FAscentiaPresentationSettingsProof
  ScenarioTag
  BaselineGameplayTelemetryHash
  ReducedIntensityGameplayTelemetryHash
  ChangedPresentationRoutes
  UnchangedGameplayFacts
  Violations[]
```

UI performance report:

```text
FAscentiaUIPresentationReport
  RunId
  WidgetsChecked
  ViewModelsChecked
  BoundAttributes
  TickHeavyWidgets
  InvalidationsPerSecond
  DamageNumberSpawnRate
  CommonUIFocusFindings
  TextOverflowFindings
```

Accessibility is not a post-process blur over the game. It is data attached to
the same cue/audio/camera/UI profiles that own presentation. If a profile cannot
say how it scales, it is not production-ready.

### 14.10 Performance budgets

Presentation must have budgets before the open world multiplies content.

Budget table:

```text
DT_PresentationBudgets
  PlatformProfile
  MaxActiveNiagaraSystems
  MaxImpactEventsPerSecond
  MaxAudioVoicesCombat
  MaxDamageNumbers
  MaxStatusBars
  MaxDecals
  MaxCameraShakesPerSecond
  MaxWidgetInvalidationsPerSecond
```

Runtime budget behavior:

- low-priority impacts can merge into a shared Niagara Data Channel;
- old damage numbers can aggregate;
- distant status loops can downgrade;
- decals can reuse pooled components;
- repeated audio can use concurrency rules;
- camera shakes can rate-limit by source and event tag;
- UI should invalidate only on data change.

Proof needs packaged runtime capture, not only editor PIE.

Runtime budget snapshot:

```text
FAscentiaPresentationBudgetSnapshot
  PlatformProfile
  ActiveNiagaraSystems
  ImpactEventsPerSecond
  AudioVoicesCombat
  DamageNumbers
  StatusBars
  Decals
  CameraShakesPerSecond
  WidgetInvalidationsPerSecond
  GameThreadMs
  RenderThreadMs
  GPUMs
```

Budget report:

```text
FAscentiaPresentationBudgetReport
  RunId
  Map
  PlatformProfile
  ScenarioTag
  BudgetSnapshots
  Maxima
  AverageFrameTimes
  SpikeFrames
  NiagaraSuppressions
  AudioConcurrencySuppressions
  UMGInvalidationFindings
  UnrealInsightsTracePath
  BlockingFailures[]
```

Use Unreal Insights/Slate Insights for broad performance evidence, but keep a
small Ascentia report beside it. Designers need a readable budget verdict even
when the deeper trace exists for engineering.

```text
EUW_AscentiaCameraFeedbackPreview
EUW_AscentiaAccessibilityPresentationMatrix
EUW_AscentiaUIPerformanceAudit
```

Designer-facing validation examples:

- "GameplayCue.Ascentia.Attack.Impact.Slash has no cue profile for Surface.Stone."
- "PDA_CueProfile_SlashImpact references NS_SlashImpact, but User.ImpactNormal
  is not exposed on the Niagara system."
- "MS_GreatswordWhoosh is assigned to heavy attacks but has no intensity
  parameter."
- "CameraFeedback.GuardBreak belongs to Accessibility.CameraShake but ignores
  camera shake scale."
- "WBP_DamageNumberLayer uses bound text attributes on every damage number row;
  use event-driven updates."
- "UE5_2H_Attack_01_Seq has a strike window but no swing-audio notify/profile
  window."
- "PDA_AudioCueProfile_SwordWhoosh has no concurrency group, so dense combat can
  spam overlapping one-shots."
- "NDC_AscentiaImpactEvents expects SurfaceType, but the writer sends SurfaceTag."
- "PDA_PresentationSettingsProfile_LowIntensity does not scale
  Accessibility.Flash."

Evidence folders:

```text
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P20/EventTrace/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P20/CueCoverage/{RunId}/report.json
```

### 14.12 P20 proof gates

Minimum proof:

```text
Tools/remote_playtest.py presentation_smoke
  light attack
  heavy attack
  block/guard break
  dodge
  status trigger
  spell cast
  loot drop
  boss phase
  confirms expected presentation event count and no missing cue routes

Tools/remote_playtest.py cue_coverage
  loads DataAssets/DataTables
  checks every declared event/cue/profile has assets and required parameters

Tools/remote_playtest.py presentation_accessibility
  toggles camera shake, rumble, VFX intensity, damage numbers
  proves presentation scales while gameplay telemetry is unchanged
  emits presentation settings invariance proof

Tools/presentation_sweep.py
  captures component state, Niagara activation, audio counters, cue route,
  screenshot/video, and `choice.json` human verdict fields

Tools/remote_playtest.py presentation_budget
  runs dense combat/loot/status/boss scenario
  captures Ascentia budget report and Unreal Insights trace path
```

Packaged proof:

```text
Ascentia.exe -presentation-smoke
  no missing cue warnings
  no runaway Niagara count
  no audio concurrency spam
  no UMG tick/binding explosion
  stable frame budget in target arena scenario
```

Remote proof IDs:

```text
presentation_event_route
cue_coverage
niagara_data_channel
niagara_visibility
audio_state_transition
camera_rumble_hitstop
animation_notify_audit
presentation_accessibility
ui_invalidation_budget
presentation_packaged_budget
```

P20 is complete only when a Designer can answer:

- Which asset owns this effect?
- Which profile scales it?
- Which setting reduces it?
- Which validator catches it if it breaks?
- Which playtest proves it still fires?

### 14.13 P20 starter implementation package

P20 should begin with a presentation event spine, not with isolated VFX/audio
fixes. The first slice should prove that one combat action can emit a
presentation fact, route through data, play the expected cue/audio/VFX/UI
outputs, respect settings and budgets, and write evidence that explains every
suppression or missing asset.

Reference posture:

- GameplayCues are the right network-aware cosmetic hook for GAS-owned effects,
  but cue Blueprints should not hide all routing decisions. Cue profiles and
  route tables should stay Designer-visible.
- Niagara systems own authored VFX. Niagara Data Channels are the scalable path
  for high-volume repeated events such as impacts, footsteps, status pips, and
  loot glints.
- MetaSounds and Sound Cues should both remain available. Use MetaSounds when
  audio designers need parameterized or procedural behavior; use Sound Cues and
  concurrency assets for simpler authored variations.
- Camera shakes, rumble, hit-stop, and FOV kicks must be profile-driven and
  settings-scaled. They are presentation, never gameplay truth.
- CommonUI/UMG presentation should be event-driven and invalidation-aware. Avoid
  bound attributes or per-widget ticks for dense combat HUD elements.
- Budget proof should produce readable Ascentia reports beside deeper Unreal
  Insights/Slate Insights traces.

The first runtime ownership split:

```text
UAscentiaPresentationSubsystem : UWorldSubsystem
  Receives presentation events, builds route requests, calls cue/audio/VFX/UI/
  camera subsystems, and writes event-route evidence.

UAscentiaCueRouter : UGameInstanceSubsystem
  Resolves `DT_PresentationCueRoutes`, `PDA_CueProfile_*`, fallback profiles,
  GameplayCue tags, and missing-route diagnostics.

UAscentiaAudioStateSubsystem : UGameInstanceSubsystem
  Owns one-shot routing, loop ownership, music state stack, stingers, mix states,
  concurrency diagnostics, and audio accessibility scales.

UAscentiaCameraFeedbackSubsystem : ULocalPlayerSubsystem
  Applies local-only camera shakes, FOV kicks, hit-stop policy, and rumble after
  presentation settings and proximity checks.

UAscentiaVFXBudgetSubsystem : UWorldSubsystem
  Tracks active Niagara systems, data-channel writes, decal pools, visibility
  failures, and suppression decisions by priority.

UAscentiaPresentationSettingsSubsystem : UGameInstanceSubsystem
  Provides the current intensity/accessibility snapshot for camera, rumble,
  flash, blood, trails, damage numbers, loot beams, boss presentation, and music.
```

```text
FAscentiaPresentationRouteResult

  SuppressedByBudget
  SuppressedBySettings
  SuppressedByConcurrency
  MissingRouteReasons
  EvidenceId
```

When the light-attack whoosh or weapon trail vanishes, the report should answer
which of these happened:

- attack notify/window did not emit an event;
- event reached the router but no route matched;
- route matched but cue profile was missing;
- cue profile resolved but asset path was unloaded or invalid;
- Niagara component spawned but did not activate;
- Niagara bounds/cull/renderer/user parameters made it invisible;
- audio profile resolved but concurrency/settings suppressed it;
- packaged build did not cook the referenced asset.

P20 starter tickets:

- P20-001: Add presentation event structs, route request/result, and
  `UAscentiaPresentationSubsystem` with a light-attack swing route proof.
- P20-002: Add `UAscentiaCueRouter`, `DT_PresentationCueRoutes_MVP`,
  starter cue profiles, fallback route policy, and cue coverage audit.
- P20-003: Add Niagara visibility report, weapon-trail/impact cue profiles,
  `EUW_AscentiaNiagaraVisibilityDoctor`, and packaged cook-reference checks.
- P20-004: Add audio state/profile routing, sword whoosh profile, MVPArena music
  state profile, concurrency diagnostics, and audio report.
- P20-005: Add camera/rumble/hit-stop profile structs and settings-scaled local
  feedback proof.
- P20-006: Add presentation settings snapshots, low-intensity profile, and
  gameplay-facts-invariance proof.
- P20-007: Add UI presentation report for HUD/inventory/loot/save/target layers,
  invalidation budget, text overflow, and CommonUI focus notes.
- P20-008: Add presentation budget subsystem, MVP arena budget asset, packaged
  dense-combat budget proof, and Unreal Insights trace path capture.
- P20-009: Add animation notify/cue timeline audit for swing audio, trail,
  damage, cancel, iframe, and hit-reaction windows.
- P20-010: Wire P20 dashboard cards, evidence folders, package gates, and
  Designer-facing EUWs.

Recommended implementation order:

1. P20-001 and P20-002 first. The event spine and cue coverage report make every
   later presentation problem inspectable.
2. P20-003 and P20-004 next because current pain is visible trails and swing
   audio. These should become reports, not guesswork.
3. P20-005 and P20-006 add feel and accessibility without changing gameplay
   truth.
4. P20-007 makes UI performance/readability part of presentation proof.
5. P20-008 should run in packaged builds because budgets are only meaningful in
   the runtime environment the player will actually see.
6. P20-009 closes the montage/notify side, then P20-010 connects evidence to the
   dashboard.

### 14.14 Global performance and scalability starter package

P20 proves presentation budgets, but Ascentia needs a global performance spine
that every production lane can answer. A boss fight, zone, UI screen, asset
pack, character creator preset, weather profile, and packaged build should all
report against the same target-platform budget vocabulary.

The goal is not to optimize everything early. The goal is to make performance
truth visible before content scale hides the cause.

Budget posture:

- measure in packaged Development builds for milestone proof;
- use PIE/editor profiling for fast iteration, not final budget acceptance;
- start with `stat unit`/`stat fps`/targeted stat groups to identify the likely
  bottleneck, then use Unreal Insights traces for durable evidence;
- keep readable Ascentia JSON reports beside `.utrace`, screenshots, logs,
  Asset Audit output, and cook/package reports;
- bind settings presets to UE scalability groups and Device Profiles instead of
  scattering CVars through arbitrary Blueprints;
- treat memory, load time, cook time, shader compile, and package size as
  production budgets, not only frame-rate support issues;
- make Designers see the budget owner: world, AI, VFX, UI, audio, animation,
  material, asset registry, package profile, or plugin adapter.

Target profiles:

```text
PDA_PerformanceTarget_DesktopHigh_MVP
  TargetPlatform=Win64
  Resolution=2560x1440
  TargetFPS=60
  FrameBudgetMs=16.67
  GameThreadBudgetMs=5.5
  RenderThreadBudgetMs=5.5
  GPUBudgetMs=8.0
  MainMemoryBudgetMB=12000
  GPUMemoryBudgetMB=8000
  LoadToGameplayBudgetSec=15
  StreamingHitchBudgetMs=20

PDA_PerformanceTarget_DesktopMid_MVP
  TargetPlatform=Win64
  Resolution=1920x1080
  TargetFPS=60
  FrameBudgetMs=16.67
  GameThreadBudgetMs=6.0
  RenderThreadBudgetMs=6.0
  GPUBudgetMs=9.5
  MainMemoryBudgetMB=8000
  GPUMemoryBudgetMB=6000

PDA_PerformanceTarget_SteamDeckLike_Future
  TargetPlatform=LinuxOrWinHandheld
  Resolution=1280x800
  TargetFPS=40
  FrameBudgetMs=25.0
  GameThreadBudgetMs=8.0
  RenderThreadBudgetMs=8.0
  GPUBudgetMs=14.0
  MainMemoryBudgetMB=6000
  GPUMemoryBudgetMB=4000
```

These numbers are starting targets, not promises. Each must be revisited after
the first real world zone, lighting pass, character creator, and boss encounter
exist.

Runtime/editor layout:

```text
Source/Ascentia/Public/Performance/
  AscentiaPerformanceTypes.h
  AscentiaPerformanceBudgetSubsystem.h
  AscentiaScalabilityProfileTypes.h
```

Designer tools:

```text
EUW_AscentiaPerformanceDashboard
  Shows last scenario result by target, frame budget, hitch count, memory peak,
  trace path, screenshot, and owning phase.

EUW_AscentiaScenarioPerfRunner
  Runs approved local scenarios, displays required editor/package state, target
  profile, trace channels, warmup/capture duration, and output report path.

EUW_AscentiaAssetWeightBrowser
  Lists the largest assets/reference chains by phase, plugin/source, content
  owner, chunk assignment, and package profile.

EUW_AscentiaScalabilityMatrix
  Compares presets against Device Profiles, settings menu rows, accessibility
  policy, and unsupported CVar drift.

EUW_AscentiaHitchTriage
  Opens report spikes by frame, links screenshot/bookmark/trace time, and lets
  engineering assign owner tags such as World, AI, UI, VFX, Audio, Animation,
  Streaming, or Save.
```

Budget ownership table:

```text
World/P17
  loaded cells, HLOD state, PCG counts, foliage density, nav build, streaming
  latency, map readability screenshots

Combat/P11/P13/P14/P15
  montage update cost, root-motion solver cost, hit sweeps, projectile count,
  status tick rate, summon count, ability activation spikes

AI/P18
  perception queries, EQS runs, BT service frequency, crowd/path count, group
  coordination, boss planner spikes

Items/P16/P23
  resolver lookup cost, tooltip generation, affix roll batch cost, inventory
  transaction replay, Asset Manager scan size

Presentation/P20
  Niagara systems, audio voices, decals, camera shakes, damage numbers, Slate
  invalidation, cue route misses

Shell/P21
  load-to-title, settings apply/rollback, package size, localization gather,
  support bundle generation

Pipeline/15.15
  cook time, package time, artifact size, missing reports, release manifest
  completeness
```

Rules:

- Every expensive system must be able to name its owner and budget group.
- A profile is not accepted because it looks good on the author machine. It is
  accepted when the target profile report is green or the risk is documented.
- Scalability presets change presentation fidelity first. They cannot silently
  change combat timing, hit windows, stamina/Breath rules, AI fairness, loot
  rolls, or save semantics.
- Performance captures need warmup time. First-frame shader/streaming spikes
  are tracked separately from steady-state hitches.
- Every report stores build configuration, map, target profile, scenario tag,
  engine version, source revision, and settings/scalability preset.
- A beautiful screenshot with a red frame budget is a failing milestone asset.
- A green average frame with repeated P99 hitches is a failing gameplay route.

Proof matrix:

```text
Proof                                  Evidence
MVP arena dense combat frame budget     packaged report + .utrace + screenshot
Zone traversal streaming budget         report with level/streaming stats
Inventory UI stress budget              Slate/UI trace + text overflow report
Character creator preview budget        avatar/material/groom memory report
Asset weight stays under phase budget   AssetWeight report + SizeMap notes
Scalability preset maps to device data  Scalability audit + settings rows
Low setting preserves gameplay truth    telemetry hash invariance report
Cook/package weight is visible          Cooking Insights path + package report
```

First next package:

1. Implement PERF-001 and PERF-002 before chasing individual hot spots.
2. Implement PERF-003 on `MVP_Arena` because the current gameplay loop already
   exists and can produce proof quickly.
3. Implement PERF-004 before settings/scalability UI ships.
4. Implement PERF-005 before more plugin/content-pack ingestion.

### 14.15 Player photo mode, capture, screenshots, and share-safe media package

P20 owns the sensory language of the game. P21 owns shell, settings, platform
integration, and packaged-product behavior. Photo mode sits across both. It is
not just a camera toy; it is also how the team creates repeatable evidence
screenshots, how store-page shots are planned, how spoiler-sensitive captures
are controlled, and how player-facing media avoids leaking internal paths,
debug strings, save identifiers, or account identifiers.

Relevant engine affordances:

- HighResShot is the first still-image capture path. It supports explicit
  output filenames, crop regions, multipliers, HDR output, masks, GBuffer dumps,
  and timestamped filenames.
- `FHighResScreenshotConfig` is the native wrapper point for filename,
  resolution, HDR, mask, and image-write task parameters.
- Movie Render Queue and Movie Render Graph belong in the production-capture
  lane for cinematic, marketing, and batch renders. They should not be the only
  path for a player pressing a photo-mode capture button.
- Gameplay Camera assets are attractive for Designer-authored camera behavior,
  but the system is experimental. Treat it as a future authoring path until the
  project has a stable P20/P21 camera shell.
- Very large screenshot multipliers are a crash-risk and must be capped by
  profile, platform, and build configuration.

Core data contracts:

```cpp
struct FAscentiaPhotoModeProfile
{
    bool bEnabledInCombat;
    bool bEnabledInBossArena;
    bool bEnabledInDialogue;
    bool bEnabledInCinematics;
    bool bEnabledInBelow;
    bool bPauseWorld;
    float TimeDilation;
    float MaxCameraDistance;
    FGameplayTag CollisionPolicy;
    FGameplayTag SubjectLockPolicy;
    FGameplayTag HudHidePolicy;
    FGameplayTag SpoilerPolicy;
    FGameplayTag SavePathPolicy;
    FGameplayTagContainer CapturePresetTags;
    FGameplayTag AccessibilityPolicyTag;
};

struct FAscentiaPhotoCameraState
{
    FTransform CameraTransform;
    TWeakObjectPtr<AActor> SubjectActor;
    FVector2D OrbitYawPitch;
    float FieldOfView;
    float Aperture;
    float FocusDistance;
    float ExposureCompensation;
    float Roll;
    float CropAspect;
    FGameplayTag CameraModeTag;
};

struct FAscentiaPhotoCaptureRequest
{
    FGuid RequestId;
    FGameplayTag CapturePresetTag;
    FGameplayTag OutputPolicyTag;
    FIntPoint RequestedResolution;
    float ResolutionMultiplier;
    bool bHDR;
    bool bDumpBufferTargets;
    bool bUseMask;
    bool bHideUI;
    bool bDateTimeAsFilename;
    FString CustomFilenameStem;
    FGameplayTag MetadataPolicyTag;
    bool bPlayerInitiated;
};

struct FAscentiaPhotoCaptureResult
{
    bool bSucceeded;
    FString OutputPath;
    FString ThumbnailPath;
    int32 Width;
    int32 Height;
    FString Format;
    int64 FileBytes;
    FAscentiaShareSafeMediaMetadata Metadata;
    FGameplayTag FailureReasonTag;
    TArray<FText> Warnings;
    FString EvidencePath;
};

struct FAscentiaShareSafeMediaMetadata
{
    FString BuildVersion;
    FGameplayTag MapTag;
    FGameplayTag ZoneTag;
    FGameplayTag StoryProgressBand;
    FGameplayTagContainer ContainsSpoilerTags;
    FString CharacterGuidHash;
    FString SaveSlotHash;
    FDateTime CaptureUtc;
    FString Culture;
    FString Platform;
    bool bForEvidenceOnly;
};
```

Subsystem boundary:

```cpp
UCLASS()
class UAscentiaPhotoModeSubsystem : public ULocalPlayerSubsystem
{
    GENERATED_BODY()

public:
    bool CanEnterPhotoMode(const FAscentiaPhotoModeContext& Context) const;
    bool EnterPhotoMode(FGameplayTag ProfileTag);
    void ExitPhotoMode(FGameplayTag ReasonTag);
    bool ApplyCameraState(const FAscentiaPhotoCameraState& State);
    FAscentiaPhotoCaptureRequest BuildCaptureRequest(FGameplayTag PresetTag) const;
    void CapturePhoto(const FAscentiaPhotoCaptureRequest& Request);
    bool WritePhotoEvidence(const FAscentiaPhotoCaptureResult& Result) const;
};
```

Rules:

- Photo mode is a shell/presentation mode. It requests pause, UI focus, input
  context, HUD hiding, and settings access through the P21 shell/CommonUI layer.
- The gameplay world freezes only when the active profile says so. Capture must
  never mutate saves, loot, quest state, or encounter state.
- Player captures hide debug UI and strip internal metadata by default. Evidence
  captures may include overlay/debug metadata and must be marked evidence-only.
- Public metadata must not include raw project paths, OneDrive paths, platform
  account IDs, Epic account IDs, machine usernames, save filenames, or internal
  issue IDs.
- Spoiler policy can block capture, downgrade it to evidence-only, or mark a
  story progress band. Boss intros, unrevealed zones, cinematics, and secret
  quest states need explicit rules.
- Capture presets cap resolution, multipliers, HDR, and buffer dumps by platform
  profile. Shipping player presets default to clean PNG/JPEG-style output.
- Camera movement obeys collision, subject distance, arena bounds, fog-wall
  policy, accessibility sensitivity, and no-clip/debug permissions.
- Photo controls are remappable and must expose keyboard/mouse/gamepad glyphs.
- Storefront, key-art, and cinematic captures use a separate production path so
  marketing needs do not pollute player runtime rules.

Designer-facing tools:

```text
EUW_AscentiaPhotoModePolicyBoard
  Shows every profile, where it can be entered, pause policy, capture cap,
  spoiler behavior, HUD policy, and owner.

EUW_AscentiaPhotoCapturePresetLab
  Previews capture presets, resolution caps, output path policy, HDR/mask flags,
  and estimated file-size risk.

EUW_AscentiaShareSafeScreenshotAudit
  Scans recent screenshots/evidence captures for raw paths, account IDs, save
  IDs, spoiler tags, debug overlays, and missing build/map metadata.

EUW_AscentiaEvidenceScreenshotRunner
  Runs deterministic evidence captures for dashboard cards, docs, bug reports,
  and milestone sign-off.

EUW_AscentiaPhotoCameraRigPreview
  Lets Designers tune orbit/free camera constraints, collision radius,
  subject-lock strength, FOV bounds, and focus controls.

EUW_AscentiaStorefrontShotPlanner
  Tracks planned store/social shots, required map state, character loadout,
  weather/time profile, MRQ/MRG preset, and approval evidence.
```


1. Implement P20C-001 through P20C-003 after the core P20 presentation event

   public-facing store-page shot planning.

### 14.16 Audio, adaptive music, ambience, mix, and soundscape production package

P20 has an audio state subsystem in the starter package, but production audio
needs its own package. Music, ambience, combat whooshes, impacts, UI, dialogue,
weather, boss phases, The Below, and accessibility settings all compete for the
same listener. The goal is not louder audio. The goal is readable sonic
priority, authored emotional state, performance safety, and Designer control.

Engine posture:

- Sound Waves and Sound Cues remain valid for simple authored one-shots and
  variations.
- MetaSounds should own parameterized or procedural sources: weapon whooshes,
  spell charges, wind, weather beds, boss layers, status loops, and stingers.
- Audio Modulation should own global and contextual control buses: master, music,
  voice, gameplay SFX, ambient SFX, UI, low-health emphasis, combat intensity,
  underwater, menu ducking, and accessibility mixes.
- Soundscape can provide dynamic ambient beds for biomes, weather, settlements,
  caves, and The Below, but Ascentia profiles still own when a soundscape is
  valid.
- Audio Gameplay Volumes are spatial listener-region tools for reverb, filters,
  exterior/interior transitions, caves, temples, Below rooms, and occlusion-like
  mix behavior. They are not a replacement for gameplay state.
