Subsystem boundary:

```text
UAscentiaAudioStateSubsystem : UWorldSubsystem
  RequestAudioCue(Request)
  PushMusicState(MusicStateTag, SourceTag)
  PopMusicState(SourceTag)
  RequestStinger(StingerTag, Context)
  ApplyMixProfile(MixProfileTag, Context)
  ApplyAmbienceProfile(AmbienceTag, Context)
  NotifyWorldStateChanged(Tag)
  NotifyEncounterStateChanged(Context)
  NotifySettingsChanged(AudioSettingsSnapshot)
  BuildAudioDebugView()
  WriteAudioEvidence(OutputPath)
```

Rules:

- Gameplay systems emit presentation events or audio requests. They do not play
  arbitrary sounds directly except for prototype-only debug paths reported as
  fallback debt.
- Every combat, UI, music, dialogue, ambience, and weather audio event has a
  profile, settings category, concurrency policy, and evidence path before it
  enters milestone content.
- Music state is stack/priority based. Boss, Below, combat, menu, death, and
  cinematic states should not fight through level Blueprints.
- MetaSound parameters are validated. If a profile declares parameters such as
  intensity, surface, weapon mass, status amount, or weather strength, missing
  bindings block the report.
- Audio Modulation buses are the settings bridge. Sliders and accessibility
  profiles affect buses/mixes, not individual hand-tuned components.
- Soundscape and ambient scatter cannot mask critical gameplay cues. Combat
  readability and subtitle/dialogue clarity win over ambient richness.
- Audio Gameplay Volumes must be authored through Ascentia profiles for
  production caves, interiors, temples, and Below rooms; raw placed volumes are
  allowed only in sandbox maps and reported as fallback debt.
- Packaged evidence must include concurrency suppressions, missing assets,
  missing MetaSound parameters, mix state transitions, and setting-scale results.

Designer-facing tools:

```text
EUW_AscentiaAudioStateBoard
  inspect active music, ambience, mix profiles, cue requests, suppression
  reasons, settings scales, and latest evidence

EUW_AscentiaMusicStateLab
  simulate exploration, combat, boss, Below, menu, death, cinematic, and return
  transitions with fade/stinger/priority proof

EUW_AscentiaMetaSoundParameterAudit
  scan MetaSound profiles for missing inputs, invalid ranges, unbound gameplay
  parameters, fallback assets, and Designer notes

EUW_AscentiaSoundscapeBiomeLab
  preview biome/weather/time/interior ambience profiles, Soundscape palettes,
  scatter density, volume transitions, and critical-cue masking risk

EUW_AscentiaAudioMixAccessibilityLab
  preview master/music/voice/gameplay/ambient/UI sliders, mono, dynamic range,
  menu ducking, low-intensity mix, and subtitle/voice clarity fixtures
```

Evidence folders:

```text
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P20/Audio/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P20/MusicStates/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P20/MetaSounds/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P20/Soundscape/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P20/AudioMixes/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P20/AudioGameplayVolumes/{RunId}/report.json
```

Commandlet contracts:

```text
UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe Ascentia.uproject -run=AscentiaAudioPresentationAudit
  -ProfileRoot=/Game/Ascentia/Audio/Profiles
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P20/Audio

UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe Ascentia.uproject -run=AscentiaMusicStateSimulation
  -ScenarioProfile=/Game/Ascentia/Audio/Profiles/PDA_MusicStateProfile_MVPArena
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P20/MusicStates
```

Starter proof matrix:

```text
AudioCue_SwordWhoosh_ProfileConcurrencySettings
AudioCue_SwordImpact_SurfaceParameter
MusicState_MVPArena_ExploreCombatReturn
MusicState_BossIntroPhaseDefeat
MusicState_BelowEnterGuardianReturn
MetaSound_WeaponWhoosh_ParametersBound
MetaSound_WeatherBed_IntensityBound
Soundscape_Zone01_BiomeWeatherTime
AudioGameplayVolume_CaveInteriorExterior
AudioMix_SettingsSlidersApplyToBuses
AudioMix_MonoDynamicRangeLowIntensity
DialogueDuck_CombatAndMusic
Packaged_Audio_NoMissingAssetsOrConcurrencySpam
```

Implementation tickets:

```text
P20A-001 Audio cue, music state, ambience, and mix profile structs
  Add `FAscentiaAudioCueProfile`, `FAscentiaMusicStateProfile`,
  `FAscentiaAmbienceProfile`, `FAscentiaAudioMixProfile`, and initial assets.

P20A-002 Audio state subsystem routing
  Add `UAscentiaAudioStateSubsystem` request, music stack, stinger, ambience,
  mix, settings, and evidence functions.

P20A-003 Sword whoosh, impact, and dodge audio profile proof
  Convert current combat audio into profiles with concurrency, attenuation,
  settings category, and notify-window evidence.

P20A-004 MVPArena and Below music state proof
  Replace ad hoc level music behavior with exploration/combat/boss/Below/menu
  music state profiles, fades, stingers, and suppression proof.

P20A-005 MetaSound parameter audit
  Add MetaSound profile validation, missing-parameter report, fallback asset
  policy, and first weapon/weather/boss MetaSound fixtures.

P20A-006 Soundscape and Audio Gameplay Volume bridge
  Add biome ambience, cave/interior/Below volume profiles, critical-cue masking
  checks, and Soundscape density reports.

P20A-007 Audio mix accessibility lab
  Add Audio Modulation bus/mix profiles, settings bridge, mono/dynamic range/
  low-intensity proof, and dialogue ducking fixtures.

P20A-008 Dashboard/package gates
  Add P20 audio dashboard cards and block milestone packages on missing profiles,
  missing assets, invalid MetaSound parameters, or unreadable mix evidence.
```

Recommended sequencing:

1. Build P20A-001 through P20A-003 alongside P20-004 so current sword whoosh and
   arena music stop living as fragile one-offs.
2. Add P20A-004 before expanding boss and Below presentation.
3. Add P20A-005 before adopting MetaSounds broadly for combat or magic.
4. Add P20A-006 when Zone01 ambience, UDS weather, or Below room acoustics start.
5. Add P20A-007 before accessibility/storefront claims mention audio options.

## 15. Shell, Settings, Platform, QA, and Ship Readiness

### 15.1 Player-facing goal

The player should experience Ascentia as a coherent product, not a playable test
map. Boot, title, load, character creation, settings, input remapping, save-slot
selection, pause/menu flow, credits, legal, crash recovery, and packaged play all
need to feel deliberate.

P21 is where the project stops saying "press console command" or "use this debug
widget" and starts behaving like a game that can be handed to someone.

### 15.2 Current Ascentia state

Existing useful seams:

- `UAscentiaGameInstance` stores the active save slot name.
- `UAscentiaSaveSubsystem` exposes three MVP save slots, slot summaries, and
  Blueprint-callable save/load functions.
- `AAscentiaPlayerController` exposes exec commands for save/load, save-slot
  cycling, MVP verification, inventory, debug overlays, screenshots, and
  gameplay probes.
- `UAscentiaMenuScreen` has a wireframe system page with Save, Settings, and
  Quit, but Settings is currently disabled in the native fallback.
- `UAscentiaMenuScreen` uses `BindWidgetOptional`, which is the right pattern
  for letting WidgetBlueprints and InterfaceArtForge own visuals while C++ keeps
  a fallback contract.
- Enhanced Input assets exist for gameplay actions such as move, look, dodge,
  interact, inventory, and light attack.
- `UAscentiaMVPVerifyCommandlet` and `FAscentiaMVPSmokeTest` provide the first
  commandlet and automation-test proof layer.
- `DefaultGame.ini` already configures Asset Manager scans and initial packaging
  always-cook directories.

Current limitations:

- no production title/load/save-slot flow;
- no `UAscentiaSettingsSubsystem`;
- no persistent user settings object;
- no input remap persistence;
- no CommonUI/CommonInput shell contract yet;
- no localization target/string-table policy;
- no platform/device profile policy;
- no packaged build profile matrix;
- no crash/log/evidence workflow beyond ad hoc logs;
- no dashboard connection for package, localization, settings, or input proof.

### 15.3 Production shell stack

The shell should be a state machine, not a pile of widgets. CommonUI is valuable
because it was built for layered, cross-platform menu navigation, focus return,
activatable widgets, action bars, and platform glyph differences. Enhanced Input
should continue owning gameplay actions; CommonUI/CommonInput should own menu
action routing, back handling, focus, confirmation modals, and input-mode
changes.

State flow:

```text
Boot
  -> splash/legal/health warnings if needed
  -> title screen
  -> profile/save-slot selection
  -> character creation or load
  -> game
  -> pause/system menu
  -> settings/remap/save/quit
```

Native ownership should begin small but be explicit:

```text
UAscentiaShellSubsystem
  current shell state tag
  active local player
  requested transition queue
  load/save flow status
  modal ownership
  active CommonUI layer
  active gameplay input context policy
  pending map travel/loading state
  quit policy

UAscentiaSettingsSubsystem
  gameplay settings
  accessibility settings
  audio settings
  camera settings
  input settings facade
  graphics/scalability facade
  persistence and rollback

UAscentiaInputProfileSubsystem
  Player Mappable Input Config ownership
  keybind overrides
  glyph lookup
  device-change events

UAscentiaPlatformSubsystem
  build flavor
  platform capabilities
  device profile/scalability summary
  online/offline capability flags
```

Core structs:

```text
EAscentiaShellState
  Boot
  Title
  SaveSlotSelect
  CharacterCreation
  Loading
  InGame
  Paused
  Settings
  InputRemap
  Credits
  Error

FAscentiaShellTransitionRequest
  FromState
  ToState
  RequestSource
  OwningLocalPlayer
  OptionalSlotGuid
  OptionalWorldTravelTarget
  RequiredModalPolicy
  bBlocksGameplayInput
  bRequiresSaveFlush
  TimeoutSeconds

FAscentiaShellStateSnapshot
  State
  ActiveLayerTag
  FocusedWidgetId
  ActiveInputMode
  ActiveMappingContexts
  PendingModalId
  PendingAsyncOperation
  LastErrorTag
```

Transition result:

```text
FAscentiaShellTransitionResult
  RequestId
  FromState
  ToState
  bSucceeded
  FailureReasonTag
  PushedLayers
  PoppedLayers
  FocusRestoredToWidgetId
  InputModeApplied
  MappingContextsApplied
  SaveFlushResult
  LoadingScreenResult
  DurationMs
```

Shell route table:

```text
DT_ShellStates
  StateTag
  ScreenClass
  LayerTag
  AllowedPreviousStates
  AllowedNextStates
  RequiresSaveFlush
  RequiresInputMode
  DefaultFocusWidgetId
  ErrorFallbackState

FAscentiaShellFlowReport
  RunId
  TransitionsTested
  FailedTransitions
  FocusFailures
  InputModeFailures
  ModalOwnershipFailures
  SaveFlushFailures
  Errors[]
  Warnings[]
```

Blueprint ownership:

```text
WBP_AscentiaTitle
WBP_AscentiaSaveSlotScreen
WBP_AscentiaCharacterCreation
WBP_AscentiaSettingsScreen
WBP_AscentiaInputRemapScreen
WBP_AscentiaCredits
WBP_AscentiaLegal
WBP_AscentiaConfirmModal
WBP_AscentiaErrorModal
```

The core rule:

```text
Gameplay input belongs to Enhanced Input.
Menu routing belongs to CommonUI/CommonInput.
Display glyphs come from data, not hard-coded text.
```

### 15.4 Shell flow, CommonUI layers, and modal policy

CommonUI should be introduced through a thin Ascentia shell adapter instead of
letting every widget push itself into the viewport. The goal is that Designers
see named layers and screens, while native code protects transition order,
input-mode changes, save flushing, loading, and errors.

Layer stack:

```text
Layer.GameHUD
  WBP_AscentiaHUD
  gameplay prompts
  loot prompts
  boss bars

Layer.Menu
  title
  pause
  inventory
  character
  map
  settings

Layer.Modal
  confirm
  overwrite
  delete slot
  apply video settings
  error

Layer.Loading
  travel/loading spinner
  save/load progress
  async asset wait

Layer.System
  legal
  credits
  crash recovery/support bundle prompt
```

Policy assets:

```text
PDA_CommonUILayerPolicy_Main
  LayerTag
  RootWidgetClass
  bAllowsInputBelow
  bAllowsMouseCapture
  bRestoresFocusOnPop
  BackActionPolicy
  DefaultInputConfig

PDA_ShellFlow_Main
  ValidTransitions
  DefaultStartState
  ModalScreenMap
  LoadingScreenMap
  ErrorScreenMap
  TransitionAudioCue
  TransitionAnalyticsTag
```

Modal policy:

- Every destructive action needs a modal with an owner, an action tag, and a
  result callback.
- A modal should block gameplay input unless it is explicitly a non-blocking
  notification.
- Closing a modal must restore focus to the screen that opened it.
- Save/load/delete/overwrite flows must be asynchronous and must expose pending,
  success, failure, and timeout states.
- Error modals should have player copy and developer repair metadata; the player
  should not see raw log spam, but the support bundle should contain the raw
  evidence.
- No widget should hard-code "press E" or "press Esc." It asks the action/glyph
  service for the current display.

Input mode transition matrix:

```text
Title/Menu
  UIOnly or GameAndUI depending on platform
  Gameplay contexts removed
  UI action set enabled

InGame
  GameOnly
  Gameplay mapping context active
  UI action set limited to pause/system actions

Paused/Settings/InputRemap
  GameAndUI or UIOnly
  Gameplay contexts paused or lowered
  UI action set owns accept/back/navigation

Loading
  input ignored except system abort/debug in editor builds
```

Proof should include keyboard, mouse, and gamepad focus recovery. If a modal is
opened from settings, dismissed with gamepad back, then settings itself is
dismissed, focus must return to the expected pause-menu element instead of
falling through into gameplay.

CommonUI action row:

```text
DT_CommonUIActions
  ActionTag
  DisplayTextKey
  KeyboardMouseKey
  GamepadKey
  HoldPolicy
  InputType
  AllowedShellStates
  bConsumesGameplayInput
```

Layer stack report:

```text
FAscentiaCommonUILayerReport
  RunId
  ScenarioTag
  LayersPushed
  LayersPopped
  BackActionsHandled
  FocusRestorationResults
  DeviceSwitchResults
  UnexpectedInputLeaks
  Errors[]
  Warnings[]
```

The Settings button in the current menu should become a shell transition request
to `Settings`, not a direct widget creation. That route lets the shell own focus,
input mode, save/apply policy, and rollback behavior.

### 15.5 Settings registry contract

Settings should be data-authored and natively enforced. A setting definition
needs:

```text
SettingId
CategoryTag
DisplayTextKey
TooltipTextKey
ValueType
DefaultValue
AllowedRange
AllowedOptions
PlatformPolicy
RequiresRestart
ApplyFunction
ResetGroup
AccessibilityGroup
TelemetryName
```

Production settings should separate definition, current value, application, and
persistence:

```text
FAscentiaSettingDefinition
  SettingId
  CategoryTag
  DisplayTextKey
  TooltipTextKey
  ValueType
  DefaultValue
  AllowedRange
  AllowedOptions
  PlatformPolicy
  RequiresRestartPolicy
  ApplyPolicy
  ResetGroup
  AccessibilityGroup
  TelemetryName
  ValidationTag

FAscentiaSettingValue
  SettingId
  VariantValue
  Source
  LastAppliedValue
  bIsDirty
  bRequiresRestart

FAscentiaSettingsApplyResult
  SettingId
  Result
  PlayerMessageKey
  DeveloperReason
  bNeedsConfirmation
  RollbackSeconds

UAscentiaUserSettingsSaveGame
  SettingsVersion
  UserProfileGuid
  SettingValues
  InputBindingOverrides
  LastCulture
  LastDisplayMode
  LastKnownDeviceProfile
```

Setting categories:

- gameplay: input buffering, lock-on behavior, camera assists, auto-target
  preferences, prompt density;
- controls: remapping, mouse sensitivity, gamepad sensitivity, inversion,
  dead zones, hold/toggle options;
- camera: shake strength, FOV, lock-on camera strength, motion blur,
  cinematic intensity;
- audio: master/music/SFX/voice/UI volumes, dynamic range, subtitles;
- video: display mode, resolution, VSync, frame cap, brightness/gamma,
  scalability preset, DLSS/TSR/vendor options if supported;
- accessibility: text size, colorblind palettes, damage-number density,
  combat feedback intensity, rumble, flashes, subtitle style, menu narration if
  supported;
- interface: HUD scale, item tooltip verbosity, input glyph mode, inventory sort
  defaults.

Apply policy:

```text
ImmediateSafe
  audio volume
  subtitle toggle
  camera shake scale
  HUD scale

ImmediateWithPreview
  brightness/gamma
  colorblind profile
  damage-number density
  menu text size

ApplyThenConfirm
  resolution
  display mode
  monitor
  HDR toggle

RequiresRestart
  culture set if package data is not loaded
  renderer feature requiring startup config
  plugin/platform-sensitive option
```

Persistence:

- per-user settings live outside character saves;
- character-specific preferences, if any, must be explicit;
- settings save immediately or through an Apply/Revert window depending on risk;
- display-mode and resolution changes need confirmation and rollback;
- remaps must serialize by action identity, not widget text;
- every setting needs a default, a reset-to-category operation, and a reset-all
  operation.

The video/scalability bridge should use `UGameUserSettings` for engine-owned
display and scalability values, while `UAscentiaSettingsSubsystem` remains the
game-facing facade. Gameplay, accessibility, and UI preferences should not be
hidden in generic graphics presets. When scalability changes affect world
budgets, the relevant presentation/world budget profiles should be re-applied
through named Ascentia policy assets.

Validation examples:

- "Setting Gameplay.LockOnAssist has no default value for gamepad."
- "Setting Video.DisplayMode requires confirmation but has no rollback copy."
- "Accessibility.CameraShakeScale affects camera shakes but no cue profile reads
  it."
- "UI.HudScale has English text but no Ascentia.Settings string-table key."
- "Video.FoliageQuality changes foliage CVars but no packaged proof profile
  captures a world-zone benchmark."

Settings transaction:

```text
FAscentiaSettingsChangeRequest
  SettingId
  RequestedValue
  SourceScreen
  PreviewOnly
  LocalUserIndex

FAscentiaSettingsChangeResult
  SettingId
  PreviousValue
  RequestedValue
  AppliedValue
  ApplyResult
  bNeedsConfirmation
  RollbackDeadlineUtc
  FailureReasonTag

FAscentiaSettingsTransaction
  TransactionId
  ChangedSettings
  RequiresRestartSettings
  RequiresConfirmationSettings
  PreviewResults
  CommitResults
  RollbackResults
```

Settings proof report:

```text
FAscentiaSettingsPersistenceReport
  RunId
  SettingsChanged
  SaveResult
  RestartLoadResult
  RestoredValues
  RollbackResults
  UGameUserSettingsResults
  MissingDefaults
  Errors[]
  Warnings[]
```

`EUW_AscentiaSettingsRegistry` should show every setting, default value,
platform policy, persistence owner, apply function, localization key, and proof
coverage. A setting that cannot name its apply function or proof scenario is not
ready for a settings screen.

### 15.6 Input remapping and glyphs

Enhanced Input should own gameplay mapping. Player Mappable Input Configs should
own player-facing presets. UI should ask for a display glyph by action identity.

Data:

```text
DT_InputActions
  ActionTag
  InputAction
  DefaultKeyboardMouse
  DefaultGamepad
  GlyphIdKeyboard
  GlyphIdGamepadXbox
  GlyphIdGamepadPlayStation
  GlyphIdSteamDeck
  Remappable
  ConflictGroup

PDA_InputProfile
  ProfileTag
  PlayerMappableInputConfig
  PlatformPolicy
  DefaultGlyphSet
```

Runtime remap state:

```text
FAscentiaInputBindingOverride
  ActionTag
  InputProfileTag
  DeviceType
  Slot
  Key
  ModifierKeys
  ConflictResolution
  Source

FAscentiaInputConflict
  PrimaryActionTag
  ConflictingActionTag
  ConflictGroup
  DeviceType
  Key
  Severity
  SuggestedResolution
```

Validation:

- every remappable gameplay action has a display name and glyph;
- every required action has at least one keyboard/mouse and one gamepad default;
- no two actions in the same conflict group share a blocking binding unless
  deliberately allowed;
- UI prompts reference action tags, not raw keys;
- platform glyph packs have complete coverage.

Remapping policy:

- Store overrides by action identity and mapping slot, never by localized display
  text.
- Separate gameplay remaps from menu action bindings, while keeping shared
  glyph lookup.
- Let Designers mark conflict groups such as movement, combat, camera, menu,
  debug, photo, and accessibility.
- Support per-device preview rows for keyboard/mouse, Xbox, PlayStation, Steam
  Deck, and generic gamepad.
- Treat "unbound required action" as a blocking validation error.
- Keep editor/debug actions out of the shipping remap screen.
- Add import/export/reset for input profiles once the player profile system is
  stable.

Designer surface:

- `WBP_AscentiaInputRemapScreen`;
- `EUW_AscentiaInputGlyphBinder`;
- conflict preview;
- reset profile button;
- keyboard/mouse and controller preview rows;
- pseudo-device test mode.

Proof should cover:

```text
Tools/remote_playtest.py input_remap_smoke
  remap LightAttack from LMB to keyboard key
  confirm LMB no longer attacks
  confirm new key attacks
  restart game
  confirm override persisted
  reset profile
  confirm LMB attacks again

Tools/remote_playtest.py glyph_smoke
  switch keyboard/mouse, Xbox, PlayStation, Steam Deck modes
  capture title, pause, loot prompt, inventory, settings, spell HUD
  report missing glyphs and mismatched action tags
```

Input profile report:

```text
FAscentiaInputProfileReport
  RunId
  ProfilesChecked
  ActionsChecked
  RequiredActionsMissingDefaults
  MissingGlyphs
  ConflictFindings
  PersistedOverrides
  ResetResults
  DevicePreviewScreenshots
```

Glyph lookup result:

```text
FAscentiaInputGlyphLookupResult
  ActionTag
  DeviceType
  PlatformTag
  GlyphId
  GlyphBrush
  DisplayText
  bFallbackUsed
  FailureReasonTag
```

Enhanced Input's Player Mappable Input Configs are the right source for gameplay
presets, but the shell still needs an Ascentia report around them: required
actions, conflict groups, glyph coverage, persistence, and reset must be visible
to Designers.

### 15.7 Localization contract

All user-facing text should be `FText`, string tables, DataTables with text
fields, or localized assets. Permanent `FString` UI copy should be treated as
technical debt.

Namespaces:

```text
Ascentia.UI
Ascentia.Items
Ascentia.Skills
Ascentia.Spells
Ascentia.World
Ascentia.Dialogue
Ascentia.Settings
Ascentia.Tutorial
Ascentia.Errors
```

Recommended source layout:

```text
StringTables/Ascentia_UI
StringTables/Ascentia_Settings
StringTables/Ascentia_Items
StringTables/Ascentia_Combat
StringTables/Ascentia_World
StringTables/Ascentia_Errors
Config/Localization/Ascentia_Game.ini
Config/Localization/Ascentia_EditorTools.ini
```

Pipeline:

1. Author text in DataTables, DataAssets, WidgetBlueprints, or string tables.
2. Gather text through Localization Dashboard targets.
3. Run pseudo-localization and font stress screenshots.
4. Validate no runtime user-facing text is assembled from unlocalized strings.
5. Export/import translations when localization becomes real.
6. Package with the selected culture set.

Proof:

- pseudo-localized title/settings/inventory/loot flow;
- missing text-key report;
- font fallback report;
- overflow screenshot report;
- controller glyph text stress;
- language switch smoke in a Development build.

Localization should be started before full translation. The immediate purpose is
to catch bad architecture: hard-coded text, string concatenation, text overflow,
font fallback gaps, unlocalized error copy, bad glyph/text combinations, and
widgets that cannot survive longer languages. P21 should introduce a
pseudo-localization proof even if the game ships only in English for a long
time.

Font and layout policy:

- UI fonts need Latin, extended Latin, punctuation, symbols, and controller glyph
  compatibility first.
- CJK, Cyrillic, Arabic, and right-to-left needs should be treated as future
  requirements with explicit risk notes, not accidental "maybe later" scope.
- WidgetBlueprints should prefer layout containers over manually placed text.
- Designers should see screenshot failures with widget path, culture, DPI scale,
  resolution, and text key.
- Dynamic text should use format arguments, not manual `FString` concatenation.

Localization target profile:

```text
PDA_LocalizationTargetProfile
  TargetName
  NativeCulture
  SupportedCultures
  GatherConfigPath
  StringTableRoots
  WidgetRoots
  DataAssetRoots
  FontPolicy
  PseudoLocalizationPolicy
  ScreenshotScenarios
```

Localization report:

```text
FAscentiaLocalizationReport
  RunId
  TargetName
  CulturesChecked
  TextKeysGathered
  MissingTextKeys
  HardCodedTextFindings
  StringConcatFindings
  FontFallbackFindings
  OverflowScreenshots
  GlyphTextFindings
  Errors[]
  Warnings[]
```

`EUW_AscentiaLocalizationAndFontStress` should run pseudo-localized screenshots
for the screens named in the target profile and produce fix links to the widget,
text key, string table, or data asset that failed.

### 15.8 Save slots, profiles, and character selection

P19 defines save data. P21 defines how the player touches it.

Required screens:

- title screen;
- continue;
- load game;
- new game;
- save slot detail;
- delete slot confirmation;
- overwrite confirmation;
- character creation resume;
- cloud/local conflict screen if cloud saves are later supported.

Slot header:

```text
SlotGuid
SlotDisplayName
SaveVersion
BuildVersion
LastPlayedUtc
CharacterName
CharacterLevel
Origin
ClassIdentity
PlayTimeSeconds
LastRegion
LastCheckpoint
PreviewImage
CorruptionState
MigrationState
```

The active save slot currently lives as a simple name in `UAscentiaGameInstance`.
Production should move to a richer local profile plus slot headers, while
keeping `UAscentiaSaveSubsystem` as the facade used by UI and gameplay.

Save UI should include migration and corruption states:

```text
Clean
  playable now

NeedsMigration
  playable after migration
  show build/version info

MigrationFailed
  not playable until repaired
  offer duplicate/backup if supported

Corrupt
  not playable
  explain support bundle path

FutureVersion
  created by newer build
  read-only or blocked
```

Screens should never silently delete or overwrite. Character creation should be
resume-safe: if a player starts character creation and exits, the slot should
clearly show incomplete creation rather than a broken save.

Profile and slot report:

```text
FAscentiaSaveSlotUIReport
  RunId
  SlotsDiscovered
  SlotHeadersLoaded
  MigrationStatesShown
  DeleteConfirmations
  OverwriteConfirmations
  FutureVersionFindings
  CorruptSlotFindings
  CharacterCreationResumeResults
  Errors[]
  Warnings[]
```

Local profile:

```text
FAscentiaLocalProfileState
  LocalUserIndex
  ProfileGuid
  LastSelectedSlotGuid
  LastInputProfileTag
  LastCulture
  UserSettingsVersion
  bAcceptedLegal
  RecentSlots
```

### 15.9 Platform, scalability, and build profiles

Ascentia needs named build targets:

```text
EditorDevelopment
PIESmoke
PackagedDevelopment
PackagedTest
PackagedShipping
ContentValidation
LocalizationValidation
PerformanceCapture
```

Build profile asset:

```text
PDA_PackageProfile_Win64Development
  TargetPlatform
  ClientConfig
  CookPolicy
  MapList
  CultureList
  AssetAuditPolicy
  PakIoStorePolicy
  PrereqPolicy
  ArchiveDirectory
  PostPackageSmokeScenario
  EvidenceOutputDirectory

FAscentiaBuildEvidenceReport
  ProfileId
  BuildVersion
  ChangelistOrCommit
  EngineVersion
  TargetPlatform
  Config
  StartUtc
  EndUtc
  CookResult
  PackageResult
  SmokeResult
  WarningCount
  ErrorCount
  MissingAssetCount
  LocalizationMissingCount
  LogPath
  ArtifactPath
  DashboardCardPath
```

Device/scalability policy:

- Default scalability presets must be visible in settings.
- Device profiles own platform/hardware-specific CVars.
- Gameplay settings must not silently change scalability CVars.
- Accessibility settings must not be hidden inside "Low/Medium/High."
- Niagara, weather, shadow, foliage, post-process, UI, audio, and world
  streaming budgets need named profiles.

Packaging gates:

```text
Build
Cook
Stage
Package
Deploy/Run when applicable
Automation smoke
Validation reports
Dashboard evidence
```

P21 should add `Tools/package_ascentia.ps1` or equivalent only after the build
profile matrix is written down. That script should call Unreal Automation Tool
or the Project Launcher profile path, collect logs, normalize warnings, and
write a JSON evidence card.

No packaging script should be considered "done" if it only prints console logs.
It should produce artifacts that the Dashboard and a human can read:

```text
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/Package/{ProfileId}/{Timestamp}/build_evidence.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/Package/{ProfileId}/{Timestamp}/warnings.csv
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/Package/{ProfileId}/{Timestamp}/asset_audit.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/Package/{ProfileId}/{Timestamp}/localization_report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/Package/{ProfileId}/{Timestamp}/smoke_result.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/Package/{ProfileId}/{Timestamp}/screenshots/
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/Package/{ProfileId}/{Timestamp}/logs/
```

Package command request/result:

```text
FAscentiaPackageRequest
  ProfileId
  UProjectPath
  TargetPlatform
  ClientConfig
  CookMaps
  CultureList
  ArchiveDirectory
  bRunPostPackageSmoke
  bCollectInsightsTrace

FAscentiaPackageResult
  ProfileId
  bSucceeded
  BuildResult
  CookResult
  StageResult
  PackageResult
  SmokeResult
  WarningSummary
  ErrorSummary
  ArtifactPaths
  EvidencePath
```

Asset/package audit:

```text
FAscentiaPackageAssetAudit
  ProfileId
  AssetsCooked
  MissingAssets
  EditorOnlyAssetRefs
  FallbackContentRefs
  UnusedAlwaysCookDirs
  LocalizationCulturesCooked
  PrimaryAssetScanFindings
```

`EUW_AscentiaPackageProfileManager` should not be a decorative launcher. It
should show the exact UAT/build operation command, maps, cultures, always-cook
roots, smoke scenario, output folder, and last evidence card for each profile.

### 15.10 QA matrix, automation, and dashboard gates

Automation should be layered by cost. Fast C++ smoke tests protect boot-critical
contracts. Commandlets validate content. Functional tests and remote PIE prove
flows. Packaged/Gauntlet-style scenarios prove that the cooked game behaves.
Screenshot comparison and pseudo-localization catch UI regressions that code
tests will miss.

QA proof profile:

```text
PDA_QAProofProfile_P21Shell
  ScenarioId
  RequiredBuildProfile
  RequiredMap
  RequiredCulture
  RequiredInputDevice
  Steps
  ExpectedSignals
  ScreenshotPoints
  LogFailurePatterns
  EvidenceSchema
```

Minimum P21 proof scenarios:

```text
title_boot
  boot packaged Development build
  reach title
  verify no fatal startup warnings

settings_persistence
  change audio, camera, accessibility, HUD scale
  restart
  verify values restored

video_apply_rollback
  change display setting
  decline confirmation
  verify rollback

input_remap
  remap, use action, restart, reset

localization_pseudoloc
  capture title, save slot, settings, input remap, inventory, loot, HUD

save_slot_migration
  load old fixture
  show migration state
  migrate
  verify header

packaged_smoke
  boot, load arena, move, dodge, light attack, loot, save, quit

support_bundle_smoke
  trigger controlled error
  confirm player copy
  confirm evidence bundle contains logs/report/session metadata
```

Dashboard cards:

```text
Shell
  last title boot
  last transition failure
  modal/focus failures

Settings
  last persistence proof
  dirty settings count
  missing defaults

Input
  unmapped required actions
  glyph coverage
  conflict count

Localization
  missing keys
  pseudo-loc screenshot failures
  font fallback warnings

Package
  last green package
  last failed profile
  warning trend

Support
  crash/support bundle smoke
  known fatal log patterns
```

QA run report:

```text
FAscentiaQAProofRunReport
  RunId
  ProfileTag
  BuildProfile
  ScenarioResults
  ScreenshotResults
  LogFailureMatches
  RequiredSignalsMissing
  EvidencePaths
  DashboardSummary
  BlockingFailures[]
```

Proof scenario result:

```text
FAscentiaQAProofScenarioResult
  ScenarioId
  BuildProfile
  Map
  Culture
  InputDevice
  StepsRun
  ExpectedSignalsObserved
  ScreenshotsCaptured
  FailureReasonTag
  DurationSeconds
```

`EUW_AscentiaQAProofMatrix` should let the team see which phase gates have
evidence, which are stale, and which only passed in PIE. The distinction matters:
packaged proof catches cook, Asset Manager, config, localization, and input
issues that the editor can mask.

### 15.11 Crash, logging, telemetry, and support evidence

Shipping readiness needs a logging taxonomy:

```text
LogAscentiaCombat
LogAscentiaInventory
LogAscentiaSave
LogAscentiaUI
LogAscentiaWorld
LogAscentiaAI
LogAscentiaPresentation
LogAscentiaBuild
```

Additional P21-specific categories:

```text
LogAscentiaShell
LogAscentiaSettings
LogAscentiaInputProfile
LogAscentiaLocalization
LogAscentiaPackage
LogAscentiaSupport
```

Every system should log with:

- category;
- actor/system identity;
- relevant gameplay tag or asset ID;
- save slot or session ID where safe;
- concise failure reason;
- suggested repair when the failure is content-authored.

Support bundle:

```text
FAscentiaSupportBundle
  SessionId
  BuildVersion
  EngineVersion
  Platform
  DeviceProfile
  ActiveMap
  ActiveSaveSlotGuid if safe
  LastShellState
  LastInputDevice
  Culture
  RecentErrors
  SanitizedLogs
  ValidationReports
  ScreenshotPath
```

Support bundle report:

```text
FAscentiaSupportBundleReport
  BundleId
  CreatedAtUtc
  SanitizationPolicy
  IncludedLogs
  IncludedReports
  IncludedScreenshots
  IncludedSettingsSnapshot
  IncludedInputProfile
  PlayerFacingMessageKey
  DeveloperSummary
  MissingExpectedArtifacts[]
```

Log failure pattern:

```text
FAscentiaLogFailurePattern
  PatternId
  LogCategory
  RegexOrSubstring
  Severity
  PlayerFacingMessageKey
  SuggestedRepair
  BlocksPackageProfile
```

The dashboard should ingest:

- commandlet results;
- automation tests;
- packaged smoke tests;
- validation reports;
- presentation sweeps;
- settings/remap/localization evidence;
- crash summaries if available.

Telemetry should be conservative and optional. For the compendium, the important
part is not analytics monetization; it is reproducibility. When a player reports
"settings broke" or "input disappeared," the project needs a support artifact
that names the build, culture, input profile, shell state, and relevant logs.

The Settings button in the current system menu should eventually open the real

P21 gap, not an accidental dead button.

### 15.13 P21 proof gates

P21 is not complete until:

1. A packaged Development build boots to title.
2. Continue/load/new game flow works without console commands.
3. Settings persist across restart.
4. Input remaps persist across restart.
5. Keyboard/mouse and gamepad glyphs come from the same action data.
6. Inventory, loot, HUD, save slots, settings, and title pass
   pseudo-localization stress.
7. Build/cook/package logs are collected and summarized.
8. Commandlet and automation smoke are clean.
9. Data Validation is clean for all shippable content roots.
10. Dashboard shows last green package, last failed gate, and evidence links.
11. CommonUI layer push/pop/back behavior survives keyboard, mouse, and gamepad.
12. Settings rollback works for risky video/display changes.
13. Input remap conflict rules are visible in the UI and enforced by validation.
14. Pseudo-localization screenshots include title, settings, inventory, loot,
    HUD, and error modals.
15. Support bundle smoke produces a readable report without exposing raw internal
    copy to the player.

Remote proof IDs:

```text
shell_title_boot
shell_commonui_focus
settings_persistence
settings_video_rollback
input_remap_persistence
input_glyph_coverage
localization_pseudoloc
save_slot_ui_migration
package_development_smoke
qa_proof_matrix
support_bundle_smoke
```

### 15.14 P21 starter implementation package

The first P21 slice should prove that Ascentia can boot like a product, open a
real settings stack, persist safe settings, run a packaged smoke, and write
evidence. It does not need every final screen, but it must establish the shell
and settings spine correctly.

Runtime module files:

```text
Source/Ascentia/Public/Shell/AscentiaShellTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Shell/AscentiaShellSubsystem.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Settings/AscentiaSettingsTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Settings/AscentiaSettingsSubsystem.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Input/AscentiaInputProfileSubsystem.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Platform/AscentiaPlatformSubsystem.h

Source/Ascentia/Private/Shell/AscentiaShellSubsystem.cpp
Source/Ascentia/Private/Settings/AscentiaSettingsSubsystem.cpp
Source/Ascentia/Private/Input/AscentiaInputProfileSubsystem.cpp
Source/Ascentia/Private/Platform/AscentiaPlatformSubsystem.cpp
```

Editor/module files:

```text
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Shell/AscentiaShellValidation.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Settings/AscentiaSettingsValidation.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Package/AscentiaPackageTypes.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/QA/AscentiaQAProofTypes.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Support/AscentiaSupportBundleTypes.h

Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Settings/AscentiaSettingsValidation.cpp

Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Commandlets/AscentiaQAProofCommandlet.cpp

Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Tests/AscentiaShellFlowTest.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Tests/AscentiaSettingsPersistenceTest.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Tests/AscentiaPackageProfileTest.cpp
```

Shell subsystem signatures:

```cpp
UCLASS()
class UAscentiaShellSubsystem : public UGameInstanceSubsystem
{
    GENERATED_BODY()

public:
    UFUNCTION(BlueprintCallable, Category="Ascentia|Shell")
    FAscentiaShellTransitionResult RequestShellTransition(
        const FAscentiaShellTransitionRequest& Request);

    UFUNCTION(BlueprintPure, Category="Ascentia|Shell")
    FAscentiaShellStateSnapshot GetShellStateSnapshot() const;

    UFUNCTION(BlueprintCallable, Category="Ascentia|Shell")
    void PushModal(const FGameplayTag ModalTag, UObject* Owner);

    UFUNCTION(BlueprintCallable, Category="Ascentia|Shell")
    void PopModal(const FGameplayTag ModalTag);

    UFUNCTION(BlueprintCallable, Category="Ascentia|Shell")
    void RecordShellError(const FGameplayTag ErrorTag, const FText& PlayerText);
};
```

Rules:

- Shell transitions are requested, not improvised by widgets.
- State changes produce `FAscentiaShellTransitionResult`.
- Title, save-slot select, settings, input remap, pause, and error modal routes
  must all be representable in `DT_ShellStates`.
- Shell code may ask settings/input/save subsystems for work, but it must not own
  their data.
- Every failed transition needs a player-safe message key and developer reason.

Settings subsystem signatures:

```cpp
UCLASS()
class UAscentiaSettingsSubsystem : public UGameInstanceSubsystem
{
    GENERATED_BODY()

public:
    UFUNCTION(BlueprintCallable, Category="Ascentia|Settings")
    void LoadUserSettings();

    UFUNCTION(BlueprintCallable, Category="Ascentia|Settings")
    FAscentiaSettingsChangeResult PreviewSettingChange(
        const FAscentiaSettingsChangeRequest& Request);

    UFUNCTION(BlueprintCallable, Category="Ascentia|Settings")
    FAscentiaSettingsChangeResult ApplySettingChange(
        const FAscentiaSettingsChangeRequest& Request);

    UFUNCTION(BlueprintCallable, Category="Ascentia|Settings")
    FAscentiaSettingsTransaction CommitPendingSettings();

    UFUNCTION(BlueprintCallable, Category="Ascentia|Settings")
    FAscentiaSettingsTransaction RollbackPendingSettings();

    UFUNCTION(BlueprintPure, Category="Ascentia|Settings")
    FAscentiaSettingValue GetSettingValue(FName SettingId) const;
};
```

The first setting set should be boring and representative:

```text
Audio.MasterVolume
Audio.MusicVolume
Audio.SFXVolume
Camera.ShakeScale
Camera.InvertY
UI.HudScale
UI.TextScale
Accessibility.FlashIntensity
Accessibility.RumbleScale
Video.WindowMode
Video.Resolution
Video.VSync
```

`UGameUserSettings` should own engine display/scalability values such as
resolution, display mode, VSync, and frame cap. `UAscentiaSettingsSubsystem`
should own Ascentia's registry, apply policy, rollback behavior, user-save
payload, accessibility settings, and the UI-facing facade. This keeps engine
graphics behavior on the supported path while still making game-specific
settings visible and testable.

Input profile subsystem signatures:

```cpp
UCLASS()
class UAscentiaInputProfileSubsystem : public ULocalPlayerSubsystem
{
    GENERATED_BODY()

public:
    UFUNCTION(BlueprintCallable, Category="Ascentia|Input")
    FAscentiaInputProfileReport LoadInputProfile(FGameplayTag ProfileTag);

    UFUNCTION(BlueprintCallable, Category="Ascentia|Input")
    FAscentiaInputProfileReport ApplyBindingOverride(
        const FAscentiaInputBindingOverride& Override);

    UFUNCTION(BlueprintPure, Category="Ascentia|Input")
    FAscentiaInputGlyphLookupResult GetGlyphForAction(
        FGameplayTag ActionTag,
        FGameplayTag DeviceTag) const;

    UFUNCTION(BlueprintCallable, Category="Ascentia|Input")
    FAscentiaInputProfileReport SaveInputOverrides();
};
```

Rules:

- Enhanced Input remains the gameplay input authority.
- Player-facing remaps are saved by action/tag identity, not localized text.
- CommonUI action rows and HUD prompts ask the glyph service for display.
- Conflict groups must be visible in the remap UI and in validation.
- Input remap persistence is a P21 proof gate, not polish.

Platform and package structs:

```text
FAscentiaPackageRequest
  PackageProfile
  BuildConfiguration
  TargetPlatform
  MapList
  CookCultures
  ArchiveRoot
  bRunAfterPackage
  bCollectLogs
  bFailOnWarnings

FAscentiaPackageResult

```

First package profiles:

```text
PDA_PackageProfile_DevSmoke
  Development Win64

  English only
  non-shipping logs enabled
  run packaged smoke

PDA_PackageProfile_LocalizationStress
  Development Win64
  MVP_Arena

PDA_PackageProfile_ContentAudit

  all required maps
  scans Asset Manager primary assets, always-cook roots, and plugin content
```

`Tools/package_ascentia.ps1` contract:

```text
Inputs
  -Profile DevSmoke
  -Configuration Development
  -Platform Win64
  -RunSmoke
  -ReportRoot Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P21/Package

Outputs
  report.json
  package.log
  uat_command.txt
  asset_audit.json
  smoke_summary.json
  support_bundle_preview.json
```

The script should wrap UAT `BuildCookRun`, write the exact command line, preserve
logs, normalize exit codes, and emit `FAscentiaPackageResult`. Project Launcher
profiles can come later when the profile matrix stabilizes.

Localization starter package:

```text
PDA_LocalizationTargetProfile_UI
  TargetName
  NativeCulture
  Cultures
  StringTables
  GatherPaths
  ExcludedPaths
  RequiredScreens

ST_Ascentia_UI
ST_Ascentia_Settings
ST_Ascentia_Items
ST_Ascentia_Errors
```

`FText` is the boundary for user-facing text. `FString` is allowed for developer
logs, IDs, filenames, tags, and internal debug. UI copy should be string-table
backed or localization-target backed before it becomes production shell copy.

Support bundle structs:

```text
FAscentiaSupportBundle
  BundleId
  CreatedUtc
  BuildVersion
  PackageProfile
  UserFacingReason
  IncludedReports
  IncludedLogs
  IncludedSettingsSnapshot
  IncludedSaveSlotSummaries
  IncludedScreenshots
  PrivacyRedactions

FAscentiaSupportBundleReport
  BundlePath
  FilesIncluded
  FilesSkipped
  RedactionsApplied
  MissingExpectedArtifacts
  Result
  Errors[]
  Warnings[]
```

Support bundle rules:

- never include full save files by default;
- include save-slot summaries and schema versions;
- redact local usernames from paths when exported;
- include settings snapshot, package result, recent shell errors, and last
  validation report;
- include raw logs only inside the bundle, not in player-facing UI copy.

P21 dashboard card:

```text
FAscentiaP21DashboardCard
  LastShellFlowRun
  LastSettingsPersistenceRun
  LastInputRemapRun
  LastLocalizationStressRun
  LastPackageRun
  LastSupportBundleRun
  LastGreenPackagePath
  BlockingFailures
  HumanVerdict
```

First P21 implementation tickets:

```text
P21-001 Add shell/settings/input/platform subsystem stubs
  acceptance: game boots, subsystems initialize, no runtime behavior changes

P21-002 Add settings registry structs and first safe settings
  acceptance: audio/camera/UI/accessibility values preview/apply/rollback

P21-003 Bridge engine display settings through UGameUserSettings
  acceptance: window mode/resolution/VSync preview, confirm, rollback, persist

P21-004 Add input glyph/remap facade
  acceptance: LMB, E, I, dodge, pause, accept, back resolve through action tags

P21-005 Add shell transition reporting
  acceptance: title, in-game, pause, settings, input remap, error route produce
  `FAscentiaShellTransitionResult`

P21-006 Add `Tools/package_ascentia.ps1` DevSmoke profile
  acceptance: UAT command is recorded, package result JSON is written, package
  log path is linked

P21-007 Add localization/string-table audit
  acceptance: title/settings/inventory/loot/HUD text keys are reported

P21-008 Add support bundle writer
  acceptance: bundle contains settings snapshot, package report, log excerpt,
  shell errors, save-slot summaries, and privacy redaction report

P21-009 Add P21 dashboard card writer
  acceptance: dashboard can show last green package, last failed gate, and links
  to shell/settings/input/localization/package/support evidence
```

Starter proof sequence:

```text
shell_title_boot
  boot to title or title placeholder through shell transition

settings_persistence
  change safe settings, save, reload, confirm restored values

settings_video_rollback
  preview risky display setting, confirm rollback path exists

input_glyph_coverage
  resolve keyboard/mouse and gamepad glyphs for core actions

package_development_smoke
  run DevSmoke package profile and collect package result

support_bundle_smoke
  generate sanitized support bundle from latest P21 evidence
```

P21's first milestone is complete when a packaged Development build can produce
one readable evidence set for boot, settings, input glyphs, package logs, and a
support bundle. Beautiful final screens can follow; the state, persistence, and
proof spine must come first.

### 15.15 Production pipeline and release engineering package

P21 cannot stop at a local package script. Ascentia also needs a production
pipeline that answers a harder question: which changelist/build is safe for a
Designer, playtester, or future external branch to sync, open, cook, and trust?

This package owns the operational spine around source control, build automation,
asset validation, artifact retention, release channels, and evidence. Section
11 owns Editor tools and local doctors. Section 15 owns the pipeline that turns
those reports into repeatable green builds.

Production posture:

- use source control from inside the Editor for binary assets and OFPA world
  work whenever possible;
- treat `.uasset` and `.umap` as lock/submit assets, not merge-friendly text;
- use One File Per Actor for large-world collaboration so actors do not force
  whole-map locks;
- wrap package/cook/build flows through UAT first, then BuildGraph once the
  node graph needs dependency reuse or build-farm execution;
- run Data Validation commandlets in CI before accepting content-heavy changes;
- keep generated reports as artifacts, not only terminal text;
- label every accepted build with source revision, engine version, content
  registry version, package profile, and evidence root;
- make Designer-visible dashboards read the same reports CI reads.

Recommended source-control stance:

```text
Prototype / current Ascentia
  Git + Git LFS for code/docs and selected binary assets
  Content caveat documented when .uasset changes stay local
  no broad team asset locking yet

First production team
  Perforce stream for Content and Config
  Git/GitHub may remain for docs, tools, and code if the team prefers
  Unreal Editor source-control workflow for binary asset submit/revert
  OFPA enabled for World Partition/open-world maps

Large production
  Perforce + UGS/Horde-style build visibility
  precompiled editor/build artifacts for non-programmers
  BuildGraph nodes for editor, cook, package, smoke, symbols, and archive
```

Ascentia should not switch repository models impulsively. The production
contract is to name the active source-control mode, expose its risks, and make
the pipeline reports honest about what is tracked versus local-only.

Runtime/editor files:

```text
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Pipeline/
  AscentiaPipelineTypes.h
  AscentiaBuildGraphProfile.h
  AscentiaSourceRevisionTypes.h
  AscentiaCookPackageTypes.h

  AscentiaReleaseChannelTypes.h
```


CI gate layering:

```text
Preflight
  compile editor target
  no generated project drift
  no invalid plugin/dependency state
  source revision recorded

Content
  Data Validation commandlet
  content registry audit
  redirector scan
  missing asset/reference scan
  OFPA dangling actor scan

Gameplay
  native automation tests
  focused remote smoke tests for active gate
  packaged smoke for accepted milestone

Package
  UAT BuildCookRun exits 0
  cook/package logs scanned for configured errors
  required maps and primary assets are included
  package result JSON written

Release
  manifest written
  artifact hashes written
  support bundle preview written
  dashboard card updated
```

Designer-visible pipeline tools:

```text
EUW_AscentiaPipelineDashboard
  Shows last green build, last failed gate, current source revision, dirty/local
  content warnings, package profile, artifact links, and human verdict.

EUW_AscentiaBuildGraphRunner
  Runs approved local BuildGraph profiles only. Displays nodes before execute,
  estimated cost, required editor-close state, and output artifact root.

EUW_AscentiaSourceControlDoctor
  Shows provider, branch/stream, dirty files, untracked content caveats,
  read-only asset state, OFPA external actor submit risks, and redirectors.

EUW_AscentiaReleaseCandidatePanel
  Lets production pick a release channel, review required evidence, write known
  issues, and generate a release manifest without editing JSON by hand.
```

Rules:

- A build is not "green" unless the gate profile and artifact root say so.
- Terminal output is not a durable artifact. Every gate writes a report.
- Local `.uasset` changes are acceptable during prototype, but every local-only
  asset path must be named in the source revision report.
- Build scripts must record the exact UAT or BuildGraph command line they ran.
- Pipeline scripts may delete their own temp/staging roots only after resolving
  and logging the absolute paths.
- CI should fail on missing required reports, not merely on command exit codes.
- Release manifests are immutable after publishing. Fixes produce a new build
  ID, not an edited old manifest.
- Designers can run local checks, but only approved profiles can publish a green
  dashboard card.

Evidence roots:

```text
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/Pipeline/{RunId}/source_revision.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/Pipeline/{RunId}/pipeline_result.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/Pipeline/{RunId}/ci_gate_report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/Pipeline/{RunId}/cook_package_audit.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/Pipeline/{RunId}/artifact_manifest.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/Pipeline/{RunId}/release_manifest.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/Pipeline/{RunId}/logs/
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/Pipeline/{RunId}/screenshots/
```

Implementation tickets:

```text
PIPE-001 Source revision report
  Add `FAscentiaSourceRevision`, provider detection for Git/current local mode,
  dirty/untracked/local-content warning rows, and a report writer.

PIPE-002 UAT BuildCookRun wrapper
  Promote `Tools/package_ascentia.ps1` into `Tools/pipeline` with exact command
  capture, normalized exit codes, log preservation, and artifact roots.

PIPE-003 CI gate evaluator
  Add `FAscentiaCIGate`, `FAscentiaCIGateReport`, required-report checks, log
  pattern scans, and gate-profile DataAssets.

PIPE-004 Data Validation and content audit profile
  Add a profile that runs Data Validation, content registry audit, redirector
  scan, missing-reference scan, and OFPA dangling actor scan.

PIPE-005 BuildGraph local profile
  Add `Build/Graph/Ascentia_Local.xml` with compile, validation, package, smoke,
  and collect-artifact nodes.

PIPE-006 Pipeline dashboard EUW
  Add Designer-visible last-green/last-failed cards, source revision warnings,
  report links, and local-only content caveats.

PIPE-007 Release manifest writer
  Add `FAscentiaReleaseManifest`, artifact hash collection, known-issue notes,
  and immutable build ID output.

PIPE-008 Artifact retention policy
  Add policy data for which logs, reports, screenshots, packages, and symbols are
  kept locally, archived, or trimmed.

PIPE-009 Production source-control migration note
  Document when Ascentia should move Content to Perforce/UGS-style workflow and
  what must be true before doing so.
```

First next package:

1. Implement PIPE-001 and PIPE-002 before adding heavier CI. We need honest
   source/build evidence from the current local setup first.
2. Implement PIPE-003 after reports exist, so the dashboard can fail on missing
   proof instead of vibes.
3. Implement PIPE-004 before any large content push from Landscry, Asset
   Factory, Skin Forge, Essential Bow, or Ultra Dynamic Sky adapters.
4. Implement PIPE-005 only after the PowerShell wrapper has stabilized.

### 15.16 Observability, playtest analytics, crash triage, and bug reproduction package

P21 also needs the loop that turns "something felt wrong" into a durable
production artifact. QA matrices and package gates prove known scenarios. This
package covers the messy middle: playtest sessions, crash reports, ensures,
support bundles, analytics events, human notes, repro clips, and issue triage.

The goal is not surveillance or monetization. The goal is reproducibility:

- when the Editor crashes, we know project, map, active tool, automation owner,
  dirty packages, recent scenario, and the last evidence root;
- when PIE smoke fails, we know input device, pawn class, map, build revision,
  scenario step, expected signal, log pattern, and screenshot;
- when a playtester says "dodge went the wrong way" or "loot vanished," we can
  replay the relevant state and find the owning system;
- when content grows, dashboard trends show which systems are repeatedly
  breaking rather than treating every bug as a new surprise.

Engine surfaces to use:

- Automation Framework for fast unit, feature, content stress, and screenshot
  comparison tests;
- Functional Testing Framework for authored level tests that Designers can
  inspect in maps;
- Gauntlet-style packaged sessions when a test must prove cooked/runtime
  behavior instead of PIE;
- Crash Reporter and crash context game data for crash GUID, call stacks,
  machine/build context, logs, and custom gameplay/session context;
- Analytics interface and File Logging Analytics provider for development-only
  event streams that can be compared against expected telemetry;
- Unreal Insights traces for performance-related repro packages.

Breadcrumb policy:

- keep an in-memory ring buffer, not an unbounded log;
- never store personal data in gameplay breadcrumbs;
- include asset IDs/tags instead of raw object dumps;
- update crash context at major state transitions, not every frame;
- emit development file analytics only for internal/playtest builds;
- expose all telemetry schemas in Editor so Designers can see what is captured;
- any event that affects privacy must be disabled unless the active profile
  explicitly allows it.

Starter proof matrix:

```text
crash_context_smoke
  set map, pawn class, active tool, scenario step, evidence root
  trigger controlled ensure or handled error path
  verify crash/support context contains Ascentia keys

playtest_session_smoke
  start a named session, run three marked steps, capture screenshot, add human
  note, close session, and verify session/evidence JSON

telemetry_schema_validation
  emit valid and invalid development-only events
  verify schema validator accepts required properties and rejects missing ones

repro_package_from_failed_signal
  run a scenario with one deliberately missing expected signal
  verify a repro package is created with logs, step ID, map, input device, and
  suggested owner system

issue_triage_grouping
  ingest one crash, one playtest note, and one missing signal
  verify board groups them by phase/system and flags duplicate evidence
```

Implementation tickets:

```text
OBS-001 Session ID, breadcrumb ring, and crash-context keys
  Add `UAscentiaSessionBreadcrumbSubsystem`, session IDs, recent breadcrumb
  writing, and crash context updates for map/pawn/tool/scenario/evidence root.

OBS-002 Development telemetry schema and file provider profile
  Add `PDA_TelemetrySchema_*`, `UAscentiaTelemetrySubsystem`, event validation,
  and a development-only file logging analytics path.

OBS-003 Playtest recorder EUW
  Add `EUW_AscentiaPlaytestRecorder`, session profiles, human verdict notes,
  screenshot capture points, and `FAscentiaPlaytestSession` reports.

OBS-004 Repro package writer
  Add `FAscentiaReproPackage`, scenario step capture, expected/observed signals,
  logs/screenshots/traces links, and a repro package browser.

OBS-005 Crash triage scanner
  Scan `Saved/Crashes`, extract crash GUID, callstack hash where available,
  Ascentia custom context, local logs, and suggested owner system.

OBS-006 Issue triage board
  Ingest crash records, playtest sessions, missing signals, and telemetry
  validation failures into `FAscentiaIssueTriageRecord` rows.

OBS-007 Evidence trend dashboard
  Add repeated-failure cards, stale evidence cards, crash frequency cards, and
  top owner-system blockers.

OBS-008 Privacy and retention policy validation
  Validate development-only telemetry, sanitization policy, retention windows,
  and forbidden fields before evidence can be exported.
```

Recommended sequencing:

1. Build OBS-001 first so every later crash/playtest artifact has context.
2. Add OBS-003 before broader human playtests; notes without session IDs age
   badly.
3. Add OBS-004 and OBS-005 before longer automation runs, because failed
   automation without repro packages is expensive archaeology.
4. Keep analytics file-backed and development-only until the game has a real
   privacy policy and external telemetry decision.

### 15.17 Platform services, achievements, cloud saves, storefront, and release presence package

P21 cannot be only "make a settings screen and package the build." A shipped
PC game also needs platform identity, achievements, stats, cloud-save policy,
storefront metadata, overlay behavior, entitlement assumptions, provider
configuration, and release-readiness evidence. These systems are not the same
as gameplay progression. They are product trust systems.

Ascentia should keep all provider details behind an Ascentia platform service
layer. Combat, inventory, exploration, save, bosses, and world systems should
emit durable facts. The platform layer decides whether those facts unlock a
Steam achievement, write an EOS stat, update a local Null-provider fixture, or
remain internal playtest telemetry.

Reference posture:

- Unreal's Online Subsystem is asynchronous and interface-based. Treat every
  provider call as a request that completes later, can fail, can be unsupported,
  and must report a failure reason.
- Achievements are authored in provider backends as well as in-game data. The
  game can query/write progress, but the provider's portal owns public names,
  icons, hidden flags, stat links, limits, and release visibility.
- Steam stats and achievements are persistent, roaming, and account-bound. For
  a single-player ARPG, use achievements to reward progression, mastery, build
  experimentation, and secrets; use stats sparingly for durable counters that
  can survive offline play and multi-machine sync.
- Steam Cloud can be Auto-Cloud path configuration or explicit Cloud API. MVP
  should prefer Auto-Cloud if Ascentia save files live in predictable, small,
  user-specific paths. Do not sync machine-specific settings such as video
  configuration.
- EOS Player Data Storage, achievements, stats, and title storage are viable
  provider concepts, but Ascentia should not lock save architecture to EOS file
  semantics. Keep provider adapters behind `UAscentiaPlatformServiceSubsystem`.
- Provider reset/debug functions belong behind non-shipping guards and internal
  tools. Shipping builds must not expose reset-achievement or test-cloud paths.

Subsystem boundary:

```text
UAscentiaPlatformServiceSubsystem : UGameInstanceSubsystem
  ResolveActiveProvider()
  QueryPlatformCapabilities()
  QueuePlatformOperation(Request)
  SubmitAchievementProgress(AchievementTag, Percent, SourceFact)
  SubmitStatDelta(StatTag, Delta, SourceFact)
  QueryCloudSaveState(SlotId)
  BuildCloudSaveManifest()
  OpenExternalOverlay(OverlayTag)
  WritePlatformEvidence(Result)

IAscentiaPlatformProviderAdapter
  Initialize(Profile)
  QueryCapabilities()
  QueryAchievements(LocalUser)
  WriteAchievementProgress(Request)
  QueryStats(LocalUser)
  WriteStats(Request)
  QueryCloudFiles(LocalUser)
  OpenOverlay(Request)
  Shutdown()
```

Facts enter the platform layer through gameplay-facing events:

```text
FAscentiaDurableProgressFact
  FactTag
  CharacterGuid
  SaveSlotGuid
  WorldZoneTag
  SourceSystemTag
  Count
  Value
  OccurredAtUtc
  bCanUnlockAchievement
  bCanUpdateExternalStat
```

Examples:

```text
Progress.Boss.FirstChoirDefeated
Progress.Zone.FirstRuinDiscovered
Progress.Build.FirstRespecCommitted
Combat.Riposte.Count
Combat.NoHit.BossAttemptSucceeded
Exploration.Below.FirstEntry
Item.Unique.FirstLegendaryFound
Magic.SpellSchool.NatureMastery
```

Rules:

- Gameplay systems emit progress facts; they do not call Steam, EOS, or another
  provider directly.
- Achievement provider IDs must be stable, ASCII, and validated against the
  provider profile. Renaming a public provider ID is migration debt.
- Hidden achievements require spoiler policy and internal display names so
  Designers can test without leaking player-facing secrets.
- Progressive achievements must submit monotonic progress. Never send lower
  progress than the last confirmed provider value.
- Development reset is available only through internal tools and non-shipping
  compilation guards.
- Cloud-save policy must separate gameplay saves, slot summaries, backups,
  settings, and machine-specific settings. Video/device settings should remain
  local.
- Every clouded file path must be user-specific. Shared Windows accounts and
  multiple Steam users on one machine cannot overwrite each other's saves.
- Demo/full-game save sharing must be explicit. If a demo save can load in the
  full game, P19 migrations and P21 cloud shared-AppID policy must agree.
- Provider failure must never corrupt the local save. The local P19 save is the
  authority; cloud/provider state is an async product-service layer.
- Overlay opening can affect pause/input focus. Shell/CommonUI must know when an
  external overlay is requested, active, dismissed, or unsupported.

Designer tools:

```text
EUW_AscentiaPlatformServiceBoard
EUW_AscentiaAchievementMatrix
EUW_AscentiaPlatformStatLab
EUW_AscentiaCloudSaveDoctor
EUW_AscentiaStorefrontPresenceChecklist
EUW_AscentiaProviderConfigDiff
EUW_AscentiaOverlayFocusTester
EUW_AscentiaPlatformReleaseGate
```

`EUW_AscentiaPlatformServiceBoard` shows active provider, build profile,
capabilities, provider initialization result, unsupported features, queued
operations, and latest evidence.

`EUW_AscentiaAchievementMatrix` maps durable progress facts to in-game
achievement definitions, provider IDs, icons, localization keys, hidden/spoiler
policy, progress thresholds, test reset availability, and backend configuration
status.

`EUW_AscentiaPlatformStatLab` previews stat deltas from fixture gameplay facts,
checks increment-only/max-delta/min/max rules, and reports stats that can become
inconsistent.

`EUW_AscentiaCloudSaveDoctor` compares local save paths, provider cloud policy,
file counts, expected bytes, slot summaries, backups, machine-specific settings,
demo/full sharing, cross-platform root overrides, and conflict rules.

`EUW_AscentiaStorefrontPresenceChecklist` tracks storefront capsule text links,
legal/support URLs, language list, achievement visibility, cloud-save status,
controller/input claims, accessibility claims, demo/full save compatibility,
and build branch readiness.

`EUW_AscentiaOverlayFocusTester` launches overlay requests from title, pause,
combat, loading, and settings fixtures, then verifies shell focus, input mode,
pause policy, and restoration.

P21P starter tickets:

- P21P-001: Add platform service profile structs, provider capability report,
  Null provider adapter, and `EUW_AscentiaPlatformServiceBoard`.
- P21P-002: Add achievement/stat catalog assets, durable progress fact bridge,
  monotonic-progress guard, and `EUW_AscentiaAchievementMatrix`.
- P21P-003: Add Cloud Save policy asset, local cloud manifest generator, save
  path/user-specific validation, and `EUW_AscentiaCloudSaveDoctor`.
- P21P-004: Add Steam prototype provider profile and provider-config diff report
  without making Steam a hard runtime dependency for non-Steam builds.
- P21P-005: Add EOS prototype provider profile and Player Data Storage policy
  notes without coupling P19 local saves to EOS file semantics.
- P21P-006: Add overlay focus tester and shell restoration proof.
- P21P-007: Add storefront presence checklist for build branch, demo/full save
  compatibility, language/platform claims, legal/support links, achievements,
  cloud-save readiness, and controller/accessibility claims.
- P21P-008: Add platform release gate dashboard cards and package blockers.

Recommended implementation order:

1. P21P-001 first. Provider abstraction and Null-provider evidence make the
   rest testable without waiting on Steam/EOS credentials.
2. P21P-002 next. Achievements should be designed from durable facts before
   designers write public names, icons, or hidden spoilers.
3. P21P-003 should happen before external cloud setup so local save paths,
   file sizes, machine-specific settings, and user-specific directories are
   already clean.
4. P21P-004 and P21P-005 are provider adapters, not the architecture. Add them
   only after the Ascentia profiles and Null proofs are stable.
5. P21P-006 and P21P-007 close the player-facing release loop.
6. P21P-008 makes platform service readiness visible in the dashboard and
   package gates.

### 15.18 Release QA, regression matrix, certification readiness, and final candidate package

P21 still needs a hard release-quality spine. Build pipeline, crash evidence,
platform services, and settings screens are necessary, but they do not prove
that a candidate is shippable. Ascentia needs a release QA package that turns
manual smoke tests, automation tests, localization checks, platform claims,
performance traces, save compatibility, accessibility promises, and content
integrity into one explicit release-candidate decision.

Reference posture:

- Epic's Automation Test Framework supports unit, feature, smoke, content
  stress, and screenshot-comparison tests. Ascentia should use those categories
  deliberately: Smoke for fast editor/startup promises, Feature for gameplay
  system behavior, Content Stress for maps/assets/Blueprint loads, and
  Screenshot Comparison for visual regressions.
  https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/automation-test-framework-in-unreal-engine
- Epic's automation test guidelines say tests should not assume shared editor
  state, should clean up generated files, and should assume the previous run
  left bad state. Ascentia's release gates should therefore create isolated
  save slots, temp report roots, and deterministic fixture names.
  https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/automation-test-framework-in-unreal-engine
- Gauntlet runs Unreal sessions and validates results across editor, packaged,
  and multi-process scenarios. Use it for release-candidate packaged smokes,
  boot flow, save/load, shell, settings, controller, graphics settings, and
  long-form scenario proof rather than depending only on PIE.
  https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/gauntlet-automation-framework-in-unreal-engine
- Unreal Insights captures structured trace data and includes Timing, Memory,
  Networking, Slate, Asset Loading, and Cooking views. Release QA should archive
  `.utrace` and derived summary data for known scenarios, not just FPS notes.
  https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/unreal-insights-in-unreal-engine
- Unreal localization is centered on `FText`, string tables, localization
  targets, gather/compile flows, and culture-aware display. Release QA must
  block user-facing `FString` text, missing string table rows, pseudo-loc
  clipping, unlocalized platform/store claims, and subtitle/caption omissions.
  https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/text-localization-in-unreal-engine

Subsystem boundary:

```text
UAscentiaReleaseCandidateSubsystem : UGameInstanceSubsystem
  BuildCandidateRun(Profile)
  RegisterGateResult(Result)
  RegisterScenarioResult(Result)
  ResolveCandidateVerdict(RunId)
  QueryReleaseGateState(CandidateTag)
  WriteReleaseCandidateEvidence(Run)
```

Rules:

- Release gates must read evidence from existing system reports where possible.
  Do not create a second truth for combat, saves, UX, platform, or data.
- A release candidate is not a branch name. It is a source revision, package
  artifact, build profile, platform set, evidence root, and explicit verdict.
- PIE smoke is diagnostic only. Candidate gates require packaged runs for boot,
  save/load, shell, settings, controller, and performance scenarios.
- Manual QA is allowed, but manual results must be entered as structured
  evidence with scenario tag, build version, tester, platform, reproduction
  notes, screenshot/video links, and verdict.
- Any public-facing store claim must map to a certification claim and a recent
  evidence run. If Ascentia says "full controller support", the input, glyph,
  remap, pause, overlay, inventory, combat, photo, and settings scenarios must
  prove it.
- Localization gates must run pseudo-localization before real translation is
  expensive. Long German/Finnish-like strings, CJK-width checks, missing
  subtitles, and right-to-left risk should be visible before content lock.
- Save compatibility gates must load old fixture saves, migrate them, validate
  headers/fragments, verify inventory/world/progression restoration, and save
  again without losing data.
- Content stress gates should load all release maps, all primary asset bundles,
  all Blueprint classes promoted to production, and known third-party content
  dependency roots.
- Performance gates must include trace artifacts and summary numbers. A "felt
  fine" note is not a release gate.
- Crash/support gates must produce symbol/build identity, log collection, repro
  bundle, save fixture, settings snapshot, and privacy-filtered support bundle.
- Waivers expire. A waived gate needs owner, ticket, channel, expiration, and
  a reason why the candidate can still ship.


`EUW_AscentiaReleaseCandidateBoard` is the release producer's home surface. It
shows candidate tag, source revision, package artifact, platform/channel,
required gates, current evidence age, waivers, known issues, blocker counts,
and final verdict.

`EUW_AscentiaRegressionMatrixRunner` lists automation groups, Gauntlet
scenarios, commandlets, screenshot comparisons, manual fixtures, estimated
duration, last result, failure owner, and rerun buttons.

`EUW_AscentiaCertificationClaimBoard` maps storefront/platform claims to the
evidence that proves them: controller support, accessibility settings, cloud
saves, languages, achievements, overlay behavior, performance target, and demo
save compatibility.

`EUW_AscentiaLocalizationReleaseGate` shows gathered targets, string table row
coverage, user-facing `FString` findings, pseudo-loc screenshots, clipped
widgets, subtitle/caption coverage, stale gather state, and culture readiness.

`EUW_AscentiaSaveCompatibilityGate` loads fixture saves from older versions,
runs migration, validates restored character/world/inventory state, performs a
new save, and compares fragment loss.

`EUW_AscentiaAutomationCoverageBoard` answers the uncomfortable question:
which shipped claims and phase gates are still manual-only?

P21Q starter tickets:

- P21Q-001: Add release candidate/gate structs, profile assets, report writer,
  `AscentiaReleaseCandidateAudit`, and `EUW_AscentiaReleaseCandidateBoard`.
- P21Q-002: Add regression matrix profile, automation group mapping, Gauntlet
  scenario declarations, and `EUW_AscentiaRegressionMatrixRunner`.
- P21Q-003: Add localization release gate, `FText`/string-table audit,
  pseudo-loc screenshot evidence hooks, and `EUW_AscentiaLocalizationReleaseGate`.
- P21Q-004: Add save compatibility gate using P19 fixture saves, migration
  validation, fragment-loss checks, and `EUW_AscentiaSaveCompatibilityGate`.
- P21Q-005: Add certification claim profile and evidence freshness audit for
  controller, accessibility, cloud save, language, achievement, and storefront
  claims.
- P21Q-006: Add manual QA evidence entry with structured scenario/verdict data,
  screenshot/video links, build identity, and ticket links.
- P21Q-007: Add Unreal Insights trace summarizer and release trace artifact
  browser for frame, memory, load, Slate/UI, networking, and cook summaries.
- P21Q-008: Add final release candidate dashboard card and package blockers.

Recommended implementation order:

1. P21Q-001 first. Without a candidate/gate profile, release readiness remains
   scattered across docs, logs, and tribal memory.
2. P21Q-002 next. The matrix defines what must be automated, what can remain
   manual for now, and what needs Gauntlet rather than PIE.
3. P21Q-003 and P21Q-004 should land before content lock. Localization and save
   migrations are painful when discovered late.
4. P21Q-005 connects player/store claims to evidence. It prevents optimistic
   product language from outrunning what the build proves.
5. P21Q-006 keeps human QA valuable without letting it vanish into chat notes.
6. P21Q-007 ties performance and loading assertions to trace artifacts.
7. P21Q-008 makes release readiness visible to the dashboard and build gates.

### 15.19 Patch, DLC, live content, game feature, rollback, and post-launch operations package

Ascentia should not wait until after release to decide how patches, DLC-sized
content, optional packs, balance updates, hotfixes, demo/full migration, and
rollback will work. A AAA open-world ARPG accumulates data, assets, quests,
bosses, saves, world-state records, localization, and platform claims over
years. Without a post-launch architecture, every patch becomes a risky ad hoc
fork of the project.

This package is not a promise that Ascentia is a live-service game. It is the
opposite: it keeps a single-player ARPG maintainable after launch without
turning every new boss, zone, item pack, or balance patch into global project
debt.

Reference posture:

- Unreal Game Features and Modular Gameplay support standalone feature plugins
  that can be activated or deactivated and help avoid accidental dependencies
  between unrelated features, which is especially relevant for products whose
  feature sets change over time.
  https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/game-features-and-modular-gameplay-in-unreal-engine
- The Game Framework Component Manager is the Modular Gameplay subsystem that
  supports Game Feature actions through receivers, extension handlers, and
  initialization states. Ascentia should use that pattern for optional runtime
  extension instead of hard-coding DLC behavior into core pawns and controllers.
  https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/game-framework-component-manager-in-unreal-engine
- Unreal patching compares post-cook content to released cooked content. Since
  a whole package is included when anything in that package changes, Ascentia
  should keep large volatile assets, DataAssets, maps, and DLC content organized
  so small balance edits do not accidentally create massive patches.
  https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/how-to-create-a-patch-in-unreal-engine
- Unreal patching requires released cooked content and stable release versions.
  Epic explicitly calls out locking serialization paths, keeping released cooked
  content, and mounting patch pak files at higher priority. Ascentia's P19 save
  schemas, P23 data schemas, and P21 release manifests must participate.
  https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/how-to-create-a-patch-in-unreal-engine
- Primary Asset Labels can assign chunk IDs and priorities to explicit assets,
  collections, or directories. Use them for DLC/content-pack chunk ownership
  rather than spreadsheet-only promises.
  https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/cooking-content-and-creating-chunks-in-unreal-engine
- The Asset Manager is the project-level framework for controlled asset
  discovery, loading, auditing, cooking, and chunking. Ascentia's content
  registry should align with Asset Manager rules before optional content grows.
  https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/asset-management-in-unreal-engine
- Unreal can divide content into `.pak` files for patching and DLC, and
  ChunkDownloader can download, mount, and safely access remotely hosted package
  files. Treat ChunkDownloader as an optional delivery adapter, not the core
  architecture.
  https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/patching-content-delivery-and-dlc-in-unreal-engine
  https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/implementing-chunkdownloader-in-your-gameplay-in-unreal-engine
