Runtime boundary:

```text
UAscentiaPostLaunchContentSubsystem : UGameInstanceSubsystem
  QueryInstalledContent()
  ResolveContentReleaseState()
  IsFeatureAvailable(FeatureTag)
  RequestRuntimeContentMount(Request)
  ValidateMountedContent(ReleaseTag)
  QueryPatchCompatibility(SaveHeader, ContentRelease)
  EmitPostLaunchEvidence(Result)
```

Rules:

- Core systems should depend on stable Ascentia interfaces, not directly on a
  DLC plugin. Optional content plugs into registries, ability sets, item
  catalogs, encounter definitions, world zones, dialogue tables, and UI routes.
- Patches cannot mutate released save schema casually. Any save-affecting patch
  requires a P19 migration fixture, old-save load proof, new-save proof, and a
  rollback stance.
- A patch note claim is a product contract. "Fixed dodge direction" must map to
  a regression scenario and evidence path.
- Balance-only patches should keep payload size small. If a numeric change
  drags a huge map or mesh package into the patch, the content layout needs
  restructuring before release.
- DLC content uses explicit chunk ownership and feature policy. A new zone,
  boss, item set, spell school, or arena should have a release tag, chunk rule,
  entitlement stance, localization targets, map markers, save fragments, and
  unload policy.
- Game Feature plugins may add components and data registries, but core
  authority remains in Ascentia native subsystems. Features extend; they do not
  become a second combat/save/world engine.
- Runtime mounted content must be hash/version checked before it is trusted.
  Failed mounts should produce support evidence and a recoverable UI state.
- Rollback is designed before the patch ships. If rollback is impossible after
  save migration, the patch gate must say so and require backup/support policy.
- Demo/full compatibility is a post-launch concern too. Demo saves, demo chunks,
  and full-game unlock state must agree with P19 save migration and P21 platform
  policy.
- Mods/UGC are out of scope until deliberately approved. Do not accidentally
  expose mutable runtime loading paths, user script execution, or untrusted pak
  mounting as a "tooling shortcut."

```text
Hotfix.CodeOnly
Hotfix.DataOnly
```

Designer tools:

```text
EUW_AscentiaPatchManifestBoard
EUW_AscentiaChunkOwnershipMap
EUW_AscentiaGameFeaturePolicyBoard
EUW_AscentiaPatchCompatibilityGate
EUW_AscentiaRuntimeContentMountLab
EUW_AscentiaPatchNotesClaimVerifier
EUW_AscentiaPatchRollbackSimulator
EUW_AscentiaDLCReadinessChecklist
```

`EUW_AscentiaPatchManifestBoard` shows base release, target release, changed
packages, changed bytes, change reason tags, patch profile, release channel,
save-schema touches, localization touches, platform claim touches, and evidence.

`EUW_AscentiaChunkOwnershipMap` maps Primary Asset Labels, chunk IDs, asset
roots, plugin roots, expected bytes, optional/required state, entitlement tags,
and unexpected cross-chunk dependencies.

`EUW_AscentiaGameFeaturePolicyBoard` reviews each Game Feature plugin for
actions, component receivers, data registries, primary assets, save fragments,
localization targets, Designer surfaces, and shipping allowance.

`EUW_AscentiaPatchCompatibilityGate` runs old saves, current saves, cloud-save
fixtures, settings profiles, achievement/stat facts, and demo/full fixture
states against the new content release profile.

`EUW_AscentiaRuntimeContentMountLab` simulates mounted content success, missing
entitlement, missing chunk, hash mismatch, version mismatch, slow download,
cancel, retry, and offline mode.

`EUW_AscentiaPatchNotesClaimVerifier` maps release notes to evidence rows. It
should make vague patch notes uncomfortable.

`EUW_AscentiaPatchRollbackSimulator` verifies backup presence, save migration
stance, content rollback possibility, player-facing messaging, and support
bundle policy.

P21L starter tickets:

- P21L-001: Add content release, patch profile, payload record, chunk ownership,
  and rollback policy structs plus `EUW_AscentiaPatchManifestBoard`.
- P21L-002: Add Primary Asset Label/chunk ownership audit and
  `EUW_AscentiaChunkOwnershipMap`.
- P21L-003: Add Game Feature policy audit for plugin actions, component
  receivers, data registries, primary assets, save fragments, and Designer
  surfaces.
- P21L-004: Add patch compatibility gate that reuses P19 save fixtures and P21Q
  release evidence.
- P21L-005: Add runtime content mount lab with ChunkDownloader-compatible
  request/result structs but no production dependency on remote hosting yet.
- P21L-006: Add patch notes claim verifier tied to regression/evidence rows.
- P21L-007: Add rollback simulator and backup/support policy checks.
- P21L-008: Add DLC readiness checklist for release tag, chunk plan, save/loc
  compatibility, entitlement stance, platform claims, and dashboard blockers.

Recommended implementation order:

1. P21L-001 first. A content release profile gives every patch and DLC pack a
   stable identity.
2. P21L-002 next, before optional content exists. Chunk mistakes are cheapest
   before the first expansion is authored.
3. P21L-003 should land before Game Feature plugins become a content habit.
4. P21L-004 protects player trust by proving old saves survive content updates.
5. P21L-005 can remain a lab until real remote delivery exists.
6. P21L-006 and P21L-007 make patch notes and rollback honest.
7. P21L-008 turns DLC readiness into a dashboard gate.

### 15.20 Source control, workspace, OFPA, asset locking, review, and team operations package

Ascentia can remain nimble as a solo project for a while, but the architecture
needs a production-grade path before the content count and collaborator count
rise. Unreal projects fail in very ordinary ways: a designer edits a binary
asset without a lock, an OFPA external actor file is not submitted with the
level change, a plugin upgrade lands without regression evidence, a Blueprint
fix lives only in one person's local workspace, or a branch is merged because
the code compiles while cooked content is broken. This package makes those
risks visible in the Editor and in dashboard evidence.

The current local OneDrive workspace is acceptable for personal iteration, but
it should not become the team authority. Team production needs an explicit
source-control provider profile, workspace policy, binary asset lock policy,
OFPA changelist validation, review gates, and verified-sync stance.

Reference posture:

- Epic's team-scaling guidance treats version control as the first production
  step before people work on code or assets. Unreal supports Perforce, Git with
  Git LFS, Subversion, and Diversion; Epic primarily uses Perforce, and its
  collaboration guidance emphasizes workspaces, review/commit, workstreams,
  locking, rollback, UGS, build automation, and test automation.
  https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/resources-for-scaling-your-unreal-engine-team
- Unreal source control exists to coordinate game development and track code and
  data changes over time. The Editor exposes checkout, submit, add, and status
  operations in Content Browser and Editor settings, so Ascentia's team rules
  should be visible to Designers instead of hidden in scripts only.
  https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/source-control-in-unreal-engine
  https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/using-source-control-in-the-unreal-editor
- Unreal assets and maps are binary files. Epic's Perforce documentation calls
  out that `.uasset` and `.umap` files cannot be text-merged normally; the
  Editor locks checked-out files to prevent concurrent edits. Ascentia should
  treat binary merge attempts as production hazards, not as normal review work.
  https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/using-perforce-as-source-control-for-unreal-engine
- One File Per Actor reduces level-file contention by saving actor instances as
  external files. Epic specifically warns that partial submits can leave
  dangling references, recommends submitting OFPA content and actor files from
  within the Editor, and notes changelist support is Perforce-only. Ascentia's
  World Partition and Data Layer workflows must validate OFPA submissions.
  https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/one-file-per-actor-in-unreal-engine
- Unreal Game Sync lets teams pick a project, sync/build working revisions, and
  consume verified builds from Perforce-backed workspaces. Ascentia does not
  need UGS on day one, but the plan should keep a verified-sync lane ready for
  a team transition.
  https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/unreal-game-sync-client-setup-for-unreal-engine
- Epic's C++ coding standard is relevant because Unreal code is long-lived,
  maintained by people other than its original author, and needs to remain
  readable across modules and compilers. Ascentia's review gates should include
  module-boundary, Blueprint exposure, reflection, lifetime, save, replication,
  and gameplay-authority checks, not just style comments.
  https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/epic-cplusplus-coding-standard-for-unreal-engine
- Unreal's SourceControl developer APIs expose workspace, changelist, shelve,
  status, sync preview, file-status monitor, and move-to-changelist concepts.
  AscentiaEditor tools can use those APIs to build Designer-visible status
  boards and commandlet gates.
  https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/API/Developer/SourceControl

```text
FAscentiaOFPAChangelistReport
  Map
  ExternalActorFiles
  LevelFile
  MissingExternalActors
  DanglingExternalActorRefs
  DataLayerRefs
  WorldPartitionCellRefs
  PartialSubmitRisk
  BlockingFindings

FAscentiaAssetLockReport

  Owner
  LockProvider
  CheckedOutBy
  NotAtHead
  WritableWithoutCheckout
  BinaryMergeRisk

  ChangeId
  Author
  ReviewerRows
  CodeReviewState
  AssetReviewState
  BuildGateState
  PackageGateState
  EvidenceLinks
  Waivers
  FinalVerdict
```

Rules:

- Solo Git LFS can remain the near-term authority for Ascentia, but the moment
  multiple designers or heavy binary collaboration enter the project, the team
  should deliberately evaluate Perforce plus Editor changelists and verified
  sync instead of drifting into unlocked binary-file chaos.
- Binary assets are locked and reviewed as binary assets. A Blueprint, map,
  skeleton, animation database, PhysicsAsset, material instance, or DataAsset
  change needs an owner, evidence, and latest-revision status.
- OFPA maps must submit external actor files and related level/Data Layer files
  together. Partial submits are blockers because they can create dangling world
  references that do not show up as friendly text conflicts.
- Cloud-synced local folders can be personal convenience, not production truth.
  Workspace Doctor should warn when a team-authority workspace lives under
  OneDrive, Dropbox, Google Drive, or another sync folder.
- Changelists and PRs must declare phase/system tags, asset roots touched,
  migration/save impact, cook/package impact, Designer-facing changes, evidence
  paths, reviewer expectations, and rollback risk.
- Redirectors, stale locks, unchecked-out writable packages, not-at-head binary
  assets, missing source-control status, and generated content without source
  records block merge or require an explicit waiver.
- Code review checks Epic-style readability, module dependencies, public/private
  boundary drift, reflected API surface, Blueprint exposure, UObject lifetime,
  GC references, async/thread use, save compatibility, replication authority,
  security-sensitive input, and tests/evidence.
- Asset review checks provenance, license, folder/naming policy, skeleton and
  retarget profile, PhysicsAsset/body coverage, collision, sockets, LOD/Nanite,
  materials, physical surfaces, Data Layers, HLOD, package/cook warnings, and
  Designer acceptance.
- Verified sync records should identify the revision/changelist, build artifact,
  smoke-test matrix, known blockers, waivers, and compatible engine/plugin
  versions. Designers should know which revision is safe without reading CI
  logs.

Designer tools:

```text
EUW_AscentiaWorkspaceDoctor
EUW_AscentiaChangelistComposer
EUW_AscentiaOFPAChangelistValidator
EUW_AscentiaAssetLockBoard
EUW_AscentiaReviewGateBoard
EUW_AscentiaVerifiedSyncBoard
EUW_AscentiaBranchStreamMap
EUW_AscentiaRedirectorAndMovePlanner
```

`EUW_AscentiaWorkspaceDoctor` shows project root, cloud-sync risk, provider
connection, engine version, plugin state, path length risk, source-control
status, local dirty packages, active editor sessions, and recommended next
action.

`EUW_AscentiaChangelistComposer` helps authors build a clean change: system
tags, phase tags, files, binary count, evidence links, review requests,
Designer verdict, package impact, save impact, and rollback notes.

`EUW_AscentiaOFPAChangelistValidator` decodes external actor submissions into
human-readable level, actor, Data Layer, World Partition, and dangling-reference
rows.

`EUW_AscentiaAssetLockBoard` exposes checked-out assets, owners, stale locks,
not-at-head packages, writable-without-checkout packages, and risky binary
merge state.

`EUW_AscentiaReviewGateBoard` gathers code review, asset review, automation,
package, Designer, and release evidence into a single verdict for a change.

`EUW_AscentiaVerifiedSyncBoard` lists green revisions/build artifacts, smoke
status, compatible engine/plugin versions, required DDC notes, and open
blockers.

  lock, and review-gate structs plus `EUW_AscentiaWorkspaceDoctor`.
- P21T-002: Add `EUW_AscentiaChangelistComposer` and changelist policy audit
  with phase tags, evidence links, binary counts, package impact, save impact,
  and Designer verdict rows.
- P21T-003: Add `EUW_AscentiaOFPAChangelistValidator` with decoded external
  actor, level, Data Layer, World Partition, dangling-reference, and partial
  submit checks.
- P21T-004: Add `EUW_AscentiaAssetLockBoard` using source-control status APIs
  for checkout owner, stale lock, not-at-head, writable-without-checkout, and
  binary merge risk.
- P21T-005: Add code/asset review gate profiles and `EUW_AscentiaReviewGateBoard`.
- P21T-006: Add `EUW_AscentiaVerifiedSyncBoard` and BuildGraph/UGS-readiness
  snapshot support.
- P21T-007: Add `EUW_AscentiaBranchStreamMap` and branch/stream policy audit.
- P21T-008: Add `EUW_AscentiaRedirectorAndMovePlanner` for approved dry-run
  asset moves, redirector fixes, and source-control-safe promotions.

Recommended implementation order:

1. P21T-001 first. The workspace/provider policy makes current solo mode honest
   and defines the team transition point.
2. P21T-002 and P21T-003 next. Changelist hygiene and OFPA validation are the
   highest-risk daily collaboration gaps for Unreal content.
3. P21T-004 before multiple designers share maps, Blueprints, animations,
   materials, or DataAssets.
4. P21T-005 before review volume grows beyond direct conversation.
5. P21T-006 before a second regular developer needs a known-good sync.
6. P21T-007 and P21T-008 once content promotion and branch/release lanes become
   more frequent than one-off local experiments.

### 15.21 Ratings, privacy, legal text, credits, storefront claims, and player trust package

This package is production governance, not legal advice. Final binding decisions
still belong to counsel, rating authorities, storefronts, platform holders, and
regional law. The engineering point is that Ascentia should not discover its
rating, privacy, credits, data-request, regional availability, or public-claim
evidence gaps during store submission. Player trust is a build artifact.

Ascentia's MVP may begin as a mostly offline single-player game, but even a
single-player PC title can collect support bundles, crash reports, telemetry,
cloud-save metadata, achievement state, screenshots, store-page claims, public
trailers, soundtrack credits, third-party asset attributions, and age-rating
content disclosures. Those surfaces need owners, evidence, freshness, and
Designer-visible review before public release.

Reference posture:

- ESRB describes its system as rating categories, content descriptors, and
  interactive elements. ESRB also notes that DLC usually inherits the base
  product rating, but DLC or updates that exceed the original rating must be
  submitted and may receive a different rating. Incomplete or inaccurate
  disclosure can result in corrected ratings and, for boxed products, sanctions.
  https://www.esrb.org/faqs/
- IARC lets developers obtain ratings for multiple territories through a
  participating digital storefront's ingestion portal. The questionnaire is
  available only through participating storefronts, ratings are currently free
  to developers at the point of use, and ratings are issued immediately after
  the questionnaire is completed.
  https://globalratings.com/faq/
- Steam's German rating documentation states that, starting November 15, 2024,
  Steam no longer displays games to customers in Germany when a valid age rating
  is missing. Valve's rating path depends on the developer truthfully completing
  Steam's built-in content questionnaire.
  https://partner.steamgames.com/doc/gettingstarted/contentsurvey/germany
- Steam age gates can be enabled when entering regional ratings such as ESRB or
  PEGI, so regional age-gate behavior is part of release configuration, not just
  copywriting.
  https://partner.steamgames.com/doc/store/age_gate
- PEGI ratings are age-suitability guidance rather than difficulty guidance.
  The PEGI descriptor space includes violence, fear, bad language, sex, drugs,
  discrimination, gambling, in-game purchases, paid random items, online
  interaction/communication, pressure to play, time-limited offers, and related
  player-protection descriptors.
  https://pegi.info/page/pegi-age-ratings
- Steam's store description guidance says the short description should focus on
  the product's value proposition, avoid formatting, avoid time-based text, and
  keep longer feature detail in the About This Game area. Text embedded in
  images must also be localized when the store page is localized.
  https://partner.steamgames.com/doc/store/page/description
- California's CCPA gives California residents rights to know, delete, opt out
  of sale/sharing, correct inaccurate information, limit certain sensitive data
  use, receive notice at collection, and avoid discrimination for exercising
  rights. A privacy policy and notice-at-collection posture therefore need a
  data map rather than a last-minute paragraph.
  https://oag.ca.gov/privacy/ccpa
- EU data protection rules apply to EU and non-EU organizations that offer goods
  or services in the EU and request or reuse personal data from individuals in
  the EU. Direct or indirect personal identification triggers data-protection
  rights such as access, correction, deletion, portability, and information
  about retention and recipients.
  https://europa.eu/youreurope/citizens/consumers/internet-telecoms/data-protection-online-privacy/index_en.htm

Runtime module layout:

```text
Source/Ascentia/Public/Compliance/AscentiaPlayerTrustTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Compliance/AscentiaPrivacyDataMapTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Compliance/AscentiaRatingContentTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Compliance/AscentiaStoreClaimTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Compliance/AscentiaPlayerDataRequestTypes.h
```

The runtime layer should stay thin. It owns build identity, privacy-visible
settings, consent/preferences, telemetry opt-out state, support-bundle export
metadata, and player-facing policy surfaces. Rating submission packs, storefront
claims, legal text, credits, regional visibility, and evidence generation live
in Editor/build tooling unless a shipped screen needs to display them.

Editor module layout:

```text
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Compliance/AscentiaRatingEvidenceAudit.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Compliance/AscentiaPrivacyDataMapAudit.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Compliance/AscentiaStoreClaimAudit.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Compliance/AscentiaLegalTextCreditAudit.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Compliance/AscentiaRegionalAvailabilityAudit.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Compliance/AscentiaPlayerDataRequestAudit.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Compliance/AscentiaRatingEvidenceAudit.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Compliance/AscentiaPrivacyDataMapAudit.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Compliance/AscentiaStoreClaimAudit.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Compliance/AscentiaLegalTextCreditAudit.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Commandlets/AscentiaComplianceReleaseGateCommandlet.cpp
```

Tool and script layout:

```text
Tools/compliance/build_rating_evidence_pack.py
Tools/compliance/audit_privacy_data_map.py
Tools/compliance/audit_store_claims.py
Tools/compliance/audit_credits.py
Tools/compliance/audit_age_gate_matrix.py
Tools/compliance/simulate_player_data_request.py
```

Starter folders:

```text
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Compliance/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Compliance/Ratings/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Compliance/Privacy/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Compliance/Storefront/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Compliance/Legal/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Compliance/Credits/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Compliance/Regional/
/Game/Ascentia/Editor/Compliance/
```

Starter assets:

```text
PDA_PlayerTrustPolicy_MVP
PDA_RatingEvidenceProfile_MVP
PDA_ESRBContentDisclosure_MVP
PDA_IARCQuestionnaireEvidence_MVP
PDA_PEGIDescriptorEvidence_MVP
PDA_PrivacyDataMap_MVP
PDA_TelemetryConsentPolicy_MVP
PDA_StoreClaimProfile_Steam_MVP
PDA_StoreClaimProfile_EGS_MVP
PDA_LegalTextRegistry_MVP
PDA_CreditsPolicy_MVP
PDA_RegionalAgeGatePolicy_MVP
PDA_PlayerDataRequestPolicy_MVP
```

Core data:

```text
FAscentiaRatingContentSignal
  SignalTag
  SourceAsset
  SourceSystem
  RatingConcernKind:
    Violence, Blood, Gore, Fear, Horror, Language, Sex, Nudity, Drugs,
    Gambling, InGamePurchases, PaidRandomItems, UserInteraction,
    UnrestrictedCommunication, UGC, CryptoNFT, Other
  SeverityBand
  ContextDescription
  EvidenceClips
  Screenshots
  BuildVersion
  DLCOrPatchTag
  ReviewerNotes

FAscentiaRatingSubmissionProfile
  TerritorySystemTag
  StorefrontTag
  RatingAuthority
  SubmissionKind: IARC, StoreContentSurvey, Traditional, UpdateCheck
  RequiredEvidence
  ContentWarnings
  InteractiveElements
  DescriptorRows
  RegionAgeGatePolicy
  LastSubmissionId
  CertificationCode
  ChangeReviewRequired

FAscentiaPrivacyDataElement
  DataElementTag
  DisplayName
  CollectedBySystem
  RuntimeSource
  PersonalDataRisk: None, Pseudonymous, Personal, Sensitive
  CollectionTrigger
  Purpose
  LegalBasisCandidate
  RetentionPolicy
  SharedWithProviders
  UserVisibleSetting
  ExportPath
  DeleteBehavior
  OptOutBehavior
  Owner

FAscentiaStoreClaim
  ClaimId
  StorefrontTag
  Surface:
    ShortDescription, About, Screenshot, Trailer, PatchNotes, FeatureList,
    AccessibilityClaim, RatingClaim
  ClaimText
  SupportedByEvidence
  ScreenshotOrClip
  LocalizationKeys
  DateSensitive
  RegionSensitive
  RequiredFreshness
  ApprovalState

FAscentiaLegalTextEntry
  TextId
  TextKind:
    EULA, PrivacyPolicy, Credits, Attribution, ThirdPartyNotice,
    HealthWarning, AccessibilityStatement, SupportNotice
  Owner
  SourceAuthority
  Version
  LocaleCoverage
  InGameSurface
  StoreSurface
  LastCounselReview
  EvidencePath

FAscentiaPlayerDataRequestScenario
  ScenarioTag
  RequestKind: Access, Delete, Correct, OptOut, LimitSensitive, ExportSupportBundle
  DataElementsTouched
  ProviderDependencies
  ExpectedResponseArtifact
  DeleteOrAnonymizePolicy
  Exceptions
  ManualOwner
  EvidencePath
```

Rules:

- Every public claim needs an evidence path, owner, approval state, locale
  coverage, and freshness policy.
- Ratings evidence is a content/system inventory, not optimism. Violent boss
  finishers, blood, horror, gambling-like mechanics, paid/random items, online
  interaction, unrestricted communication, UGC, chat, crypto/NFT references,
  DLC changes, and patch changes all produce rating signals.
- If DLC or a patch exceeds the base rating disclosure, the release gate blocks
  until the rating-review path is resolved.
- If the Steam Germany target is enabled, rating/content survey status is a
  release gate.
- Privacy data map rows own telemetry, support bundles, crash reporting,
  cloud-save metadata, platform identity, achievement services, analytics,
  newsletter/community handoff, and any external provider export.
- No new player data collection is allowed without a `FAscentiaPrivacyDataElement`
  row, owner, purpose, retention policy, opt-out/delete behavior, and player
  visibility decision.
- Store text must avoid unsupported claims, time-based short-description text,
  unlocalized text inside images, and screenshots or trailers that imply
  mechanics not present in the target build.
- Credits and attribution are generated from the P23 external content registry,
  audio/music records, VO/performance records, vendor records, and generated
  content policy.
- Player data request simulation verifies access/export/delete/correct/opt-out
  behavior where applicable. The simulator produces engineering evidence for
  counsel and support operations; it does not replace legal review.
- For the default offline single-player posture, data minimization wins: local
  only unless a platform feature, support feature, telemetry setting, or
  explicit development option says otherwise.

Designer tools:

```text
EUW_AscentiaRatingsEvidenceBoard
EUW_AscentiaPrivacyDataMap
EUW_AscentiaStoreClaimComplianceBoard
EUW_AscentiaLegalTextAndCreditsBoard
EUW_AscentiaAgeGateAndRegionalAvailabilityLab
EUW_AscentiaPlayerDataRequestSimulator
EUW_AscentiaMarketingAssetTruthAudit
EUW_AscentiaComplianceReleaseGate
```

`EUW_AscentiaRatingsEvidenceBoard` shows content signals, source assets,
evidence clips/screenshots, DLC/update deltas, territory rows, interactive
elements, descriptor concerns, missing disclosure notes, and rating-submission
readiness.

`EUW_AscentiaPrivacyDataMap` shows collected data elements, runtime source,
provider sharing, purpose, retention, opt-out/delete/export behavior, player
visibility, settings surface, and owner.

`EUW_AscentiaStoreClaimComplianceBoard` shows every public claim by surface,
evidence, freshness, region, localization key, screenshot/trailer support,
approval state, and blocker status.

`EUW_AscentiaLegalTextAndCreditsBoard` shows EULA/privacy/support/legal notices,
third-party attributions, soundtrack/VO/vendor credits, locale coverage,
in-game/store surfaces, counsel-review status, and generated credits output.

`EUW_AscentiaAgeGateAndRegionalAvailabilityLab` shows storefront, territory,
rating authority, age-gate state, valid rating presence, Steam Germany state,
regional visibility, and publish blockers.

`EUW_AscentiaPlayerDataRequestSimulator` runs access, export, delete, correct,
opt-out, limit-sensitive, and support-bundle scenarios against the privacy data
map and provider dependency rows.

`EUW_AscentiaMarketingAssetTruthAudit` cross-checks screenshots, trailers, GIFs,
capsules, press-kit copy, and store text against shipped features, rating
signals, accessibility claims, and localization obligations.

Commandlets:

```text
AscentiaRatingEvidenceAudit
AscentiaPrivacyDataMapAudit
AscentiaStoreClaimAudit
AscentiaLegalCreditsAudit
AscentiaRegionalAgeGateAudit
AscentiaPlayerDataRequestAudit
AscentiaComplianceReleaseGate
```

Evidence:

```text
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P21/Compliance/Ratings/{RunId}/rating_report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P21/Compliance/PrivacyDataMap/{RunId}/privacy_data_map_report.json

```

Proof matrix:

```text
Compliance_RatingEvidence_ContentSignalsComplete
Compliance_IARC_EvidencePackReady
Compliance_DLC_RatingCompatibilityChecked
Compliance_SteamGermany_RatingStatusPresent
```

P21G starter tickets:

- P21G-001: Add compliance/player-trust structs, policy assets, and
  `EUW_AscentiaRatingsEvidenceBoard`.
- P21G-002: Add privacy data map, telemetry/support/cloud/provider rows, and
  `EUW_AscentiaPrivacyDataMap`.
- P21G-003: Add store claim compliance board and marketing asset truth audit.
- P21G-004: Add legal text, credits, attribution, soundtrack, VO, vendor, and
  generated-content registry bridge.
- P21G-005: Add regional age-gate and rating matrix, including Steam Germany
  valid-rating gate.
- P21G-006: Add player data request simulator for access, export, delete,
  correct, opt-out, limit-sensitive, and support-bundle scenarios.
- P21G-007: Add DLC/patch rating compatibility bridge to P21L release manifests.
- P21G-008: Add compliance release gate and dashboard blockers for unreviewed
  public claims, missing rating evidence, missing privacy data rows, incomplete
  credits, and regional availability risks.

Recommended implementation order:

1. P21G-001 through P21G-003 before any public store page, demo page, trailer,
   press kit, or public accessibility claim goes live.
2. P21G-004 before external content, audio, VO, generated assets, contractors,
   or credits broaden.
3. P21G-005 before regional store visibility is treated as a publishable target.
4. P21G-006 before telemetry, support bundles, cloud saves, newsletters, account
   linking, or analytics become player-facing features.
5. P21G-007 before patch/DLC production.
6. P21G-008 before any release candidate can call itself candidate-complete.

## 16. Multiplayer, Online, Co-op, and Invasion Future-Proofing

### 16.1 Player-facing goal

P22 should allow Ascentia to grow toward co-op, invasion-like pressure,
messages/ghosts, or asynchronous traces without rewriting the single-player
game. The player should feel that online features extend the same world rules,
not that a separate multiplayer mode has replaced the game.

### 16.2 Current Ascentia state

Existing future-proofing:

- GAS is already present and supports replication/prediction patterns.
- Many gameplay components already replicate state or telemetry.
- `AAscentiaPlayerState` owns an ability system component with mixed
  replication.
- pawn/character fallbacks use minimal replication in places.
- Mover and NetworkPrediction are enabled, and GASP/Mover integration already
  treats movement as a system boundary.
- `UAscentiaCombatComponent` owns replicated combat state and centralizes many
  combat requests.
- inventory/equipment has native components and data structures rather than
  pure widget state.
- world state has a subsystem, which can become an authority/sync boundary.

Current gaps:

- no Online Subsystem/session module enabled;
- no host/join/session UI;
- no network test maps;
- no listen-server smoke;
- no dedicated-server stance;
- no replication audit for inventory, save, world state, abilities, UI events,
  or boss encounters;
- no latency/prediction proof for dodge, attacks, montages, projectiles, loot, or
  spell casts.

### 16.3 Multiplayer design options

Do not implement all of these by default. P22 should pick deliberately:

```text
Solo online
  messages, ghosts, bloodstains, asynchronous world traces

Summoned co-op
  small party, host world authority, guest restrictions

Invasions
  hostile guest rules, matchmaking constraints, anti-grief boundaries

Shared world pockets
  limited social hubs or dungeon instances

Dedicated co-op
  server-authoritative session without host migration expectations
```

Each mode needs:

- session policy;
- save progression policy;
- loot ownership policy;
- death/respawn policy;
- matchmaking restrictions;
- world-state authority;
- disconnect recovery;
- UI and messaging;
- anti-cheat-sensitive path review.

### 16.4 Authority model

Default rule:

```text
Server owns gameplay truth.
Owning client predicts allowed local feel.
Non-owning clients receive replicated results and cosmetic cues.
Blueprint presentation reacts; it does not decide authority.
```

Authority boundaries:

- Health, Breath, Eth, status, death, loot drops, save-relevant world state, boss
  phase, enemy AI decisions, encounter completion, and item grants are server
  authoritative.
- Local camera, HUD, screen effects, input buffering display, local audio mix,
  and accessibility presentation are client local.
- GameplayCue presentation can replicate or run locally depending on cue type,
  but it must not be used as the source of gameplay truth.
- Save writes are host/server mediated for multiplayer sessions. Guests should
  receive explicit progress grants, not arbitrary host save writes.

Authority policy:

```text
PDA_NetworkAuthorityPolicy
  PolicyTag
  SystemTag
  AuthorityOwner
  ClientPredictionPolicy
  RPCPolicy
  ReplicationPolicy
  SavePersistencePolicy
  AntiCheatSensitivity
  DesignerNotes
```

Authority audit row:

```text
FAscentiaNetworkAuthorityAuditRow
  SystemTag
  GameplayPathTag
  OwningClassOrAsset
  AuthorityOwner
  ClientCanRequest
  ClientCanPredict
  ServerValidates
  ReplicatesToOwner
  ReplicatesToOthers
  SaveRelevant
  ExploitSensitivity
  Unknowns[]
```

Authority audit report:

```text
FAscentiaNetworkAuthorityAuditReport
  RunId
  PathsChecked
  UnknownAuthorityPaths
  ClientAuthoritativeFindings
  MissingServerValidation
  SaveRelevantReplicationFindings
  ExploitSensitiveFindings
  RecommendedRepairs[]
```

`EUW_AscentiaReplicationAudit` should let a Designer or engineer filter by
system, authority owner, save relevance, and exploit sensitivity. The first
network milestone is not "host a lobby"; it is "no important gameplay path has
unknown authority."

### 16.5 GAS, prediction, and ability policy

Ability classes need a network policy:

```text
LocalPredicted
  dodge, light attack startup, block raise, camera-local responsiveness

ServerInitiated
  enemy abilities, boss phases, world-state triggered effects

ServerOnly
  loot grants, save mutations, final reward claims, anti-cheat-sensitive actions

NonReplicated/LocalOnly
  purely local menu, camera, audio, accessibility effects
```

Ability proof must cover:

- client activation accepted;
- client activation rejected;
- cost predicted then confirmed;
- cooldown predicted then confirmed;
- montage starts and cancels consistently;
- target/hit authority matches server trace;
- GameplayCues appear on relevant machines;
- rollback/reconciliation does not feel worse than no prediction.

Ability network policy:

```text
PDA_AbilityNetworkPolicy
  AbilityTag
  NetExecutionPolicy
  PredictionPolicy
  CostPredictionPolicy
  CooldownPredictionPolicy
  TargetDataAuthority
  MontageReplicationPolicy
  GameplayCueVisibilityPolicy
  ReconciliationPolicy
```

Ability proof report:

```text
FAscentiaAbilityNetworkProofReport
  RunId
  AbilityTag
  ScenarioTag
  ClientActivationResult
  ServerValidationResult
  CostPredictionResult
  CooldownPredictionResult
  MontageSyncResult
  TargetDataResult
  GameplayCueResult
  ReconciliationFindings
```

The policy asset should be referenced by P13/P14/P15 ability reports. A weapon
skill, spell, dodge, block, or boss move should not invent its own networking
story later.

### 16.6 Movement and combat motion

The combat motion solver from section 6.9 is the right abstraction for
multiplayer because it separates player intent, authored motion, and authority.
P22 should not let raw montage root motion become the network contract.

Network motion data should include:

```text
MotionRequestId
MotionType
IntentVector
TargetActor/TargetLocation if any
Montage/Section
StartTime
PredictionKey
MovementModeAtStart
Floor/Ledge classification
CancelPolicy
```

Proof maps:

- flat arena;
- stair descent/ascent;
- ledge fall;
- narrow corridor wall slide;
- enemy hit reaction;
- boss attack root motion;
- high-latency dodge/attack cancel.

Mover/NetworkPrediction should be evaluated as the long-term path, but the game
contract must remain `FAscentiaCombatMotionRequest` and related interfaces so
the project can swap movement implementation if needed.

Network motion report:

```text
FAscentiaNetworkMotionReport
  RunId
  ScenarioTag
  MotionRequestId
  IntentVector
  PredictedPath
  ServerPath
  CorrectionEvents
  RootMotionDelta
  FloorLedgeClassification
  CancelWindowResult
  LatencyMs
  PacketLossPercent
  ReadabilityNotes
```

Motion replication policy:

```text
PDA_MotionNetworkPolicy
  MotionTypeTag
  PredictionMode
  ServerCorrectionPolicy
  RootMotionPolicy
  FloorLedgeAuthorityPolicy
  CancelReplicationPolicy
  MaxCorrectionDistance
  DebugDrawPolicy
```

This report should reuse the P10/P14 motion inspector data. Network proof should
not be a separate universe from root-motion, ledge, dodge-intent, and cancel
proof.

### 16.7 Inventory, loot, and save policy

Inventory rules:

- server validates grants, consumes, equips, trades, drops, and crafts;
- clients request actions by item instance ID and action tag;
- server replies with accepted/rejected result and replicated state delta;
- UI never assumes an item exists because it was clicked locally;
- item instance IDs must be stable enough for replication and save.

Loot ownership options:

```text
Host-owned world loot
  simplest, guest rewards explicit

Personal loot
  preferred for co-op fairness, more data and replication

Shared loot
  friction-heavy, needs pickup locks and grief rules
```

Save progression:

- host world state saves to host slot;
- guests save character progress they are allowed to keep;
- world-state unlocks need explicit grant rules;
- deaths and corpse runs need multiplayer ownership rules;
- disconnects should not duplicate loot or lose paid costs.

Multiplayer loot policy:

```text
PDA_MultiplayerLootPolicy
  PolicyTag
  LootOwnershipMode
  PickupLockPolicy
  VisibilityPolicy
  GuestRewardPolicy
  DisconnectPolicy
  DuplicateProtectionPolicy
  AntiGriefPolicy
```

Network item transaction:

```text
FAscentiaNetworkItemTransactionRequest
  RequestId
  PlayerStateId
  ItemInstanceId
  ActionTag
  ClientContainerHash
  ClientTime
  PredictionKey

FAscentiaNetworkItemTransactionResult
  RequestId
  bAccepted
  FailureReasonTag
  ServerContainerHash
  ReplicatedDelta
  SaveGrantResult
  AntiDuplicationResult
```

Loot/save report:

```text
FAscentiaNetworkLootSaveReport
  RunId
  ScenarioTag
  LootPolicy
  PickupResults
  EquipResults
  ConsumeResults
  CraftResults
  DisconnectReconnectResults
  GuestProgressGrants
  DuplicateFindings
  SaveFindings
```

`EUW_AscentiaMultiplayerLootPolicyPreview` should simulate host-owned, personal,
and shared loot for the same encounter and show who sees, can pick up, can keep,
and can save each reward.

### 16.8 World state, AI, bosses, and encounters

Server owns:

- enemy spawning;
- AI decisions;
- boss phase state;
- encounter completion;
- world gate state;
- loot claim state;
- campsite rest side effects;
- Data Layer runtime activation that affects gameplay.

Clients own:

- local discovery presentation;
- map reveal animation;
- subtitles and UI hints;
- local-only atmosphere/accessibility presentation.

Bosses need special proof:

- phase transitions replicate once;
- boss health bar appears/disappears on all relevant clients;
- fog wall state is consistent;
- death rewards do not duplicate;
- late joiners see correct phase/world state;
- disconnect/reconnect does not leave boss encounter corrupted.

Network world-state delta:

```text
FAscentiaNetworkWorldStateDelta
  DeltaId
  SourceSystemTag
  ChangedTags
  ClearedTags
  CounterChanges
  SaveRelevant
  ReplicationAudience
  LateJoinReplayPolicy
```

Boss network state:

```text
FAscentiaBossNetworkState
  BossTag
  EncounterTag
  PhaseTag
  HealthFraction
  FogWallState
  AddsAlive
  ArenaStateTags
  RewardClaimState
  LastReplicatedMoveTag
```

Boss/world network report:

```text
FAscentiaBossNetworkProofReport
  RunId
  BossTag
  ScenarioTag
  PhaseReplicationResult
  HealthBarReplicationResult
  FogWallReplicationResult
  LateJoinResult
  DisconnectReconnectResult
  RewardDuplicationResult
  WorldStateDeltaResults
  Errors[]
  Warnings[]
```

Encounter network report:

```text
FAscentiaEncounterNetworkReport
  RunId
  EncounterTag
  SpawnReplicationResults
  AIStateVisibilityResults
  CompletionReplicationResults
  LeashResetReplicationResults
  DataLayerReplicationFindings
  LateJoinFindings
```

### 16.9 Session and online layer

Ascentia should hide platform APIs behind:

```text
UAscentiaOnlineSubsystem
  CreateSession
  FindSessions
  JoinSession
  LeaveSession
  InviteFriend
  ResolveTravel
  GetOnlineCapabilities

UAscentiaSessionSettings
  MaxPlayers
  JoinPolicy
  Password/InvitePolicy
  PvPPolicy
  Region
  BuildCompatibility
  Mod/ContentCompatibility
```

The Online Subsystem/session provider should be a platform adapter, not the
gameplay contract. The UI should speak in Ascentia terms: Host, Join, Summon,
Invade, Password, Friends, Recent, Leave World.

```text
  ProfileTag
  SessionMode

  InvitePolicy
```

Session UI should be designed even before a provider is chosen. It needs failure
copy, retry paths, password/invite surfaces if used, build mismatch text,
disconnect recovery, and host/guest save policy explanations.

### 16.10 Anti-cheat and exploit-sensitive paths

Even if no anti-cheat provider is selected, P22 should mark sensitive actions:

- item grants;
- currency grants/spends;
- stat investment/refund;
- save migration;
- boss rewards;
- quest/world-state unlocks;
- teleport/fast travel;
- damage application;
- status application;
- cooldown/resource bypass;
- movement bursts and root-motion skills.

Rule:

```text
If cheating it would damage another player or persistent economy, the server
must own it.
```

Exploit review row:

```text
FAscentiaExploitSensitivePath
  PathTag
  SystemTag
  ClientInputs
  ServerValidation
  PersistentStateTouched
  EconomyTouched
  OtherPlayersAffected
  DetectionTelemetry
  BlockingRisk
```

Exploit review report:

```text
FAscentiaNetworkExploitReviewReport
  RunId
  PathsReviewed
  MissingServerValidation
  ClientTrustFindings
  PersistenceRiskFindings
  EconomyRiskFindings
  RecommendedRepairs[]
```

This is not an anti-cheat vendor plan. It is a trust-boundary plan. Vendor choice
can wait; server ownership and validation cannot.

### 16.12 P22 proof gates

P22 is not complete until:

1. Listen-server smoke passes for move, dodge, attack, block, spell, loot, save
   grant, death, respawn, and boss kill.
2. Dedicated-server smoke either passes or the project explicitly chooses not to
   support it.
3. Packet lag/loss scenarios cover dodge, root-motion attacks, projectiles,
   pickups, and boss phase transitions.
4. Inventory/equipment replication survives equip, unequip, pickup, consume,
   death, disconnect, and reconnect scenarios.
5. World-state sync survives late join and travel.
6. Session host/join/leave UI handles failure states.
7. GameplayCue and presentation events are visible to the correct clients.
8. Save progression rules are written, tested, and visible in UI.
9. Replication audit has no "unknown authority" gameplay paths.
10. Dashboard shows network proof evidence beside P22 status.

Remote proof IDs:

```text
network_authority_audit
listen_server_combat_motion
listen_server_inventory_loot
listen_server_spell_projectile
boss_phase_late_join
world_state_late_join
session_host_join_leave
network_disconnect_reconnect
network_prediction_latency
network_exploit_review
```

### 16.13 P22 starter implementation package

P22 should begin with an audit/proof spine, not with a platform provider. The
first goal is to make current single-player systems name their authority,
replication, save, and exploit assumptions before any Online Subsystem provider
or session UI becomes production-critical.

Runtime files:

First runtime stance:

```text
Single-player runtime remains authoritative locally.
Network-ready data names future authority.
No online provider is required for P22 audit work.
No client-authored persistent economy/save path is allowed.
```

`UAscentiaOnlineSubsystem` should be a facade:

```cpp
UCLASS()
class UAscentiaOnlineSubsystem : public UGameInstanceSubsystem
{
    GENERATED_BODY()

public:
    UFUNCTION(BlueprintCallable, Category="Ascentia|Online")
    FAscentiaSessionResult CreateSession(
        const FAscentiaSessionRequest& Request);

    UFUNCTION(BlueprintCallable, Category="Ascentia|Online")
    FAscentiaSessionSearchResult FindSessions(
        const FAscentiaSessionSearchRequest& Request);

    UFUNCTION(BlueprintCallable, Category="Ascentia|Online")
    FAscentiaSessionResult JoinSession(
        const FAscentiaSessionRequest& Request);

    UFUNCTION(BlueprintCallable, Category="Ascentia|Online")
    FAscentiaSessionResult LeaveSession(
        const FAscentiaSessionRequest& Request);

    UFUNCTION(BlueprintPure, Category="Ascentia|Online")
    FAscentiaOnlineCapabilities GetOnlineCapabilities() const;
};
```

Facade rules:

- returns Ascentia request/result structs, not raw provider structs;
- maps Online Subsystem errors to `DT_NetworkErrorText`;
- exposes provider capability gaps in UI;
- supports Null/LAN/dev provider before platform provider;
- keeps session UI independent from Steam/EOS/console specifics.


First scenario facts:

```text
listen_server_combat_motion
  host and client possess players
  client dodges
  client light attacks
  server validates motion/damage
  both clients receive presentation cue

listen_server_inventory_loot
  host spawns reward
  client requests pickup
  server grants or rejects
  inventory/equipment delta replicates
  save grant policy records result

world_state_late_join
  host sets gate/discovery/objective state
  late client joins
  late client receives correct visible world state

session_host_join_leave
  host session
  find session
  join session
  leave session
  failure copy resolves from `DT_NetworkErrorText`
```

Replication scaling stance:

```text
MVP / P22 starter
  standard Unreal replication, explicit relevancy, small scenarios

Open-world actor density milestone
  evaluate Replication Graph for large actor sets and zone-based relevancy

Iris pilot
  evaluate only after replicated object schemas are stable
  do not mix RepGraph and Iris as simultaneous production answers
```

The compendium should keep Replication Graph and Iris as named choices, not
vague "optimize networking later." Epic documents Replication Graph as a
persistent-node system for building per-client actor replication lists, while
Iris is an opt-in replication system and is not the same network driver path as
Replication Graph. Ascentia should pick one route per milestone.

Exploit-sensitive starter paths:

```text
Exploit.InventoryGrant
Exploit.CurrencySpend
Exploit.BossRewardClaim
Exploit.SaveMigration
Exploit.TeleportFastTravel
Exploit.DamageApplication
Exploit.StatusApplication
Exploit.ResourceCostBypass
Exploit.RootMotionBurst
Exploit.WorldStateUnlock
```

First P22 implementation tickets:

```text
P22-001 Add network authority data structs and policy assets
  acceptance: core gameplay paths can be listed with authority owner,
  prediction policy, replication policy, save relevance, and exploit sensitivity

P22-002 Add `UAscentiaNetworkAuditSubsystem`
  acceptance: dry-run audit emits `FAscentiaNetworkAuthorityAuditReport`
  with no mutation and links every unknown authority path

P22-003 Add exploit-sensitive path registry
  acceptance: inventory grants, currency spends, boss rewards, fast travel,
  damage, status, save migration, and world-state unlocks are reviewed

P22-004 Add `UAscentiaOnlineSubsystem` facade stub
  acceptance: Null/providerless mode reports capabilities and friendly failure
  copy without requiring Steam/EOS

P22-005 Add session profile validation
  acceptance: `PDA_SessionProfile` rows validate max players, join policy,
  provider policy, travel policy, and error text

P22-006 Add listen-server combat/motion scenario profile
  acceptance: two-client proof plan covers move, dodge, attack, damage,
  presentation cue, and correction logging

P22-007 Add network loot/save scenario profile
  acceptance: pickup/equip/consume/disconnect/reconnect paths produce a
  `FAscentiaNetworkLootSaveReport`

P22-008 Add boss/world late-join scenario profile
  acceptance: boss phase, fog wall, reward claim, and world-state deltas define
  late-join expected facts

P22-009 Add network dashboard card writer
  acceptance: P22 dashboard shows authority audit, exploit review, session proof,
  combat/motion proof, loot/save proof, and world/boss proof
```

Commandlet/script contracts:

```text
UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe Ascentia.uproject -run=AscentiaNetworkAudit
  -Authority
  -ExploitReview
  -AssetRoot=/Game/Ascentia
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P22/Authority

Tools/run_network_scenario.ps1
  -Scenario ListenServerCombat
  -Clients 2
  -LatencyMs 100
  -PacketLossPercent 2
  -ReportRoot Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P22/Motion
```

P22 starter proof target:

```text
network_authority_audit
  every named starter path has authority owner, validation policy, replication
  policy, save relevance, and exploit sensitivity

network_exploit_review
  every exploit-sensitive starter path has server-validation notes

session_profile_validation
  session profiles are valid even before provider implementation
```

The first P22 milestone is complete when the project can say, with evidence,
"we know who owns every important gameplay truth if multiplayer arrives." Host,
join, lag, and late-join simulations come next; provider integration comes after
the contracts stop moving.

### 16.14 Network scenario, replication proof, latency, and multiplayer lab package

The P22 starter package names authority and risk. This companion package makes
those claims executable. It defines the repeatable network scenario lab that can
launch server/client fixtures, apply latency and packet-loss profiles, watch
replicated facts, collect traces, and present the results to Designers without
requiring them to read raw network logs.

Reference posture:

- Use PIE multiplayer options only for fast local iteration and debugging.
- Use Gauntlet/UAT-style multi-session launches for real server/client proof.
- Treat Functional Tests as useful for single-instance logic, but do not rely on
  them alone to prove cross-instance replicated property behavior.
- Prefer Networking Insights for detailed network-traffic investigation; keep
  the legacy Network Profiler only as a high-level compatibility report.
- Start with standard Unreal replication and explicit relevancy. Evaluate
  Replication Graph only when actor density or connection count proves it is
  needed.
- Treat Iris as an opt-in, experimental replication route until the project has
  stable replicated object schemas and a deliberate migration plan.

Runtime/editor layout:

```text

  AscentiaNetEmulationTypes.h
```

Core structs:

```cpp
struct FAscentiaNetworkClientSpec
{
    FName ClientId;
    EAscentiaNetworkRole ExpectedRole;
    FString PlayerProfileTag;
    FString SaveSlotPolicyTag;
    bool bRecordsVideo;
    bool bRecordsInsightsTrace;
};

struct FAscentiaNetworkScenarioStep
{
    FGameplayTag StepTag;
    FText DesignerLabel;
    FGameplayTag RequiredClientTag;
    FGameplayTag ActionTag;
    TMap<FName, FString> ActionParameters;
    float TimeoutSeconds;
    FGameplayTagContainer ExpectedFactTags;
};

struct FAscentiaReplicationExpectedFact
{
    FGameplayTag FactTag;
    FString SourcePath;
    FString ExpectedAuthorityValue;
    FString ExpectedClientValue;
    float MaxPropagationSeconds;
    bool bRequiredOnLateJoin;
};

struct FAscentiaNetworkScenarioProfile
{
    FGameplayTag ScenarioTag;
    TSoftObjectPtr<UWorld> Map;
    EAscentiaNetworkLaunchMode LaunchMode;
    int32 ClientCount;
    FGameplayTag NetEmulationProfileTag;
    TArray<FAscentiaNetworkClientSpec> Clients;
    TArray<FAscentiaNetworkScenarioStep> Steps;
    TArray<FAscentiaReplicationExpectedFact> ExpectedFacts;
    FGameplayTag EvidenceProfileTag;
};

struct FAscentiaNetworkScenarioRunRequest
{
    FGuid RunId;
    FGameplayTag ScenarioTag;
    FGameplayTag NetEmulationProfileTag;
    FString BuildPath;
    FString ReportRoot;
    bool bUseEditorPIE;
    bool bUseGauntlet;
    bool bCaptureNetworkingInsights;
    bool bFailOnWarnings;
};

struct FAscentiaNetworkScenarioObservation
{
    FGameplayTag StepTag;
    FGameplayTag FactTag;
    FName ObservingClientId;
    FString AuthorityValue;
    FString ClientValue;
    float PropagationSeconds;
    bool bMatched;
    FString Notes;
};

struct FAscentiaNetworkScenarioRunResult
{
    FGuid RunId;
    FGameplayTag ScenarioTag;
    bool bSucceeded;
    TArray<FAscentiaNetworkScenarioObservation> Observations;
    TArray<FString> LogPaths;
    TArray<FString> TracePaths;
    TArray<FString> ScreenshotPaths;
    TArray<FText> Errors;
    TArray<FText> Warnings;
};
```

Designer-facing tools:

```text
EUW_AscentiaNetworkScenarioLab
  Selects scenario profile, launch mode, client count, latency preset, map,
  expected facts, and evidence profile; runs dry-run validation before launch.

EUW_AscentiaReplicationWatchBoard
  Shows watched replicated facts by system, owner, authority source, expected
  client visibility, late-join policy, and last proof result.

EUW_AscentiaNetEmulationPresetLab
  Lets Designers tune named latency/loss/jitter presets with warnings about
  which scenarios must pass under each preset.

EUW_AscentiaNetworkTraceBrowser
  Opens latest report, logs, screenshots, Networking Insights trace paths,
  actor/RPC/property summaries, and comparison against prior runs.

EUW_AscentiaMultiplayerUXFailureLab
  Previews host/join/leave/disconnect/build-mismatch failure text and validates
  that session UI can explain every scenario failure.
```
