Recommended sequencing:

1. Build P12F-001 and P12F-002 after P12 world-state tags stabilize but before
   multiple vendors, factions, or protected NPCs ship.
2. Add P12F-003 before attacks, theft, or trespass can affect friendly NPCs.
3. Add P12F-004 before writing large amounts of reactive dialogue.
4. Add P12F-005 before vendors/crafting/services become important economy
   levers.
5. Keep first proof tiny: one friendly faction, one protected NPC, one vendor,
   one witnessed assault, one forgiveness action, and one save/load fixture.

## 18. Itemization Depth, Loot Economy, Crafting, Vendors, and Rewards

### 18.1 Player-facing goal

Items should make the player ask interesting questions:

- Does this weapon change how I fight?
- Does this affix support my stat/passive direction?
- Is this armor worth the equip-load burden?
- Is this unique effect worth losing a better rare roll?
- Should I sell, salvage, upgrade, craft, or keep this?
- Is this boss reward memorable enough to shape a build?

P16 is where Ascentia turns item data into the long-term ARPG engine.

### 18.2 Current Ascentia state

Working pieces:

- `EAscentiaItemQuality` has Common, Magic, Rare, Legendary, Mythic, Unique.
- `EAscentiaItemCategory` separates Weapon, Armor, Consumable, CraftingMaterial,
  Currency, and KeyItem.
- `FAscentiaItemStack` saves item tag, quantity, seed, rolled quality, rolled
  affixes, and grid slot.
- `FAscentiaEquipmentEntry` binds item stacks to equipment slots.
- `UAscentiaItemDefinition` is a PrimaryDataAsset with item tag, display name,
  icon, category, quality, slot, meshes, grip transform, trail system, stat
  requirements, light attack bonuses, weight, stack limit, vendor value, and
  equip GameplayEffects.
- `UAscentiaAffixDefinition` is a PrimaryDataAsset with prefix/suffix text,
  minimum quality, allowed item tags, legacy stat field, magnitude range, and
  `GrantedModifierTags`.
- `UAscentiaLootTableAsset` owns deterministic and weighted rewards.
- `UAscentiaItemRegistrySubsystem` registers native fallbacks and lets designer
  assets override by GameplayTag.
- `UAscentiaLootSubsystem` rolls deterministic plus one weighted entry, builds
  item stacks from seeds, rolls prefix/suffix affixes by quality, and builds
  generated item names.
- `UAscentiaInventoryComponent` owns replicated inventory/equipment, grid slots,
  equip/unequip, stack consumption, display names, and equip-effect refresh.
- `UAscentiaCraftingSubsystem` can check ingredient/cairn-tag requirements and
  build a crafted item stack.
- `AAscentiaVendorNPC` can sell stock for Stones, mark limited items sold out,
  and record trade telemetry.
- `UAscentiaProgressionComponent` owns Stones as the current primary currency
  for leveling and vendor purchases.

Current limitations:

- affix count is fixed at prefix for Magic and prefix+suffix for Rare+;
- affix pools filter mostly by item tag, not by family/category/slot/build role;
- legacy `EAscentiaAffixStat` still narrows power to light attack damage/poise;
- no unique/legendary power architecture;
- no upgrade, tempering, socket, rune, aspect, imprint, or sanctify-style
  deterministic/chaotic modification loop;
- crafting has no inventory-consuming transaction, UI, station, or economy sink;
- vendors do not yet support selling, buyback, restock, discounts, or service
  profiles;
- item lore/flavor text is absent;
- no item compare/sort/filter/salvage;
- no economy simulator or faucet/sink reports.

### 18.3 Item design pillars

Ascentia should mix two item traditions:

```text
Soulslike authored rewards
  memorable placements, boss drops, lore identity, build-defining uniques

ARPG generated rewards
  affix-driven comparison, loot-table variance, crafting/upgrading, economy
```

Principles:

- Items are build language, not just reward confetti.
- Every item has a lifecycle: find, inspect, equip, compare, upgrade, sell,
  salvage, craft with, store, or discard.
- Random drops provide texture; authored rewards provide memory.
- Affixes should create compatibility questions, not only higher DPS.
- Bad or off-build rolls are allowed, but useless clutter is not.
- Economy sinks must scale with faucets.
- Item identity must survive save/load, migration, multiplayer, and content
  refactors.

### 18.4 Item instance and identity contract

Production item instances need stable identity beyond a stack row.

```text
FAscentiaItemInstance
  InstanceGuid
  ItemTag
  Quantity
  ItemSeed
  RolledQuality
  ItemLevel
  WorldTierBorn
  SourceTag
  SourceActorGuid
  SourceWorldStateTag
  RolledAffixes
  CraftedAffixes
  UniquePowerTags
  UpgradeState
  SocketState
  DurabilityState if ever used
  LockFavoriteState
  DiscoveredLoreState
  MutationHistory
  BoundState
  GridSlot
```

Rules:

- `ItemTag` identifies the base definition.
- `InstanceGuid` identifies this rolled/crafted/saved item.
- `ItemSeed` gives deterministic rolls for affix magnitudes and generated
  presentation.
- item instances must be serializable without hard actor references;
- stackable materials/currencies can use compressed stack records;
- equipped items, stash items, vendor buyback items, and dropped corpse loot all
  use the same instance model.

Mutation history:

```text
FAscentiaItemMutationRecord
  MutationId
  MutationType
  SourceSystemTag
  SourceActorOrServiceTag
  PreviousHash
  ResultHash
  ConsumedCurrencies
  ConsumedItemInstanceIds
  AppliedAffixTags
  AppliedPowerTags
  CreatedUtcOrServerTime
```

Do not store huge item history forever for every item. Store enough to support
debugging, save migration, economy telemetry, and player trust for meaningful
mutations: crafted, upgraded, socketed, imprinted, salvaged, traded, or boss
reward grants.

#### 18.4.1 Item transaction authority

Every item mutation should use one transaction path:

```text
FAscentiaItemTransactionRequest
  RequestId
  RequestType
  Instigator
  SourceServiceTag
  TargetItemInstanceIds
  OfferedCurrencies
  OfferedMaterials
  ContextTags
  PreviewOnly

FAscentiaItemTransactionResult
  RequestId
  Result
  FailureReasonTag
  SpentCurrencies
  ConsumedItemInstanceIds
  CreatedItemInstances
  ModifiedItemInstances
  RemovedItemInstanceIds
  AppliedWorldEffects
  PlayerTextKey
  EvidenceId
```

Transaction rules:

- server-authoritative where persistence or economy is affected;
- preview path uses the same validation as commit path;
- failure reasons are tagged and localizable;
- partial commits are not allowed unless the transaction type explicitly
  supports multiple independent results;
- every successful transaction marks save data dirty;
- every economy-affecting transaction writes telemetry/evidence.

#### 18.4.2 Item resolver, containers, and save records

The item registry should not hand gameplay code raw DataAssets plus loose stack
state. It should resolve stable item views that all systems can inspect.

```text
UAscentiaItemResolverSubsystem
  ResolveItemDefinition(ItemTag, ContextTags)
  ResolveItemInstance(ItemInstance, ResolveContext)
  BuildTooltipView(ItemInstance, TooltipContext)
  BuildComparisonView(LeftInstance, RightInstance, CompareContext)
  WriteItemizationReport(OutputPath)
```

Resolved definition:

```text
FAscentiaResolvedItemDefinition
  ItemTag
  DisplayNameKey
  LoreTextKey
  Category
  EquipmentSlot
  WeaponFamilyTags
  ArmorRoleTags
  BaseQuality
  BaseItemLevel
  BaseStats
  Requirements
  Weight
  StackPolicy
  VendorValuePolicy
  MeshRefs
  IconRef
  EquipGameplayEffects
  GrantedAbilitySets
  SourceTrace[]
  ValidationSeverity
```

Resolved instance:

```text
FAscentiaResolvedItemInstance
  InstanceGuid
  Definition
  Quantity
  RolledQuality
  ItemLevel
  WorldTierBorn
  ResolvedAffixes
  ResolvedPowers
  ResolvedUpgradeState
  ResolvedSocketState
  ResolvedRequirements
  ResolvedVendorValue
  ResolvedWeight
  TooltipView
  ComparisonGroups
  SaveHash
```

Container snapshot:

```text
FAscentiaItemContainerSnapshot
  ContainerId
  ContainerTypeTag
  OwnerActorOrSaveId
  Items
  GridState
  StackState
  CapacityPolicy
  SortFilterState
  DirtyReasonTag
```

Save record:

```text
FAscentiaItemInstanceSaveRecord
  Version
  InstanceGuid
  ItemTag
  Quantity
  ItemSeed
  RolledQuality
  ItemLevel
  WorldTierBorn
  RolledAffixTags
  RolledAffixMagnitudes
  CraftedAffixTags
  PowerTags
  UpgradeState
  SocketState
  LockFavoriteState
  BoundState
  SourceTags
  MutationSummaryHash
```

Rules:

- only save stable IDs, compact values, and versioned structs;
- runtime actors, DataAsset pointers, widget state, and temporary tooltip strings
  never belong in item save records;
- containers own placement and capacity, while item instances own identity and
  mutation state;
- every resolver output should include source trace so an item browser can show
  whether a value came from a DataTable row, PrimaryDataAsset, affix, power,
  upgrade, socket, or fallback.

### 18.5 Quality, affix count, and loot grammar

Suggested quality grammar:

```text
Common
  base item only

Magic
  1-2 affixes, narrow but clean

Rare
  3-5 affixes, main random-build workhorse

Legendary
  rare-like affixes plus one authored legendary power or aspect

Mythic
  high-end limited legendary/unique-adjacent chase item

Unique
  authored stat package and unique behavior, not fully random
```

The current system's Magic prefix and Rare prefix+suffix is an MVP seed. P16
should replace it with data:

```text
DT_ItemQualityRules
  Quality
  MinAffixes
  MaxAffixes
  PrefixLimit
  SuffixLimit
  AllowsLegendaryPower
  AllowsUniquePower
  AllowsCraftedAffixes
  AllowsSockets
  AllowsImprint
  SalvageTier
  BeamProfile
  TooltipStyle
```

Affix selection should filter by:

- item category;
- equipment slot;
- weapon family;
- damage school;
- armor role;
- world tier;
- item level;
- quality;
- source type;
- biome/region;
- exclusion group;
- build/stat tag;
- unique/legendary compatibility.

Affix pool profile:

```text
PDA_AffixPoolProfile
  PoolTag
  SourceTypes
  AllowedItemCategories
  AllowedEquipmentSlots
  AllowedWeaponFamilies
  RequiredQuality
  RequiredWorldTier
  RequiredBiomeTags
  RequiredSourceTags
  AffixWeights
  ExclusionGroups
  BadRollProtectionPolicy
```

Loot grammar should be previewable by source:

```text
FAscentiaLootRollPlan
  LootContext
  CandidateBaseItems
  CandidateAffixPools
  QualityRoll
  AffixCountRoll
  GuaranteedRewards
  WeightedRewards
  DeterministicSeed
```

Quality roll:

```text
FAscentiaQualityRollRequest
  LootContext
  SourceProfile
  BaseItemTag
  PlayerLevel
  WorldTier
  LuckModifiers
  DeterministicSeed

FAscentiaQualityRollResult
  SelectedQuality
  AffixCount
  QualityWeights
  AppliedLuckModifiers
  BeamProfile
  TooltipStyle
  SalvageTier
```

Affix roll:

```text
FAscentiaAffixRollRequest
  BaseItemTag
  ItemCategory
  EquipmentSlot
  WeaponFamilyTags
  Quality
  ItemLevel
  WorldTier
  SourceTags
  ExistingAffixTags
  BlockedExclusionGroups
  DeterministicSeed

FAscentiaAffixRollResult
  SelectedAffixes
  RejectedAffixes
  MagnitudeRolls
  ExclusionGroupsApplied
  PoolWeights
  RollTierResults
  Warnings[]
```

Loot roll result:

```text
FAscentiaLootRollResult
  LootContext
  Seed
  CreatedItemInstances
  CreatedCurrencies
  CreatedMaterials
  GuaranteedRewardsGranted
  WeightedRewardsGranted
  DuplicateProtectionApplied
  AntiFarmPolicyApplied
  PresentationEvents
  EvidenceId
```

This is where Blizzard's "easy to learn, difficult to master" itemization lesson
fits Ascentia: base damage/armor/quality give an obvious first read, but affix
tags, requirements, powers, and upgrade state carry the real build questions.

### 18.6 Modifier tag vocabulary

P16 should graduate fully from `EAscentiaAffixStat` to modifier tags and
GameplayEffects.

Modifier classes:

```text
Ascentia.Mod.Attribute.*
Ascentia.Mod.Resource.*
Ascentia.Mod.Damage.*
Ascentia.Mod.Status.*
Ascentia.Mod.Resistance.*
Ascentia.Mod.Skill.*
Ascentia.Mod.WeaponFamily.*
Ascentia.Mod.Crit.*
Ascentia.Mod.Block.*
Ascentia.Mod.Dodge.*
Ascentia.Mod.Loot.*
Ascentia.Mod.Crafting.*
Ascentia.Mod.OnHit.*
Ascentia.Mod.OnKill.*
Ascentia.Mod.Conditional.*
```

Affix data:

```text
PDA_AffixDefinition
  AffixTag
  PrefixSuffixType
  DisplayPattern
  DisplayTextKey
  ModifierTags
  GameplayEffectClass
  SetByCallerMagnitudeKeys
  MagnitudeRangeByItemLevel
  AllowedItemCategories
  AllowedEquipmentSlots
  AllowedWeaponFamilies
  RequiredQuality
  RequiredWorldTier
  ExclusionGroups
  WeightBySource
  RollTier
  TooltipPriority
  ComparisonGroup
```

Rules:

- each affix has at least one modifier tag;
- every modifier tag maps to gameplay math, UI text, and validation;
- conditional modifiers must state the condition in data;
- affixes that affect a specific skill reference skill tags;
- no affix should require bespoke UI parsing.

Resolved modifier:

```text
FAscentiaResolvedItemModifier
  ModifierTag
  SourceAffixTag
  SourcePowerTag
  Magnitude
  MagnitudeType
  AppliesToTags
  ConditionTags
  GameplayEffectClass
  SetByCallerData
  TooltipLineKey
  ComparisonGroup
```

Modifier application report:

```text
FAscentiaItemModifierApplicationReport
  ItemInstanceId
  ModifierCount
  GameplayEffectsGranted
  SetByCallerMagnitudes
  AttributePreviewDelta
  AbilityTagsAffected
  TooltipLines
  MissingMappings[]
  Conflicts[]
```

The inventory, damage model, ability system, and tooltip should all read the
resolved modifier list. UI should not parse strings like "+12 Fire Damage" to
infer gameplay meaning. Gameplay should not parse tooltip text to infer math.

### 18.7 Unique, legendary, and boss reward powers

Unique and legendary effects should be authored powers, not oversized numeric
affixes.

```text
PDA_ItemPower
  PowerTag
  DisplayName
  DescriptionText
  GameplayEffectClass
  GrantedAbilitySet
  TriggerRules
  PresentationCue
  AllowedItemTags
  AllowedSlots
  RequiredState
  BlockedState
  CompatibilityTags
  BalanceBudget
  StackPolicy
  SavePolicy
```

Power types:

- passive attribute change;
- skill mutation;
- status conversion;
- on-hit/on-kill/on-dodge trigger;
- resource loop change;
- defensive rule change;
- movement/motion rule change;
- summon/companion trigger;
- world-state or discovery utility;
- boss remembrance service.

Boss reward policy:

```text
PDA_BossRewardProfile
  BossTag
  GuaranteedRewards
  WeightedRewards
  RemembranceOptions
  VendorUnlocks
  CraftingUnlocks
  WorldStateEffects
  DuplicateProtectionPolicy
  NewGamePlusPolicy
```

For Ascentia, boss drops should often be set authored rewards with lore and
build identity, while ordinary enemies and chests can carry the random item
pressure.

Legendary/aspect profile:

```text
PDA_ItemAspectProfile
  AspectTag
  SourcePower
  ExtractionPolicy
  ImprintPolicy
  AllowedCategories
  AllowedSlots
  CostPolicy
  ConsumesSourceItem
  BindOnImprintPolicy
```

Rules:

- uniques should not be fully random rares with a special color;
- legendary/aspect powers should have compatibility data and validation;
- boss rewards must be duplicate-safe across save/load, rest, death, and NG+;
- remembrance-style choices must persist the chosen branch and expose alternate
  branches through a vendor/service profile if design permits;
- power text, tooltip, cue, and actual GameplayEffect/ability behavior must be
  validated together.

### 18.8 Crafting, upgrades, sockets, salvage, and item sinks

Crafting should be a controlled sink and agency layer, not a bypass around loot.

Crafting actions:

```text
Craft
  consume ingredients -> produce item

Upgrade
  consume Stones/materials -> raise item level or upgrade tier

Temper/Imbue
  add or reroll one crafted affix from a narrow pool

Socket
  add socket if item supports it

Set Gem/Rune
  insert removable or destructive modifier object

Salvage
  destroy item -> materials

Transmute
  exchange low-value materials/items into higher-value target

Imprint/Aspect
  apply authored power under strict compatibility rules

Sanctify/Corrupt optional later
  high-risk final modification, locks further editing
```

Data:

```text
PDA_CraftingRecipe
  RecipeTag
  StationTag
  Ingredients
  CurrencyCosts
  RequiredWorldState
  RequiredUnlocks
  ResultPolicy
  PreviewPolicy
  FailurePolicy
  PresentationCue
  ServiceProfile

PDA_UpgradeTrack
  TrackTag
  AppliesToCategories
  MaxTier
  CostsByTier
  StatScaleByTier
  UnlockRequirement
  VisualTierPolicy
  ComparisonDeltaPolicy

PDA_SalvageProfile
  Quality
  Category
  MaterialReturns
  ChanceReturns
  ProtectedItemPolicy
  PreviewPolicy
```

Rules:

- crafting consumes item instances atomically;
- preview shows the result range before confirmation;
- irreversible actions require a confirmation policy;
- crafted affixes are distinguishable from dropped affixes;
- salvage destroys items and must respect locked/favorite state;
- upgrades must not invalidate comparison clarity;
- materials should have named faucets and sinks.

Crafting preview:

```text
FAscentiaCraftingPreview
  RecipeTag
  TargetItemInstance
  RequiredCosts
  MissingCosts
  PossibleResults
  ResultStatRange
  ResultAffixRange
  FailureReasons
  ConfirmationPolicy
```

Salvage preview:

```text
FAscentiaSalvagePreview
  TargetItemInstances
  GuaranteedMaterials
  ChanceMaterials
  BlockedItems
  LockedOrFavoriteItems
  LostPowers
  ConfirmationPolicy
```

Crafting, upgrading, tempering, socketing, imprinting, and salvage should call
the item transaction path from section 18.4.1. P12 service profiles decide where
the player can request the action. P16 item/economy rules decide whether the
action is legal and what it costs.

Transaction subsystem:

```text
UAscentiaItemTransactionSubsystem
  PreviewTransaction(Request)
  CommitTransaction(Request)
  RollLoot(LootContext)
  ApplyItemMutation(MutationRequest)
  WriteTransactionLedger(OutputPath)
```

Mutation preview:

```text
FAscentiaItemMutationPreview
  RequestType
  TargetItem
  BeforeResolvedInstance
  AfterResolvedInstance
  RequiredCosts
  MissingCosts
  RiskPolicy
  Irreversible
  ConfirmationTextKey
  FailureReasons[]
```

Ledger entry:

```text
FAscentiaItemTransactionLedgerEntry
  RequestId
  TransactionType
  InstigatorId
  SourceServiceTag
  BeforeContainerHashes
  AfterContainerHashes
  SpentCurrencies
  ConsumedItems
  CreatedItems
  ModifiedItems
  FailureReasonTag
  SaveDirtyReason
  EvidenceId
```

`EUW_AscentiaItemTransactionReplay` should load ledger entries and rebuild the
before/after item views. This is useful for debugging "my loot vanished" class
bugs because the answer should be visible as data: consumed, moved, stacked,
sold, salvaged, saved, or failed.

### 18.9 Economy model: faucets, sinks, caps, and conversion

Every resource needs an economy card.

```text
FAscentiaEconomyResource
  ResourceTag
  DisplayName
  Type
  StackPolicy
  Faucets
  Sinks
  Caps
  ConversionRules
  ExpectedPerHourByTier
  TargetSpendPerHourByTier
  HoardingRisk
```

Economy profile:

```text
PDA_EconomyProfile
  ProfileTag
  WorldTierRange
  ResourceDefinitions
  FaucetProfiles
  SinkProfiles
  ConversionProfiles
  VendorPriceProfiles
  CraftingCostProfiles
  UpgradeCostProfiles
  TargetCurves
  HoardingThresholds
```

Faucet and sink rows:

```text
FAscentiaEconomyFaucetRow
  FaucetTag
  ResourceTag
  SourceType
  ExpectedPerUse
  ExpectedUsesPerHour
  WorldTierScalar
  RegionScalar
  AntiFarmPolicy

FAscentiaEconomySinkRow
  SinkTag
  ResourceTag
  SinkType
  ExpectedCostPerUse
  ExpectedUsesPerHour
  WorldTierScalar
  ProgressionGate
  OptionalOrRequired
```

Initial resources:

- Stones;
- crafting materials;
- boss/remembrance tokens if chosen;
- upgrade materials;
- socket/gem/rune materials if chosen;
- key items;
- region currencies only if truly needed.

Faucets:

- enemy kills;
- boss kills;
- chests;
- POI completion;
- The Below completion;
- resource nodes;
- salvage;
- questless world-state rewards;
- vendor buyback only if buyback returns value;
- NG+ scaling.

Sinks:

- leveling;
- vendor purchases;
- crafting;
- upgrades;
- respec;
- fast-travel tolls only if design wants friction;
- repair only if durability becomes a real mechanic;
- stash expansion if storage friction is desired;
- cosmetic/transmog if later appropriate.

Proof requires balance curves, not intuition. `EUW_AscentiaLootEconomyLab` should
simulate kills, chests, vendors, crafting, upgrades, and salvage over expected
playtime.

Economy simulation profile:

```text
PDA_EconomySimulationProfile
  ProfileTag
  WorldTierRange
  PlayerLevelRange
  EncounterMix
  ChestOpenRate
  BossKillRate
  VendorVisitRate
  CraftingUseRate
  UpgradeUseRate
  SalvageUseRate
  RunCount
  RandomSeeds
  TargetMetrics
```

Economy report:

```text
FAscentiaEconomySimulationReport
  ProfileTag
  Runs
  ItemsPerHourByQuality
  AffixFrequency
  UniquePowerFrequency
  StonesPerHour
  MaterialsPerHour
  SpendPerHour
  HoardingRiskByResource
  DeadDropRate
  VendorValueDistribution
  SalvageReturnDistribution
  UpgradeAffordabilityCurve
  CraftingAffordabilityCurve
  SuggestedTuningDeltas
```

Economy report acceptance should include percentile bands, not only averages.
The average player and the unlucky player experience different economies. A
P16 report should therefore show p10/p50/p90 for item quality, Stones, upgrade
material pressure, and crafting affordability.

Value chain:

```text
Drop -> Inspect -> Equip or Keep -> Upgrade/Craft or Sell/Salvage -> Spend
```

Each resource should have a visible path through that chain. If a material drops
but has no meaningful sink in the next several hours of play, it is clutter. If
a sink exists but the faucet cannot support it, the system feels punitive.

### 18.10 Vendors, shops, stash, and buyback

P12 defines vendors as NPC interactions. P16 defines the economy rules.

Vendor data:

```text
PDA_VendorProfile
  VendorId
  StockPools
  StaticStock
  RestockPolicy
  BuyPolicy
  SellPolicy
  BuybackPolicy
  PriceCurves
  DiscountRules
  WorldStateUnlocks
  ServiceProfiles
  BuybackLimit
  BuybackExpirationPolicy
  LimitedStockSavePolicy
```

Shop UI needs:

- buy tab;
- sell tab;
- buyback tab if supported;
- compare tooltip;
- item lock/favorite respect;
- affordability state;
- confirmation policy;
- stock sold-out/restock state;
- price explanation if modified by world state;
- controller navigation;
- accessibility text sizing.

Buyback should be short-window and bounded. A permanent buyback list becomes a
stash and can explode save size.

Vendor transaction policy:

```text
PDA_VendorTransactionPolicy
  BuyMarkupCurve
  SellValueCurve
  BuybackMarkupCurve
  RestockInterval
  LimitedStockPolicy
  DiscountRules
  ReputationOrWorldStateModifiers
  ConfirmationThresholds
```

Stash policy:

```text
PDA_StashProfile
  ProfileTag
  SlotPolicy
  GridSize
  StackPolicy
  ExpansionCosts
  SharedBetweenCharacters
  SavePolicy
  SortFilterPolicy
```

Vendor, stash, and buyback all use item instance IDs. They should not duplicate
items by copying loose item stacks without preserving instance identity and
mutation state.

### 18.11 Inventory, compare, filters, and player decision support

The inventory should help decisions without solving builds automatically.

Features:

- compare equipped item;
- stat delta preview;
- affix roll range display;
- item source/lore display;
- quality, slot, category, and tag filters;
- sort by recent, quality, slot, value, weight, item power, name;
- lock/favorite;
- mark junk;
- bulk sell/salvage with confirmation;
- tooltip verbosity setting;
- controller-friendly grid navigation.

Tooltip model:

```text
FAscentiaItemTooltipView
  Header
  Quality
  CategorySlot
  ItemLevel
  Requirements
  BaseStats
  AffixLines
  UniquePowerLines
  UpgradeState
  SocketLines
  CompareDelta
  LoreText
  SourceText
  VendorValue
  Warnings
```

The tooltip should be a view model built from resolved item data, not ad hoc UI
string assembly.

### 18.12 Loot sources, reward pacing, and world integration

Loot tables need context:

```text
FAscentiaLootContext
  SourceType
  SourceTag
  RegionTag
  BiomeTag
  WorldTier
  PlayerLevel
  EncounterDifficulty
  BossTag
  POITag
  CompletionState
  LuckModifiers
  Seed
```

Loot source profile:

```text
PDA_LootSourceProfile
  SourceTag
  SourceType
  LootTables
  GuaranteedRewards
  WorldTierPolicy
  FirstTimeRewardPolicy
  RepeatRewardPolicy
  AntiFarmPolicy
  PresentationProfile
```

Reward pacing profile:

```text
PDA_RewardPacingProfile
  ProfileTag
  WorldTier
  RegionTag
  ExpectedCombatRewardsPerHour
  ExpectedDiscoveryRewardsPerHour
  ExpectedBossRewards
  QualityTargetsBySource
  CurrencyTargetsBySource
  MaterialTargetsBySource
  DeadDropThreshold
  DuplicateThreshold
  MilestoneRewardChecks
```

Loot distribution report:

```text
FAscentiaLootDistributionReport
  SourceProfile
  Runs
  Seeds
  ItemsByQuality
  ItemsByCategory
  AffixFrequency
  PowerFrequency
  CurrencyTotals
  MaterialTotals
  DeadDropRate
  DuplicateRate
  ExpectedVendorValue
  ExpectedSalvageReturn
  OutlierDrops
```

Reward pacing report:

```text
FAscentiaRewardPacingReport
  ProfileTag
  SimulatedHours
  SourceCoverage
  RewardsPerHour
  QualityPacing
  CurrencyPacing
  MaterialPacing
  BossRewardCoverage
  POIRewardCoverage
  DeadDropFindings
  DuplicateFindings
  SuggestedTuningDeltas
```

Reward pacing:

- common enemies give currency/material texture;
- elites give higher quality chance and focused pools;
- bosses give authored rewards and unlocks;
- chests give region identity and discovery value;
- POIs give completion rewards plus map/world state;
- The Below can be the experiment space for higher variance rewards;
- vendors/crafting turn surplus into intentional progress.

No loot source should exist without a reason in the item lifecycle.

### 18.14 P16 evidence, validation, and reports

P16 report set:

```text
FAscentiaItemizationReport
  RunId
  ItemDefinitionsChecked
  AffixDefinitionsChecked
  AffixPoolsChecked
  LootTablesChecked
  ItemPowersChecked
  CraftingRecipesChecked
  UpgradeTracksChecked
  VendorProfilesChecked
  EconomyResourcesChecked
  MissingTags
  MissingEffects
  UnreachableAffixes
```

Validation examples:

- "Affix Ascentia.Affix.FireDamage has modifier tag but no SetByCaller mapping."
- "Rare quality allows 5 affixes, but UI tooltip profile displays only 4."
- "Loot source RuinsChest can roll no valid weapon for WorldTier 2."
- "Crafting recipe AshenBlade consumes item instances but has no preview policy."
- "Upgrade track SwordIron raises damage but item compare does not show the
  delta."
- "BossReward CairnHeart grants unique power but duplicate protection is None."
- "Economy resource Shard has faucet but no sink before level 20."

```text
item_instance_save
affix_modifier_application
loot_distribution_report

boss_reward_grant
crafting_transaction
upgrade_transaction
salvage_transaction
vendor_buy_sell_buyback
inventory_compare_filters
economy_simulation_report
```

### 18.15 P16 proof gates

P16 is not complete until:

1. Item registry resolves DataTables and PrimaryDataAssets into one inspected
   read model.
2. Affixes use modifier tags and GameplayEffects/SetByCaller magnitudes, not
   only the legacy enum.
3. Loot tables can be simulated by source, world tier, region, and seed.
4. Quality rules determine affix counts, beams, salvage tier, and UI treatment.
5. At least one unique or legendary power changes gameplay behavior and is
   validated.
6. At least one boss reward profile grants a memorable authored reward.
7. Crafting consumes inventory/materials transactionally and previews results.
8. Upgrading or tempering provides a meaningful sink without invalidating loot.
9. Selling, buyback, salvage, and vendor stock have UI and save persistence.
10. Item compare/sort/filter/favorite/junk flows work on mouse and controller.
11. Economy simulator reports faucets, sinks, expected items/hour, expected
    Stones/hour, material pressure, and hoarding risks.
12. Packaged smoke proves loot, equip, vendor, craft/upgrade/salvage, save/load,
    and tooltip presentation without native fallback-only content.

### 18.16 P16 starter implementation package

The first P16 package should make item truth inspectable and transactions
replayable before the loot surface expands. The immediate goal is not endgame
crafting depth. It is stable item identity, a resolver, a transaction ledger,
deterministic loot simulation, tooltip views, and economy evidence.

Implementation tickets:

```text
P16-001 Item instance identity and save records
  Introduce `FAscentiaItemInstance`, `FAscentiaItemInstanceSaveRecord`,
  instance GUIDs, deterministic seeds, source tags, versioning, and save/load
  fixture tests.

P16-002 Item resolver and tooltip views
  Implement `UAscentiaItemResolverSubsystem`,
  `FAscentiaResolvedItemDefinition`, `FAscentiaResolvedItemInstance`,
  `FAscentiaItemTooltipView`, source trace, and compare view.

P16-003 Transaction request/result/ledger path
  Implement `UAscentiaItemTransactionSubsystem`,
  `FAscentiaItemTransactionRequest`, `FAscentiaItemTransactionResult`, and
  `FAscentiaItemTransactionLedgerEntry` for loot, equip, buy, sell, and move.

P16-004 Quality and affix roll grammar
  Add `PDA_ItemQualityProfile_MVP`, `FAscentiaQualityRollRequest`,
  `FAscentiaQualityRollResult`, `FAscentiaAffixRollRequest`, and
  `FAscentiaAffixRollResult`.

P16-005 Modifier tag and GAS application bridge
  Move affix behavior toward `FAscentiaResolvedItemModifier`, Gameplay Tags,
  GameplayEffects, and SetByCaller magnitude mappings with validation reports.

P16-006 Loot source profiles and distribution reports
  Add `PDA_LootSourceProfile_*`, `FAscentiaLootContext`,
  `FAscentiaLootRollResult`, deterministic seed replay, and 10,000-run reports.

P16-007 Craft/upgrade/salvage transaction previews
  Add `PDA_CraftingRecipe_*`, `PDA_UpgradeTrack_*`, `PDA_SalvageProfile_*`,
  `FAscentiaItemMutationPreview`, and preview/commit proof.

P16-008 Vendor, buyback, stash, and transaction policy
  Add `PDA_VendorTransactionPolicy_MVP`, `PDA_StashProfile_MVP`, buy/sell/
  buyback transaction rows, favorite/locked item protection, and save proof.

P16-009 Economy simulation and reward pacing
  Add `PDA_EconomyProfile_MVP`, `PDA_EconomySimulationProfile_MVPArena_OneHour`,
  `FAscentiaEconomySimulationReport`, and `FAscentiaRewardPacingReport`.

P16-010 P16 packaged proof suite
  Run item save, resolver, affix application, loot distribution, transaction,
  crafting/upgrade/salvage, vendor, tooltip, economy, and reward pacing proofs
  in PIE and packaged Development.
```

Validation gates:

- any item definition without stable item tag, icon, display text, category,
  tooltip policy, and resolver source trace fails validation;
- any item instance save record with actor refs, asset pointers, widget text, or
  unversioned payload fails validation;
- any affix without modifier tags, GameplayEffect/SetByCaller mapping, tooltip
  group, roll range, and exclusion policy fails validation;
- any loot source without deterministic replay and distribution report fails
  validation;
- any economy resource with faucet but no meaningful sink in the simulation
  horizon is reported as hoarding risk;
- any crafting/upgrade/salvage action that bypasses the transaction path fails
  validation;
- any sell/salvage path that ignores favorite/locked state fails validation;
- any tooltip view that overflows long localized text fails UI stress proof.

Recommended sequencing:

1. Add `FAscentiaItemInstance` and save records while adapting current stacks.
2. Build the resolver and tooltip views over existing item definitions.
3. Move loot corpse, equip, vendor buy, sell, and container move into the
   transaction subsystem with ledger evidence.
4. Add quality/affix roll data that reproduces current Magic/Rare behavior.
5. Add loot distribution and economy simulation commandlets before expanding
   crafting depth.
6. Add craft/upgrade/salvage previews only after transaction replay is reliable.

### 18.17 The Below endgame run, modular dungeon, reward, and reset package

The MVP already proves a Below entry, authored pocket arena, guardian, return,
and cache reward. Production needs a real run architecture. The Below should be
the replayable, high-variance experiment space for rewards, hazards, bosses,
modifiers, and world-state pressure, but it must stay deterministic, inspectable,
save-safe, and authored enough that it does not feel like random rooms glued
together.

Engine posture:

- Level Instances are the right authoring unit for reusable room chunks,
  corridors, encounter arenas, treasure rooms, shrine rooms, and exit rooms.
- Packed Level Actors can be used for static repeated room dressing when runtime
  variation is not needed.
- PCG can propose decoration, clutter, hazards, and optional branches inside a
  room, but run-critical doors, exits, bosses, rewards, and checkpoints remain
  authored and validated.
- World Partition/Data Layers still own streaming and runtime activation for
  Below entrances, return points, and any large Below pocket connected to the
  overworld.
- The run generator is data-driven and deterministic by seed. A run can be
  replayed from its profile, seed, chosen room sequence, modifier list, and
  reward seed without relying on live actor order.

Runtime module layout:

```text
Source/Ascentia/Public/Endgame/AscentiaBelowRunTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Endgame/AscentiaBelowRunSubsystem.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Endgame/AscentiaBelowRoomTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Endgame/AscentiaBelowModifierTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Endgame/AscentiaBelowRewardTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Endgame/AscentiaBelowSaveTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Private/Endgame/...
```

Core data structures:

```text
FAscentiaBelowRunProfile
  RunProfileTag
  RequiredWorldState
  TierProfileTag
  RoomSetTag
  ModifierSetTag
  RewardProfileTag
  PacingProfileTag
  TransitionProfileTag
  SaveResumePolicyTag
  FailurePolicyTag
  MaxRooms
  MinCombatRooms
  MaxOptionalBranches

FAscentiaBelowRunState
  RunGuid
  RunProfileTag
  RunSeed
  RewardSeed
  TierTag
  ActiveRoomIndex
  ChosenRooms
  ChosenModifiers
  CompletedRoomIds
  ClaimedRewardIds
  EntryWorldAnchorTag
  ReturnWorldAnchorTag
  bCompleted
  bFailed
  StartedAtUtc

FAscentiaBelowRoomDefinition
  RoomTag
  RoomTypeTag
  LevelInstanceAsset
  PackedLevelActorAsset
  AllowedDoorSockets
  RequiredDoorSockets
  EncounterProfileTag
  HazardProfileTag
  RewardNodeTags
  PCGDecorationProfileTag
  DataLayerPolicyTag
  StreamingBudgetTag
  NavValidationTag
  CameraReadabilityTag

FAscentiaBelowConnection
  FromRoomTag
  FromSocketTag
  ToRoomTag
  ToSocketTag
  DoorPolicyTag
  LockPolicyTag
  TraversalRequirementTags
  BacktrackPolicyTag

FAscentiaBelowModifier
  ModifierTag
  DisplayTextKey
  DifficultyBudgetDelta
  RewardBudgetDelta
  GameplayTagGrants
  EnemyAffixTags
  HazardTags
  PresentationTags
  AccessibilityRiskTags

FAscentiaBelowRewardNode
  RewardNodeTag
  RewardSourceProfile
  CompletionRequirementTags
  GuaranteedRewards
  WeightedRewardTables
  CurrencyGrant
  UniqueRewardPolicy
  ClaimPolicyTag
```

Subsystem boundary:

```text
UAscentiaBelowRunSubsystem : UWorldSubsystem
  CanStartRun(ProfileTag, Context)
  StartRun(ProfileTag, OptionalSeed, Context)
  BuildRunLayout(RunState)
  LoadRunRoom(RoomIndex)
  CompleteRoom(RoomTag, CompletionContext)
  ClaimReward(RewardNodeTag)
  FailRun(FailureReasonTag)
  ReturnAbove(ReturnReasonTag)
  BuildRunSaveFragment()
  RestoreRunFromSave(Fragment)
  WriteBelowRunEvidence(OutputPath)
```

Rules:

- A generated Below run is a deterministic plan first and a streamed/playable
  space second. The plan writes chosen rooms, sockets, modifiers, encounters,
  reward nodes, and seeds before gameplay begins.
- Room kits are authored assets. PCG may decorate, vary clutter, and fill
  optional dressing sockets, but it cannot invent critical doors, exits, reward
  nodes, bosses, or save anchors.
- Every room definition declares entrance/exit sockets, nav build status,
  camera readability, encounter budget, reward node policy, and performance
  budget.
- Difficulty modifiers alter declared systems only: enemy profile tags, hazard
  tags, reward budget, presentation tags, and accessibility risk tags. No
  modifier may patch arbitrary Blueprint logic at runtime.
- Rewards go through P16 loot source profiles and item transactions. Below cache
  rewards are not special-case item grants.
- Encounters go through P18 encounter definitions. A guardian room is an
  encounter profile, not a hard-coded enemy spawn.
- Entry, return, death, disconnect, save/load, and abandon all have explicit
  transition policies. No run can strand the player in an unloaded or invalid
  space.
- Run save data stores the run plan and meaningful room/reward completion state,
  not live actor pointers or generated component state.
- Accessibility profiles can reduce visual intensity, fog, flashing hazards,
  camera shake, and audio density without changing reward seeds or completion
  authority.

Designer-facing tools:

```text
EUW_AscentiaBelowRunLab
  choose profile, seed, tier, room set, modifiers, reward profile, and preview
  generated layout before launching a run

EUW_AscentiaBelowRoomKitBoard
  inspect room definitions, Level Instance assets, door sockets, nav, camera,
  encounter, hazard, reward, PCG, Data Layer, and performance validation

EUW_AscentiaBelowPacingSimulator
  simulate room count, combat/rest/treasure cadence, modifiers, expected time,
  reward value, failure rate, and resource pressure

EUW_AscentiaBelowRewardLab
  preview cache rewards, unique policies, currency grants, reward-node claims,
  and transaction ledger rows

EUW_AscentiaBelowTransitionProof
  test enter, room travel, return, death, abandon, save/load, and packaged
  streaming readiness
```

```text
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P16/BelowRewards/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P16/BelowTransitions/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P16/BelowDeterminism/{RunId}/report.json
```

Commandlet contracts:

```text
UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe Ascentia.uproject -run=AscentiaBelowRunAudit
  -Profile=/Game/Ascentia/Endgame/Below/Profiles/PDA_BelowRunProfile_MVP
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P16/BelowRuns

UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe Ascentia.uproject -run=AscentiaBelowRunSimulation
  -Profile=/Game/Ascentia/Endgame/Below/Profiles/PDA_BelowRunProfile_MVP
  -Seeds=1000
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P16/BelowPacing
```

Starter proof matrix:

```text
BelowRun_MVP_DeterministicLayout
  same profile and seed produce identical rooms, sockets, modifiers, rewards,
  and encounter tags

BelowRoomKit_DoorSocketConnectivity
  every selected room connects entrance/exit sockets without overlaps, missing
  doors, invalid backtracking, or unreachable exits

BelowRoomKit_LevelInstanceValidation
  each room Level Instance has nav, collision, lighting, Data Layer policy,
  camera readability, encounter anchors, and reward sockets

BelowRun_ModifierBudget
  modifiers alter declared tags and budgets only, with accessibility risk rows

BelowReward_TransactionLedger
  cache and room rewards grant through P16 transaction requests with replayable
  ledger output

BelowEncounter_GuardianProfile
  guardian room resolves through P18 encounter/enemy profiles and persists
  defeat/completion according to run policy

BelowTransition_EnterReturnDeathAbandon
  enter, return, death, abandon, save/load, and packaged streaming readiness
  restore the player to valid anchors

BelowRun_SaveLoadResume
  run plan, active room, claimed rewards, and completed rooms survive save/load
  without saving actor pointers

BelowPacing_OneHundredSeeds
  simulation reports p10/p50/p90 room count, time, rewards, resource pressure,
  failure risk, and dead-run conditions
```

Implementation tickets:

```text
P16B-001 Below run profile, room, modifier, reward, and save structs
  Add `FAscentiaBelowRunProfile`, `FAscentiaBelowRunState`,
  `FAscentiaBelowRoomDefinition`, `FAscentiaBelowModifier`,
  `FAscentiaBelowRewardNode`, and profile DataAssets.

P16B-002 Deterministic run builder
  Add `UAscentiaBelowRunSubsystem`, seed selection, room sequence generation,
  connection validation, modifier selection, and run evidence output.

P16B-003 Room kit and Level Instance validation
  Add room-kit audits for sockets, nav, collision, camera, encounter anchors,
  rewards, PCG decoration policy, Data Layers, and performance budget.

P16B-004 Reward and transaction bridge
  Route Below rewards through P16 loot source profiles and item transactions,
  with cache reward ledger replay and reward pacing reports.

P16B-005 Encounter, hazard, and modifier bridge
  Route guardian/combat rooms through P18 encounter definitions, hazards through
  declared profiles, and modifiers through allowed tags and budget deltas.

P16B-006 Transition, save/load, and failure proof
  Add enter/return/death/abandon/save/load policies, valid anchor checks,
  streaming readiness proof, and run save fragments.

P16B-007 Designer Below labs and dashboard cards
  Add run lab, room kit board, pacing simulator, reward lab, transition proof,
  evidence cards, and package gates for invalid runs.
```

Recommended sequencing:

1. Build P16B-001 and P16B-002 around the current MVP Below run facts.
2. Add P16B-003 before authoring more room kits or importing modular dungeon
   packs.
3. Add P16B-004 before expanding Below rewards beyond the cache shard.
4. Add P16B-005 before guardian variants, hazards, or difficulty modifiers.
5. Add P16B-006 before any run can be saved mid-Below or used in packaged
   milestone proof.

## 19. Damage Model, Build Math, Status, Criticals, and Defensive Identity

### 19.1 Player-facing goal

P13 is where Ascentia becomes a build game, not merely an action prototype with
health bars.

The player-facing promise:

- attacks have readable damage types;
- armor, resistances, poise, block, parry, dodge, and status effects create
  distinct defensive identities;
- stats matter because they change resources, scaling, crits, equipment burden,
  spell economy, and passive unlocks;
- items matter because their affixes speak the same language as attributes,
  abilities, status, and movement;
- UI explains enough that a player can make a build decision without opening a
  spreadsheet;
- Designers can tune curves, thresholds, payloads, and profiles in the Editor;
- proof tools show the exact formula path for any combat scenario.

The target feel is not "numbers everywhere." It is Soulslike commitment and
readability joined to ARPG build expressiveness. The player should understand
why a heavy armor build survives, why a glass spellblade bursts, why poison is
pressure rather than instant damage, why frost creates a punish window, and why
equip load changes dodge identity.

### 19.2 Current Ascentia state

The native MVP is already useful:

- `FAscentiaDamagePacket` carries magnitude, damage school, poise damage,
  optional status buildup tag/amount/threshold, source tags, and instigator.
- `EAscentiaDamageSchool` currently includes Physical, Slash, Strike, Pierce,
  Fire, Frost, Lightning, Poison, Rot, Arcane, and TrueDamage.
- `UAscentiaCombatComponent::ApplyDamagePacket` is server-authoritative and
  handles parry, dodge iframes, block, guard break, armor/resistance formula
  mitigation, health/poise SetByCaller magnitudes, status buildup, cue request
  telemetry, and replicated last-damage facts.
- Physical-family damage uses armor mitigation. The current MVP formula is:

```text
DamageAfterArmor = RawDamage * (100 / (100 + Armor))
```

- Per-school resistance is clamped from -95% to +95% and applied after armor:

```text
FinalDamage = DamageAfterArmor * (1 - ResistancePercent / 100)
```

- TrueDamage bypasses armor and resistance.
- Status buildup accumulates per status tag, preserves overflow after threshold,
  and emits `OnStatusBuildupChanged`.
- Status payloads currently support native Poison/Rot-style damage-over-time and
  Frost burst damage/poise, with replicated payload telemetry and
  `OnStatusPayloadTriggered`.
- Critical execution already scales off replicated `CriticalChancePercent`,
  `CriticalDamageMultiplier`, AGI-derived effective chance, and INT-derived
  effective multiplier, then applies true-damage execution output.
- `UAscentiaAttributeSet` exposes replicated primary stats, resources, armor,
  crit, poise, movement, spectrum stats, equip load, and meta incoming damage.
- Current derived defaults:

```text
MaxHealth    = 40 + 6.0 * CON
MaxBreath    = 70 + 3.0 * END
BreathRegen  = 10 + 0.5 * END
MaxEth       = 20 + 4.0 * FOC
MaxEquipLoad = 30 + 1.0 * CON
```

- `UAscentiaProgressionComponent` handles Stones, level cost, unspent stat
  points, invest/refund/reset, active threshold passive tags, and tier unlocks
  beginning at 20 and every 10 points thereafter.
- Equip load already drives roll tier, dodge Breath cost, impulse strength,
  iframe duration, and overloaded rejection.

The production gaps are also clear:

- status payload choice is string-matched from the status tag;
- formulas are component constants instead of named data profiles and curves;
- armor/resistance/crit/stat scaling has no Designer-facing formula lab yet;
- status decay, immunity, refresh, stacking, cleanse, boss resistance, and UI
  rules are not authored as data;
- random crit, backstab, riposte, stance-break execution, and weapon-specific
  crit curves are not separated enough;
- stat curves and soft caps are hard-coded linear relationships;
- threshold passives unlock tags, but the actual passive effects/icons/callouts
  still need authored data;
- build UI does not yet preview effective survivability, damage, status buildup,
  roll tier, or passive changes.

### 19.3 Production damage pipeline

Damage should move through one authoritative pipeline no matter whether the
source is a light attack, spell, throwable, bow shot, trap, status tick, boss
move, environmental hazard, or future co-op/invasion actor.

Pipeline:

```text
Ability / weapon / AI / hazard
  -> builds FAscentiaDamagePacket or FAscentiaDamageEvent
  -> validates contact, target, source tags, authority, and friendly-fire policy
  -> resolves source profile: ability, weapon, item affixes, buffs, stat scaling
  -> resolves target profile: attributes, armor, resistances, guard, poise, tags
  -> applies defensive state: parry, iframe, block, guard break, absorption
  -> applies conditional offense: backstab, riposte, crit, weak point, counter hit
  -> applies formula mitigation: armor, resistance, vulnerability, caps
  -> applies status buildup: resistance, threshold, decay, overflow, immunity
  -> writes GAS SetByCaller magnitudes for health, poise, status, and cues
  -> Attribute Set clamps resources and health
  -> emits telemetry, GameplayCues, UI events, audio/VFX, camera, rumble
```

Every stage must be observable. A Designer should be able to click a damage
report and see:

```text
RawDamage
SourceAbility
WeaponProfile
DamageSchool
SourceTags
TargetTags
Armor
ArmorMitigation
ResistancePercent
ConditionalMultipliers
FinalHealthDamage
FinalPoiseDamage
StatusBuildupAdded
StatusBuildupTotal
StatusPayloadTriggered
CueTags
```

The current `ApplyDamagePacket` function is the MVP spine. Production should
move calculation complexity into a named GAS execution or native calculation
object so it can capture source/target attributes and tags consistently.
GameplayEffects remain the unit of application; DataAssets and curves author the
policy; native code owns the final invariant.

#### 19.3.1 GAS execution and formula trace contract

Epic's GAS model fits this phase well: Gameplay Abilities own activation,
Gameplay Effects own attribute mutation, Attribute Sets own replicated values,
and Gameplay Effect Calculations can concentrate complex source/target math in
one reusable place. Ascentia should use that shape without hiding the numbers.

Recommended route:

```text
UAscentiaDamageExecutionCalculation
  captures source attributes, target attributes, source tags, target tags
  calls UAscentiaDamageFormulaService
  writes SetByCaller health, poise, status, and cue magnitudes

UAscentiaDamageFormulaService
  pure/native deterministic formula resolver
  usable by runtime execution, editor formula lab, tests, and reports

FAscentiaDamageFormulaInput
  SourceActor
  TargetActor
  SourceAbilityTag
  SourceItemId
  DamageProfile
  DamagePackets[]
  SourceAttributeSnapshot
  TargetAttributeSnapshot
  SourceTags
  TargetTags
  DefensiveStateSnapshot
  ConditionalContext
  RandomSeed
  PredictionKeyOrTraceId

FAscentiaDamageFormulaOutput
  PacketResults[]
  TotalHealthDamage
  TotalPoiseDamage
  TotalStatusBuildupByTag
  TriggeredStatusPayloads[]
  CritResult
  GuardResult
  StanceResult
  CueRequests[]
  RejectionReason
  bApplied

FAscentiaDamageFormulaTrace
  TraceId
  SourceSummary
  TargetSummary
  FormulaProfileId
  StepResults[]
  CapturedAttributes[]
  CapturedTags
  FinalOutput
  DesignerNotes
```

Why keep a formula service separate from the GAS execution? The execution is the
runtime adapter. The service is the reusable truth. `EUW_AscentiaDamageFormulaLab`
can call the service against scenario snapshots without spawning real combat.
Automation can diff trace JSON. Designers can inspect the same steps that the
server uses.

Trace rules:

- each multiplication, clamp, immunity, rejection, and SetByCaller write gets a
  named trace step;
- trace steps carry before/after values and source asset references;
- random crits record seed, roll, chance, and result;
- deterministic execution windows record angle, range, target state, and source
  state;
- true damage records the approving profile and source tag;
- status writes record threshold, resistance, decay state, overflow, and payload;
- Designer-facing reports must use display names, but machine reports must use
  asset paths, tags, and row ids.

### 19.4 Damage profile data

Use authored profiles instead of local constants.

```text
PDA_DamageProfile
  DamageProfileId
  DisplayName
  SourceKind: Weapon, Ability, Spell, Status, Hazard, EnemyMove
  BasePackets[]
  ScalingRules[]
  ConditionalRules[]
  StatusBuildupRules[]
  PoiseRules
  CueProfile
  DamageNumberProfile
  TelemetryGroup

FAscentiaDamagePacketRow
  Magnitude
  DamageSchool
  PoiseDamage
  StatusBuildupTag
  StatusBuildupAmount
  StatusTriggerThreshold
  SourceTags

FAscentiaDamageScalingRule
  Attribute
  Curve
  SoftCapProfile
  WeaponScalingGrade
  MinContribution
  MaxContribution
  SourceTagRequirements

FAscentiaConditionalDamageRule
  RuleTag
  RequiredSourceTags
  RequiredTargetTags
  ForbiddenTargetTags
  Multiplier
  AdditiveBonus
  Priority
  TelemetryLabel
```

Additional production profiles:

```text
PDA_DamageSchoolProfile
  SchoolTag
  DisplayName
  SchoolFamily: Physical, Elemental, Status, Arcane, True
  ResistanceAttribute
  DefaultDamageNumberProfile
  DefaultCueFamily
  ArmorInteractionPolicy
  VulnerabilityPolicy
  ColorPaletteKey
  TooltipTextKey

PDA_DamageFormulaProfile
  FormulaProfileId
  ArmorCurve
  ResistanceClampMin
  ResistanceClampMax
  MinimumDamagePolicy
  RoundingPolicy
  TrueDamageAllowedSourceTags
  FormulaStepOrder
  TraceVerbosity

PDA_DefenseFormulaProfile
  DefenseProfileId
  ArmorAppliesToFamilies
  ArmorMitigationCurve
  BlockBreathCostCurve
  GuardBreakPolicy
  PoiseDamageTakenCurve
  EffectiveSurvivabilityScenarios

PDA_EffectiveSurvivabilityProfile
  ScenarioSetId
  IncomingDamageSamples[]
  SchoolMix
  IncludeBlock
  IncludeDodgeAvoidance
  IncludePotionRecovery
  IncludeStatusPressure
  TooltipPolicy

FAscentiaDefenseBreakdown
  RawIncomingDamage
  ArmorValue
  ArmorMitigationPercent
  ResistancePercent
  ResistanceCappedPercent
  BlockPreventedDamage
  FinalDamage
  EffectiveHealthEquivalent
  Notes
```

Rows vs assets:

- DataTables are correct for numeric bulk: school names, base formula constants,
  stat curves, resistance curves, status thresholds, damage-number colors.
- PrimaryDataAssets are correct for composed content: weapon damage profile,
  spell profile, boss move profile, status payload profile, unique item power.
- GameplayTags are the boundary language: never branch on display strings for
  status or school behavior.

### 19.5 Formula policy

The formula must be stable enough for designers to tune and transparent enough
for players to trust.

Recommended order:

```text
1. Base packet magnitude
2. Source stat scaling
3. Weapon / spell / ability profile multiplier
4. Equipment and passive modifiers
5. Conditional source/target rules
6. Defensive state mutation: parry, iframe, block, guard break
7. Armor mitigation
8. School resistance or vulnerability
9. Final clamps and minimum-damage policy
10. Health/poise SetByCaller application
11. Status buildup and payload
```

Armor:

- keep the current `100 / (100 + Armor)` formula as the MVP physical-family
  curve because it has natural diminishing returns and is easy to explain;
- promote it into `PDA_DefenseFormulaProfile` or `CurveFloat` before content
  volume grows;
- show effective health against physical-family damage in UI and formula lab;
- decide whether Armor eventually mitigates all damage or only physical-family
  damage. Diablo IV's recent public defense overhaul is evidence that broad
  armor/resistance relationships tend to need multiple iterations and strong UI.

Resistance:

- keep clamping, but move min/max caps into data;
- support negative resistance as vulnerability, because it creates readable
  debuffs and enemy weaknesses;
- distinguish temporary resistance buffs from permanent item/attribute facts;
- show capped and uncapped values in debug/UI so players understand wasted stats.

TrueDamage:

- allow it only for execution, death/reset, and explicitly authored exceptional
  effects;
- never use TrueDamage as an easy way around formula bugs;
- require telemetry label and source tag;
- validate that normal weapons, spells, DOTs, and enemy basic attacks do not use
  TrueDamage unless approved.

Damage number policy:

```text
PDA_DamageNumberProfile
  SchoolColor
  CritStyle
  WeaknessStyle
  BlockedStyle
  GuardBreakStyle
  DOTAggregationPolicy
  AccessibilityVariant
```

Damage numbers should not become the combat UI. They are confirmation and
learning tools. The player should read enemy reaction, VFX, SFX, camera, and
health movement first.

Formula trace steps:

```text
FAscentiaFormulaStep
  StepTag
  DisplayName
  Operation
  SourceAsset
  SourceField
  Priority
  bBlocksApplication

FAscentiaFormulaTraceStep
  StepTag
  InputValue
  OutputValue
  ModifierValue
  ClampMin
  ClampMax
  bClamped
  bRejected
  RejectReason
  SourceAssetPath
```

Determinism policy:

- server is authoritative for all health, poise, status, and resource mutation;
- editor preview uses the same formula service with frozen snapshots;
- formula steps use stable ordering, not unordered tag iteration;
- floating-point output is rounded only at defined display/application
  boundaries;
- status/crit random rolls use traceable seeds in automation;
- UI never re-computes gameplay truth from display text.

Player trust policy:

- show "why" before showing every coefficient;
- expose final damage, primary mitigation reason, resistance/vulnerability, and
  status result in compact UI;
- keep full step-by-step traces in editor/debug tools;
- avoid formula changes that require players to relearn every build unless the
  dashboard marks the rebalance as an explicit design event.

### 19.6 Status lifecycle

Status should be a complete lifecycle, not a tag that happens to trigger damage.

Lifecycle:

```text
Incoming hit applies buildup
  -> target status resistance modifies buildup
  -> current buildup increases
  -> optional decay pauses/resumes
  -> threshold reached
  -> overflow retained or cleared by policy
  -> payload starts
  -> immunity/resistance window may begin
  -> cue/UI/audio state activates
  -> payload ticks, bursts, debuffs, or modifies attributes
  -> refresh/stack/extend policy applies on repeat triggers
  -> cleanse/cure/death/save rules resolve
```

Data:

```text
PDA_StatusPayload
  StatusTag
  DisplayName
  DamageSchool
  BuildupThresholdCurve
  BuildupDecayDelay
  BuildupDecayRate
  TargetResistanceAttribute
  TriggerPolicy: Burst, DOT, Debuff, Stagger, Control, Hybrid
  OverflowPolicy: Retain, ClampToZero, CarryPercent
  RefreshPolicy: Extend, Restart, Stack, IgnoreWhileActive
  StackLimit
  ImmunityAfterTriggerSeconds
  CleanseTags
  SavePolicy
  GameplayCueOnBuildup
  GameplayCueOnTrigger
  GameplayCueWhileActive
  UIIcon
  ColorblindPaletteKey
```

Runtime state:

```text
FAscentiaStatusState
  StatusTag
  CurrentBuildup
  TriggerThreshold
  DecayDelayRemaining
  ActiveStackCount
  ActiveDurationRemaining
  ImmunityRemaining
  LastSourceActor
  LastSourceProfile
  LastAppliedWorldTime
  bActive
  bImmune

FAscentiaStatusApplicationRequest
  SourceActor
  TargetActor
  StatusTag
  BuildupAmount
  DamageSchool
  SourceTags
  ApplicationProfile
  TraceId

FAscentiaStatusApplicationResult
  StatusTag
  AddedBuildup
  EffectiveBuildupAfterResistance
  NewBuildup
  Threshold
  bTriggered
  bPayloadStarted
  bImmuneRejected
  bOverflowRetained
  PayloadTag
  CueRequests[]
```

Status resistance:

```text
PDA_StatusResistanceProfile
  TargetArchetypeTag
  StatusTag
  BuildupMultiplier
  ThresholdMultiplier
  DecayMultiplier
  PayloadDamageMultiplier
  ImmunityAfterTriggerSeconds
  BossPhaseOverrides[]

PDA_StatusUIProfile
  StatusTag
  BuildupBarStyle
  ActiveIcon
  TriggerCallout
  TooltipTextKey
  ColorblindPaletteKey
  NumberAggregationPolicy
```

Initial Ascentia statuses:

- Poison: sustained health pressure; low poise impact; clear DOT number cadence.
- Rot: heavier long pressure, possibly slower cure and more world/biome identity.
- Frost: buildup into burst plus poise/slow/stagger opportunity.
- Bleed: future burst on threshold, likely physical-family and weapon dependent.
- Burn: future DOT plus panic/readability risk in VFX-heavy encounters.
- Arcane: future resource disruption, vulnerability, or spectral interaction.

Rules:

- payload behavior must come from `PDA_StatusPayload`, not string matching;
- status UI must show buildup separately from active debuff state;
- bosses can have different thresholds, decay, immunity, and payload reduction;
- DOTs should support aggregation display so poison does not spam unreadable
  numbers;
- every status requires color, icon, audio state, cue, accessibility variant, and
  tooltip copy before it is production-ready.
- save/load rules must distinguish transient combat buildup from long-lived
  active conditions. As a default, normal buildup should not persist through a
  reload, while authored long-term curses or world-state afflictions need their
  own save fragment and UI explanation.

### 19.7 Criticals, backstabs, ripostes, and executions

Critical systems should be split by intent:

```text
RandomCrit
  probability-based damage spike, mostly item/stat/build expression

Backstab
  positioning reward, deterministic if angle/range/state rules pass

Riposte
  defense reward after parry or guard break

StanceBreakExecution
  poise/stance reward after pressure

WeakPoint
  target anatomy or boss-mechanic reward
```

Do not treat these as one "crit" flag.

Data:

```text
PDA_CriticalProfile
  RandomCritEnabled
  ChanceCurve
  DamageMultiplierCurve
  BackstabAngleDegrees
  BackstabRange
  BackstabAllowedWeaponFamilies
  RiposteWindowSeconds
  ExecutionDamageProfile
  VictimAlignmentPolicy
  CameraProfile
  CueProfile
  PromptProfile
```

Execution decision data:

```text
FAscentiaCriticalCheckInput
  SourceActor
  TargetActor
  AttackProfile
  WeaponFamily
  SourceStats
  TargetState
  SourceTags
  TargetTags
  RelativeAngle
  Distance
  RandomSeed

FAscentiaCriticalCheckResult
  CriticalMode: None, RandomCrit, Backstab, Riposte, StanceBreakExecution, WeakPoint
  bAllowed
  Chance
  Roll
  Multiplier
  ExecutionProfile
  PromptProfile
  RejectReason
  TraceSteps[]

PDA_ExecutionProfile
  ExecutionTag
  AllowedWeaponFamilies
  RequiredTargetStateTags
  RequiredSourceStateTags
  AlignmentPolicy
  InvulnerabilityPolicy
  DamageProfile
  MontageSet
  CameraProfile
  CueProfile
  RewardPolicy
```

Current AGI/INT scaling is useful MVP proof:

```text
EffectiveCritChance = CriticalChancePercent + max(0, AGI - 10) * 0.5
EffectiveCritDamage = CriticalDamageMultiplier + max(0, INT - 10) * 0.02
```

Production changes:

- move these constants into crit/stat curves;
- let weapon families opt into different crit identities;
- keep random crit modest in Soulslike melee so animation commitment remains
  readable;
- use backstab/riposte/stance-break execution for large deterministic payoff;
- avoid true damage for all crits. True damage should be execution-specific, not
  the generic answer to "crit ignores defense."
- weak points should belong to target anatomy or boss mechanics, not generic
  damage math. A skeleton socket, hit region, phase tag, or exposed weak-point
  component should provide the fact; the P13 formula should only consume it.

### 19.8 Poise, stance, guard, and defensive identity

Ascentia has health and poise damage already. P13 should turn that into a
defensive grammar.

Concepts:

```text
Health
  death pressure

Poise
  interruption/stagger pressure

Guard
  Breath conversion and angle/state defense

GuardBreak
  failed resource defense, punish window

StanceBreak
  poise threshold state, execution opportunity

Armor
  physical-family mitigation

Resistance
  school-specific mitigation/vulnerability

Toughness / Effective Survivability
  UI/report view of how much raw damage a build can absorb by school
```

Data:

```text
PDA_PoiseProfile
  MaxPoiseCurve
  PoiseDamageTakenMultiplierBySchool
  PoiseRecoveryDelay
  PoiseRecoveryRate
  StanceBreakThreshold
  StanceBreakDuration
  BossStanceBreakPolicy

PDA_BlockProfile
  BlockAngle
  BreathCostCurve
  HealthDamageMultiplier
  PoiseDamageMultiplier
  GuardBreakDamageMultiplier
  GuardCounterWindow
```

Runtime defense state:

```text
FAscentiaDefenseStateSnapshot
  bHasIframes
  bParryWindowOpen
  bBlocking
  bGuardBroken
  bStanceBroken
  BlockFacingDegrees
  CurrentBreath
  CurrentPoise
  MaxPoise
  Armor
  ResistanceBySchool
  EquipLoadTier
  ActiveDefenseTags

FAscentiaGuardResult
  bBlocked
  bPerfectBlock
  bParried
  bGuardBroken
  BreathCost
  HealthDamagePrevented
  PoiseDamagePrevented
  GuardCounterWindowOpened
  RejectReason

PDA_StanceBreakProfile
  TargetArchetypeTag
  PoiseThreshold
  RecoveryDelay
  PunishWindowSeconds
  ExecutionProfile
  BossPhaseInteraction
  CueProfile

PDA_GuardProfile
  GuardTag
  BlockAngle
  BreathCostCurve
  ChipDamagePolicy
  PerfectBlockWindow
  GuardBreakPolicy
  CounterWindowPolicy
```

Build identities:

- Heavy guard: high armor, high poise, strong block, slower roll, Breath pressure.
- Light evasive: low armor, fast roll, longer iframe, less poise, better crit or
  status access.
- Spellblade: moderate defense, Eth scaling, crit/status burst, weaker guard.
- Rot/poison build: lower immediate burst, high uptime pressure and status
  threshold manipulation.
- Execution build: poise pressure, riposte/backstab windows, high deterministic
  payoff.

Designer proof should compare these identities side by side. If two builds use
different stats/items but survive and kill in the same way, the build system is
not deep yet.

### 19.9 Stats, scaling, soft caps, and threshold passives

Stats should answer three questions:

1. What can I do more often?
2. What can I survive?
3. What new build identity opens up?

Current stat roles:

```text
CON -> MaxHealth, MaxEquipLoad
END -> MaxBreath, BreathRegen
FOC -> MaxEth
STR -> weapon/build identity
AGI -> effective crit chance
INT -> effective crit damage
Conviction / Entropy / Nature -> spectrum identity, future spell/world/build axis
```

Production stat curve:

```text
PDA_StatCurveProfile
  PrimaryStat
  DisplayName
  DerivedAttributeRules[]
  ScalingSoftCaps[]
  TooltipRows[]
  ThresholdPassiveRows[]
  PreviewScenarioSet

FAscentiaDerivedAttributeRule
  SourceStat
  TargetAttribute
  Curve
  MinValue
  MaxValue
  RoundingPolicy
```

Build preview data:

```text
FAscentiaBuildSnapshot
  CharacterLevel
  PrimaryStats
  DerivedAttributes
  EquippedItems
  ActivePassives
  ActiveBuffs
  DamageProfiles
  DefenseProfiles
  EquipLoadTier
  SchoolResistances
  StatusResistances

FAscentiaDerivedAttributePreview
  Attribute
  CurrentValue
  PreviewValue
  Delta
  SourceStat
  Curve
  SoftCapBand
  TooltipRows[]

PDA_BuildArchetypeProfile
  ArchetypeTag
  DisplayName
  StatPriorityHints
  WeaponFamilyHints
  DefenseIdentity
  StatusIdentity
  ExampleItemTags
  PreviewScenarioSet
```

Soft caps:

- avoid endless linear gains for core survivability and damage;
- expose breakpoints in tooltips;
- let early points feel meaningful;
- prevent one stat from being mandatory for every build;
- verify with level-range scenarios, not one default character.

Threshold passives:

```text
PDA_ThresholdPassiveProfile
  PassiveTag
  PrimaryStat
  RequiredValue
  Tier
  DisplayName
  Icon
  GameplayEffect
  GrantedTags
  UnlockCalloutProfile
  RespecPolicy
```

The native component can keep evaluating tags. The authored profile should decide
what each tag does, how it is explained, and what presentation appears when it
unlocks.

The build planner should answer these questions before a point is committed:

```text
What changes immediately?
What threshold passive is closer?
Does roll tier change?
Does effective survivability change by school?
Does this stat affect my equipped weapon/spell scaling?
Does this stat unlock an item, spell, or passive requirement?
```

### 19.10 Equip load and roll tier

Equip load is build math and combat feel at the same time. It must be tuned with
motion, animation, Breath economy, and collision proof.

Current tier shape:

```text
Light  -> ratio < 0.30
Medium -> ratio <= 0.70
Heavy  -> ratio <= 1.00
Overloaded -> ratio > 1.00
```

Production data:

```text
PDA_EquipLoadProfile
  LightMaxRatio
  MediumMaxRatio
  HeavyMaxRatio
  BreathCostByTier
  ImpulseByTier
  IframeByTier
  RecoveryByTier
  AnimationSetByTier
  UIWarningProfile
```

Rules:

- roll tier must be visible in stat/equipment UI;
- changing gear should preview roll tier before confirm/equip;
- overloaded must explain exactly why dodge is blocked;
- roll-tier tuning must be tested with the combat motion solver on flat ground,
  stairs, ledges, slopes, and wall collisions;
- Designer tools should show animation length, root-motion distance, capsule
  path, intended direction, accepted cancel window, and actual movement result.

Motion proof output:

```text
FAscentiaEquipLoadMotionReport
  BuildSnapshot
  Tier
  DodgeMontage
  RootMotionDistance
  CapsuleDistance
  FloorProjectionResult
  LedgeResult
  StairResult
  WallSlideResult
  BreathCost
  IframeWindow
  RecoveryWindow
  DesignerVerdict
```

### 19.12 P13 evidence, formula lab, and reports

P13 needs evidence that can be reviewed by Designers and consumed by the
dashboard.

Formula lab scenarios:

```text
FAscentiaDamageFormulaScenario
  ScenarioId
  SourceBuildSnapshot
  TargetBuildSnapshot
  SourceActionProfile
  TargetDefenseState
  ExpectedBands
  RequiredTraceSteps
  Notes

FAscentiaDamageFormulaReport
  ReportId
  FormulaProfile
  ScenarioResults[]
  ArmorCurveFindings
  ResistanceCurveFindings
  TrueDamageFindings
  CritFindings
  StatusFindings
  FailedExpectations[]
  SuggestedTuningActions[]

FAscentiaBuildMathReport
  ReportId
  LevelRange
  ArchetypeResults[]
  SoftCapFindings
  ThresholdPassiveFindings
  EffectiveSurvivabilityFindings
  EquipLoadFindings
  DeadStatWarnings[]

FAscentiaStatusPayloadReport
  ReportId
  StatusPayloads[]
  ThresholdScenarios[]
  DecayScenarios[]
  RefreshStackScenarios[]
  BossResistanceScenarios[]
  UIAndCueCoverage
  AccessibilityCoverage
```

Required proof scenarios:

```text
Tools/remote_playtest.py damage_formula_matrix
Tools/remote_playtest.py damage_school_resistance
Tools/remote_playtest.py status_decay_refresh
Tools/remote_playtest.py status_payload
Tools/remote_playtest.py critical_stats
Tools/remote_playtest.py poise_guard_break
Tools/remote_playtest.py equip_load_roll_tiers
Tools/remote_playtest.py threshold_passives
Tools/remote_playtest.py build_summary_preview
```

Editor evidence outputs:

```text
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P13/DamageFormula/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P13/DamageFormula/{RunId}/trace_steps.csv
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P13/BuildMath/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P13/StatusPayloads/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P13/EquipLoadMotion/{RunId}/report.json
```

Acceptance shape:

- no formula profile can pass validation without at least one scenario;
- no status payload can pass validation without UI, cue, decay, and resistance
  coverage;
- no threshold passive can pass validation without an effect, icon, tooltip,
  unlock callout, and respec behavior;
- no build archetype can pass validation if all recommended stats produce the
  same effective survivability/damage identity;
- no damage school can pass validation if it lacks icon/color/cue/resistance
  policy and display text;
- every formula change should regenerate old scenario traces so balance drift is
  explicit.

### 19.13 Interconnects

P13 touches nearly every later system:

- P10/P11 combat: damage and poise determine stagger, guard break, riposte,
  cancel reward, cue intensity, and boss pressure.
- P14 weapons: weapon families need damage profiles, scaling grades, crit rules,
  status access, poise profiles, and equip weight.
- P15 magic: spells need school, Eth cost, scaling, resist interactions, status
  payloads, and resource/status UI.
- P16 itemization: affixes must grant attributes, modifiers, status changes,
  resistance changes, skill modifications, or unique powers through the same tag
  vocabulary.
- P17 world: biomes and zones can bias enemy resistances, status hazards, weather
  readability, and loot/status identity.
- P18 AI: enemies need defensive profiles, status vulnerability, poise/stance
  behavior, and readable elite affixes.
- P19 save/death: stats, passives, respec state, active long-term effects, and
  character identity must serialize safely.
- P20 presentation: damage schools, status, crit, block, guard break, and poise
  need cue coverage, audio, VFX, damage-number styles, and accessibility settings.
- P21 shell/settings: remapping, large text, damage-number visibility, colorblind
  status palettes, and stat tooltip verbosity belong in settings.
- P22 multiplayer: source/target attribute capture, status payload authority,
  damage prediction, and inventory/affix validation must be network-safe.

### 19.14 Validation and proof gates

P13 is not complete until:

1. Damage formulas are data-backed and can be inspected in
   `EUW_AscentiaDamageFormulaLab`.
2. Every damage school has a row/profile, display color, resistance policy, cue
   family, and damage-number style.
3. Armor/resistance/true-damage paths are separately verified, including negative
   resistance and cap behavior.
4. Status payloads are data-driven, not string-matched, and support threshold,
   overflow, decay, refresh/stack policy, immunity, cleanse, UI, cues, and
   accessibility palette.
5. Poison, Rot, Frost, and at least one future burst status such as Bleed have
   authored payload profiles and playtest scenarios.
6. Crit, backstab, riposte, and stance-break execution are separate policies with
   separate telemetry.
7. Stat curves and soft caps are visible in a build planner and validated across
   level ranges.
8. Threshold passives unlock authored effects/icons/callouts and survive
   save/load/respec.
9. Equip load previews roll tier in UI and motion proof confirms flat/stair/ledge
   behavior for each tier.
10. Damage/status/crit/passive/roll settings candidates are registered for P21 UI.
11. Remote proof covers:
    - `damage_model`
    - `status_payload`
    - `critical_stats`
    - `stat_investment`
    - `threshold_passive`
    - `roll_type`
    - `damage_formula_matrix`
    - `damage_school_resistance`
    - `status_decay_refresh`
    - `poise_guard_break`
    - `equip_load_roll_tiers`
    - `effective_survivability`
    - `build_summary_preview`
12. Dashboard evidence links to the latest formula lab report and status payload
    report.

### 19.15 P13 starter implementation package

The first P13 package should make Ascentia's damage/build math reproducible
before it becomes broad. Damage should remain server-authoritative, GAS-backed,
and data-authored, while the exact formula path is available to Designers in
the Editor.

```text

```

Subsystem and class boundaries:

```text
UAscentiaDamageExecutionCalculation : UGameplayEffectExecutionCalculation
  GAS adapter
  captures source/target attributes and tags
  calls `UAscentiaDamageFormulaService`
  writes SetByCaller magnitudes and GameplayCue requests

UAscentiaDamageFormulaService : UObject or UGameInstanceSubsystem
  reusable deterministic formula resolver
  ResolveDamageFormula(Input) -> Output
  BuildFormulaTrace(Input, Output) -> Trace
  WriteFormulaReport(Scenarios, OutputPath)

UAscentiaStatusSubsystem : UWorldSubsystem
  owns status buildup, decay, payload, immunity, and active state
  ApplyStatusBuildup(Request) -> Result
  TickStatusState(Target, DeltaSeconds)
  WriteStatusPayloadReport(OutputPath)

UAscentiaBuildMathSubsystem : UGameInstanceSubsystem
  resolves stat curves, soft caps, threshold passives, and preview snapshots
  BuildSnapshot(Character, Equipment)
  PreviewStatInvestment(Snapshot, Stat, Delta)
  WriteBuildMathReport(OutputPath)

UAscentiaCombatComponent
  remains the authoritative gameplay entry point for actual damage application
  delegates formula work instead of owning all constants locally
```
