
Implementation tickets:

```text
P13-001 Damage formula service extraction
  Move armor, resistance, true-damage, crit, poise, and status calculation from
  local component constants into `UAscentiaDamageFormulaService` with trace
  output and unit tests.

P13-002 GAS execution adapter
  Add `UAscentiaDamageExecutionCalculation` as the GAS-facing adapter that
  captures source/target attributes and delegates to the formula service.

P13-003 Damage school and formula profiles
  Add `PDA_DamageSchoolProfile_*`, `PDA_DamageFormulaProfile_MVP`, and
  `PDA_DefenseFormulaProfile_MVP`; validate display text, resistance policy,
  cue family, and true-damage permissions.

P13-004 Status payload data path
  Replace tag-string-matched payload behavior with `PDA_StatusPayload_*`,
  `FAscentiaStatusState`, and `FAscentiaStatusApplicationResult`.

P13-005 Critical, backstab, riposte, and execution split
  Implement `FAscentiaCriticalCheckResult`, `PDA_CriticalProfile_MVP`, and
  separate telemetry for random crit, backstab, riposte, and stance execution.

P13-006 Defense identity profiles
  Add `FAscentiaDefenseStateSnapshot`, `FAscentiaGuardResult`,
  `PDA_GuardProfile_MVP`, and `PDA_StanceBreakProfile_MVP`.

P13-007 Stat curve and threshold passive assets
  Move derived stat constants into `PDA_StatCurveProfile_MVP` and author the
  first threshold passive profiles with icon, effect, tooltip, and respec proof.

P13-008 Build planner and effective survivability preview
  Implement `FAscentiaBuildSnapshot`, `FAscentiaDerivedAttributePreview`,
  `EUW_AscentiaBuildPlanner`, and `EUW_AscentiaEffectiveSurvivabilityPreview`.

P13-009 Equip load profile and motion report bridge
  Add `PDA_EquipLoadProfile_MVP` and make roll tier preview emit
  `FAscentiaEquipLoadMotionReport` references from the combat motion solver.

P13-010 P13 packaged proof suite
  Run formula matrix, status lifecycle, crit/execution, build planner, threshold
  passive, and equip-load proof in PIE and packaged Development, then publish
  dashboard-linked reports.
```

Validation gates:

- any `PDA_DamageProfile` without a formula scenario fails validation;
- any damage school without icon/color/resistance/cue policy fails validation;
- any true-damage source without an approved source tag fails validation;
- any status payload without buildup, decay, refresh, immunity, UI, cue, and
  accessibility policy fails validation;
- any threshold passive without icon, GameplayEffect or granted tags, tooltip,
  unlock callout, and respec behavior fails validation;
- any formula profile change must regenerate scenario traces and list drift;
- any equip-load profile change must reference a motion report fixture.

Recommended sequencing:

1. Extract formula math into `UAscentiaDamageFormulaService` without changing
   gameplay behavior.
2. Add trace output and scenario rows for the current MVP formulas.
3. Add DataAssets that reproduce current values, then point runtime at them.
4. Replace status string matching with payload assets.
5. Add formula lab and scenario matrix before adding more schools/statuses.
6. Only then deepen soft caps, build archetypes, executions, and survivability
   previews.

## 20. Moveset and Weapon Breadth

### 20.1 Player-facing goal

P14 is where weapons stop being stat sticks and become verbs.

The player-facing promise:

- a dagger, sword, greatsword, shield, bow, staff, throwable, and future exotic
  weapon all ask different decisions from the player;
- a weapon's identity is visible in stance, locomotion overlays, attack timing,
  range, damage shape, poise pressure, recovery, cancel windows, sound, trail,
  hitbox, and UI;
- two-handing, dual wield, power stance, shield pairing, ammo, quick items, and
  weapon arts are authored systems, not special cases;
- Designers can add a new weapon family or weapon type by authoring data,
  montages, notifies, projectiles, cues, and validation assets without spelunking
  through fallback C++ branches;
- every weapon action has proof: input accepted, cost consumed, montage played,
  notifies fired, hit window matched, damage packet emitted, cue/audio/VFX/UI
  appeared, and cancel policy behaved.

The production target is not "lots of animations." It is a grammar where every
weapon exposes a compact, readable kit and every kit plugs into the same combat,
damage, inventory, animation, VFX, audio, UI, settings, and validation systems.

### 20.2 Current Ascentia state

P14 already has more native proof than the shallow phase plan implies:

- light attack combo chains advance through a native three-step sequence,
  preserve step-1 base damage, scale step-2/step-3 damage and poise, expire via a
  combo window, and emit `OnLightComboStepStarted`;
- movement attacks exist for running, rolling, and backstep variants, reuse the
  light-attack GAS/contact-sweep path, keep separate telemetry, and do not
  advance standing combo progression;
- guard counters open after successful block, consume their window, apply boosted
  damage/poise, and emit telemetry;
- jump attacks require the owner to be airborne, apply boosted damage/poise, and
  stay isolated from normal combo progression;
- `UAscentiaAbility_WeaponSkill` exists as a Breath/cooldown-gated weapon-skill
  ability with a default weapon-art tag, source item tag, scaled damage/poise,
  and `OnWeaponSkillStarted`;
- `UAscentiaCombatComponent` resolves equipped right-hand item tags into
  replicated weapon family/type/moveset snapshots;
- native fallback mappings currently cover Unarmed/Fists, Sword/OneHand,
  Dagger/Quick, Greatsword/Heavy, Shield/Buckler, and Bow/Short;
- off-hand equipment resolves into ShieldPair, DualWield, or PowerStance with
  paired moveset tags, two-hand suppression, and conservative multipliers;
- Ash Shortbow plus Bone Arrow is the first ranged/ammo proof: bow family
  required, ammo consumed from the Ammo slot, ranged telemetry emitted, missing
  bow/ammo rejected safely;
- Ruin Throwing Knife is the first throwable proof: quick item slot required,
  stack consumed, throwable telemetry emitted, empty/non-knife rejected safely;
- GASP character Blueprints already expose some weapon animation state, while the
  native combat component exposes BlueprintImplementableEvents for presentation.

The production gaps:

- weapon type resolution is hard-coded fallback logic instead of authored
  `PDA_WeaponType` or `DT_WeaponTypes`;
- combo/movement/counter/jump/weapon-skill actions need distinct montages,
  sections, slots, root-motion policy, notify windows, and cue/audio/VFX timing;
- weapon skills need per-weapon assignment, UI, cooldown/resource presentation,
  montage selection, and balance;
- ranged needs draw/aim/release flow, reticle/lock-on policy, projectile/trace
  choice, ammo HUD, projectile trails, and hit readability;
- throwables need projectile actor/arc policy, target assist, quick-item HUD,
  trajectory preview if desired, and stack/balance rules;
- dual wield and power stance need authored pairing rows, restrictions,
  off-hand visuals, paired montage sets, and conflict messaging;
- sockets, trail origins, hand offsets, sheathe locations, and collision profiles
  must be Designer-adjustable in the Editor;
- validation must prove each weapon action has animation, notifies, hit windows,
  cue coverage, input policy, cost, and damage profile.

### 20.3 Production weapon identity model

Weapon identity should be resolved in layers:

```text
Equipped item instance
  -> weapon type row / asset
  -> weapon family
  -> moveset tags
  -> action map
  -> montage set
  -> damage profile
  -> motion profile
  -> cue/audio/VFX profiles
  -> UI prompt and settings keys
```

The runtime snapshot should remain compact and replicated:

```text
FAscentiaWeaponSnapshot
  ItemTag
  ItemInstanceId
  WeaponFamily
  WeaponTypeTag
  MovesetTags
  DamageProfile
  ActionMap
  MontageSet
  WeaponSkill
  Weight
  SocketProfile
```

Off-hand snapshot:

```text
FAscentiaOffHandSnapshot
  ItemTag
  WeaponFamily
  WeaponTypeTag
  OffHandMode: None, ShieldPair, DualWield, PowerStance
  OffHandMovesetTags
  PairedMovesetTags
  SuppressedByTwoHanding
  DamageMultiplier
  PoiseMultiplier
  GuardProfile
```

Resolver contract:

```text
UAscentiaWeaponResolverSubsystem
  ResolveWeaponSnapshot(Inventory, Slot, WorldContext)
  ResolveOffHandSnapshot(Inventory, MainHandSnapshot, OffHandSlot)
  ResolveActionDef(WeaponSnapshot, ActionTag, ContextTags)
  ResolveWeaponSkill(WeaponSnapshot, ItemInstance)
  ResolveAmmoSnapshot(Inventory, RangedProfile)
  ResolveThrowableSnapshot(Inventory, QuickSlot)

FAscentiaWeaponResolutionReport
  SourceItem
  SourceInstance
  WeaponTypeSource
  ActionMapSource
  DamageProfileSource
  MontageSetSource
  SocketProfileSource
  FallbacksUsed[]
  MissingRequirements[]
  ValidationWarnings[]
```

Rules:

- runtime code may keep a native fallback for missing content, but the fallback
  must be visible in `EUW_AscentiaResolvedContentBrowser`;
- every snapshot records whether it came from authored item data, generated pack
  data, fallback defaults, or temporary Blueprint overrides;
- weapon snapshots should update when equipment changes, weapon skill changes,
  ammo changes, quick item changes, two-hand state changes, or save data restores;
- UI reads the snapshot, not item rows directly;
- combat actions read the snapshot, then resolve action data through tags and
  Primary Asset IDs;
- Blueprint presentation reads events and view models, not private C++ defaults.

### 20.4 Weapon type and action data

Core data:

```text
PDA_WeaponType

  DisplayName
  WeaponFamily
  RequiredEquipmentSlot
  MovesetTags
  DefaultActionMap
  DefaultDamageProfile
  DefaultMotionProfile
  DefaultMontageSet
  DefaultCueProfile
  DefaultSocketProfile

  RangedPolicy

```

```text
PDA_WeaponActionMap

  LightChain

  ChargedAction
  RunningAttack
  RollingAttack
  BackstepAttack
  JumpAttack
  GuardCounter
  Backstab

  RangedDraw
  RangedRelease
  ThrowableThrow
  InterruptionRules
  InputBufferPolicy
```

Action definition:

```text
FAscentiaWeaponActionDef
  ActionTag
  AbilityClass
  Montage
  MontageSection
  SlotName
  RootMotionPolicy
  MotionWarpingPolicy
  DamageProfile
  CostProfile
  TargetingProfile
  ProjectileProfile
  CancelWindows
  DamageWindows
  IframeWindows
  AudioNotifyRequirements
  VFXNotifyRequirements
  CueProfile
  CameraProfile
  RumbleProfile
  UIHintProfile
```

Action state:

```text
FAscentiaWeaponActionRequest
  Instigator
  ActionTag
  InputTag
  WeaponSnapshot
  OffHandSnapshot
  AmmoSnapshot
  TargetingContext
  IntentVector
  bFromBufferedInput
  ClientRequestTime

FAscentiaWeaponActionResult
  ActionTag
  bAccepted
  RejectReason
  AbilitySpecHandle
  Montage
  MontageSection
  ConsumedResources[]
  ConsumedItems[]
  OpenedWindows[]

FAscentiaActiveWeaponActionState
  ActionTag
  WeaponTypeTag
  Montage
  Section
  StartTime
  CurrentPhase: Startup, Active, Recovery, Cancel, Interrupted, Complete
  OpenDamageWindows[]
  OpenCancelWindows[]
  OpenIFrameWindows[]
  PendingProjectileRelease
  bRootMotionOwned
  bCanBufferNextAction
```

This lets a sword, dagger, and greatsword share the same native "light attack"
verb while using different action definitions. It also lets a bow use the same
ability/action system without pretending a shot is a melee sweep.

### 20.5 Montage, slot, notify, and root-motion contract

Unreal's correct authoring primitives for P14:

- Animation Montages for one-shot or sectioned action playback;
- Montage Sections for combo branching, charge/release, looping draw, and
  conditional follow-ups;
- Animation Slots for full-body committed actions and upper-body actions such as
  aiming or quick throws;
- Animation Notifies and Notify States for damage windows, trail windows, swing
  audio, projectile release, cancel windows, iframe windows, footstep emphasis,
  lock-on/warp markers, and cue markers;
- Root Motion for authored grounded displacement;
- Motion Warping for target-aligned actions such as ripostes, backstabs,
  executions, grabs, mantles, and selected boss moves.

Action timing should live on the animation asset where Designers can see it, but
native code must validate it.

Required notify vocabulary:

```text
ANS_Ascentia_DamageWindow
ANS_Ascentia_CancelWindow
ANS_Ascentia_IFrameWindow
ANS_Ascentia_TrailWindow
ANS_Ascentia_AimWindow
ANS_Ascentia_ChargeWindow
AN_Ascentia_SwingAudio
AN_Ascentia_ProjectileRelease
AN_Ascentia_FootstepEmphasis
AN_Ascentia_GameplayCueMarker
AN_Ascentia_MotionWarpTarget
```

Notify manifest:

```text
FAscentiaAnimNotifyWindowManifest
  Montage
  SlotName
  SectionName
  DamageWindows[]
  CancelWindows[]
  IFrameWindows[]
  TrailWindows[]
  AimWindows[]
  ChargeWindows[]
  ProjectileReleaseFrames[]
  SwingAudioFrames[]
  GameplayCueMarkers[]
  MotionWarpTargets[]
  FootstepMarkers[]

FAscentiaNotifyWindow
  WindowTag
  StartTime
  EndTime
  SourceNotifyName
  RequiredByActionTag
  GameplayMeaning
  bDesignerApproved

FAscentiaMontageActionContract
  ActionTag
  Montage
  ExpectedSlot
  RequiredSections[]
  RequiredNotifyWindows[]
  OptionalNotifyWindows[]
  RootMotionPolicy
  MotionWarpingPolicy
  DedicatedServerNotifyPolicy
```

Validation examples:

- every melee action has at least one damage window;
- every weapon with a trail profile has a trail window and valid sockets;
- every ranged action has one projectile release notify or explicit trace timing;
- every cancelable action has cancel windows named by policy;
- every root-motion montage declares whether ledge/stair/fall behavior uses
  combat motion solver rules;
- every Motion Warping window has a named warp target provider;
- every montage section named in the action map exists in the montage.
- every notify required by gameplay authority is configured to fire where
  authority needs it. Cosmetic-only notifies may be presentation-only, but damage
  windows, projectile releases, and resource-relevant windows need an explicit
  server/runtime policy.
- every montage used by a weapon action has a manifest generated by
  `EUW_AscentiaNotifyWindowDoctor` and a compact report attached to the action
  definition.

Root-motion stance:

```text
FAscentiaWeaponRootMotionPolicy
  ActionTag
  bUsesRootMotion
  bAllowsFloorProjection
  bAllowsLedgeCommit
  bAllowsStairDescent
  bAllowsWallSlide
  FallConversionPolicy
  InputIntentOverridePolicy
  MotionSolverProfile
```

For ordinary melee, root motion expresses animation commitment. For skills whose
distance is a gameplay variable, use combat motion requests or root motion
sources instead. For target-aligned executions, use Motion Warping windows with
named targets and validation.

### 20.6 Combo and chain grammar

Combos should be authored per weapon family/type rather than as global step
multipliers.

```text
PDA_ComboChainProfile
  ChainTag
  WeaponTypeTags
  Steps[]
  ResetWindowSeconds
  InputBufferWindowSeconds
  BranchRules
  RecoveryRules
  UIChainPolicy

FAscentiaComboStep
  StepIndex
  ActionTag
  MontageSection
  DamageMultiplier
  PoiseMultiplier
  MotionProfile
  DamageWindowName
  CancelWindowName
  NextInputWindows
```

Combo state:

```text
FAscentiaComboRuntimeState
  ChainTag
  CurrentStepIndex
  LastAcceptedInputTag
  LastActionTag
  ResetDeadline
  BufferedNextInput
  bCanBranch
  bWasInterrupted

FAscentiaComboBranchRule
  FromStep
  InputTag
  RequiredStateTags
  ForbiddenStateTags
  NextActionTag
  BufferWindowName
  Priority
```

Rules:

- combo progression must be driven by accepted inputs, not by montage completion
  alone;
- standing combo, movement attack, rolling attack, guard counter, and jump attack
  must remain separate action states unless a chain explicitly branches between
  them;
- Designers should be able to preview a chain as a timeline: input, startup,
  active, recovery, cancel, cue, and next accepted input;
- action buffering must preserve player intent without making the character feel
  autonomous.
- chain rules should support failed-input telemetry. "Input ignored because
  recovery window was not cancelable" is useful; silence is not.

### 20.7 Two-handing, dual wield, power stance, and shields

Pairing is content, not a pile of if statements.

```text
PDA_PairedWeaponProfile
  MainHandFamilies
  OffHandFamilies
  RequiredTypeTags
  ForbiddenTypeTags
  Mode: ShieldPair, DualWield, PowerStance
  PairedMovesetTags
  MontageSet
  DamageMultiplier
  PoiseMultiplier
  GuardProfile
  EquipLoadAdjustment
  UIIndicator
  ConflictMessage
```

Pairing result:

```text
FAscentiaWeaponPairingQuery
  MainHandSnapshot
  OffHandSnapshot
  bTwoHandRequested
  CharacterStats
  RequiredTags
  ForbiddenTags

FAscentiaWeaponPairingResult
  Mode
  PairedProfile
  bAccepted
  bSuppressedByTwoHand
  bOffHandVisualVisible
  DamageMultiplier
  PoiseMultiplier
  GuardProfile
  RejectionReasonTextKey
  UIIndicatorTag
```

Rules:

- two-handing should suppress off-hand behavior through a visible state and UI
  prompt, not silent side effects;
- shield pairing should preserve guard/block/counter identity;
- dual wield should emphasize speed, pressure, or alternating-hit identity;
- power stance should require meaningful weapon similarity or profile match and
  reward commitment with distinct paired actions;
- every pairing rule needs a Designer-facing reason string for rejection or
  suppression.
- weapon pair validation should run both ways. A sword plus dagger and dagger
  plus sword may intentionally produce different output, but if not, the rule
  should be symmetric and validated as such.

### 20.8 Weapon skills and per-weapon abilities

Weapon skills are not merely "special attack." They are a per-weapon identity
slot.

```text
PDA_WeaponSkill
  SkillTag
  DisplayName
  RequiredWeaponTypes
  AbilityClass
  CostProfile
  CooldownProfile
  DamageProfile
  MontageSet
  TargetingProfile
  ProjectileProfile
  CueProfile
  CameraProfile
  UIIcon
  TooltipText
```

Skill assignment:

```text
FAscentiaWeaponSkillSlot
  SourceItemInstance
  DefaultSkill
  OverrideSkill
  ImprintedSkill
  bLocked
  UnlockCondition

FAscentiaWeaponSkillView
  SkillTag
  DisplayName
  Cost
  Cooldown
  InputGlyph
  RequiredWeaponText
  bUsableNow
  RejectReasonText
```

Policy:

- item definition chooses the default weapon skill;
- equipment, upgrades, or special items may override or augment it later;
- Skills tab displays equipped weapon skill, cost, cooldown, input, and preview;
- native ability owns authority, cost, cooldown, and damage application;
- Blueprint owns montage/cue/camera/audio presentation;
- no weapon skill is production-ready without a proof scenario and cue coverage.
- weapon skill replacement is an itemization decision. If Ascentia later supports
  imprinting, extraction, or skill gems, the mutation must use the P16 item
  transaction path and write provenance.

### 20.9 Ranged, ammo, target, and projectile policy

Ranged actions need a separate delivery contract.

```text
PDA_RangedWeaponProfile
  WeaponTypeTag
  DrawAction
  AimAction
  ReleaseAction
  RequiredAmmoFamilies
  DefaultAmmoProfile
  ReticleProfile
  LockOnPolicy
  CameraProfile
  ProjectileOrTracePolicy

PDA_AmmoProfile
  AmmoItemTag
  AmmoFamilyTag
  DamageProfile
  ProjectileProfile
  StackConsumption
  TrailCue
  ImpactCue
```

Projectile/trace:

```text
PDA_ProjectileProfile
  DeliveryType: Projectile, HitscanTrace, ArcTrace, Area
  ProjectileClass
  Speed
  GravityScale
  Range
  Radius
  CollisionChannel
  TargetingPreset
  HomingPolicy
  ClientPredictionPolicy
  ImpactDamageProfile
  ImpactCueProfile
```

Ammo and delivery transaction:

```text
FAscentiaAmmoSnapshot
  AmmoItemTag
  AmmoInstanceId
  AmmoFamilyTag
  StackCount
  DamageProfile
  ProjectileProfile
  bValidForWeapon

FAscentiaProjectileDeliveryRequest
  SourceActor
  WeaponSnapshot
  AmmoSnapshot
  ActionTag
  AimOrigin
  AimDirection
  TargetingResult
  ProjectileProfile
  ClientRequestTime

FAscentiaProjectileDeliveryResult
  bAccepted
  RejectReason
  ConsumedAmmo
  SpawnedProjectile
  TraceHitResults[]
  AppliedDamageEvents[]
  CueRequests[]
  RemainingAmmo
```

Rules:

- ammo consumption is server-authoritative and transactional;
- missing bow, missing ammo, invalid ammo, and blocked fire states must be
  rejected without consuming inventory;
- reticle and lock-on behavior belong in data/settings;
- projectile visuals must be readable at combat camera distance;
- the first production bow should support draw, hold, release, cancel, and
  no-ammo feedback before more bow content is authored.

The Gameplay Targeting System should be considered for the next step because it
already frames targeting as reusable data assets composed of selection, filter,
and sort tasks. Even if Ascentia begins with simple traces/projectile actors, the
data shape should not paint us into a corner.

Targeting evidence:

```text
FAscentiaTargetingProof
  ActionTag
  TargetingProfile
  SourceLocation
  AimVector
  CandidateTargets[]
  FilteredTargets[]
  ChosenTarget
  RejectionReasons[]
  DebugScreenshotPath
```

### 20.10 Throwables and quick-item attacks

Throwables live between inventory and combat.

```text
PDA_ThrowableProfile
  ItemTag
  SkillTag
  RequiredSlot
  ConsumeCount
  ThrowAction
  ProjectileProfile
  DamageProfile
  AimAssistPolicy
  TrajectoryPreviewPolicy
  QuickItemHUDPolicy
  OnEmptyPrompt
```

Throwable transaction:

```text
FAscentiaThrowableUseRequest
  QuickSlot
  ItemInstance
  ThrowableProfile
  AimContext
  bFromBufferedInput

FAscentiaThrowableUseResult
  bAccepted
  RejectReason
  ConsumedCount
  RemainingCount
  ProjectileDeliveryResult
  CooldownApplied
  HUDUpdate
```

Rules:

- quick-item stack consumption must be transactional;
- throw animation and projectile release notify must agree;
- arc preview is optional, but if enabled it must use the same projectile
  parameters as the real throw;
- lock-on and free-aim throws should be separate policies;
- quick-item HUD should show count, cooldown/lockout if any, and no-item state.

### 20.11 Weapon mounts, sockets, trails, and visuals

P14 must expose physical weapon fit to Designers. This is not a minor polish
task; wrong sockets break readability, trails, hitboxes, and character identity.

```text
PDA_WeaponSocketProfile
  HandSocket
  OffHandSocket
  SheatheSocket
  TrailStartSocket
  TrailEndSocket
  GripOffset
  GripRotationOffset
  SheatheOffset
  CollisionProfile
  PreviewCharacter
  PreviewMontages
```

Fit proof:

```text
FAscentiaWeaponMountReport
  WeaponItem
  WeaponMesh
  SocketProfile
  PreviewCharacter
  PreviewBodyPreset
  PreviewMontages[]
  HandFitFrames[]
  SheatheFitFrames[]
  TrailSocketFrames[]
  CollisionFindings[]
  ClippingFindings[]
  DesignerVerdict

FAscentiaWeaponHitboxReplay
  ActionTag
  Montage
  DamageWindow
  TraceSockets
  TraceSamples[]
  HitTargets[]
  MissedExpectedTargets[]
  DebugOverlayPath
```

Tools:

- `EUW_AscentiaWeaponMountLab` previews hand fit, sheathe fit, trail sockets, and
  montage frames;
- `EUW_AscentiaHitboxReplay` overlays actual trace/sweep path against weapon
  sockets and target capsules;
- `EUW_AscentiaNotifyWindowDoctor` shows trail/audio/damage/cancel windows on the
  same timeline;
- `EUW_AscentiaMotionInspector` compares root-motion path, capsule path, and
  intended direction.

Validation:

- every visible weapon has hand and sheathe socket profile;
- trail profile has valid start/end sockets;
- damage windows use sockets that exist on the equipped mesh;
- two-hand/off-hand sockets do not collide with body mesh in preview poses;
- generated Asset Factory meshes are audited before entering weapon rows.
- weapon mount reports should be stored as evidence before a generated or
  marketplace weapon leaves staging.

### 20.13 P14 evidence, matrices, and reports

P14 should be proven through matrices, not by trying one weapon in PIE.

Reports:

```text
FAscentiaNotifyAuditReport

  ExtraUnknownNotifies[]

  DedicatedServerPolicyWarnings[]

FAscentiaProjectileDeliveryReport
  ReportId

  ThrowableProfiles[]
  AmmoProfiles[]

  InventoryTransactionResults[]

FAscentiaWeaponMountReportSet
  ReportId
  WeaponMountReports[]

  TrailFindings[]

```

Evidence folders:

```text
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P14/WeaponActionMaps/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P14/NotifyAudit/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P14/ProjectileDelivery/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P14/WeaponMounts/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P14/MovesetMatrix/{RunId}/report.json
```

Required proof scenarios:

```text
Tools/remote_playtest.py weapon_action_map
Tools/remote_playtest.py weapon_notify_audit
Tools/remote_playtest.py weapon_mount_profile
Tools/remote_playtest.py projectile_delivery
Tools/remote_playtest.py ranged_ammo
Tools/remote_playtest.py throwable_item
Tools/remote_playtest.py paired_weapon_rules
Tools/remote_playtest.py combo_chain
Tools/remote_playtest.py movement_attack
Tools/remote_playtest.py weapon_skill
```

Acceptance shape:

- every shippable weapon type has an action map, montage set, socket profile,
  damage profile, cue profile, and report row;
- every shippable montage referenced by an action has a notify manifest;
- every ranged/throwable profile has a transaction proof and a targeting proof;
- every generated weapon mesh has a mount/fit report;
- every fallback action is visible in the matrix and blocks phase completion
  unless the fallback is explicitly phase-approved;
- every Designer verdict is preserved next to machine output so feel decisions
  do not vanish into logs.

### 20.14 Interconnects

P14 touches:

- P10/P11 combat motion: action requests, root motion, cancel windows, dodge
  cancel, parry/riposte/guard counter, and hitbox proof;
- P13 damage/build math: damage profiles, scaling, poise, crit/backstab, status,
  equip load, and stat requirements;
- P15 magic: staff/focus weapons, target profiles, projectile profiles, casts,
  spell slots, and Eth cost UI;
- P16 itemization: weapon item instances, affixes, unique powers, upgrade tracks,
  socket profiles, and generated names/tooltips;
- P17 world: placed weapon rewards, biome/region ammo, traversal tools, and
  ranged encounter spaces;
- P18 AI: enemy weapons, ranged/caster moves, shield behavior, elite affixes, and
  boss weapon grammar;
- P19 save/death: equipped weapons, ammo stacks, quick items, weapon skills,
  unlocked movesets, and respec/equipment constraints;
- P20 presentation: trails, impact cues, swing audio, reticles, camera, rumble,
  damage numbers, and accessibility variants;
- P21 settings: input mode, reticle visibility, ammo HUD, quick-item HUD,
  trajectory preview, aim assist, feedback intensity, and input buffering
  readability;
- P22 multiplayer: ammo consumption, projectile authority, target prediction,
  replicated montages/root motion, and anti-cheat-sensitive hit validation.

### 20.15 P14 proof gates

P14 is not complete until:

1. Weapon type resolution is data-backed and no shippable weapon relies only on
   hard-coded fallback item-tag branches.
2. Every weapon family has a default action map, montage set, damage profile,
   cue profile, socket profile, and validation report.
3. Combo chains are authored per weapon type/family and visible in an editor
   timeline.
4. Movement attacks, jump attacks, guard counters, and weapon skills have
   distinct montages, not only multipliers on the light-attack path.
5. Damage, cancel, iframe, trail, swing-audio, projectile-release, and cue notify
   requirements are machine-validated.
6. Two-hand, shield-pair, dual-wield, and power-stance rules are data-authored
   with Designer-readable rejection/suppression messages.
7. At least one weapon skill is item-assigned, appears in UI, consumes resources,
   respects cooldown, plays presentation, and emits damage through the P13
   damage pipeline.
8. Bow/ranged proof includes draw, aim, release, cancel, ammo consumption,
   no-ammo rejection, reticle, projectile/trace policy, trail, impact cue, and
   ammo HUD.
9. Throwable proof includes stack consumption, projectile/arc policy, release
   notify, quick-item HUD, empty rejection, and impact cue.
10. Weapon mount proof shows hand socket, off-hand socket, sheathe socket, trail
    sockets, and hitbox overlay for at least sword, dagger, greatsword, bow, and
    throwing knife.
11. Remote proof covers:
    - `combo_chain`
    - `movement_attack`
    - `guard_counter`
    - `jump_attack`
    - `weapon_skill`
    - `weapon_moveset`
    - `dual_wield`
    - `ranged_ammo`
    - `throwable_item`
    - `weapon_notify_audit`
    - `paired_weapon_rules`
    - `weapon_action_map`
    - `projectile_delivery`
    - `weapon_mount_profile`
12. Dashboard evidence links the latest moveset matrix, notify audit, projectile
    delivery report, and weapon mount report.

### 20.16 P14 starter implementation package

The first P14 package should convert the current weapon proof into a weapon
authoring pipeline. The immediate goal is not dozens of weapon families. It is
one trustworthy grammar where a Designer can see what an equipped weapon is,
which action it will play, which montage/section/slot it uses, which windows
fire, which sockets drive fit/trails/hitboxes, and which report proves it.

```text
Source/Ascentia/Public/Weapons/AscentiaWeaponTypes.h
```

```text
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Commandlets/AscentiaNotifyWindowAuditCommandlet.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Commandlets/AscentiaWeaponMountAuditCommandlet.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Tests/AscentiaWeaponResolverTest.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Tests/AscentiaPairedWeaponRulesTest.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Tests/AscentiaProjectileDeliveryTest.cpp
```

Subsystem boundaries:

```text
UAscentiaWeaponResolverSubsystem : UGameInstanceSubsystem
  ResolveWeaponSnapshot(Inventory, Slot, Context)
  ResolveOffHandSnapshot(Inventory, MainHand, OffHandSlot)
  ResolveActionDef(WeaponSnapshot, ActionTag, ContextTags)
  ResolveWeaponSkill(WeaponSnapshot, ItemInstance)
  ResolveAmmoSnapshot(Inventory, RangedProfile)
  ResolveThrowableSnapshot(Inventory, QuickSlot)
  WriteWeaponResolutionReport(OutputPath)

UAscentiaCombatComponent
  consumes resolved weapon snapshots and action defs
  no longer owns weapon family/type fallback as invisible private truth

UAscentiaAvatarComponent
  consumes socket profile, trail profile, and notify windows for presentation
  does not decide gameplay legality

UAscentiaProjectileDeliverySubsystem : UWorldSubsystem
  validates ammo/throwable requests
  spawns projectile or executes trace policy
  writes projectile delivery evidence
```

Weapon action request signatures:

```text
FAscentiaWeaponActionResult TryStartWeaponAction(

FAscentiaWeaponActionDef ResolveWeaponAction(

  const USkeletalMesh* PreviewCharacter);
```

Action tags should be stable and shared by weapons, abilities, UI, and reports:

```text
Weapon.Action.Light
Weapon.Action.Heavy
Weapon.Action.Charged
Weapon.Action.Running
Weapon.Action.Rolling
Weapon.Action.Backstep
Weapon.Action.Jump

Weapon.Action.Backstab
Weapon.Action.Riposte

Weapon.Action.Ranged.Draw
Weapon.Action.Ranged.Release
Weapon.Action.Throw
```

Notify authority policy:

```text
Damage windows:
  authoritative or server-reconstructed; never cosmetic only

Projectile release:
  authoritative request plus client presentation; no inventory consumption from
  cosmetic notify alone

Trail/audio/camera/rumble:
  cosmetic, but validated for coverage against action contract

Cancel/iframe windows:
  gameplay relevant; exported to manifest and checked by runtime policy
```

```text
Sword_OneHand_LightCombo3
Greatsword_Heavy_LightCombo3
```

```text
EUW_AscentiaWeaponAuthoring

EUW_AscentiaMovesetMatrix
```

Implementation tickets:

```text
P14-001 Weapon resolver extraction
  Move hard-coded weapon family/type resolution behind
  `UAscentiaWeaponResolverSubsystem`, preserving current fallback results but
  reporting each fallback.

P14-002 Weapon type and action map assets
  Add starter `PDA_WeaponType_*` and `PDA_WeaponActionMap_*` assets for sword,
  greatsword, dagger, shield, bow, and throwing knife.

P14-003 Weapon action request/result path
  Introduce `FAscentiaWeaponActionRequest`, `FAscentiaWeaponActionResult`, and
  `FAscentiaActiveWeaponActionState` so light, movement, guard counter, jump,
  skill, ranged, and throwable actions share one accepted/rejected path.

P14-004 Montage contract and notify manifest
  Generate `FAscentiaAnimNotifyWindowManifest` and
  `FAscentiaMontageActionContract` for current attack, roll attack, bow, and
  throwable montages.

P14-005 Weapon mount lab first slice
  Add `PDA_WeaponSocketProfile_*` assets and `EUW_AscentiaWeaponMountLab`
  preview for AssassinGirl sword, greatsword, shield, bow, and throwing knife.

P14-006 Hitbox replay and trail proof
  Emit `FAscentiaWeaponHitboxReplay` and validate trace sockets, trail sockets,
  damage windows, swing whoosh, and Niagara trail windows together.

P14-007 Paired weapon profile path
  Replace implicit shield/dual/power-stance behavior with
  `PDA_PairedWeaponProfile_*`, pairing reports, and Designer-readable rejection
  text.

P14-008 Ranged/ammo projectile delivery
  Add `FAscentiaAmmoSnapshot`, `FAscentiaProjectileDeliveryRequest`,
  `FAscentiaProjectileDeliveryResult`, `PDA_RangedWeaponProfile_*`,
  `PDA_AmmoProfile_*`, and `PDA_ProjectileProfile_*`.

P14-009 Throwable profile path
  Add `PDA_ThrowableProfile_*`, `FAscentiaThrowableUseResult`, release notify
  validation, quick-item HUD evidence, and empty-slot rejection proof.

P14-010 P14 packaged proof suite
  Run action map, notify audit, weapon mount, hitbox replay, projectile delivery,
  paired weapon, weapon-swap, ranged ammo, throwable, and weapon skill proofs in
  PIE and packaged Development.
```

Validation gates:

- any shippable weapon type without an action map fails validation;
- any action map entry without montage, damage profile, cost policy, cue policy,
  and notify contract fails validation;
- any montage referenced by gameplay without required notify windows fails
  validation;
- any weapon with trail profile but missing valid start/end sockets fails
  validation;
- any ranged/throwable profile without server-authoritative inventory
  transaction proof fails validation;
- any generated or marketplace weapon without a mount report remains in staging;
- any fallback action must be shown in the matrix and explicitly phase-approved.

Recommended sequencing:

1. Extract the resolver while preserving current weapon behavior.
2. Add assets that reproduce current sword, greatsword, shield, bow, and
   throwing-knife results.
3. Add notify and socket reports for the attacks already being tested in PIE.
4. Add weapon-swap and shield-pair proof before expanding movesets.
5. Add projectile/throwable delivery reports before authoring more ranged
   weapons.
6. Only then grow combo chains, weapon skills, power stance, and exotic weapon
   families.

## 21. Magic, Eth, Spellbook, Flasks, Summons, and Spell Delivery

### 21.1 Player-facing goal

P15 is where magic becomes a second ability economy instead of a debug button
that spends blue resource.

The player-facing promise:

- Eth supports a build identity distinct from Breath: spells, rituals, weapon
  imbuements, buffs, debuffs, summons, and utility;
- spell decisions are visible in the Spellbook, HUD, reticle, cast animation,
  projectile or target behavior, impact feedback, status buildup, and recovery;
- the player can understand why a spell cannot fire: insufficient Eth, cooldown,
  missing focus, invalid target, blocked state, no flask charge, or silenced
  status;
- Designers can author a spell as data plus Blueprint presentation without
  editing native combat code;
- every spell has proof: input accepted, Eth spent, cooldown applied, montage
  played, cast/release notify fired, targeting resolved, delivery applied damage
  or status, cues appeared, UI updated, and save/load preserved the spellbook.

The production target is not simply "sorceries exist." It is a coherent magic
pipeline that shares the same authority, targeting, damage, presentation,
settings, validation, save, and future network rules as weapons.

### 21.2 Current Ascentia state

P15 already has three real native slices:

- `UAscentiaGameplayAbility` supports both Breath and Eth costs through
  `CheckCost`, `CommitAbility`, and SetByCaller Gameplay Effects;
- `UAscentiaEthCostEffect` subtracts Eth and emits
  `GameplayCue.Ascentia.SpellCast`;
- `UAscentiaAbility_SpellCast` is a native GAS ability with a 16 Eth fallback
  cost, `Ascentia.Cooldown.Spell`, and `Ascentia.Ability.SpellCast`;
- `UAscentiaSpellbookComponent` replicates known spell tags, equipped spell tag,
  learn/equip counts, rejection telemetry, and Blueprint-readable spell views;
- `UAscentiaSpellDefinition : UPrimaryDataAsset` registers the `AscentiaSpell`
  primary asset type and exposes skill definition, description, damage, poise,
  and cue identity;
- a native Ether Spark fallback keeps the game playable when no authored spell
  assets exist;
- `UAscentiaCombatComponent::ExecuteSpellCast` records spell tag, damage, poise,
  range, Eth cost, server time, action count, and fires `OnSpellCastStarted`;
- `UAscentiaAbility_UseEthPotion` restores Eth through GAS, consumes replicated
  `EthPotionCharges`, rejects full-Eth/no-charge use, and emits
  `OnEthPotionUsed`;
- Eth and Eth potion charges refill through campsites, save restore, and respawn
  hooks;
- HUD/debug readouts expose Eth and Eth potion counts;
- remote proofs already exist for `spell_eth`, `spellbook_surface`,
  `eth_potion`, and baseline regressions.

The production gaps:

- spell casting currently records telemetry and presentation events, but does
  not yet own a cast montage lifecycle, cast window, release notify, projectile,
  trace, targeting preset, damage application, status application, or impact cue;
- Spellbook UI is still a placeholder tab rather than a learn/equip/loadout
  screen;
- `StartingSpellAssets` is a useful Blueprint surface, but there is not yet a
  full asset registry, authoring widget, validation report, or content folder
  convention for production spell catalogs;
- Eth recovery policy is not designed beyond flask refill/restoration and max
  Eth scaling;
- Eth flask charge allocation/upgrades are not authored;
- spell cooldown/resource HUD and low-resource prompts are not production UI;
- buff/debuff spells, summons, utility spells, and spell status payloads are not
  implemented;
- input binding, reticle, cast montage, VFX, audio, camera, and settings are
  still pending.

### 21.3 Resource model

Eth should not be "Breath with another color." Breath governs physical tempo:
attack, block, dodge, sprint, and immediate body commitment. Eth governs arcane
tempo: spell access, ranged pressure, buffs, debuffs, summons, and build
investment.

Current formula:

```text
MaxEth = 20 + 4.0 * FOC
```

Resource policy:

```text
FAscentiaEthResourceProfile
  MaxEthFormula
  RegenPolicy: None, Delayed, SlowAlwaysOn, EncounterOnly
  RegenDelaySeconds
  RegenPerSecondCurve
  LowEthThreshold
  LowEthPromptPolicy
  SpellCostScaleByStat
  CooldownScaleByStat
  FlaskRestoreProfile
```

Runtime resource state:

```text
FAscentiaEthRegenState
  CurrentEth
  MaxEth
  RegenPolicy
  RegenDelayRemaining
  RegenPerSecond
  LastEthSpendTime
  LastDamageTakenTime
  bInCombat
  bRegenSuppressed

FAscentiaSpellCostRequest
  SpellTag
  AbilitySpec
  BaseEthCost
  SourceStats
  CostScaleTags
  CooldownProfile
  bPreviewOnly

FAscentiaSpellCostResult
  FinalEthCost
  CooldownSeconds
  bCanPay
  RejectReason
  CostGameplayEffect
  CooldownGameplayEffect
  SetByCallerMagnitudes
```

Decisions:

- if Eth regenerates, it must not erase flask pressure or make low-cost spells
  trivial spam;
- if Eth does not regenerate, the game needs strong flask allocation, camp, item,
  and world-restoration language;
- delayed slow regen can support exploration magic while keeping boss pressure
  meaningful;
- high-cost spells should remain emotionally legible: the player should feel the
  cast, cost, risk, and payoff.

Ascentia should keep Health, Breath, and Eth as first-class AttributeSet values
so GAS can own costs, cooldowns, buffs, debuffs, UI queries, and future
replication discipline. Any Designer-facing cost curve should resolve into the
native attribute path, not bypass it.

### 21.4 Spell identity model

Spell identity should resolve in layers:

```text
Spellbook equipped spell
  -> spell definition primary asset
  -> school profile
  -> resource profile
  -> cast profile
  -> targeting profile
  -> delivery profile
  -> damage/status profile
  -> cue/audio/VFX/camera profile
  -> UI icon and tooltip
```

Core asset:

```text
PDA_Spell
  SpellTag
  DisplayName
  Description
  SchoolTag
  AbilityClass
  EthCost
  CooldownSeconds
  RequiredFocus
  RequiredItemTags
  CastProfile
  TargetingProfile
  DeliveryProfile
  DamageProfile
  StatusPayloads
  BuffDebuffProfile
  SummonDefinition
  MagicCueProfile
  UIIcon
  TooltipText
```

Spell resolution:

```text
UAscentiaSpellResolverSubsystem
  ResolveSpellDefinition(SpellTag)
  ResolveEquippedSpell(Spellbook, Slot)
  ResolveSpellCastProfile(SpellDefinition, SourceBuild)
  ResolveSpellDeliveryProfile(SpellDefinition, SourceBuild)
  ResolveSpellCost(SpellDefinition, SourceAttributes)
  ResolveSpellView(SpellDefinition, SourceBuild)

FAscentiaResolvedSpell
  SpellTag
  PrimaryAssetId
  SourceAsset
  SchoolProfile
  CastProfile
  DeliveryProfile
  TargetingProfile
  DamageProfile
  StatusPayloads[]
  BuffDebuffProfile
  SummonDefinition
  CostResult
  CooldownResult
  CueProfile
  FallbacksUsed[]
  ValidationWarnings[]

FAscentiaSpellView
  SpellTag
  DisplayName
  SchoolName
  Icon
  EthCost
  CooldownSeconds
  Range
  DamagePreview
  StatusPreview
  RequirementText
  RejectReasonText
  TooltipRows[]
  bFallback
```

The existing `UAscentiaSpellDefinition` is the right seed. Its next expansion
should add explicit cast, targeting, delivery, status, school, UI, and asset
bundle references. Since it is already a `UPrimaryDataAsset`, it can use Unreal's
Asset Manager path for discovery, audit, chunking, and soft-reference loading.

Spell schools should be data, not enum-only:

```text
PDA_SpellSchool
  SchoolTag
  DisplayName
  DamageSchool
  ScalingStats
  StatusFamilies
  ResistanceProfile
  CueFamily
  UITheme
  LoreText
```

Example school families:

- Ember: fire, burn, area denial, panic pressure;
- Frost: slow, brittle, stamina/Breath pressure, spacing control;
- Storm: lightning, stagger, fast projectile pressure;
- Venom: poison, damage over time, attrition;
- Grave: rot, curse, death-loop pressure, summon synergy;
- Ether: neutral arcane, projectile fundamentals, utility;
- Radiance: healing, barrier, conviction-style buffs;
- Thorn: nature, bleed, rooting, trap-like terrain control.

The school list is a design call, but the architecture should allow schools to
map into the P13 damage/status pipeline rather than creating a separate magic
damage universe.

### 21.5 Spellbook lifecycle

The Spellbook should own four concepts:

```text
Known spells
Equipped spells
Loadout slots
Last selection/rejection telemetry
```

Current native state:

```text
KnownSpellTags
EquippedSpellTag
SpellLearnCount
SpellEquipCount
bLastSpellbookEquipRejected
LastSpellbookSpellTag
LastSpellbookSpellName
LastSpellbookEthCost
LastSpellbookCooldownSeconds
LastSpellbookRange
```

Next production state:

```text
FAscentiaSpellbookSaveData
  KnownSpellTags
  LoadoutSlots[]
  EquippedSlotIndex
  FavoriteSpellTags
  RecentlyLearnedSpellTags
  SpellUpgradeState

FAscentiaSpellLoadoutSlot
  SlotTag
  SpellTag
  InputTag
  bLocked
  UnlockSourceTag
```

Spellbook view model:

```text
FAscentiaSpellbookView
  KnownSpells[]
  LoadoutSlots[]
  EquippedSlotIndex
  SelectedSpell
  FilteredSchoolTags
  NewSpellTags
  FallbackSpellWarnings[]
  SaveStateSummary

FAscentiaSpellbookEquipRequest
  SpellTag
  TargetSlot
  SourceActor
  bPreviewOnly

FAscentiaSpellbookEquipResult
  bAccepted
  RejectReasonTextKey
  PreviousSpell
  NewSpell
  UpdatedView
```

Rules:

- learn/equip must be server-authoritative and save-persistent;
- UI should read a Spellbook view model rather than reconstructing spell rules;
- invalid equip should give a Designer-authored reason string;
- fallback Ether Spark should be visibly marked as fallback in editor tools and
  validation reports;
- future character creation/origin systems can grant starting spells by adding
  spell tags or spell assets, not by editing player class defaults.

Spellbook UI should support:

- list/grid of known spells;
- school filter;
- equipped slot strip;
- cost/cooldown/range/damage/status preview;
- required focus/item messaging;
- hotbar or single-slot policy depending on design decision;
- unsaved/new spell callouts;
- keyboard/gamepad navigation;
- settings-driven detail density.

### 21.6 Casting pipeline

The production casting pipeline:

```text
Input action
  -> ability activation by input tag
  -> CanActivateAbility: Eth, cooldown, state, silence, equipment, target policy
  -> CommitAbility: Eth cost and cooldown
  -> cast montage / cast stance
  -> cast window notify
  -> targeting request or aim snapshot
  -> release notify
  -> delivery actor, trace, area, buff, or summon
  -> damage/status/buff/debuff GameplayEffect application
  -> GameplayCue, Niagara, MetaSound, camera, HUD
  -> recovery/cancel/end ability
```

The current native `ActivateAbility` path commits cost, plays a generic action
animation, calls `ExecuteSpellCast`, and ends immediately. That is correct for a
small proof slice, but production spells need asynchronous ability execution:
cast tasks, montage wait tasks, release delegates, cancel handling, and cleanup.
Epic's GAS documentation explicitly frames Gameplay Abilities as multi-stage
asynchronous flows with costs, cooldowns, input, animation, replication policy,
and Ability Tasks. Ascentia should lean into that rather than building a parallel
spell executor outside GAS.

Cast profile:

```text
PDA_SpellCastProfile
  CastType: Instant, TimedCast, Charged, Channeled, HeldAim, Ritual
  Montage
  SlotName
  StartSection
  LoopSection
  ReleaseSection
  CancelSection
  RootMotionPolicy
  MovementLockPolicy
  RotationPolicy
  CancelWindows
  InputBufferPolicy
  ReleaseNotifyName
  CastCue
  LoopCue
  ReleaseCue
```

Cast runtime contract:

```text
FAscentiaSpellCastRequest
  SourceActor
  InputTag
  SpellTag
  LoadoutSlot
  ResolvedSpell
  AimContext
  TargetingContext
  bFromBufferedInput
  ClientRequestTime

FAscentiaSpellCastResult
  bAccepted
  RejectReason
  ResolvedSpell
  CostResult
  AbilitySpecHandle
  Montage
  StartSection
  OpenedCastWindows[]
  TraceId

FAscentiaActiveSpellCastState
  SpellTag
  CastType
  StartTime
  CurrentPhase: Startup, Holding, Channeling, Release, Recovery, Cancelled, Complete
  Montage
  Section
  PendingTargetingRequest
  PendingDeliveryRequest
  EthCommitted
  CooldownCommitted
  bCanCancel
  bCanRelease
  bInterrupted

FAscentiaSpellCastWindowManifest
  Montage
  SlotName
  CastWindow
  ChargeWindow
  ChannelWindow
  ReleaseNotify
  CancelWindows[]
  CueMarkers[]
  MotionLockWindows[]
```

Rules:

- the release of a projectile/trace/area should come from an animation notify or
  explicit instant policy, not arbitrary code timing;
- a failed target check should not spend Eth unless the spell is intentionally
  self-committing before target validation;
- cancel policy must be readable: can the player dodge out, block out, swap
  spells, or get interrupted;
- channeled spells need start, tick, cost-over-time, interrupt, and end rules;
- movement-locked spells must use the same combat motion/ledge/stair policy as
  attacks and dodges.
- ability tasks should own asynchronous wait points: montage blend-out,
  gameplay-event release, target data arrival, channel tick, cancel, and cleanup.
  Native code should not fake an asynchronous spell by firing all effects in
  `ActivateAbility` unless the cast profile is explicitly `Instant`.

### 21.7 Targeting and delivery types

Spell delivery should be data-authored by profile:

```text
PDA_SpellDeliveryProfile
  DeliveryType
  TargetingProfile
  ProjectileProfile
  TraceProfile
  AreaProfile
  BuffDebuffProfile
  SummonDefinition
  ImpactDamageProfile
  ImpactStatusPayloads
  GameplayCueProfile
  bRequiresValidTargetBeforeCommit
```

Delivery transaction:

```text
FAscentiaSpellTargetingRequest
  SourceActor
  SpellTag
  TargetingProfile
  AimOrigin
  AimDirection
  LockOnTarget
  GroundPoint
  ClientRequestTime

FAscentiaSpellTargetingResult
  bAccepted
  RejectReason
  CandidateTargets[]
  ChosenTargets[]
  ChosenLocation
  ChosenNormal
  TargetDataHandle
  DebugTrace[]

FAscentiaSpellDeliveryRequest
  SourceActor
  ResolvedSpell
  TargetingResult
  DeliveryProfile
  ReleaseTransform
  ChargeAlpha
  ChannelTickIndex
  TraceId

FAscentiaSpellDeliveryResult
  bAccepted
  RejectReason
  SpawnedActors[]
  TraceHits[]
  AppliedDamageEvents[]
  AppliedStatusResults[]
  AppliedGameplayEffects[]
  SummonResults[]
  CueRequests[]
```

Delivery types:

- Self: buffs, heals, cleanses, barriers, stance changes;
- Touch/melee imbue: weapon-coated hits, close-range palms, spellblade bursts;
- Projectile: slow/fast missiles, homing shards, arcing bolts;
- Hitscan/trace: lightning, beam tick, instant line spell;
- Ground target: rune, trap, area pool, delayed eruption;
- Area around caster: nova, pulse, fear, cleanse;
- Aura: sustained buff/debuff radius;
- Summon: temporary actor or companion;
- Utility/world: reveal, unlock, activate, cleanse hazard, bridge/traversal
  spell.

Targeting profile:

```text
PDA_MagicTargetingProfile
  TargetingMode: None, ForwardAim, LockOnTarget, GroundPoint, AreaQuery, Cone
  TargetingPreset
  MaxRange
  Radius
  Angle
  RequiredTags
  BlockedTags
  SortPolicy
  AimAssistPolicy
  ReticlePolicy
```

The Gameplay Targeting System should be the preferred shape for reusable target
selection/filter/sort rules. Simple projectiles and traces are still valid for
MVP, but profile names should match the future Targeting preset route. That
keeps bow, throwable, spell, enemy caster, summon, weak point, and lock-on
systems from each inventing their own acquisition grammar.

Commit timing policy:

- if `bRequiresValidTargetBeforeCommit` is true, target validation happens
  before Eth and cooldown are committed;
- if a spell is intentionally risky, it may commit before final delivery, but
  the UI and tooltip must say so;
- channeled spells may commit an upfront cost plus periodic tick costs;
- cancelled casts must define whether cooldown applies, partial cost applies,
  or nothing is spent.

### 21.8 Damage, status, buffs, and debuffs

Magic must plug into P13:

```text
Spell delivery impact
  -> FAscentiaDamagePacket
  -> damage school formula
  -> resistance/defense profile
  -> status payload application
  -> GameplayEffects
  -> GameplayCues
```

Buffs and debuffs should be Gameplay Effects:

```text
PDA_BuffDebuffProfile
  EffectTags
  GameplayEffectClass
  DurationPolicy
  DurationSeconds
  StackPolicy
  MaxStacks
  RefreshPolicy
  AttributeModifiers
  GrantedTags
  RemovedTags
  PeriodicTickPolicy
  CueProfile
  UIIcon
  TooltipText
```

Buff/debuff application result:

```text
FAscentiaBuffDebuffApplicationRequest
  SourceActor
  TargetActor
  Profile
  SourceSpell
  StackContext
  TraceId

FAscentiaBuffDebuffApplicationResult
  bApplied
  RejectReason
  ActiveEffectHandle
  StackCountAfter
  DurationRemaining
  GrantedTags[]
  RemovedTags[]
  CueRequests[]
  UIStateUpdate
```

Rules:

- duration, stacking, refresh, overflow, immunity, and cleanse behavior must be
  authored and validated;
- buff/debuff UI should read active effect tags and view models, not parse cue
  names;
- spell status buildup must share enemy/boss resistance and immunity windows;
- spell damage numbers need profile control so magic readability does not drown
  melee feedback;
- every buff/debuff spell needs an icon, tooltip, active-state cue, removal cue,
  and validation report.

### 21.9 Summons and companion spells

Summons are deceptively large. Even a "simple" temporary ally touches AI,
encounters, save, networking, UI, performance, and world readability.

Summon definition:

```text
PDA_SummonDefinition
  SummonTag
  DisplayName
  ActorClass
  AbilitySet
  AIBehaviorProfile
  LifetimePolicy
  MaxConcurrent
  SpawnPolicy
  OwnershipPolicy
  AggroPolicy
  ScalingProfile
  DeathBehavior
  DespawnCue
  UIFramePolicy
```

Summon runtime contract:

```text
FAscentiaSummonRequest
  SourceActor
  SourceSpell
  SummonDefinition
  SpawnTransform
  TargetingResult
  OwnerTeam
  ScalingSnapshot

FAscentiaSummonResult
  bAccepted
  RejectReason
  SpawnedActor
  OwnershipTags
  LifetimeSeconds
  CleanupTriggers[]
  UIFrame

FAscentiaSummonRuntimeState
  SummonTag
  OwnerActor
  SpawnedActor
  SpawnTime
  LifetimeRemaining
  CurrentTarget
  DamageCreditOwner
  bPersistent
  bCleanupPending
```

Rules:

- MVP summons should be temporary and cleanup-safe unless persistent companions
  are explicitly chosen;
- summons should have owner attribution for damage, XP, loot credit, and aggro;
- summon actors must despawn on owner death, camp rest, map transition, or save
  unload unless a profile says otherwise;
- summon UI should show presence, health if relevant, and expiration only when
  useful;
- enemy targeting should distinguish player, summon, and neutral actors through
  tags rather than ad hoc class checks.
- summon behavior should reuse P18 enemy/AI profiles where possible. A summon is
  not a special AI universe; it is an owned combatant with different faction,
  cleanup, scaling, and credit rules.

### 21.10 Flask and resource recovery

Current native Eth flask behavior is the right authority base:

- Eth flask charge count lives on the AttributeSet;
- use requires missing Eth and at least one charge;
- restore and charge consumption apply through a Gameplay Effect;
- campsite/save/respawn refill restores charges.

Production flask profile:

```text
PDA_FlaskProfile
  FlaskTag
  ResourceType: Health, Eth, Hybrid
  RestoreAmount
  RestorePercent
  ChargeAttribute
  MaxChargeAttribute
  UseAbility
  UseMontage
  UseCue
  CooldownSeconds
  FullResourceRejectText
  EmptyRejectText
  HUDIcon
```

Upgrade/allocation:

```text
PDA_FlaskUpgrade
  UpgradeTag
  DisplayName
  RequiredItemTag
  Cost
  AddsCharges
  AddsRestoreAmount
  AffectedFlaskTags
  AllocationPolicy
```

Allocation/save state:

```text
FAscentiaFlaskAllocationState
  HealthCharges
  EthCharges
  SharedCharges
  MaxSharedCharges
  RestoreStrengthByFlask
  AppliedUpgrades[]
  LastCampAllocationTime

FAscentiaFlaskUseRequest
  SourceActor
  FlaskTag
  bPreviewOnly

FAscentiaFlaskUseResult
  bAccepted
  RejectReason
  ResourceBefore
  ResourceAfter
  ChargesBefore
  ChargesAfter
  AppliedGameplayEffect
  CueRequests[]

FAscentiaFlaskUpgradePreview
  UpgradeTag
  Cost
  BeforeState
  AfterState
  RequirementText
  bCanApply
```

Policy options:

- fixed separate Health and Eth flask pools;
- shared charge pool allocated at camp;
- upgrade tree where items choose charge count vs restore strength;
- hybrid system where rare upgrades add special secondary effects.

The Soulslike-readable route is shared allocation at camp, but Ascentia does not
need to copy it blindly. The important architecture point is that allocation
should be authored, visible in UI, saved, validated, and replayable in tests.

### 21.12 Tooling opportunities

P15 deserves tools because spell content multiplies quickly.

`EUW_AscentiaSpellbookAuthoring`:

- list all `AscentiaSpell` primary assets;
- show school, cost, cooldown, delivery, damage, status, cue, icon, and loadout
  readiness;
- flag fallback-only spells;
- create spell asset templates by school/delivery type;
- open Spellbook UI preview with sample player stats.

`EUW_AscentiaSpellDeliveryPreview`:

- simulate projectile arc, range, lock-on target, ground target, cone, area, and
  hit radius;
- show expected release frame from montage notify;
- compare targeting preset results against projectile path;
- preview impact cue and damage/status payload.

`EUW_AscentiaMagicBalanceLab`:

- sweep Focus, spell level, enemy resistance, cooldown, cast time, and Eth pool;
- report damage per Eth, damage per second, poise per Eth, status buildup per
  cast, casts per flask, and boss time-to-kill estimates;
- compare spell against same-tier melee and ranged actions.

`EUW_AscentiaFlaskPlanner`:

- preview health/Eth charge allocation;
- show upgrade costs, restore amounts, camp refill result, and save data;
- run no-charge/full-resource rejection proofs.

`EUW_AscentiaSummonPreview`:

- spawn a summon in a test cell;
- show owner tags, aggro targets, lifetime, cleanup events, and performance
  footprint;
- verify death/camp/map-transition despawn behavior.

### 21.13 P15 evidence, spell reports, and validation output

P15 needs spell evidence that covers authoring, runtime delivery, balance,
flasks, and summons.

Reports:

```text
FAscentiaSpellbookAuthoringReport

FAscentiaMagicBalanceReport
  ReportId

  PoisePerEth
  StatusPerEth
  CastsPerFlask
  CooldownPressure
  MeleeRangedComparison

FAscentiaFlaskPlannerReport

  UseResults[]
  RefillResults[]
  SaveLoadResults[]
  RejectionProofs[]

FAscentiaSummonPreviewReport
  ReportId
  SummonDefinition
  SpawnResults[]
  AggroResults[]
  CleanupResults[]
  DamageCreditResults[]
  PerformanceFindings[]
```

Evidence folders:

```text
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P15/Spellbook/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P15/SpellDelivery/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P15/MagicBalance/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P15/Flasks/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P15/Summons/{RunId}/report.json
```

Required proof scenarios:

```text
Tools/remote_playtest.py spell_eth
Tools/remote_playtest.py spellbook_surface
Tools/remote_playtest.py spell_delivery
Tools/remote_playtest.py spellbook_save
Tools/remote_playtest.py spell_targeting
Tools/remote_playtest.py buff_debuff_spell
Tools/remote_playtest.py eth_potion
Tools/remote_playtest.py flask_upgrade
Tools/remote_playtest.py flask_allocation
Tools/remote_playtest.py summon_spell
```

Acceptance shape:

- every shippable spell has a primary asset id, icon, text, school, cast profile,
  delivery profile, targeting policy, cost/cooldown profile, cue profile, and
  proof row;
- every non-instant cast has a cast window/release manifest;
- every projectile/area/trace spell has targeting and delivery evidence;
- every buff/debuff has effect, stack, refresh, UI, cue, removal, and save rules;
- every flask upgrade/allocation change has preview, transaction, save/load, and
  camp-refill evidence;
- every summon profile proves ownership, aggro, cleanup, credit, and UI;
- Ether Spark fallback remains visible and blocks shipping if any shippable
  loadout still depends on it.

### 21.14 Interconnects

P15 touches:

- P10/P11 combat motion: cast movement locks, dodge cancel, root motion, stairs,
  ledges, interruption, rotation, and input buffering;
- P13 damage/build math: magic schools, resistances, status payloads, critical
  rules, poise, Focus scaling, and threshold passives;
- P14 weapons: staff/focus weapons, spellblade imbuements, projectile profiles,
  targeting profiles, reticles, montage notifies, and weapon skill overlap;
- P16 itemization: spell-granting items, talismans, affixes, flask upgrades,
  crafting materials, unique powers, and economy sinks;
- P17 world: spell rewards, locked traversal, magic interactions, hazards,
  region school identity, and discovery;
- P18 AI: caster enemies, summon AI, magic-resistant enemies, boss spells, and
  anti-cheese targeting;
- P19 save/death: known spells, equipped loadout, flask allocation, spell
  upgrades, summon cleanup, and death-state resource resets;
- P20 presentation: GameplayCues, Niagara, MetaSounds, camera, damage numbers,
  accessibility, and performance budgets;
- P21 shell/settings: spell HUD visibility, cast input mode, reticle behavior,
  low-Eth prompts, buff/debuff notification density, VFX intensity, and input
  remapping;
- P22 multiplayer: predicted casts, server-authoritative projectiles, target
  validation, summon ownership, cooldown/resource replication, and anti-cheat.

### 21.15 P15 proof gates

P15 is not complete until:

1. Spell assets are discoverable as `AscentiaSpell` primary assets and no
   shippable spell relies only on native Ether Spark fallback.
2. Spellbook UI can learn, equip, show cost/cooldown/range/status, reject invalid
   choices with reason text, and preserve state through save/load.
3. Spell input activates through Enhanced Input/GAS binding and updates HUD
   cooldown/resource feedback.
4. At least one projectile spell uses a cast montage, release notify, targeting
   or aim snapshot, projectile/trace/area delivery, impact cue, and real damage
   application through the P13 damage path.
5. At least one buff or debuff spell applies a duration Gameplay Effect, shows UI
   state, refreshes/stacks according to profile, and removes cleanly.
6. Eth flask UI shows charges, rejects full/no-charge use, plays use montage/cue,
   restores exact Eth through GAS, saves/restores allocation, and refills at
   campsites.
7. Flask upgrade/allocation data exists and can be previewed in an editor tool.
8. If summons enter MVP scope, at least one summon proves ownership, aggro,
   lifetime, damage credit, UI, cleanup, and save/map/death behavior.
9. Settings include spell reticle visibility, cast input mode, spell HUD/resource
   visibility, Eth flask HUD visibility, low-Eth prompt behavior, spell/flask VFX
   intensity, summon visibility/readability, buff/debuff notification density,
   and flask-use feedback intensity where applicable.
10. Validation covers missing icons, missing cues, invalid tags, invalid
    GameplayEffects, absent release notifies, invalid projectile classes,
    missing target presets, missing spellbook text, invalid flask charges, and
    fallback-only content.
11. Remote proof covers:
    - `spell_eth`
    - `spellbook_surface`
    - `eth_potion`
    - `spell_delivery`
    - `spellbook_save`
    - `spell_targeting`
    - `flask_upgrade`
    - `flask_allocation`
    - `buff_debuff_spell`
    - `summon_spell`
12. Dashboard evidence links the latest Spellbook authoring report, spell
    delivery preview report, magic balance report, and flask planner report.

### 21.16 P15 starter implementation package

The first P15 package should turn the current Ether Spark, Spellbook, Eth cost,
and Eth potion proofs into a spell production lane. The immediate goal is one
fully authored spell path with visible cast, targeting, delivery, damage/status,
cue, UI, save, and proof contracts.

Runtime module layout:

```text
Source/Ascentia/Public/Magic/AscentiaSpellTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Magic/AscentiaSpellDefinition.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Magic/AscentiaSpellResolverSubsystem.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Magic/AscentiaSpellCastTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Magic/AscentiaSpellDeliveryTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Magic/AscentiaSpellTargetingTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Magic/AscentiaEthResourceTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Magic/AscentiaMagicBalanceTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Spellbook/AscentiaSpellbookTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Spellbook/AscentiaSpellbookComponent.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Flasks/AscentiaFlaskTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Summons/AscentiaSummonTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Private/Magic/...
Source/Ascentia/Private/Spellbook/...
Source/Ascentia/Private/Flasks/...
Source/Ascentia/Private/Summons/...
```

Editor module layout:

```text
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Magic/AscentiaSpellValidation.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Magic/AscentiaSpellDeliveryValidation.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Magic/AscentiaMagicBalanceValidation.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Flasks/AscentiaFlaskValidation.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Summons/AscentiaSummonValidation.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Commandlets/AscentiaSpellbookAuditCommandlet.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Commandlets/AscentiaSpellDeliveryAuditCommandlet.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Commandlets/AscentiaMagicBalanceAuditCommandlet.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Tests/AscentiaSpellResolverTest.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Tests/AscentiaSpellCostCommitTest.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Tests/AscentiaFlaskUseTest.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Tests/AscentiaSummonLifecycleTest.cpp
```

Subsystem and class boundaries:

```text
UAscentiaSpellResolverSubsystem : UGameInstanceSubsystem
  ResolveSpellDefinition(SpellTag)
  ResolveEquippedSpell(Spellbook, Slot)
  ResolveSpellCastProfile(SpellDefinition, SourceBuild)
  ResolveSpellDeliveryProfile(SpellDefinition, SourceBuild)
  ResolveSpellCost(SpellDefinition, SourceAttributes)
  ResolveSpellView(SpellDefinition, SourceBuild)
  WriteSpellbookAuthoringReport(OutputPath)

UAscentiaAbility_SpellCast : UGameplayAbility
  GAS activation, cost/cooldown, ability tasks, montage/cast/release lifecycle
  delegates spell lookup to resolver and delivery to the delivery path

UAscentiaSpellbookComponent
  owns known spells, loadout slots, equipped selection, save-facing state, and
  Spellbook view model events

UAscentiaSpellDeliverySubsystem : UWorldSubsystem
  resolves targeting result into projectile, trace, area, buff/debuff, summon, or
  utility delivery

UAscentiaFlaskSubsystem : UGameInstanceSubsystem or UWorldSubsystem
  previews flask allocation/upgrades and owns non-ability helper queries; actual
  flask use still applies through GAS effects

UAscentiaSummonSubsystem : UWorldSubsystem
  owns summon spawn, ownership tags, damage credit, cleanup, and reports
```

Starter content folders:

```text
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Magic/Spells/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Magic/Schools/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Magic/CastProfiles/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Magic/DeliveryProfiles/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Magic/Targeting/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Magic/Cues/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Magic/Balance/
```

```text
PDA_Spell_EtherSpark_MVP
PDA_Spell_FrostShard_MVP

PDA_Spell_SummonEcho_Prototype

PDA_SummonDefinition_Echo_Prototype

```

Primary asset policy:

```text
PrimaryAssetType:
  AscentiaSpell
  AscentiaSpellSchool
  AscentiaSpellCastProfile
  AscentiaSpellDeliveryProfile
  AscentiaMagicTargetingProfile
  AscentiaFlaskProfile
  AscentiaSummonDefinition

Rules:
  spell catalogs scan `/Game/Ascentia/Design/Magic/Spells`;
  cooked builds load spell bundles through Asset Manager;
  fallback Ether Spark is allowed in editor/dev reports but blocks shipping
  unless it is represented by `PDA_Spell_EtherSpark_MVP`.
```

Spell resolver signatures:

```text
FAscentiaResolvedSpell ResolveSpell(
  FGameplayTag SpellTag,
  const FAscentiaSpellResolveContext& Context);

FAscentiaSpellView BuildSpellView(
  const FAscentiaResolvedSpell& Spell,
  const FAscentiaBuildSnapshot& SourceBuild);

FAscentiaSpellCostResult ResolveSpellCost(
  const FAscentiaResolvedSpell& Spell,
  const FAscentiaSpellCostRequest& Request);

FAscentiaSpellbookView BuildSpellbookView(
  const UAscentiaSpellbookComponent& Spellbook,
  const FAscentiaSpellbookViewContext& Context);
```

Cast and commit policy:

```text
Instant:
  validate activation, commit Eth/cooldown, deliver immediately, end ability

Timed projectile:
  validate activation, optionally validate target before commit, commit Eth at
  cast start, play montage, release on notify, apply cooldown on release or cast
  start according to profile

Held aim:
  validate activation, enter hold state, update targeting preview, commit on
  release, cancel without spend if profile permits

Channeled:
  commit entry cost, tick periodic Eth/cooldown/payload, cleanup on interrupt

Ritual:
  movement-locked, long cast, world-state/service integration, explicit
  interruption and refund policy
```

Starter proof matrix:

```text
EtherSpark_Fallback_AuthoredAsset
EtherSpark_Instant_EthCostCooldownCue
FrostShard_TimedProjectile_ReleaseNotify
FrostShard_TargetBeforeCommit
PoisonWisp_StatusPayload_P13
SelfWard_BuffDuration_UIRefreshRemove
Spellbook_LearnEquipSaveLoad
Spellbook_InvalidEquip_ReasonText
EthFlask_FullRejected
EthFlask_NoChargeRejected
EthFlask_UseRestoresExactEth
FlaskUpgrade_EthChargePreviewSave
SummonEcho_SpawnCreditCleanup
LowEthPrompt_SettingsVisibility
```

Editor surfaces:

```text
EUW_AscentiaSpellbookAuthoring
  browse spell primary assets, school/cost/cooldown/delivery/readiness, fallback
  warnings, and Spellbook UI preview

EUW_AscentiaSpellDeliveryPreview
  preview cast montage release frame, targeting result, projectile/trace/area,
  impact, damage/status, and cues

EUW_AscentiaSpellTargetingDebugger
  run Targeting preset or simple trace fixtures and show candidate/filter/sort
  decisions

EUW_AscentiaBuffDebuffPreview
  preview duration, stack, refresh, removal, active UI, and cue lifecycle

EUW_AscentiaMagicBalanceLab
  compare damage per Eth, poise per Eth, status per Eth, casts per flask, and
  melee/ranged parity

EUW_AscentiaFlaskPlanner
  preview charge pools, upgrades, camp refill, use rejection, and save state

EUW_AscentiaSummonPreview
  spawn test summon, show owner, aggro, lifetime, cleanup, damage credit, and UI
```

```text
UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe Ascentia.uproject -run=AscentiaSpellbookAudit
  -SpellRoot=/Game/Ascentia/Design/Magic/Spells

UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe Ascentia.uproject -run=AscentiaMagicBalanceAudit

Tools/run_p15_spell_delivery.ps1
  -Scenario SpellDelivery
```
