```text
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P15/Spellbook/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P15/SpellDelivery/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P15/SpellTargeting/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P15/MagicBalance/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P15/BuffDebuff/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P15/Flasks/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P15/Summons/{RunId}/report.json
```

Implementation tickets:

```text
P15-001 Spell primary asset expansion
  Expand `UAscentiaSpellDefinition` into the full `PDA_Spell` contract with
  school, cast, delivery, targeting, damage/status, cue, UI, and bundle refs.

P15-002 Spell resolver and view model
  Implement `UAscentiaSpellResolverSubsystem`, `FAscentiaResolvedSpell`,
  `FAscentiaSpellView`, and fallback reporting.

P15-003 Spellbook save/load and UI bridge
  Implement `FAscentiaSpellbookView`, `FAscentiaSpellbookSaveData`, loadout slot
  state, learn/equip requests, and invalid-equip reason text.

P15-004 Cast lifecycle and window manifest
  Implement `FAscentiaSpellCastRequest`, `FAscentiaSpellCastResult`,
  `FAscentiaActiveSpellCastState`, `FAscentiaSpellCastWindowManifest`, and the
  first timed projectile release-notify path.

P15-005 Targeting and delivery contracts
  Implement `FAscentiaSpellTargetingRequest`, `FAscentiaSpellTargetingResult`,
  `FAscentiaSpellDeliveryRequest`, `FAscentiaSpellDeliveryResult`, and the first
  projectile/trace delivery report.

P15-006 Buff/debuff spell profile
  Implement `PDA_BuffDebuffProfile`, duration/stack/refresh/remove behavior,
  active UI view, cue lifecycle, and report rows.

P15-007 Eth resource and flask profiles
  Implement `PDA_EthResourceProfile`, `FAscentiaEthRegenState`,
  `PDA_FlaskProfile`, `FAscentiaFlaskUseResult`, and full/no-charge rejection
  proof.

P15-008 Flask upgrade and allocation planner
  Implement `PDA_FlaskUpgrade`, `PDA_FlaskAllocationProfile`,
  `FAscentiaFlaskAllocationState`, `FAscentiaFlaskUpgradePreview`, save/load,
  and camp-refill reports.

P15-009 Summon prototype contract
  Implement `PDA_SummonDefinition`, `FAscentiaSummonRequest`,
  `FAscentiaSummonResult`, `FAscentiaSummonRuntimeState`, owner credit, aggro,
  and cleanup proof.

P15-010 P15 packaged proof suite
  Run Spellbook, spell delivery, targeting, buff/debuff, Eth flask, flask
  upgrade/allocation, summon, and magic balance proofs in PIE and packaged
  Development.
```

Validation gates:

- any shippable spell without a primary asset id, icon, text, school, cast
  profile, delivery profile, targeting policy, cost/cooldown, cue profile, and
  proof row fails validation;
- any non-instant spell without a release notify or explicit release policy
  fails validation;
- any projectile/trace/area spell without targeting and delivery evidence fails
  validation;
- any buff/debuff without GameplayEffect, stack/refresh policy, UI, cue, and
  removal behavior fails validation;
- any flask profile without full/no-charge rejection text and exact GAS restore
  proof fails validation;
- any summon without ownership, cleanup, damage credit, and UI policy remains
  prototype-only;
- Ether Spark fallback must be represented as an authored asset or block
  shipping.

Recommended sequencing:

1. Convert Ether Spark into a real `PDA_Spell_EtherSpark_MVP`.
2. Implement the spell resolver and Spellbook view model while preserving
   current behavior.
3. Add a timed projectile spell with montage release notify and delivery report.
4. Add Eth flask profile data and exact use/rejection proof.
5. Add one buff/debuff spell before adding more projectile variants.
6. Treat summons as prototype-only until ownership, cleanup, and AI credit are
   proven.

## 22. Enemies, AI, Encounters, Bosses, and Population Production

### 22.1 Player-facing goal

P18 is where Ascentia stops having "an enemy" and starts having an enemy
production system.

The player-facing promise:

- trash enemies teach timing, spacing, status, terrain, and resource discipline;
- elites recombine familiar rules into pressure tests;
- bosses define zones through spectacle, readable moves, phase changes, music,
  arena rules, reward identity, and memory;
- groups create decision pressure without becoming unfair noise;
- enemy behavior respects terrain, leashes, perception, world state, summons,
  stealth, ranged pressure, and multiplayer future-proofing;
- Designers can make enemy families, encounters, boss moves, patrols, and
  behavior variants without rewriting the pawn.

The production target is an authoring pipeline where a new enemy is mostly:
definition asset, stat rows, ability set, behavior asset, EQS/positioning policy,
animations, cues, loot, and validation.

### 22.2 Current Ascentia state

Working pieces:

- `AAscentiaEnemyCharacter` exists as the native enemy embodiment;
- `AAscentiaEnemyAIController` exists with Behavior Tree hooks;
- boss phase, target, health-fraction, movement request, and selected ability
  sync have native BT-facing footholds;
- `UAscentiaBossDefinition` exists with display name, intro presentation, phase
  rows, optional phase ability sets, music state/cue, behavior tag, and fog-wall
  hook;
- persistent `BB_DraugrBoss_P11` / `BT_DraugrBoss_P11` assets run a first
  service-to-move-to-boss-melee loop;
- `UAscentiaAbility_BossMelee` activates through GAS, respects enemy Breath
  affordability, and delegates the strike to the enemy;
- enemy attack resource gating and telemetry have proof through
  `enemy_resource`;
- fog wall, boss intro/name card, phase music state, defeat presentation, and
  stagger/poise punish-window telemetry exist;
- `AAscentiaEncounterZoneActor.ManagedSpawns` is already an editor-visible MVP
  spawn surface;
- remote proof scenarios already touch `bt_asset_ai`, `boss_phase`,
  `boss_presentation`, `fog_wall`, `stagger`, `enemy_resource`, and MVP baseline
  combat loops.

Production gaps:

- enemy variety is essentially one authored enemy family;
- enemy stats, resistances, loot, moves, movement, perception, and encounter
  budgets are not resolved through production `PDA_EnemyDefinition` assets;
- Behavior Tree work is still a foothold, not a complete branch policy for
  patrol, idle, investigate, leash, retreat, ranged, caster, group, and boss
  phase behavior;
- no EQS spacing/positioning model exists for melee slots, ranged line of sight,
  flanking, retreat, or arena anchors;
- AI perception is shallow: sight exists conceptually, but hearing, damage,
  memory, investigation, target switching, and faction/team policy are not
  production systems;
- group coordination is not authored, so future packs can easily dogpile, stall,
  or attack all at once;
- current AI still carries player-0 assumptions in places and needs target
  acquisition through perception/aggro policy;
- boss move grammar is not a data-authored matrix with range bands, cooldowns,
  telegraphs, notifies, root-motion policy, and counterplay notes;
- encounter zones need data assets, leash/reset/save policy, budget validation,
  and world-state integration.

### 22.3 Production AI stack

Recommended default stack:

```text
Enemy pawn/controller
  -> ASC, AttributeSet, movement, animation, perception, telemetry

Enemy definition
  -> identity, stats, resistances, ability set, loot, budget, presentation

Behavior Tree + Blackboard
  -> readable combat flow and current facts

EQS
  -> spatial decisions and target positions

GameplayAbilities
  -> enemy attacks, spells, blocks, dodges, skills, boss moves

Encounter definition
  -> spawn plan, group rules, leash/reset/save, rewards, world state
```

Use Behavior Trees for core combat because Ascentia already has BT footholds and
BTs are readable for Designers. Unreal's Behavior Tree model pairs execution
branches with Blackboard keys, which is exactly the shape needed for target,
phase, distance, cooldown, health, and group-role state.

Use EQS for "where should I be?" It should not be hard-coded into every enemy:
melee approach points, circle points, ranged line-of-sight points, retreat
points, flank points, boss anchors, summon points, and return-to-post points
should be query profiles.

Use AI Perception for awareness. Sight, hearing, damage, prediction, team, and
touch senses should feed aggro/investigation state rather than direct player
lookups.

Use StateTree for ambient or structured non-combat behavior: patrol/rest/work,
investigate loops, Smart Object usage, simple NPC schedules, and future distant
simulation.

Use Smart Objects for world-authored activities and reservations: guard posts,
ambush points, ritual sites, ranged perches, campfires, levers, ladders, and
boss arena anchors.

Use Mass only for distant non-combat populations, wildlife, crowds, or settlement
life. Do not move core boss combat into Mass; bosses require bespoke animation,
GAS, root motion, camera, cue, and presentation control.

### 22.4 Enemy definition model

Enemy definition:

```text
PDA_EnemyDefinition
  EnemyTag
  DisplayName
  EnemyFamilyTags
  WorldTier
  AttributeProfile
  ResistanceProfile
  StatusVulnerabilityProfile
  PoiseProfile
  ResourceProfile
  PerceptionProfile
  MovementProfile
  AbilitySet
  BehaviorTree
  StateTree
  BlackboardAsset
  EQSProfiles
  LootTable
  CorpseLootPolicy
  EncounterBudgetCost
  SpawnTags
  FactionTag
  VoiceCueProfile
  HitReactionProfile
  DeathPresentationProfile
  SmartObjectUsageTags
  NavAgentType
  ValidationNotes
```

Enemy family row:

```text
FAscentiaEnemyFamilyRow
  EnemyFamilyTag
  DisplayName
  DefaultBehaviorTree
  DefaultAbilitySet
  DefaultPerceptionProfile
  DefaultMovementProfile
  DefaultLootTable
  AllowedBiomeTags
  AllowedEncounterTags
  DefaultBudgetCost
  DefaultFactionTag
```

Enemy stat row:

```text
FAscentiaEnemyStatRow
  EnemyTag
  WorldTier
  Level
  MaxHealth
  MaxBreath
  BreathRegen
  MaxPoise
  Armor
  Resistances
  AttackPowerScalar
  StatusBuildupScalar
  RewardStones
  ProgressionValue
  EncounterBudgetCost
```

Rules:

- DataTables own bulk numeric tuning and tier sweeps.
- PrimaryDataAssets own object references, ability sets, behavior assets, cues,
  movement profiles, and validation notes.
- Runtime enemy snapshots should resolve once on spawn, then expose what was
  resolved to debug/UI/automation.
- Designers must be able to see whether a value came from family default, enemy
  override, encounter override, world-tier row, or native fallback.

Resolution service:

```text
UAscentiaEnemyDefinitionResolver
  ResolveEnemyDefinition(EnemyTag, WorldTier, EncounterContext)
  BuildSpawnRequest(EncounterSpawnGroup, SpawnAnchor, RuntimeTags)
  BuildEnemyRuntimeSnapshot(EnemyActor)
  WriteEnemyDefinitionReport(OutputPath)
```

Resolved enemy definition:

```text
FAscentiaResolvedEnemyDefinition
  EnemyTag
  DisplayName
  FamilyTags
  WorldTier
  Level
  AttributeSnapshot
  ResistanceSnapshot
  StatusVulnerabilitySnapshot
  PoiseProfile
  ResourceProfile
  PerceptionProfile
  MovementProfile
  AbilitySet
  BehaviorTree
  BlackboardAsset
  StateTree
  EQSProfiles
  LootTable
  FactionTag
  EncounterBudgetCost
  PresentationProfiles
  SourceTrace[]
  ValidationSeverity
```

Spawn request/result:

```text
FAscentiaEnemySpawnRequest
  EnemyTag
  EncounterTag
  SpawnGroupTag
  SpawnAnchor
  WorldTier
  InitialFactionTag
  InitialGroupRoleTag
  InitialBehaviorTag
  RuntimeTags

FAscentiaEnemySpawnResult
  SpawnedActor
  AIController
  ResolvedDefinition
  GrantedAbilities
  BlackboardInitialization
  SpawnCueResult
  Warnings[]
  Errors[]
```

Runtime snapshot:

```text
FAscentiaEnemyRuntimeSnapshot
  EnemyActor
  EnemyTag
  EncounterTag
  CurrentTarget
  CurrentHealthFraction
  CurrentBreathFraction
  CurrentPoiseFraction
  CurrentBehaviorTag
  CurrentPhaseTag
  ActiveMoveTag
  ActiveGameplayEffects

```

Definition report:

```text
FAscentiaEnemyDefinitionReport
  RunId
  EnemyCount
  FamilyCount
  MissingProfileRefs[]
  MissingBehaviorRefs[]
  MissingPresentationRefs[]
  FallbackSources[]
  ResolvedEnemies[]
  Errors[]
  Warnings[]
```

The resolver should be callable from commandlets, validation, Editor Utility
Widgets, PIE debug tools, and encounter spawning. That keeps enemy truth out of
one-off spawn Blueprints while still making the resolved result visible in the
Editor.

### 22.5 Perception, aggro, factions, and investigation

Perception profile:

```text
PDA_AIPerceptionProfile
  SightRadius
  LoseSightRadius
  PeripheralVisionAngle
  HearingRange
  DamageMemorySeconds
  InvestigationMemorySeconds
  DominantSense
  TeamPolicy
  TargetSwitchPolicy
  AlertPropagationRadius
  StimulusTagFilters
  DebugColor
```

Aggro profile:

```text
PDA_AggroProfile
  InitialAggroRules
  DamageAggroWeight
  ProximityAggroWeight
  SpellAggroWeight
  SummonAggroWeight
  HealingAggroWeight
  ThreatDecaySeconds
  TargetSwitchHysteresis
  AlertAlliesPolicy
  LeashBreakPolicy
```

Faction profile:

```text
PDA_FactionProfile
  FactionTag
  HostileFactionTags
  FriendlyFactionTags
  NeutralFactionTags
  CanDamageSameFaction
  AssistPolicy
  SummonOwnershipPolicy
```

Investigation states:

- saw target;
- lost sight;
- heard noise;
- took damage;
- found corpse;
- found player trace;
- returned to post;
- alerted group;
- de-escalated.

Rules:

- target selection should never assume player 0;
- summons, companions, co-op players, neutral NPCs, and hostile factions need
  targetable identities;
- hearing/noise should support stealth, thrown objects, projectiles, footsteps,
  combat impacts, and spell cues later;
- perception memory must be visible in AI debug output and proof reports.

Perception bridge:

```text
UAscentiaAIPerceptionBridge
  OnStimulusReceived(AIActor, Stimulus)
  RecordStimulus(StimulusRecord)
  UpdateThreatTable(ThreatEvent)
  SelectTarget(SelectionContext)
  ExportPerceptionSnapshot(AIActor)
```

Stimulus record:

```text
FAscentiaPerceptionStimulusRecord
  StimulusId
  SourceActor
  SourceTeamOrFactionTag
  SenseTag
  StimulusLocation
  ReceiverLocation
  Strength
  AgeSeconds
  WasSuccessfullySensed
  SourceGameplayTags
  EvidenceTags
```

Threat entry:

```text
FAscentiaThreatEntry
  TargetActor
  TargetIdentityTag
  FactionRelationship
  DamageThreat
  ProximityThreat
  HealingThreat
  SpellThreat
  SummonThreat
  ObjectiveThreat
  RecencyBonus
  CurrentTotalThreat
  LastStimulusTime
  LastKnownLocation
  HasLineOfSight
```

Target selection:

```text
FAscentiaTargetSelectionResult
  SelectedTarget
  PreviousTarget
  SwitchReasonTag

  HysteresisApplied

```

```text
FAscentiaInvestigationState

  SourceStimulusId
  InvestigationLocation
  SearchRadius
  StartedAtTime
  ExpiresAtTime
  AlertedAllies[]
  ReturnBehaviorTag
```

`EUW_AscentiaThreatTableDebugger` should show the table as a sorted list with
source columns. A Designer should be able to answer "why did the boss attack my
summon instead of me?" without reading C++ or stepping through perception code.

### 22.6 Blackboard and behavior contract

Core Blackboard keys:

```text
TargetActor
HomeLocation
LeashAnchor
CurrentEnemyTag
CurrentBehaviorTag
CurrentPhaseTag
CurrentPhaseIndex
HealthFraction
BreathFraction
PoiseFraction
TargetDistance
TargetAngle
HasLineOfSight
LastKnownTargetLocation
InvestigationLocation
DesiredCombatRange
SelectedAbilityTag
SelectedMoveTag
SelectedEQSLocation
EncounterTag
GroupRoleTag
IsAlerted
IsLeashing
IsReturningHome
```

Boss keys:

```text
BossPhaseId
BossPhaseBehaviorTag
ArenaTag
FogWallSealed
LastMoveTag
MoveCooldownGroup
EnrageState
AddsAliveCount
```

Contract:

- every BT task/service documents which keys it reads and writes;
- validators fail when a BT references keys missing from its Blackboard;
- boss phase sync is a service, not duplicated inside every move branch;
- target acquisition writes facts into Blackboard, while ability execution reads
  those facts through a narrow request interface;
- fallback native tick AI should become an emergency fallback only and should be
  visible in validation when still active.

Behavior contract artifacts:

```text
FAscentiaBlackboardKeyContract
  KeyName
  KeyType
  OwnerSystemTag
  RequiredForBehaviorTags
  WrittenByTasks[]
  ReadByTasks[]
  DefaultValuePolicy
  DebugDescription

FAscentiaBlackboardContract
  BlackboardAsset
  RequiredKeys[]
  OptionalKeys[]
  ForbiddenKeys[]
  BehaviorTags
  BossPhaseKeys
```

AI state snapshot:

```text
FAscentiaAIStateSnapshot
  AIActor
  Controller
  BehaviorTree
  BlackboardAsset
  CurrentBTNode
  CurrentBehaviorTag
  BlackboardValues
  CurrentTarget
  CurrentMoveRequest
  CurrentEQSRequest
  CurrentGameplayAbility
  NativeFallbackActive
```

BT validation report:

```text
FAscentiaBehaviorTreeContractReport
  BehaviorTree
  BlackboardAsset
  MissingKeys[]
  UnusedKeys[]
  TypeMismatches[]
  NativeFallbackPaths[]
  TasksWithoutReadWriteDocs[]
  ServicesWithoutTickBudget[]
  Errors[]
  Warnings[]
```

`EUW_AscentiaAIValidationPanel` should open a BT/Blackboard pair and show the
contract as a table. Key ownership matters because otherwise Blackboard state
becomes another invisible global variable pile.

### 22.7 Movement, spacing, and EQS

Enemy movement should be profiled:

```text
PDA_AIMovementProfile
  WalkSpeed
  RunSpeed
  StrafeSpeed
  TurnRate
  Acceleration
  Braking
  PreferredRange
  MinRange
  MaxRange
  CirclePolicy
  RetreatPolicy
  FlankPolicy
  NavAgentType
  RootMotionPolicy
  MotionWarpPolicy
  StuckRecoveryPolicy
```

EQS profiles:

```text
PDA_EQSProfile
  QueryAsset
  Contexts
  RequiredTags
  BlockedTags
  ScorePolicy
  MinScore
  DebugColor
  FailureFallback
```

Required EQS query families:

- melee attack slot;
- strafing/circle slot;
- ranged line-of-sight;
- retreat/recover;
- flank;
- ambush reveal;
- boss arena anchor;
- summon/add spawn;
- return-to-post;
- Smart Object selection.

Rules:

- multiple melee enemies should reserve different attack slots;
- ranged enemies should choose line-of-sight positions, not run into melee by
  accident;
- retreat should respect arena boundaries and not flee through fog walls;
- boss anchor movement should be authored per arena;
- stuck recovery should be telemetry-backed, not invisible teleporting.

EQS request/result:

```text
FAscentiaEQSRequest
  RequestingActor
  TargetActor
  QueryTag
  QueryAsset
  ContextTags
  RequiredLocationTags
  BlockedLocationTags
  MinScore
  FailureFallback
  DebugReason

FAscentiaEQSResult
  QueryTag
  ChosenLocation
  Score
  CandidateCount
  RejectedCandidates[]
  FailureReasonTag
  BlackboardWrites
```

Spacing reservation:

```text
FAscentiaSpacingReservation
  ReservationId
  EncounterTag
  OwnerActor
  TargetActor
  SlotTag
  Location
  Radius
  ExpiresAtTime
  ReleaseReasonTag
```

EQS proof report:

```text
FAscentiaEQSProofReport
  RunId
  QueryTag
  ScenarioTag
  CandidateCount
  SelectedScore
  ReservationConflicts
  FailedQueries[]
  Screenshots[]
  DebugTracePath
```

`EUW_AscentiaEQSPreview` should be able to run a profile from a selected enemy
or encounter marker and draw chosen, rejected, and reserved points. This is the
Editor-facing place where "ranged enemy stands somewhere sensible" becomes
inspectable instead of vibes.

### 22.8 Enemy moves and ability sets

Enemy moves are data plus GameplayAbilities:

```text
PDA_EnemyMove
  MoveTag
  AbilityClass
  RequiredEnemyFamilyTags
  RangeMin
  RangeMax
  AngleMin
  AngleMax
  CooldownTag
  CooldownSeconds
  BreathCost
  EthCost
  Weight
  PhaseTags
  TargetStateRequirements
  SelfStateRequirements
  MotionProfile
  MontageSet
  TelegraphCue
  ActiveWindowNames
  RecoveryWindowNames
  CounterplayNotes
```

Ability set:

```text
PDA_EnemyAbilitySet
  EnemyTagOrFamilyTag
  GrantedAbilities[]
  PassiveEffects[]
  StartupEffects[]
  InputOrAICommandTags[]
```

Selection score:

```text
Score =
  range fit
  + angle fit
  + phase weight
  + cooldown availability
  + target-state bonus
  + self-state bonus
  + recent-move penalty
  + group-role adjustment
  + Designer-authored weight
```

Rules:

- Behavior Trees choose when to request a move; GameplayAbilities own authority,
  costs, cooldowns, montages, notifies, damage, and cues;
- Designers should be able to inspect why a move was selected or rejected;
- every attack montage needs damage windows, cue markers, root-motion policy,
  and counterplay notes;
- enemy Breath/Eth economies should use the same GAS resource path as player
  abilities.

Move selection query:

```text
FAscentiaEnemyMoveQuery
  EnemyActor
  TargetActor
  EnemyTag
  EnemyFamilyTags
  BehaviorTag
  PhaseTag
  RangeToTarget
  AngleToTarget
  LineOfSight
  GroupRoleTag
  CurrentResources
  RecentMoveTags
  TargetStateTags
  SelfStateTags
```

Move score:

```text
FAscentiaEnemyMoveScoreBreakdown
  MoveTag
  IsLegal
  FinalScore
  RangeScore
  AngleScore
  PhaseScore
  CooldownScore
  ResourceScore
  TargetStateScore
  SelfStateScore
  GroupRoleScore
  RepeatPenalty
  DesignerWeight
  RejectionReasons[]
```

Move selection/execution:

```text
FAscentiaEnemyMoveSelection
  SelectedMoveTag
  SelectedAbilityClass
  ScoreBreakdown
  CandidateScores[]
  BlackboardWrites
  GameplayAbilityRequest

FAscentiaEnemyMoveExecutionResult
  MoveTag
  AbilitySpecHandle
  CommitResult
  MontageResult
  DamageWindowsObserved
  CueEventsObserved
  CancelWindowObserved
  FailureReasonTag
```

The move selector should be deterministic enough to debug and varied enough to
avoid robotic repetition. The report should preserve both the winner and the
interesting losers, because rejected moves often explain tuning problems faster
than the selected move.

### 22.9 Encounter definitions and reset policy

Encounter definition:

```text
PDA_EncounterDefinition
```

```text
PDA_EncounterLeashProfile
  LeashRadius
  HardLeashRadius
  VerticalTolerance
  ReturnBehavior
  ResetHealthPolicy
  ResetResourcePolicy
  ResetPositionPolicy
  ResetAggroPolicy
```

Save/reset policy:

- trash enemies respawn on rest;
- patrols reset to route;
- ambushers reset hidden state unless discovered state says otherwise;
- unique elites can stay dead until world reset;
- story bosses stay dead after defeat;
- summoned adds despawn with encounter owner;
- repeatable POIs separate discovered state from resettable reward attempts.

Encounter runtime state:

```text
FAscentiaEncounterRuntimeState
  EncounterTag
  ZoneTag
  ActiveSpawnGroups
  AliveActors
  DefeatedActors
  AlertedGroups
  CurrentBudgetUsed
  CompletionStateTag
  LeashState
  RewardState
  RuntimeDataLayers
  GroupCoordinationState
```

Reset request/result:

```text
FAscentiaEncounterResetRequest
  EncounterTag
  ResetReasonTag
  RestPointTag
  WorldStateTags
  SaveSlotId
  PreserveDiscoveredState

FAscentiaEncounterResetResult

  RespawnedGroups[]

  ClearedStateTags[]
  RewardResetResult

```

Save fragment:

```text
FAscentiaEncounterSaveFragment
```

```text
FAscentiaEncounterBudgetReport

```

`EUW_AscentiaEncounterResetPolicyPreview` should show what will reset on rest,
death, reload, boss defeat, and world-state change. This is one of the places
where the Soulslike loop must be authored rather than inferred.

### 22.10 Group coordination and roles

Group roles:

```text
Ascentia.AI.Role.Frontliner
Ascentia.AI.Role.Flanker
Ascentia.AI.Role.Ranged
Ascentia.AI.Role.Caster
Ascentia.AI.Role.Support
Ascentia.AI.Role.SummonedAdd
```

Group coordination profile:

```text
PDA_GroupCoordinationProfile
  MaxSimultaneousAttackers
  AttackSlotCount
  RangedSlotCount
  FlankSlotCount
  AlertPropagationPolicy
  RoleAssignmentPolicy
  SlotReservationTimeout
  FriendlyFirePolicy
  RetreatWhenOutnumberedPolicy
```

Coordinator responsibilities:

- assign roles on spawn;
- reserve attack, flank, and ranged slots;
- throttle simultaneous committed attacks;
- broadcast alert state;
- prevent body-block piles;
- release reservations on death, leash, reset, stun, or target switch;
- expose telemetry for selected role, slot, and attack permission.

Start this as `UAscentiaEncounterGroupComponent` on the encounter zone. Promote
later only if cross-zone populations need it.

Group reservation artifacts:

```text
FAscentiaGroupSlotReservation
  ReservationId
  EncounterTag
  GroupTag
  OwnerActor
  TargetActor
  RoleTag
  SlotTag
  SlotLocation
  StartedAtTime
  ExpiresAtTime
  ReleaseReasonTag

FAscentiaGroupCoordinationState
  EncounterTag
  TargetActor
  ActiveRoles
  ActiveReservations
  SimultaneousAttackers
  RangedPressureCount
  FlankPressureCount
  AlertedActors
  WaitingActors
```

Coordination report:

```text
FAscentiaGroupCoordinationReport
  RunId
  EncounterTag
  MaxSimultaneousAttackers
  ObservedSimultaneousAttackers
  ReservationConflicts
  SlotStarvationEvents
  BodyBlockEvents
  FriendlyFireEvents
  RoleAssignmentTrace
  Errors[]
  Warnings[]
```

This report is the antidote to "five enemies all swing through each other at
once." Designers should be able to loosen or tighten pressure through the group
profile and immediately see how it changes reservations and attack permission.

### 22.11 Boss production grammar

Bosses are encounter systems, not larger trash enemies.

Boss assets:

```text
PDA_BossDefinition

  DisplayName
  Subtitle
  ArenaTag
  PhaseDefinitions
  DefaultBehaviorTree
  DefaultBlackboard
  BossAbilitySet

```

Boss phase:

```text
PDA_BossPhase

  AbilitySetOverride

  AddSpawnPolicy
```

Boss move:

```text
PDA_BossMove
  MoveTag
  AbilityClass
  Montage
  RangeBand
  AngleBand
  MinCooldown
  MaxCooldown
  RepeatPenalty
  PhaseTags
  TelegraphCue
  ActiveDamageWindows
  RecoveryWindows
  CancelOrInterruptRules
  RootMotionPolicy
  MotionWarpPolicy
  ArenaConstraints
  CounterplayNote
  CameraProfile
  RumbleProfile
  MusicStinger
```

Each boss phase should change at least one real system:

- move weights;
- new move unlocks;
- arena state;
- add pressure;
- music state;
- resource economy;
- counterplay rhythm;
- traversal hazard.

If a phase only changes text or music, it is presentation, not a boss design
phase.

Boss move query and selection:


`EUW_AscentiaBossQualityReport` should sit beside the boss move matrix. The move
matrix tells Designers what the boss can do; the quality report tells the team
whether the boss is readable, fair, persistent, and arena-safe enough to enter a
milestone.

### 22.13 Tooling opportunities

`EUW_AscentiaEnemyDefinitionBrowser`:

- list enemy definitions, families, stats, loot, behavior assets, ability sets,
  and budget cost;
- show resolved family defaults vs enemy overrides;
- highlight missing animations, cues, BT/BB keys, and loot tables.

`EUW_AscentiaEnemyMoveMatrix`:

- display moves by enemy family, range band, angle band, phase, cooldown, cost,
  montage, damage window, cue, and counterplay note;
- show repeat-penalty and utility-scoring weights;
- preview legal moves at a sample target distance/angle/phase.

`EUW_AscentiaEncounterPlaytestLab`:

- spawn encounter definitions in a sandbox cell;
- simulate rest/reset/save policies;
- show live budget, role assignment, leash state, and kill/reward outcomes.

`EUW_AscentiaPerceptionDebugger`:

- visualize sight cones, hearing radius, last known target, investigation point,
  threat score, target switch score, and alert propagation.

`EUW_AscentiaBossMoveMatrix`:

- show boss moves per phase with range, angle, cooldown, repeat penalty,
  telegraph, active, recovery, punish, root-motion, and cue coverage.

`EUW_AscentiaThreatTableDebugger`:

- sort threat entries by target and contribution source;
- show target switch hysteresis, faction relationship, line of sight, last known
  location, and stimulus age;
- replay one target-selection decision from a proof report.

`EUW_AscentiaEncounterResetPolicyPreview`:

- simulate rest, death, reload, boss defeat, and world-state reset for an
  encounter definition;
- show spawned, preserved, despawned, and reward-reset outcomes;
- flag policies that conflict with POI completion, unique elites, or story boss
  persistence.

`EUW_AscentiaBossQualityReport`:

- collect move-matrix coverage, arena-motion proofs, phase-transition proofs,
  presentation coverage, persistence checks, and death-loop checks into one
  milestone-facing view;
- highlight whether a boss is prototype-only, playtest-ready, milestone-ready,
  or blocked.

The Dashboard card should link these reports directly. Designers should be able
to open the latest failed proof, click through to the offending DataAsset or
Behavior Tree, and see the fix path in plain language.

### 22.15 Interconnects

P18 touches:

- P10/P11 combat feel: poise, stagger, punish windows, boss presentation, fog
  walls, combat motion, and BT replacement of tick AI;
- P12 world scripting: world-state tags, objective triggers, NPC reactions,
  vendors, tutorials, and encounter-completion state;
- P13 damage/build math: enemy resistances, status vulnerabilities, poise,
  critical windows, defense formulas, and player build counterplay;
- P14 weapons: enemy weapon families, ranged enemies, shield behavior, dual-wield
  enemies, weapon skills, trails, and hit windows;
- P15 magic: caster enemies, buff/debuff enemies, summons, magic resistances,
  spell targeting, and status payloads;
- P16 itemization: enemy loot tables, boss drops, elite affixes, reward budgets,
  crafting materials, and economy sinks;
- P17 world: zone budgets, patrol routes, POI encounters, World Partition,
  Smart Object collections, nav build, HLOD/streaming behavior, and Data Layers;
- P19 save/death: boss persistence, enemy respawn, encounter reset, corpse-run
  interaction, and death-state cleanup;
- P20 presentation: telegraphs, impact cues, music state, VO/barks, camera,
  damage numbers, corpse presentation, and performance budgets;
- P21 shell/settings: combat assist presets, enemy aggression/resource pressure
  settings if supported, boss intro skip/intensity, HUD readability, and debug
  proof surfaces;
- P22 multiplayer: target selection across players/summons, replicated aggro,
  authority, prediction, boss phase sync, and anti-cheat-sensitive damage.

### 22.16 P18 proof gates

P18 is not complete until:

1. At least three enemy families resolve from `PDA_EnemyDefinition` plus stat,
   movement, perception, ability, loot, and presentation profiles.
2. Behavior Tree/Blackboard assets are the primary combat control path for core
   enemies; native tick AI fallback is disabled or explicitly reported.
3. AI Perception drives target acquisition, investigation, damage response, and
   target memory without player-0 assumptions.
4. EQS chooses at least melee attack slots, ranged line-of-sight positions, and
   retreat/return points.
5. Encounter definitions own spawn groups, budget, group roles, leash/reset/save
   policy, and reward policy.
6. Group coordination throttles simultaneous attacks and reserves positions for
   at least one multi-enemy encounter.
7. Enemy moves are data-authored, ability-backed, cost/cooldown-gated, and
   validated for montage, damage window, cue, range, and counterplay.
8. At least one boss uses phase definitions, move matrix rules, music state,
   fog-wall policy, boss presentation, and persistent defeat policy.
9. Smart Objects are validated if used by patrol, ambush, guard, arena, or world
   interaction behavior.
10. Designer tools show resolved enemy definitions, boss move matrices, encounter
    budgets, EQS selections, perception state, and validation failures.
11. Remote proof covers:
    - `bt_asset_ai`
    - `enemy_spawn_resolution`
    - `enemy_resource`
    - `enemy_perception`
    - `perception_threat_table`
    - `enemy_leash`
    - `enemy_eqs_spacing`
    - `enemy_move_matrix`
    - `encounter_budget`
    - `encounter_reset_policy`
    - `encounter_playtest`
    - `encounter_reset_save`
    - `group_coordination`
    - `boss_move_grammar`
    - `boss_move_matrix`
    - `boss_quality`
    - `boss_persistence`
12. Dashboard evidence links the latest enemy definition report, encounter
    budget report, encounter playtest report, EQS spacing proof, threat-table
    proof, boss move matrix, boss quality report, and AI validation panel.

### 22.17 P18 starter implementation package

P18 should begin with one complete enemy/encounter/boss vertical slice instead of
separate partial systems. The first slice should prove that an enemy definition
can resolve into stats, perception, behavior, movement, moves, loot, encounter
budget, group coordination, and boss-quality evidence without relying on hidden
Blueprint defaults or native-only assumptions.

Reference posture:

- Behavior Trees should remain the visible combat behavior graph for ordinary
  enemies, with Blackboard keys treated as a versioned contract rather than
  informal variable names.
- AI Perception should own sensory input and stimulus memory. Player targeting
  should come from perceived actors, damage stimuli, threat entries, factions,
  and target-switch policy, never from "player 0."
- EQS should answer spatial questions such as attack slots, flank points, ranged
  line of sight, retreat points, and leash-return points. C++ can request and
  validate queries, but the questions should remain Designer-visible as EQS
  assets.
- StateTree and Smart Objects are appropriate for ambient, patrol, guard,
  investigation, and world-interaction behavior. Combat commitment remains in
  the BT/GAS/move stack until there is a strong reason to split it.
- GAS should launch enemy moves for the same reason it launches player moves:
  costs, cooldowns, tags, effects, cues, cancellation, authority, and audit
  traces belong in one ability framework.
- AI Debugging, EQS Testing Pawn usage, and Ascentia evidence reports should be
  part of the normal proof path, not emergency-only tools.


The first native ownership split should be:

```text
UAscentiaEnemyDefinitionResolver : UGameInstanceSubsystem
  Resolves DataAssets/DataTables/family defaults into immutable enemy snapshots.

UAscentiaAIPerceptionBridge : UActorComponent
  Lives on enemy AI controllers or enemy pawns; converts UAIPerceptionComponent
  stimuli, damage memory, faction rules, and authored aggro profiles into a
  threat table and target-selection result.

UAscentiaEncounterSubsystem : UWorldSubsystem
  Owns encounter runtime state, spawn requests, reset requests, save fragments,
  budget reports, and evidence collection.

UAscentiaEncounterGroupComponent : UActorComponent
  Lives on BP_AscentiaEncounterZone; owns role assignment, slot reservation,
  simultaneous attacker caps, group alert, and group proof telemetry.

UAscentiaBossMoveResolver : UGameInstanceSubsystem
  Scores boss move candidates from phase, range, angle, cooldown, repeat
  penalty, arena state, and counterplay/readability weights.
```

```text
/Game/Ascentia/Design/AI/Enemies/
```

```text
BP_AscentiaEnemyBase
```

`BB_Enemy_Combat_MVP` should include a small, explicit contract first:

```text
TargetActor
HomeLocation
EncounterTag
GroupRoleTag
CurrentThreatScore
LastKnownTargetLocation
InvestigationLocation
SelectedMoveTag
SelectedEQSLocation
LeashState
CanCommitAttack
CurrentPhaseTag
```

`FAscentiaBlackboardContract` should validate key names, key types, required
writers, required readers, and branch ownership. `BT_Enemy_Combat_MVP` should
fail the audit if it reads a key that is not in the contract, if a required key
has no writer, or if a branch writes target state directly instead of through
the perception/threat bridge.

The first resolver report should be intentionally boring:

```text
FAscentiaEnemyDefinitionReport
  RunId
  EnemyDefinition
  FamilyDefaultsApplied
  StatProfile
  MovementProfile
  PerceptionProfile
  AggroProfile
  FactionProfile
  BehaviorTree
  Blackboard
  AbilitySet
  MoveList
  LootTable
  BudgetCost
  PresentationRefs
  MissingRefs[]
  OverrideTrace[]
  Errors[]
  Warnings[]
```

The first perception/threat proof should include:

```text
FAscentiaThreatEntry
  TargetActor
  TargetTeamTag
  TargetTypeTag
  LastKnownLocation
  LastVisibleTime
  LastDamageTime
  SightScore
  HearingScore
  DamageScore
  ProximityScore
  ScriptedPriorityScore
  FactionScore
  CurrentThreatScore
  DecayRate

FAscentiaTargetSelectionResult
  SelectedTarget
  PreviousTarget
  SwitchReasonTag
  HysteresisApplied
  CandidateEntries
  BlackboardWrites
  Warnings[]
```

This is the point where multi-summon, future co-op, charmed enemies, decoys,
stealth, and scripted aggro stop being scary. They become extra threat entries
and target-selection policy, not special cases wired into a Behavior Tree.

The first EQS request/result contract:

```text
FAscentiaEQSRequest
  RequestId
  Querier
  TargetActor
  EncounterTag
  QueryTag
  EQSAsset
  ContextTags
  DesiredRoleTag
  MinDistance
  MaxDistance
  RequireLineOfSight
  ReservationPolicyTag

FAscentiaEQSResult
  RequestId
  QueryTag
  SelectedLocation
  SelectedActor
  Score
  CandidateCount
  RejectionReasons[]
  BlackboardWrites
  Reservation
  DebugSnapshotPath
```

EQS profiles should define Designer-facing question types, not hidden C++
algorithms:

```text
Ascentia.AI.EQS.MeleeAttackSlot
Ascentia.AI.EQS.FlankSlot
Ascentia.AI.EQS.RangedLineOfSight
Ascentia.AI.EQS.RetreatPoint
Ascentia.AI.EQS.LeashReturn
Ascentia.AI.EQS.AmbushReveal
Ascentia.AI.EQS.BossArenaSafePoint
```

Encounter runtime should start with one spawn definition and one placed-map
definition. This preserves the current preference that the MVP arena should not
force-spawn a boss every time PIE begins.

```text
PDA_EncounterDefinition_MVPArena_MVP
  EncounterTag = Ascentia.Encounter.MVP.Arena
  SpawnMode = PlacedActorsPlusOptionalSpawnGroups
  AutoStartPolicy = DisabledInPIEUnlessRequested
  SpawnGroups = DraugrTrashOptional, DraugrWardenPlaced
  Budget = MVP_Arena
  GroupCoordinationProfile = PDA_GroupCoordinationProfile_MVP
  LeashProfile = PDA_EncounterLeashProfile_MVP
  ResetPolicy = RestOrReload
  RewardPolicy = ManualPrototype
```

Boss quality should be treated as a production report, not just a playtest
opinion:

```text
FAscentiaBossQualityReport
  RunId
  BossTag
  ArenaTag
  PhaseCoverage
  MoveCoverage
  TelegraphCoverage
  ActiveDamageWindowCoverage
  RecoveryPunishWindowCoverage
  RootMotionArenaCoverage
  CameraReadabilityIssues
  CueCoverageIssues
  EQSSafePointCoverage
  GroupCoordinationIssues
  PersistenceIssues
  DeathLoopIssues
  ScreenshotPaths[]
  Errors[]
  Warnings[]
```

Commandlets:

```text
AscentiaEnemyDefinitionAudit
  Inputs: enemy definitions, families, stats, behavior assets, moves, loot.
  Output: Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P18/EnemyDefinitions/{RunId}/report.json

AscentiaAIBehaviorAudit
  Inputs: Behavior Trees, Blackboards, StateTrees, Smart Objects, EQS assets.
  Output: Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P18/BehaviorTrees/{RunId}/report.json

AscentiaEncounterAudit
  Inputs: encounter definitions, spawn groups, leash profiles, budgets,
    placed actors, reward policies.
  Output: Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P18/Encounters/{RunId}/report.json

AscentiaBossQualityAudit
  Inputs: boss definitions, boss move matrices, arenas, EQS profiles, cue
    manifests, montage contracts.
  Output: Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P18/BossQuality/{RunId}/report.json
```

Editor Utility Widgets:

```text
EUW_AscentiaEnemyDefinitionBrowser
  Opens a definition and shows resolved family defaults, overrides, behavior
  assets, move list, loot, budget cost, and latest resolver report.

EUW_AscentiaThreatTableDebugger
  Replays one target selection with sight/hearing/damage/faction/scripted
  scores, hysteresis, and Blackboard writes.

EUW_AscentiaEQSPreview
  Runs the selected EQS profile from a chosen enemy/target pair and visualizes
  candidate locations, scores, rejected candidates, and slot reservations.

EUW_AscentiaEncounterResetPolicyPreview
  Simulates rest, death, reload, boss defeat, and world-state reset outcomes
  without entering PIE.

EUW_AscentiaBossQualityReport
  Shows phase/move coverage, telegraph/cue coverage, arena/root-motion issues,
  screenshots, and milestone status.
```

Proof matrix:

```text
EnemyDefinition_Draugr_Resolve
EnemyDefinition_ThreeFamilies_Resolve
BlackboardContract_MVP_NoMissingKeys
BT_Enemy_Combat_MVP_NoNativeTickFallback
Perception_SightDamageHearing
ThreatTable_NoPlayerZeroAssumption
ThreatTable_TargetSwitchHysteresis
EQS_MeleeSlot
EQS_RangedLOS
EQS_ReturnToLeash
EnemyMove_Draugr_LightAttack
EnemyMove_MissingMontageFailsAudit
EnemySpawn_MVPArena_Group
EncounterBudget_MVPArena
EncounterReset_SaveLoad
Encounter_AutoStartDisabledInPIE
GroupCoordination_ThreeEnemies
BossMoveMatrix_DraugrWarden_Phase1
BossQuality_DraugrWarden_Arena
```

P18 starter tickets:

- P18-001: Create enemy definition resolver structs, subsystem, first three
  DataAssets, resolver report, and `EnemyDefinition_Draugr_Resolve` proof.
- P18-002: Create `BB_Enemy_Combat_MVP`, `BT_Enemy_Combat_MVP`,
  `FAscentiaBlackboardContract`, BT/BB validation, and no-native-tick fallback
  report.
- P18-003: Add `UAscentiaAIPerceptionBridge`, threat entries, target-selection
  result, sight/hearing/damage stimuli proof, and no-player-0 proof.
- P18-004: Add EQS profile/request/result structs, three starter EQS assets,
  EQS preview widget, and melee/ranged/leash spatial proof.
- P18-005: Convert enemy moves into data-authored GAS requests with montage,
  damage window, cue, cost, cooldown, range, and counterplay validation.
- P18-006: Add encounter definition, runtime state, spawn request/result, budget
  report, and optional-spawn policy for the MVP arena.
- P18-007: Add leash/reset/save fragments and prove rest, reload, death, and
  placed-boss persistence behavior.
- P18-008: Add group coordination component, attack-slot reservation, role
  assignment, simultaneous attacker cap, and three-enemy pressure proof.
- P18-009: Add boss move resolver, phase runtime state, boss quality profile,
  and first Draugr Warden quality report.
- P18-010: Wire P18 commandlets, dashboard evidence links, packaged smoke, and
  `Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P18/...` output folders.

Recommended implementation order:

1. P18-001 and P18-002 first. The team needs a resolved enemy snapshot and a
   visible BT/BB contract before perception, moves, and encounters have a stable
   target.
2. P18-003 and P18-004 next. These remove the riskiest AI assumptions:
   player-0 targeting and hand-authored spacing.
3. P18-005 only after the first enemy can perceive and choose where to stand.
   A move matrix is less valuable if the target and position inputs are wrong.
4. P18-006 through P18-008 turn individual enemies into authored encounters.
5. P18-009 should reuse the same resolver, threat, EQS, move, encounter, and
   group contracts. Bosses are a stronger grammar over the same foundations, not
   a second AI architecture.
6. P18-010 closes the loop with commandlets, dashboard evidence, and packaged
   proof.

## 23. Content Data Pipeline, Registries, Import, Validation, and Fallback Debt

### 23.1 Player-facing goal

The player never sees the content pipeline directly, but they feel its quality in
every item, spell, enemy, boss, quest, reward, and UI tooltip.

The production promise:

- Designers can create and tune content in spreadsheets, DataTables,
  PrimaryDataAssets, Blueprint assets, and Editor tools without C++ changes once
  the native contract exists;
- runtime systems consume resolved data, not half-merged fallbacks scattered
  through gameplay classes;
- every row, asset, tag, soft reference, cue, montage, loot entry, and generated
  item can be validated before playtest;
- fallback content keeps the game playable, but fallback debt is visible and
  cannot silently ship as production content;
- data changes can be traced from source sheet/CSV to generated DataTable to
  resolved registry record to gameplay/UI proof.

This chapter is the spine under P13-P18. Damage, weapons, spells, itemization,
world zones, enemies, bosses, vendors, and rewards all rely on the same data
truth.

### 23.2 Current Ascentia state

Working pieces:

- `AscentiaTypes.h` already defines rich `FTableRowBase` rows such as
  `FAscentiaItemDefinitionRow`, `FAscentiaWeaponTypeRow`,
  `FAscentiaAffixRow`, `FAscentiaSkillDefinitionRow`,
  `FAscentiaLootTableRow`, `FAscentiaCraftingRecipeRow`,
  `FAscentiaItemNamePatternRow`, and `FAscentiaAttributeThresholdRow`;
- `UAscentiaItemDefinition`, `UAscentiaAffixDefinition`, and
  `UAscentiaLootTableAsset` are PrimaryDataAssets;
- `UAscentiaSpellDefinition`, `UAscentiaAbilitySet`, and
  `UAscentiaBossDefinition` continue the PrimaryDataAsset pattern for later
  systems;
- `UAscentiaItemRegistrySubsystem` registers native fallback items/affixes/loot
  and scans Asset Manager primary assets;
- `UAscentiaLootSubsystem`, `UAscentiaInventoryComponent`, character visuals,
  UI, and combat already ask the registry or resolved item definitions for
  parts of their truth;
- the affix-GE engine has a first slice through `UAscentiaAffixEffect`,
  `UAscentiaAffixEngineLibrary`, and modifier tags;
- architecture docs already identify `Ascentia Data.xlsx`, CSV exports, and
  generated DataTables as the intended scale path.

Production gaps:

- design spreadsheet/CSV data still does not fully drive the game;
- generated DataTables are not yet the active runtime source for the registry;
- row structs and PrimaryDataAssets are not reconciled into field-level resolved
  records;
- native fallback registry carries too much content authority;
- source-layer provenance is not exposed to Designers;
- no tracked `Tools/data/` sync command owns xlsx/CSV/DataTable import as one
  repeatable pipeline;
- no content registry report proves fallback, row, asset overlay, soft-reference
  health, loot distribution, or cook readiness;
- validation is described, but not yet formalized as a unified machine-readable
  and Designer-readable gate.

### 23.3 Production source-of-truth model

Use four layers:

```text
source workbook / sheet
  -> tracked CSV
  -> generated DataTable or CurveTable
  -> resolved registry read model
  -> runtime instance state
```

Rules:

- `.xlsx` is an authoring convenience, not the only source of truth;
- CSV is the diffable, reviewable source for bulk tabular content;
- generated DataTables are editor/cook artifacts and must be reproducible;
- PrimaryDataAssets own object references, soft references, bundles,
  presentation, notes, and handcrafted overrides;
- runtime instances own rolled, saved, player-specific state;
- gameplay code reads resolved records and runtime instances, not raw CSV or
  sheet files.

Unreal supports this shape well: DataTables import CSV into `FTableRowBase`
structs, Asset Manager discovers Primary Assets such as `UPrimaryDataAsset`,
Data Registries formalize ordered source/fallback behavior, and Data Validation
provides editor/commandlet gates. Ascentia does not have to adopt every engine
plugin immediately, but its local architecture should behave like those systems.

### 23.4 Source types and ownership

Source responsibilities:

```text
Tracked CSV
  bulk numbers, tags, row IDs, curve points, loot weights, stat curves

Generated DataTable / CurveTable
  Unreal editor/cook representation of CSV truth

PrimaryDataAsset
  object references, Blueprint classes, meshes, icons, montages, cues,
  Niagara/MetaSound refs, designer notes, asset bundles

Native fallback
  minimum playable smoke-test content only

Runtime instance
  rolled affixes, seed, durability, sockets, save ID, player state
```

Programmers own:

- row structs;
- registry merge code;
- validation rules;
- provenance metadata;
- runtime read models;
- save-compatible IDs;
- commandlets/reports.

Technical Designers own:

- sheet layout;
- CSV export;
- GameplayTag taxonomy;
- import manifests;
- Editor Utility Widgets;
- validation triage.

Designers own:

- row values;
- PrimaryDataAsset references;
- Blueprint ability/cue/presentation subclasses;
- balance curves;
- content notes;
- approval state.

### 23.5 Registry and merge policy

The local `UAscentiaItemRegistrySubsystem` should become the first production
resolved registry. Later systems can copy the pattern or share a generalized
`UAscentiaContentRegistrySubsystem`.

Precedence:

```text
native fallback
  < DataTable row
  < PrimaryDataAsset object refs
  < explicit PrimaryDataAsset stat override
  < runtime instance state
```

Do not let DataAssets silently override numeric rows. Every override must be
field-marked or profile-marked.

Resolved record:

```text
FAscentiaResolvedRecordHeader
  ContentTag
  RowName
  PrimaryAssetId
  SourceSetTag
  ContentVersion
  bUsesNativeFallback
  ValidationSeverity
  FieldSources

FAscentiaFieldSource
  FieldName
  SourceLayer: NativeFallback, DataTable, PrimaryAsset, AssetOverride, Runtime
  SourcePath
  SourceRow
  SourceAssetId
  Notes
```

Item resolved record:

```text
FAscentiaResolvedItemDefinition
  Header
  DisplayName
  Category
  QualityFloor
  EquipmentSlot
  StackLimit
  Weight
  VendorValue
  StatRequirements
  WeaponFamily
  WeaponTypeTag
  MovesetTags
  BaseDamagePackets
  DefenseProfile
  EquipEffects
  GrantedAbilitySet
  Icon
  WeaponMesh
  WornMesh
  GripTransform
  SocketProfile
  SwingTrailSystem
  CueProfile
```

This pattern should be repeated for spells, enemies, bosses, world zones, POIs,
vendors, objectives, and settings.

### 23.6 Data import scripts and settings

Required local structure:

```text
Tools/data/
  export_xlsx_to_csv.py
  import_csv_to_datatables.py
  sync_content_data.ps1
  validate_content_sources.py
  report_content_registry.py
  manifests/
    content_import_manifest.json
```

Unreal project settings:

```text
UAscentiaContentDataSettings
  SourceWorkbookPath
  CsvSourceRoot
  GeneratedDataTableRoot
  ImportManifest
  PrimaryAssetScanRoots
  ValidationProfiles
  EvidenceOutputRoot
  bAllowPrototypeFallbacks
  bFailCookOnFallbackDebt
```

Import manifest:

```text
FAscentiaContentImportEntry
  SourceName
  CsvPath
  DataTablePath
  RowStruct
  KeyField
  RequiredColumns
  OptionalColumns
  OwningSystemTag
  ValidationProfile
```

Script workflow:

```text
sync_content_data.ps1
  -> export_xlsx_to_csv.py
  -> validate_content_sources.py
  -> launch UnrealEditor-Cmd import_csv_to_datatables.py
  -> run content registry report
  -> run validation commandlet
  -> write evidence summary
```

Important rule: when Python touches Unreal assets, use Unreal's Python API and
editor asset tools, not raw filesystem moves. Unreal asset files contain object
references that are easy to break from outside the editor.

### 23.7 Folder and naming contract

Canonical content root:

```text
/Game/Ascentia/Design/
  DataTables/
    DT_Items_Melee
    DT_Items_Armor
    DT_WeaponTypes
    DT_Affixes
    DT_LootTables
    DT_Skills
    DT_Spells
    DT_EnemyStats
    DT_BossMoves
    DT_WorldZones
    DT_Vendors
    DT_Settings
  Items/
    PDA_Item_*
  Affixes/
    PDA_Affix_* only if asset-bearing affixes appear later
  AbilitySets/
    PDA_AbilitySet_*
  Spells/
    PDA_Spell_*
  Enemies/
    PDA_EnemyDefinition_*
  Bosses/
    PDA_BossDefinition_*
  World/
    PDA_WorldZone_*
    PDA_POI_*
  UI/
    PDA_SettingsDefinition_*
  Tools/
    EUW_AscentiaContentSync
    EUW_AscentiaResolvedContentBrowser
```

Naming:

- DataTables: `DT_<System>_<Slice>` when a table is broad, e.g.
  `DT_Items_Melee`;
- PrimaryDataAssets: `PDA_<Type>_<Name>`;
- GameplayTags are the stable gameplay IDs;
- row names are stable import keys and should not be presentation strings;
- redirects must be tracked when IDs change.

### 23.8 Validation layers

Validation must run at four layers.

Structural:

- CSV headers match manifest;
- row struct exists;
- required columns exist;
- row names are unique and stable;
- enum/text values parse;
- numeric values are finite;
- arrays/tag containers parse.

Reference:

- GameplayTags exist;
- PrimaryAsset IDs are registered;
- soft object paths resolve;
- Blueprint classes inherit from required bases;
- DataTable row handles point to existing rows;
- GameplayCue tags begin with `GameplayCue`;
- montage/notify names exist where referenced.

Semantic:

- item category matches equipment slot;
- weapon family has moveset and socket profile;
- spell delivery has targeting and cue profile;
- enemy move range matches behavior selection bands;
- boss phase thresholds are sorted;
- loot tables have nonzero reachable weights;
- affix pools are reachable by at least one item type;
- fallback-only content is allowed only in prototype scope.

Runtime proof:

- registry initializes without missing packages;
- resolved records expose field provenance;
- loot roll is deterministic by seed;
- sample item/spell/enemy/boss definitions resolve through row plus asset;
- UI displays resolved data;
- cook/package validation sees no unresolved production references.

### 23.9 Reports and evidence

Every content sync writes:

```text
Saved/Automation/Evidence/content_data/<timestamp>/
  report.json
  import_summary.json
  registry_summary.json
  fallback_debt.json
  validation_errors.json
  loot_distribution_samples.json
  resolved_record_samples.json
```

Report schema:

```text
FAscentiaContentDataReport
  Timestamp
  GitCommit
  ImportManifestVersion
  SourceWorkbookHash
  CsvHashes
  DataTablesImported
  PrimaryAssetsScanned
  ResolvedRecordCountsByType
  FallbackOnlyRecords
  PrototypeAllowedFallbacks
  ValidationErrors
  ValidationWarnings
  SoftReferenceFailures
  CookRiskSummary
  SampleProofs
```

Fallback debt:

```text
FAscentiaFallbackDebtEntry
  ContentTag
  System
  NativeFallbackSource
  MissingDataTableRow
  MissingPrimaryAsset
  UsedByScenarios
  Severity
  Owner
  TargetPhase
```

Fallback debt is not a shame list. It is how the team keeps the game playable
while seeing exactly what still needs authoring.

### 23.10 Designer tools

`EUW_AscentiaContentSync`:

- run export/import/validate;
- show manifest entries;
- show latest report;
- open broken rows/assets;
- compare current report with previous report.

`EUW_AscentiaResolvedContentBrowser`:

- search by GameplayTag, row, asset, or display name;
- show resolved field values and provenance;
- show fallback-only fields;
- preview item, spell, enemy, boss, vendor, and loot definitions;
- run sample loot rolls;
- open the source row or DataAsset.

`EUW_AscentiaRegistryDiffViewer`:

- compare two reports;
- show changed rows, removed rows, changed soft references, changed loot
  distributions, changed budget costs, and newly introduced fallback debt.

`EUW_AscentiaDataTableSchemaDoctor`:

- check row structs vs CSV headers;
- show added/removed/renamed fields;
- produce migration notes for Designers.

`EUW_AscentiaFallbackDebtReporter`:

- group fallback debt by phase, system, owner, and severity;
- separate prototype-allowed fallback from production-blocking fallback.

### 23.11 Save compatibility and migrations

Save files should store stable gameplay IDs and runtime instance state, not raw
row copies.

Save example:

```text
FAscentiaSavedItemInstance
  InstanceId
  ItemTag
  ItemSeed
  Quality
  PrefixAffixTags
  SuffixAffixTags
  AffixMagnitudes
  SocketedItemTags
  Durability
  ContentVersionSeen
```

Rules:

- row values can change between patches;
- saved instances resolve through current content unless a migration says to
  freeze or translate values;
- removed tags require redirects or migration entries;
- production content sync should produce a save-migration risk summary;
- no save field should depend on display text.

Migration manifest:

```text
FAscentiaContentMigrationEntry
  FromTag
  ToTag
  FromVersion
  ToVersion
  MigrationPolicy
  Notes
```

### 23.12 Interconnects

This pipeline touches:

- P13 damage/build math: damage schools, status payloads, stat curves, passive
  thresholds, resistance curves;
- P14 weapons: weapon type rows, action maps, combo chains, socket profiles,
  montage/cue references;
- P15 magic: spells, schools, cast/delivery profiles, summons, flask upgrades;
- P16 itemization: items, affixes, loot, crafting, vendors, upgrades, economy;
- P17 world: zones, POIs, Data Layers, discovery, map markers, world tier;
- P18 AI: enemy stats, perception profiles, movement profiles, boss moves,
  encounter budgets;
- P19 save/death: stable IDs, content migrations, character identity, death
  echo references;
- P20 presentation: cues, icons, audio, VFX, surface profiles, readability;
- P21 shell/settings: settings rows, localization text, input glyph rows,
  package validation;
- P22 multiplayer: replicated content IDs, anti-cheat-sensitive data, server
  authority, client prediction.

### 23.13 Proof gates

The content pipeline is not production-ready until:

1. `Tools/data/sync_content_data.ps1` can run from a clean checkout and produce
   CSV/DataTable/import/registry reports.
2. `UAscentiaContentDataSettings` owns data source paths and is visible in
   Project Settings or an equivalent Editor-facing settings asset.
3. `UAscentiaItemRegistrySubsystem` ingests DataTables between native fallback
   and scanned PrimaryDataAssets.
4. Resolved records expose source-layer provenance per field.
5. `EUW_AscentiaContentSync` and `EUW_AscentiaResolvedContentBrowser` expose the
   pipeline to Designers.
6. Data validation catches malformed rows, missing tags, unresolved soft refs,
   invalid loot entries, invalid category/slot pairs, missing cue/montage refs,
   and production fallback debt.
7. Reports are written under `Saved/Automation/Evidence/content_data/...`.
8. A remote proof verifies fallback, row, asset overlay, deterministic loot,
   inventory UI display, equip effect application, and no missing package
   warnings.
9. Save migration risk is reported when tags or row IDs are removed/renamed.
10. Dashboard links the latest content data report and fallback debt report.

Remote proof names:

```text
content_data_sync
content_registry_resolution
content_fallback_debt
content_validation_gate
loot_distribution_report
save_content_migration
```

### 23.14 P23 starter implementation package

This is not a numbered gameplay phase in the same way as P12-P22, but it should
be treated like a production phase because it is the spine underneath all
content-heavy systems. The starter package should prove one repeatable path:
source workbook or CSV -> generated DataTable -> scanned PrimaryDataAsset ->
resolved record -> runtime proof -> Designer-visible evidence.

Reference posture:

- Use Unreal DataTables and CurveTables for bulk, diffable numeric and row data.
  Rows should be `FTableRowBase` structs with stable row names and stable
  gameplay identifiers.
- Use Asset Manager and Primary Assets for object-heavy content, soft-reference
  discovery, bundle loading, cook awareness, and eventual chunking rules.
- Shape local registry behavior around Data Registry principles: ordered
  sources, explicit fallback/override policy, sync and async access where
  needed, and source-order evidence.
- Use Data Validation for asset and row correctness gates. Validation should be
  callable from Editor workflows, commandlets, and package profiles.
- Use Asset Registry scans for editor-side reports and unloaded asset inspection
  instead of loading every asset just to count or classify it.
- Keep native fallback content small, visible, and phase-approved. Fallback
  should keep smoke tests alive, not become the hidden content pipeline.

Runtime module layout:

```text
Source/Ascentia/Public/Data/AscentiaContentDataSettings.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Data/AscentiaContentSourceTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Data/AscentiaResolvedRecordTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Data/AscentiaContentRegistrySubsystem.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Data/AscentiaContentRegistryReportTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Data/AscentiaFallbackDebtTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Data/AscentiaContentMigrationTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Data/AscentiaContentValidationTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Private/Data/AscentiaContentDataSettings.cpp
Source/Ascentia/Private/Data/AscentiaContentRegistrySubsystem.cpp
Source/Ascentia/Private/Data/AscentiaResolvedRecordTypes.cpp
Source/Ascentia/Private/Data/AscentiaContentRegistryReports.cpp
```

Editor module layout:

```text
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Data/AscentiaContentImportManifest.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Data/AscentiaContentSourceValidator.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Data/AscentiaDataTableSchemaValidator.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Data/AscentiaPrimaryAssetReferenceValidator.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Data/AscentiaFallbackDebtValidator.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Data/AscentiaContentImportManifest.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Data/AscentiaContentSourceValidator.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Data/AscentiaDataTableSchemaValidator.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Data/AscentiaPrimaryAssetReferenceValidator.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Data/AscentiaFallbackDebtValidator.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Commandlets/AscentiaContentDataSyncCommandlet.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Commandlets/AscentiaContentRegistryAuditCommandlet.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Commandlets/AscentiaFallbackDebtAuditCommandlet.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Commandlets/AscentiaSaveContentMigrationAuditCommandlet.cpp
```

Tooling layout:

```text
Tools/data/export_xlsx_to_csv.py
Tools/data/validate_content_sources.py
Tools/data/import_csv_to_datatables.py
Tools/data/report_content_registry.py
Tools/data/report_fallback_debt.py
Tools/data/sync_content_data.ps1
Tools/data/manifests/content_import_manifest.json
Tools/data/manifests/content_source_manifest.json
Tools/data/manifests/content_migration_manifest.json
Tools/data/samples/Ascentia_Data_Sample.xlsx
Tools/data/samples/csv/items_mvp.csv
Tools/data/samples/csv/weapon_types_mvp.csv
Tools/data/samples/csv/loot_tables_mvp.csv
```

Tests and proof helpers:

```text
Source/AscentiaTests/Private/Data/AscentiaContentDataSettingsTests.cpp
Source/AscentiaTests/Private/Data/AscentiaResolvedRecordTests.cpp
Source/AscentiaTests/Private/Data/AscentiaContentRegistryTests.cpp
Source/AscentiaTests/Private/Data/AscentiaFallbackDebtTests.cpp
Source/AscentiaTests/Private/Data/AscentiaContentMigrationTests.cpp
Tools/remote_playtest.py scenarios: content_data_sync,
  content_registry_resolution, content_fallback_debt, content_validation_gate,
  loot_distribution_report, save_content_migration
```

The first runtime ownership split:

```text
UAscentiaContentDataSettings : UDeveloperSettings
  Stores source roots, generated DataTable roots, scan roots, manifests,
  validation profiles, evidence roots, and fallback policy.

UAscentiaContentRegistrySubsystem : UGameInstanceSubsystem
  Owns resolved read models for shared content families and exposes typed lookup
  by GameplayTag, row name, and PrimaryAssetId. It can delegate item-specific
  behavior to the existing item registry until the shared registry is proven.

UAscentiaItemRegistrySubsystem
  Keeps gameplay-facing item APIs but changes its source order to native
  fallback -> DataTable rows -> PrimaryDataAsset refs/marked overrides ->
  runtime instance. It writes item registry and fallback debt reports.

UAscentiaContentValidationLibrary : UBlueprintFunctionLibrary
  Exposes read-only validation helpers for EUWs and commandlets.
```

Starter Project Settings:

```text
UAscentiaContentDataSettings
  SourceWorkbookPath = SourceData/Ascentia Data.xlsx
  CsvSourceRoot = SourceData/CSV
  GeneratedDataTableRoot = /Game/Ascentia/Design/DataTables
  ImportManifest = Tools/data/manifests/content_import_manifest.json
  SourceManifest = Tools/data/manifests/content_source_manifest.json
  MigrationManifest = Tools/data/manifests/content_migration_manifest.json
  PrimaryAssetScanRoots =
    /Game/Ascentia/Design/Items
    /Game/Ascentia/Design/Spells
    /Game/Ascentia/Design/AI
    /Game/Ascentia/Design/Encounters
    /Game/Ascentia/Design/Bosses
  EvidenceOutputRoot = Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P23/ContentData
  bAllowPrototypeFallbacks = true
  bFailCookOnFallbackDebt = false for Editor, true for Shipping profiles
```

Starter manifest entries:

```text
items_mvp
  CsvPath = SourceData/CSV/items_mvp.csv
  DataTablePath = /Game/Ascentia/Design/DataTables/DT_Items_MVP
  RowStruct = FAscentiaItemDefinitionRow
  KeyField = ItemTag
  OwningSystemTag = Ascentia.System.Itemization
  ValidationProfile = PDA_ContentValidationProfile_Items_MVP

weapon_types_mvp
  CsvPath = SourceData/CSV/weapon_types_mvp.csv
  DataTablePath = /Game/Ascentia/Design/DataTables/DT_WeaponTypes_MVP
  RowStruct = FAscentiaWeaponTypeRow
  KeyField = WeaponTypeTag
  OwningSystemTag = Ascentia.System.Weapons
  ValidationProfile = PDA_ContentValidationProfile_Weapons_MVP

loot_tables_mvp
  CsvPath = SourceData/CSV/loot_tables_mvp.csv
  DataTablePath = /Game/Ascentia/Design/DataTables/DT_LootTables_MVP
  RowStruct = FAscentiaLootTableRow
  KeyField = LootTableTag
  OwningSystemTag = Ascentia.System.Loot
  ValidationProfile = PDA_ContentValidationProfile_Loot_MVP
```

Starter DataAssets and DataTables:

```text
/Game/Ascentia/Design/DataTables/DT_Items_MVP
/Game/Ascentia/Design/DataTables/DT_WeaponTypes_MVP
/Game/Ascentia/Design/DataTables/DT_Affixes_MVP
/Game/Ascentia/Design/DataTables/DT_LootTables_MVP
/Game/Ascentia/Design/DataTables/DT_Spells_MVP
/Game/Ascentia/Design/DataTables/DT_EnemyStats_MVP
/Game/Ascentia/Design/DataTables/DT_ContentRedirects_MVP
```

Editor Utility Widgets:

```text
EUW_AscentiaContentSync
  Buttons: Dry Run, Import CSV, Rebuild Registry Preview, Run Validation,
  Open Latest Report. Shows manifest rows, changed sources, generated assets,
  and failures with click-through links.

EUW_AscentiaResolvedContentBrowser
  Search by tag, row, asset, display name, or owning phase. Shows field values,
  source layer, fallback state, soft refs, preview icon/mesh/cue, and sample
  runtime lookup.

EUW_AscentiaRegistryDiffViewer
  Compares two registry reports. Shows changed resolved values, changed source
  layers, new fallback debt, removed records, and loot distribution deltas.

EUW_AscentiaDataTableSchemaDoctor
  Compares CSV headers, manifest columns, row structs, and DataTable assets.
  Emits migration notes and blocks silent column drift.

EUW_AscentiaFallbackDebtReporter
  Groups fallback by system, phase, owner, severity, and proof dependency.
  Separates prototype-approved debt from package-blocking debt.
```

Validation profiles:

```text
PDA_ContentValidationProfile_Items_MVP
  RequiredTags: ItemTag, CategoryTag
  RequiredRefs: icon optional in prototype, mesh required for equipment
  SemanticRules: category/slot compatibility, stack limit, value, weight
  FallbackPolicy: prototype fallback allowed only for MVP smoke items

PDA_ContentValidationProfile_Loot_MVP
  RequiredTags: LootTableTag
  SemanticRules: reachable weights, no empty production table, deterministic
    sample rolls by seed, reward value envelope

PDA_ContentValidationProfile_Weapons_MVP
  RequiredTags: WeaponTypeTag, WeaponFamilyTag
  RequiredRefs: action map, socket profile, cue profile, visible weapon mesh
```

P23 starter tickets:

- P23-001: Add `UAscentiaContentDataSettings`, config defaults, Project
  Settings visibility, and settings snapshot report.
- P23-002: Add content import/source/migration manifests and `Tools/data`
  dry-run validation scripts.
- P23-003: Add shared resolved-record header, field-source trace, lookup
  request/result, and registry report structs.
- P23-004: Teach `UAscentiaItemRegistrySubsystem` to ingest MVP DataTables
  between native fallbacks and PrimaryDataAssets while preserving current
  playable behavior.
- P23-005: Add fallback debt structs, report writer, fallback policy asset, and
  `AscentiaFallbackDebtAudit`.
- P23-006: Add `AscentiaContentRegistryAudit` with field provenance samples,
  soft-reference checks, GameplayTag checks, and cook-risk summary.
- P23-007: Add `EUW_AscentiaContentSync` and `EUW_AscentiaResolvedContentBrowser`
  with read-only preview first, mutation buttons disabled until dry-run reports
  are trusted.
- P23-008: Add DataTable schema validator and registry diff viewer.
- P23-009: Add save-content migration risk audit using P19 save fixtures and
  content redirect rows.
- P23-010: Wire content data, registry, fallback debt, and migration reports into
  dashboard evidence and package profiles.

Recommended implementation order:

1. P23-001 through P23-003 first. Settings, manifests, and resolved-record
   traces are the contract that every content family will reuse.
2. P23-004 next, using items because Ascentia already has native fallback items,
   PrimaryDataAssets, loot, inventory UI, and package proofs.
3. P23-005 and P23-006 then make fallback debt and registry health visible.
4. P23-007 and P23-008 expose the system to Designers without granting unsafe
   write buttons too early.
5. P23-009 should run before broad save/death-loop work because content ID
   removal and redirects are save compatibility problems.
6. P23-010 closes the loop with dashboard/package evidence.

### 23.15 Art asset acceptance, material, LOD, collision, and kit production package

P23 also needs a gate for non-tabular content. A sword mesh, armor piece,
ruined-wall kit, foliage cluster, landscape stamp, VFX prop, or marketplace pack
can look useful while still being unsafe for production: wrong folder, unknown
license, bad pivot, no collision, wrong socket, unbounded material cost, missing
physical surface, broken LOD/Nanite policy, no Data Layer/HLOD assignment, or no
proof that it cooks.

This package defines how art assets graduate:

```text
external source / generated output / hand-authored DCC export
  -> staging package
  -> provenance record
  -> normalization pass
  -> visual/material/collision/LOD validation
  -> Designer acceptance
  -> production folder promotion
  -> registry/cook/evidence proof
```

Engine-facing posture:

- use Nanite for appropriate high-detail static geometry, but still record
  fallback, material, collision, foliage, and platform policies;
- use explicit Static Mesh LODs or UE automatic LOD generation where Nanite is
  not appropriate;
- use custom UCX collision for meshes with gameplay-important openings, ledges,
  traversal, or concavity;
- use sockets and pivots as authored gameplay data, not incidental DCC leftovers;
- use parameterized parent materials and Material Instances for art-direction
  variation without recompiling or forking parent shaders;
- keep material/weather/physical-surface response connected to P17 world, P20
  presentation, P14 weapons, P16 itemization, and P18 enemy/world interaction.

Runtime/editor files:

```text
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Art/
```


Promotion stages:

```text
Staging
  raw imported/generated/marketplace content, no gameplay references allowed

Normalized
  renamed, moved under staging taxonomy, source/provenance attached, redirectors
  fixed, obvious import errors resolved

ValidatedPrototype
  usable in prototype maps and smoke tests; warnings allowed when owner approved

ProductionCandidate
  passes acceptance profile except final human art direction verdict

ProductionAccepted
  promoted to production folder, registry/provenance report written, cook proof
  exists, and dashboard card links latest evidence
```

Policy rules:

- gameplay systems may not hard-reference `Art/Staging` assets;
- generated or marketplace assets must carry a source record before promotion;
- every accepted Static Mesh has an explicit Nanite/LOD decision, not an
  inherited default;
- traversal, camera-blocking, projectile-blocking, weapon-hit, and pickup assets
  must use named collision policy;
- modular kit pieces must declare grid, pivot, connector, snap, and collision
  continuity policy;
- weapons and armor must declare socket/mount/fit preview policy before they can
  be used by item definitions;
- parent materials live in approved parent folders; production variants should
  usually be Material Instances;
- physical material/surface response must be declared for walkable, hittable, or
  impactable surfaces;
- UDS/weather-responsive assets must declare wetness/snow/dust response or opt
  out with an explicit reason;
- every promotion writes a dry-run plan before moving or mutating assets.

Designer-facing tools:

```text
EUW_AscentiaArtAcceptanceBoard
  Lists staging assets, source records, acceptance stage, owner system, blocking
  findings, human verdict, and promotion eligibility.

EUW_AscentiaPackIngestor
  Imports or registers external packs into staging, attaches provenance, scans
  redirectors/dependencies, and refuses production references.

EUW_AscentiaMeshPolicyDoctor
  Shows Nanite/LOD decision, material slots, collision policy, sockets, pivot,
  bounds, scale, Data Layer/HLOD readiness, and cook warnings.

EUW_AscentiaMaterialSurfaceLab
  Previews material instances under clear, dusk, rain, snow, torch, hit impact,
  footstep, and blood/dust contexts.

EUW_AscentiaKitAssemblyPreview
  Snaps modular pieces on the kit grid, checks connectors, collision continuity,
  nav continuity, camera passage, and silhouette repetition.

EUW_AscentiaArtPromotionPlanner
  Builds a transaction-aware dry-run move/rename/fixup plan from staging to
  production folders with source-control and redirector warnings.
```

Implementation tickets:

```text
P23A-001 Art source/provenance record
  Add `FAscentiaArtSourceRecord`, staging manifest, source hash, license/vendor
  note, and source record validation.

P23A-002 Art acceptance profiles and stage model
  Add `PDA_ArtAcceptanceProfile_*`, asset stage enum, owner system tags,
  promotion blockers, and human verdict fields.

P23A-003 Mesh policy audit
  Add Nanite/LOD decision checks, material slot limits, socket/pivot checks,
  collision policy checks, Data Layer/HLOD readiness, and mesh report output.

P23A-004 Material surface audit
  Add parent material/Material Instance policy, required parameter checks,

P23A-005 Modular kit assembly preview
  Add kit policy, grid/snap/pivot/connector validation, nav/collision continuity
  proof, and screenshot matrix.

P23A-006 Weapon/armor fit acceptance bridge
  Connect accepted weapon/armor meshes to P14 weapon mount lab and P19 character
  creator fit/physics preview before item definitions can use them.

P23A-007 Promotion planner
  Add dry-run move/rename/fixup plans, source-control warnings, redirector
  checks, and transaction-aware approved promotion.

P23A-008 Dashboard and package gate
  Add art acceptance cards, staging-reference blockers, production-blocking
  finding counts, and package-profile failure on unsafe production references.
```

Recommended sequencing:

1. Build P23A-001 and P23A-002 before importing more external packs.
2. Add P23A-003 next because collision/LOD/Nanite mistakes become gameplay and
   performance bugs quickly.
3. Add P23A-004 before UDS/weather adoption spreads material variants.
4. Add P23A-005 when the first modular zone kit starts replacing placeholder
   arena geometry.
5. Add P23A-006 before Asset Factory armor/weapon output becomes default gear.
6. Add P23A-007 and P23A-008 once the dry-run reports are trusted.

### 23.16 Surface response, weather material, footstep, impact, and hazard package

This package turns the P23 material-surface audit into a runtime production
contract. The art gate can prove that an imported asset has an acceptable
Physical Material and Material Instance policy, but the shipped game also needs
the consequences of that surface to be consistent: footsteps, weapon impacts,
projectile puffs, decals, snow, wetness, dust, elemental status, kill volumes,
rot pools, accessibility variants, and PCG/Landscry surface provenance should
all resolve through the same authored profile layer.

Primary references:

- Unreal Physical Materials and Surface Types:
  <https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/physical-materials-user-guide-for-unreal-engine>
- Material Parameter Collections for global wetness/snow/destruction-style
  parameters:
  <https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/using-material-parameter-collections-in-unreal-engine>
- Decal Materials for projected impact/blood/scorch responses:
  <https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/decal-materials-in-unreal-engine>
- Niagara as the VFX authoring/runtime layer for footsteps, impacts, dust, rain,
  snow, and hazard bursts:
  <https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/overview-of-niagara-effects-for-unreal-engine>

The rule: gameplay never asks "what sound/decal/particle should stone play?"
directly. Gameplay asks the surface response layer with a context payload. The
surface response layer resolves profile data, sends presentation requests to
P20, sends hazard/status damage through P13, updates global weather material
state through the P17/P20 atmosphere bridge, and writes evidence.

Runtime code:

```text
Source/Ascentia/Public/Surfaces/AscentiaSurfaceTypes.h
```

```text
FAscentiaEnvironmentalHazardProfile

  ExitCue

FAscentiaHazardDamageRequest
  Instigator
  Victim
  HazardTag
  DamageSchoolTag
  StatusPayloadTag
  Magnitude
  SurfaceTag

  AuthorityContext

FAscentiaSurfaceResponseReport
  SurfacesExpected
  SurfacesFound
  MissingPhysicalMaterials
  MissingFootstepProfiles
  MissingImpactProfiles
  MissingWeatherResponses
  MissingHazardDamageRoutes
  MissingAccessibilityProfiles
  PCGDefaultSurfaceLeaks
  BlockingFindings
```

Runtime policy:

- `UAscentiaSurfaceResponseSubsystem` is the facade. Animation notifies,
  collision hits, projectile hits, traversal probes, footstep events, and hazard
  volumes call it with a context struct.
- `SurfaceType_Default` is never valid for production world geometry unless the
  profile explicitly marks the asset as silent/decorative.
- Physical Materials map to Gameplay Tags in `PDA_PhysicalSurfaceMap_MVP`; code
  should not switch directly on raw enum values outside the resolver.
- Footstep and impact requests return presentation requests, not raw Niagara or
  audio spawns. P20 then applies concurrency, settings, accessibility intensity,
  and budget policy.
- Hazard damage goes through the P13 damage packet/status pipeline. Hazard
  volumes cannot mutate health directly.
- Weather and biome changes set authored parameters on Material Parameter
  Collections or approved per-instance overrides; freeform material parameter
  mutation is rejected by the audit.
- PCG/Landscry imported surfaces must retain provenance and surface coverage so
  generated terrain does not silently fall back to generic stone.
- AI pathing can read hazard policy as cost/blocked/ignored, but damage remains
  authority-owned gameplay.
- Accessibility variants can suppress decals, reduce particle burst size, lower
  flash intensity, and provide clearer audio/visual substitutes without changing
  combat math.

Designer surfaces:

```text
EUW_AscentiaSurfaceResponseLab
  Shows Physical Material -> SurfaceTag -> response profile mapping, missing
  coverage, preview fixtures, and blocking package findings.

EUW_AscentiaFootstepAndImpactMatrix
  Previews walk/run/roll/land footsteps and weapon/projectile impacts across
  Stone, Dirt, Mud, Wood, Metal, Water, Flesh, Rot, and Snow surfaces.

EUW_AscentiaWeatherMaterialResponseLab
  Previews rain, snow, ash, and interior/dry transitions through Material
  Parameter Collections, screenshot fixtures, and required material parameters.

EUW_AscentiaHazardScenarioLab
  Runs rot pool, fall kill volume, fire patch, poison cloud, and Below hazard
  fixtures through P13 damage/status and P20 presentation requests.
```

Evidence:

```text
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P23/Surfaces/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P23/Footsteps/{RunId}/matrix.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P23/Impacts/{RunId}/matrix.json
```

Implementation tickets:

```text
P23S-001 Surface id/profile structs and physical material map
  Add `FAscentiaSurfaceId`, physical surface map asset, Gameplay Tag mapping,
  and validation that production assets do not leak default surfaces.

P23S-002 Surface response subsystem facade
```
